Balangan.name: Balangan (Chinese: 巴兰干; Indonesian: Pantai Balangan)
Balangan.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Approximately 14-16 km from Ngurah Rai (DPS) Airport (around 30-40 min by car)
Balangan.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~65-80 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: ~15-20 min by scooter
Balangan.wave_type: Left-hand reef break / point break with multiple sections; long fast walls and occasional barrels on bigger days
Balangan.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced primary; Beginners only on very small high-tide days; Pro on overhead-double-overhead
Balangan.wave_height_range: 1-10 ft (works from 1 ft up to double overhead); sweet spot 3-6 ft for intermediates
Balangan.bottom_type: Reef (flat coral shelf); shallow inside section that can be exposed at low tide
Balangan.best_season: Dry season, April/May to October (peak: June-September); most consistent in July
Balangan.best_tide: Mid to high tide (above ~1.4 m) for safest surf; works all tides but low exposes shallow reef and walking access becomes tricky
Balangan.best_wind: Offshore from East to East-Southeast (E-SE trade winds)
Balangan.best_swell_direction: Southwest (SW) primary; South (S) and SSW also produce clean lines
Balangan.swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 during dry season - one of the most consistent Bukit breaks; ~99% rideable in July [uncertain]
Balangan.wave_length_meters: Typically 100-200 m rides; on rare large clean days, multiple sections can link for 300+ m
Balangan.wave_speed_rating: Fast - flat reef means most waves run fast and don't barrel often except on bigger powerful swells
Balangan.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high - shifting peaks require positioning; drop is manageable but section ahead breaks quickly demanding immediate trim line
Balangan.secondary_peaks: Yes - main outside peak plus shifting inside sections; on bigger days outside lineup pushes further out, more peaks open along the reef
Balangan.peak_crowd_level: 4/5 - popular and busy in July-August peak season; spreads across long reef so early sessions or paddling wide can find space
Balangan.access: Drive to Balangan Beach via Jalan Pantai Balangan in Jimbaran/Pecatu; descend cliff stairs (~150 steps) to the beach; walk along the sand to the lineup; at low tide walk across the reef carefully near the headland
Balangan.paddle_out_method: Paddle out from the sand near the headland to use the keyhole/channel; at lower tides walk along the reef edge to a deeper jump-off point; booties recommended at low tide
Balangan.entry_fee: Entrance fee ~IDR 5,000 per person (sometimes waived); parking IDR 3,000 motorbike / IDR 5,000 car
Balangan.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-8:00 am) when winds are lightest and crowds thinnest; sunset session 4-6pm
Balangan.best_time_of_day: Before 8am to avoid surf-school groups and tourist crowds; after 4pm when day-trippers leave
Balangan.nearby_warung: Multiple beachfront warungs along the sand: Froggy Warung, La Joya warung, La Brisa, Flapjacks, fresh coconut and grilled fish stalls; cliff-top cafes like Sunday Beach Club and La Joya Biu Biu
Balangan.alternative_spot_within_10min: Dreamland (easier beach break), Bingin (mid-tide left reef), Padang Padang (high tide), Impossibles (longer paddle), Nyang Nyang on bigger swells
Balangan.hazards: Sharp shallow coral reef especially at low tide, strong currents on big days, shallow middle section with hidden boils, occasional sea urchins, cliff staircase slippery when wet
Balangan.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (bulu babi) on inside reef, occasional jellyfish in shoulder seasons, no significant stonefish reports; treat reef cuts with antiseptic and oral antibiotics if deep
Balangan.reef_cut_severity_index: Medium-high - flat coral reef with sharp ridges; mid-section can be deceptively shallow at low tide and has cut many surfers and dinged many boards
Balangan.rip_current_pattern: Channel current runs along the reef edge toward the outside; sweeping rip near the headland can pull surfers north; identifiable by foam lines moving offshore
Balangan.lineup_etiquette: Respect priority and rotation; locals expect courteous behaviour; don't paddle around to snake the peak; greet other surfers; share waves on crowded days
Balangan.localism_intensity: Low to medium - generally welcoming due to tourist popularity but can tense up on perfect crowded days with disrespectful visitors
Balangan.respect_protocol: Wait your turn, no dropping in, acknowledge local surfers, tip warungs/parking attendants, do not bring glass or leave trash on the sand/reef
Balangan.resident_local_legends: Many Bukit/Jimbaran-based locals surf here including up-and-coming groms; not specifically associated with one iconic figure [uncertain]
Balangan.religious_sensitivity: Respect daily Balinese offerings (canang sari) on the sand and at cliff shrines; Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - beach closed and no surfing; cover up when leaving the beach via villages
Balangan.medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (closest 24/7 with surf injury experience), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua, Siloam Hospital Denpasar
Balangan.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu ~4-5 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~10 km; Siloam Denpasar ~17 km
Balangan.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; informal help between surfers; lifeguard presence inconsistent [uncertain]
Balangan.emergency_contact: Indonesia emergency: 112; Bali Search and Rescue (Basarnas): +62 361 9351111; Unicare Pecatu: +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
Balangan.nearest_surf_school: Balangan Surf School (on the beach), local instructors with softboards on the sand, Rip Curl School of Surf (Dreamland branch nearby), Odyssey Surf School
Balangan.board_rental_availability: Available on the beach from local stalls; softboards, longboards and shortboards; from ~IDR 50,000-150,000/day depending on board
Balangan.surf_photographer_available: Yes - cliff-top land photographers regularly shoot Balangan; some water photographers during peak season
Balangan.photographer_recommendation: Local freelancers offer photo sets ~IDR 200,000-400,000; advance booking via Instagram common [uncertain]
Balangan.accommodation_options: Balangan Surf Resort, La Joya Balangan, Balangan Paradise Hostel, The Palm Grove, Mu Bali Bingin, numerous homestays and surf camps in Pecatu/Jimbaran
Balangan.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/balangan/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d3a (Surfline cam, may require subscription); https://balicams.com cam for Balangan [uncertain - availability]
Balangan.drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for personal recreational use; avoid flying over crowds and resort properties; commercial filming requires permits
Balangan.coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu, Drifter Surf Cafe, Suka Espresso (Uluwatu area) all ~15-20 min by scooter
Balangan.avg_lesson_price: ~IDR 350,000 for 1.5-hour beginner lesson on small days; private/intermediate lessons IDR 600,000-1,000,000
Balangan.board_rental_daily: IDR 50,000-150,000 per day (softboard cheapest); shortboards IDR 100,000-200,000
Balangan.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season - clear blue water; can degrade slightly in wet season due to runoff and floating debris
Balangan.annual_competition_window: Hosts regional and grom contests occasionally; not a primary WSL CT venue; surrounding Bukit competitions June-September [uncertain]
Balangan.community_events: Sunset gatherings at beachfront warungs; nearby Single Fin Sunday (Uluwatu) is the regional weekly gathering; informal sunset surfs and beach barbecues

basic_info.name: Balian (Chinese: 巴里安; Indonesian: Pantai Balian / Suraberata)
basic_info.gps_location: Approx. -8.5917, 114.9700 (Pantai Balian, Lalanglinggah, Selemadeg Barat, Tabanan Regency, Bali 82162; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Balian) [uncertain]
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~70-75 km (approx. 1.5-2 hours drive depending on traffic)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-100 min by motorbike (~50 km); From Uluwatu: ~120-150 min by motorbike (~80 km)

surf_conditions.wave_type: River-mouth break with both left and right peaks over cobblestone and sand bottom; the left typically runs longer and more reliable while the right is steeper and can pitch into a barrel; an additional beach-break section forms south of the rivermouth at low tide
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate primary; accessible to advanced beginners on smaller days; advanced surfers find punchy barrel sections on bigger swells
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-8 ft; works well at 2-6 ft (head-high range); manageable up to overhead+ for advanced surfers
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Cobblestone with sand deposits from the river mouth; not sharp reef - more forgiving than Bukit reefs
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season April-October (peak May-September); fairly consistent year-round, often working when south Bukit is flat; avoid first storms of wet season due to pollution
surf_conditions.best_tide: Works on all tides; mid tide considered best for organised peaks; low tide produces beach-break section at the river mouth; high tide softens the wave
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore N to NE; early morning land breeze ideal; SE trade winds cross-shore by mid-morning
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SW (similar window to Medewi and Bukit); responsive to most Indian Ocean groundswells
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (one of the most consistent breaks in Bali - 'ultra consistent' year-round; rideable when much of the island goes flat)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 50-150 meters typical (lefts run longer than rights; multiple connecting sections on the best sandbank days) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium (more punch than Medewi; lefts can wall up fast on bigger days; rights pitch quickly into barrel sections)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Low to moderate - friendly cobble/sand bottom (not razor reef), but shifting peaks require positioning; rivermouth current can pull surfers off the peak
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - distinct left peak, right peak, and beach-break section at the rivermouth south of the main peak; channel between left and right makes paddle-out easy
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 2-3 (generally low; busier on weekends and during epic swells with traveling surfers and small surf-camp groups; far less crowded than Canggu/Bukit)

practical_info.access: Drive west on Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk; turn south at Lalanglinggah/Suraberata toward Pantai Balian; park at the cliff-top area or beachfront warungs/guesthouses; walk down the path/stairs to the beach
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Easy paddle-out through the channel between the left and right peaks (deep water between them); for the rivermouth section, paddle out along the southern beach edge
practical_info.entry_fee: No formal beach entry fee; parking IDR 5,000-10,000 motorbike / IDR 10,000-20,000 car at beachfront warungs
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for offshore winds and uncrowded peaks; late afternoon glass-off as winds soften
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~9 AM; second window late afternoon; avoid mid-day when SE trade winds disrupt the surface
practical_info.nearby_warung: Pondok Pitaya Beach Restaurant, Sushi Surf Balian, Deki's Warung, Tekor Bali, Balian Beach Cafe, Made's Homestay warung - all clustered around the beachfront and clifftop area
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Limited within 10 min - Balian itself has multiple peaks (left, right, rivermouth beach break); Soka Beach and Kelating ~20-30 min east for backup beach-break options
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - direct beach access via short walk from parking

safety_culture.hazards: Strong rivermouth current especially after rain, occasional bull shark sightings (historic attacks reported), pollution from stormwater runoff after rain, isolated bigger-day rip currents, slippery cobblestones at entry/exit
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Occasional bull sharks reported off the coast (rare attacks over the past decade especially in murky water at dawn/dusk near the rivermouth); sea urchins on rocky sections; occasional jellyfish; rare sea snake sightings
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Low - cobblestone and sand bottom not razor reef; some cuts possible from barnacle-covered rocks but generally one of the more forgiving Bali bottoms
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Rivermouth current drains south and offshore - useful as paddle-out lane but strong after rain; channel between left and right peaks acts as natural paddle-out; side current can pull surfers toward the rivermouth
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Relaxed compared to Bukit but still observe priority; defer to local Indonesians and regulars; do not snake; greet locals; rotation respected on the left point
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low - sleepy village atmosphere; friendly hosts; minimal localism pressure; vibe described as reminiscent of pre-tourism Bali 1970s
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Be friendly, support local warungs and homestays, respect Hindu temple ceremonies in nearby Lalanglinggah, do not paddle out drunk or disrespect locals; observe priority and let regulars get waves
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Local Tabanan surfers based at Pondok Pitaya and Balian Surf Camp; longstanding instructor/guide community at Casa Balian and Balian Spirit [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Hindu Balinese village (unlike Muslim Medewi/Yeh Sumbul); nearby temples include Pura Dang Kahyangan Gading Wani, Luhur Srijong Temple (10 min east) and Pura Tanah Lot region further east; respect temple ceremonies (odalan), modest dress when entering temple grounds, women on menstruation traditionally avoid temples; Nyepi (Day of Silence ~March) - no surfing/beach access islandwide
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Klinik/Puskesmas Selemadeg (basic primary care, ~10-15 min); RSUD Tabanan / Tabanan Hospital (~45-60 min east); BIMC and Siloam Denpasar ~1.5-2 hours east for serious cases
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: ~5-10 km to Puskesmas Selemadeg; ~30-40 km to Tabanan Hospital
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No permanent rescue jet-ski; rely on self-rescue and surf-camp instructors
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Police 110; Ambulance 118/119; Tourist Police +62-361-754599 (Bali) [uncertain on most current numbers]

services.nearest_surf_school: Balian Surf Camp (long-running lessons and board rentals), Pondok Pitaya Surf School (hotel-based, all levels), Casa Balian, Balian Spirit Yoga & Surf, Bali Wave Hunter, Pacha Balian Surf Camp
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - readily available from Balian Surf Camp, Pondok Pitaya, beachfront shops and homestays; ~IDR 80,000-150,000/day for shortboards/soft-tops
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - freelance land photographers shoot from the clifftop and beach especially during dry-season swells; less developed scene than Uluwatu but present [uncertain]
services.photographer_recommendation: Inquire at Pondok Pitaya or Balian Surf Camp; freelance shooters via Instagram; typical session IDR 150,000-400,000 [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Pondok Pitaya Hotel Surfing & Yoga (mid-high, beachfront favorite), Balian Paradise Resort, Gubug Balian Beach Bungalows, Casa Balian, Villa Balian (luxury), Balian Spirit, Made's Homestay, various small homestays in Lalanglinggah
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/balian/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d41 (Surfline cam/report); Pondok Pitaya surfcam at https://pondokpitaya.com/surfcam/; balicams.com/camera/balian
services.drone_allowed: Generally permitted for personal use; respect temple grounds and ceremonies; commercial use requires permit [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Limited dedicated coworking; surf-camp wifi (Pondok Pitaya, Casa Balian) sufficient for remote work; nearest serious coworking ~1.5 hours east in Canggu/Tabanan [uncertain]

pricing.avg_lesson_price: IDR 300,000-500,000 (~USD 20-35) for 1-on-1 hour lesson; group lessons IDR 250,000-400,000 per person; multi-day packages available at Balian Surf Camp and Pondok Pitaya
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 80,000-150,000/day (~USD 5-10); per-session rentals also available

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season; declines significantly in wet season (Nov-April) due to river stormwater runoff carrying agricultural waste, plastic and sediment - avoid surfing for 24-48 hours after heavy rain, especially near the rivermouth

events_community.annual_competition_window: No regular WSL-level event; occasional local Tabanan/Balian surf contests and longboard events during dry season hosted by local surf camps [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Yoga retreats, full-moon gatherings, surf-camp group dinners at Pondok Pitaya, Balinese Hindu temple ceremonies (odalan) in nearby village, occasional live music nights at Balian Beach Cafe [uncertain]

basic_info.name: Bingin (Chinese: 賓岡; Indonesian: Pantai Bingin)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.8019, 115.1156 (Jl. Suara Ombak, Pecatu, Kuta Selatan, Badung 80163; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Bingin)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~18-20 km (approx. 40-50 min drive)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~55-70 min by motorbike; From Uluwatu (Suluban): ~10-15 min by motorbike

surf_conditions.wave_type: Left-hand reef break - short, hollow, mechanical tube. ~50-meter wall that throws over a very shallow inside reef, often producing one or two clean barrels per ride; described as wave-pool consistent
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (friendly to intermediates on medium-size days at mid tide; advanced/expert only at low tide and on bigger days)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 3-8 ft (works best 3-5 ft at mid tide for intermediates; low tide for advanced; slightly overhead is optimum)
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp shallow coral/limestone reef
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-August); same SE trade-wind window as the rest of the Bukit
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid tide is best (longer rides, safer); low tide for advanced (heaviest barrels, dry-reef risk); high tide breaks but has strong rip and is less defined
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore E to SE (East/Southeast) trade winds
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) groundswell
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 4 (very consistent during dry season, picks up smaller swells than Outside Corner Uluwatu)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: ~50 meters (short, intense ride - 1-2 barrel sections is typical)
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Fast (mechanical, throws hard and fast over shallow reef)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High - tight take-off zone with mid-steep drop directly into the barrel; small peak area means competition for position; very shallow reef under takeoff
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Little Bingin is a smaller, mellower wave adjacent to the main peak (works on smaller swell, more forgiving for intermediates); Impossibles connects from the north on big swells
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (very crowded; dense lineup of good locals and tourists battling for the small peak)

practical_info.access: Park at the clifftop on Jl. Pantai Bingin/Jl. Suara Ombak; walk down narrow path through remaining clifftop buildings, then descend steep stairs to the beach near Kelly's Warung area. Note: Following the July-September 2025 demolition of 48+ cliffside structures, access paths are less developed than before; many stairways and businesses were removed.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Paddle ~100m from shore, with quickest route to the lineup via the south of the break; on bigger days start paddle out very far to the south as current will sweep you into the takeoff zone. Time entry between sets to avoid being pounded on shallow reef.
practical_info.entry_fee: Parking ~IDR 5,000-10,000 (motorbike), IDR 10,000-20,000 (car). Beach entry fee historically minimal (~IDR 5,000) but logistics are in flux post-2025 demolition. [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy conditions and to avoid crowds; mid-tide sessions are gold; sunset session for golden-hour barrels
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~9 AM before crowds peak; weekday early sessions less crowded than weekends
practical_info.nearby_warung: Significantly reduced after 2025 demolition - many iconic beach warungs (including Kelly's Warung at the base and numerous cliffside cafes) were removed. Remaining options on the clifftop (Mu Bungalows, Mick's Place) and inland on Jl. Pantai Bingin. New temporary stalls emerging in 2026.
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Impossibles (5 min walk along reef at low tide; or short drive), Padang Padang (10 min), Dreamland (10 min), Balangan (10-15 min); you can walk from Bingin to Balangan at low tide along the reef
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - land access (now with reduced infrastructure)

safety_culture.hazards: Extremely shallow sharp reef especially at low tide, dangerous getting to/from lineup, strong current that sweeps you into takeoff zone, heavy crowd, high collision/drop-in risk in tight peak, post-demolition unstable cliff paths and exposed construction debris in some areas
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (booties recommended), occasional jellyfish, rare sea snakes; reef cuts get infected quickly in tropical water
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Very High - reef is very shallow under takeoff and inside section; classic 'Bingin tattoo' is a rite of passage; full set of booties strongly recommended for everyone
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong current sweeps from north (Impossibles direction) into the takeoff zone, especially on bigger days; rip channel on the south side of the wave used for paddle-out
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Strict priority for locals and known regulars; small peak means few waves to go around; do not paddle past locals; observe before paddling out; use 'thanks, please, and sorry'
safety_culture.localism_intensity: High - one of the most localized spots in Bali; tight-knit local crew that has surfed there for decades; respectful visitors who demonstrate skill and patience are accepted, but offenders are quickly called out
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Acknowledge and respect local Balinese surfers, do not drop in, do not snake, do not arrive in a large group and dominate the lineup, sit further back than you think appropriate, smile and greet people. As Mega Semadhi advises: 'thanks, please, and sorry'.
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Mega Semadhi (the 'Bingin Boy', 2016 Rip Curl Cup champion, grew up surfing Bingin), the Tandjung family (Rizal Tandjung), Bol Adyaksa, Made Switra, Garut Widiarta
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Small temples scattered around Pecatu and on the cliffs; respect canang sari offerings on stairs/beach (do not step on them); Nyepi (Day of Silence, ~March) - no surfing islandwide. Avoid surfing during temple ceremonies. Demolition events affected local Hindu cultural sites.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Unicare Pecatu Clinic (closest, ~5-10 min); BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (~25-30 min); Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~45 min)
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu Clinic: ~3-5 km
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - no permanent rescue jet ski stationed at Bingin
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Ambulance: 118/119; BIMC Nusa Dua: +62 361 3000 911

services.nearest_surf_school: Froggy Surf School (Bingin/Balangan), WaveHouse Bali (Bingin cliffs), Padang Padang Surf Camp, Bali Bisa, Kala Surf Camp - some operations affected by 2025 demolition; check current status before booking
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - still available on the clifftop and inland post-demolition; daily rental ~IDR 150,000-250,000
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - land-based photographers regularly shoot Bingin barrels from the cliff and beach; water photographers on big swells
services.photographer_recommendation: Freelance photographers at the cliff and through surf camps; rates approximately IDR 200,000-500,000 per session [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Post-2025 demolition, beach-level accommodation is gone. Remaining options are clifftop or inland: Mick's Place (clifftop, expat-run), Mu Bungalows (clifftop), WaveHouse Bali (Bingin cliffs), The Temple Lodge, plus inland Pecatu villas. Many traditional homestays were destroyed.
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/bingin/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d3b ; https://balicams.com (Bingin cam)
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use; commercial use requires permit. Increased sensitivity post-demolition; some local hostility toward drones. [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu Pecatu, B Work Bali (Pecatu), Suka Espresso (laptop-friendly), Bukit Cafe

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 30-50 / IDR 500,000-800,000 group lesson (beginner lessons usually held at nearby Balangan rather than Bingin's reef)

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Improved after 2025 demolition due to reduced runoff and construction waste; ocean clarity reportedly distinctly improved. Generally good in dry season; declines in wet season.

events_community.annual_competition_window: No major annual contest at Bingin itself (the Rip Curl Cup is at Padang Padang next door, August). Bingin occasionally hosts smaller local Indonesian Surfing League / WSL QS events. [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Pre-demolition: regular beach gatherings, sunset hangs at warungs (now mostly demolished). 2025-2026: community legal action and protest events related to demolition; ongoing community lawsuits against Badung Regency Government filed July 22, 2025.

basic_info.name: Surfboard Rental & Ding Repair in Bali (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪板租賃與修補; Indonesian: Sewa Papan Selancar dan Perbaikan Ding di Bali) - including Board Riders Bali (Canggu retail/rental), Balitubes (Canggu/Kuta/Seminyak/Uluwatu rental + school), Bali Ding Repairs (Berawa), Studio Repair Canggu (Amin Brothers, Batu Bolong), Doctor Ding (Palm Bay), Naruki (Uluwatu area), Aloha Bali Surf (Kuta), Padang Padang Surf Camp board hire, and BGS Bali (Uluwatu)
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (concentrated in Canggu/Berawa/Batu Bolong, Kuta/Seminyak, Uluwatu/Pecatu)
basic_info.gps_location: Board Riders Bali: Canggu (~-8.6478, 115.1378) [uncertain]; Balitubes head office Canggu (~-8.6555, 115.1376) [uncertain]; Bali Ding Repairs: Berawa shortcut Canggu (~-8.6630, 115.1340) [uncertain]; Studio Repair Canggu (Amin Brothers): Jl. Pantai Batu Bolong No.78, Canggu (~-8.6577, 115.1280); Naruki: Pecatu/Uluwatu area (~-8.8100, 115.0950) [uncertain]; Aloha Bali Surf: Kuta (~-8.7234, 115.1700) [uncertain]; Padang Padang Surf Camp: Jl. Labuansait, Pecatu (~-8.8090, 115.1050) [uncertain]
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Canggu shops (Board Riders, Balitubes, Bali Ding Repairs, Studio Repair): ~16-22 km (45-75 min depending on traffic); Kuta/Seminyak shops (Aloha Bali Surf, Balitubes Kuta): ~3-8 km (15-30 min); Uluwatu/Pecatu shops (Naruki, BGS Bali, Padang Padang Surf Camp board hire): ~20-22 km (45-60 min)

surf_conditions.wave_type: Rental shops serve adjacent breaks - Canggu cluster supplies sand-bottom beach breaks (Batu Bolong, Echo, Berawa, Pererenan); Kuta/Seminyak supplies long sand-bottom beach breaks; Uluwatu/Pecatu cluster supplies coral reef breaks (Uluwatu, Padang, Bingin, Impossibles, Balangan, Dreamland)
surf_conditions.skill_level: All levels - rental fleets include soft-tops for beginners, foamies/funboards for novice-intermediate, mid-lengths/longboards for cruising, performance shortboards for advanced; ding repair shops serve all surfers regardless of level
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Rental fleet sized to handle full range 1-10 ft; specific board choice depends on day's conditions [uncertain]
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sand bottom for Canggu/Kuta rentals; coral reef for Bukit rentals - reef wax and booties often offered as add-on by Uluwatu shops [uncertain]
surf_conditions.best_season: Year-round operation - dry season May-October sees peak demand (west coast working); wet season Nov-April demand shifts to east coast spots and continued steady ding-repair business
surf_conditions.best_tide: N/A for shops directly - rental customers choose boards based on tide/condition forecast [uncertain]
surf_conditions.best_wind: N/A for shops; relevant to which spot customer surfs
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: N/A for shops; relevant to spot selection
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 - Bali's wave consistency drives year-round rental demand [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: N/A for shops directly
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Bukit rentals tend toward narrower/thinner performance boards (fast waves); Canggu rentals tend toward wider/more volume boards (slower waves)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Rental staff usually advise board choice based on customer skill and target spot's take-off difficulty
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Rental shops near major breaks often guide customers to less-crowded secondary peaks
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: Shop foot-traffic peaks in July-August dry season holiday window; quietest in February-March (off-peak wet season) [uncertain]

practical_info.access: Walk-in retail showrooms - Board Riders Bali (Canggu showroom); Balitubes (Canggu, Kuta, Seminyak, Uluwatu locations); Bali Ding Repairs and Studio Repair Canggu have street-front workshops; Padang Padang Surf Camp board hire only during lessons/guiding sessions per their policy; most shops accept WhatsApp orders and arrange delivery to nearby villas
practical_info.paddle_out_method: N/A for shops; customer responsibility once board rented
practical_info.entry_fee: No entry fee - free to browse showrooms; deposit (usually ID/passport copy or IDR 500K-1M cash) required for rentals [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Shop hours typically 7 AM-7 PM (some open earlier for dawn patrol rentals 5:30-6 AM); ding repair drop-off best in morning to maximize turnaround time
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Drop board for repair early morning (8-10 AM) for same-day return on minor dings; pick up rental board the evening before for dawn patrol
practical_info.nearby_warung: Each shop area has dense food options - Canggu: Crate, Old Mans, Shady Shack, Peloton; Kuta: warung-row on Jl. Pantai Kuta; Uluwatu/Pecatu: Single Fin, Drifter Surf Cafe, Bukit Cafe, Suka Espresso
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Each rental shop can advise nearest alternative spot based on conditions - core service of local rental shops is daily condition advice
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - shops are land-based on Bali mainland

safety_culture.hazards: Risk borne by customer once board rented; shops disclaim liability for accidents. Renting wrong board for skill level is most common safety issue - reputable shops advise customers on appropriate board sizing.
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Standard Bali marine hazards: reef cuts, urchins, jellyfish, rip currents - shops typically provide brief safety advice
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Customers using rental boards at Uluwatu/Padang Padang at high reef cut risk; many Bukit rental shops offer booties for rent or sale
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Shop staff often brief customers on rip currents at target spot
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Shop owners (often expat or Indo surfers) typically educate first-time visitors on Balinese lineup etiquette
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Most rental shops are tourist-facing and welcoming; ding repair shops often operated by local Balinese craftsmen with deep community ties
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Treat rental boards with respect - excessive damage may result in repair fee deducted from deposit; tip ding repair craftsmen for fast turnaround
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Amin Brothers (Studio Repair Canggu) known regionally for color-matching repairs; Naruki (Uluwatu) - 15-year reputation as one of the best ding repair craftsmen on the Bukit; many shop owners are local pro surfers or shapers
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Shops close partially during major Balinese ceremonies (Galungan/Kuningan); fully closed on Nyepi Day of Silence (March, varies); plan rentals around these dates
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Canggu shops: Penta Medical Canggu, BIMC Kuta within 5-10 km; Kuta shops: BIMC Hospital Kuta on doorstep; Uluwatu shops: Unicare Pecatu Clinic within 3-5 km, BIMC Nusa Dua ~25 min
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Canggu shops: ~3-5 km to Penta Medical Canggu; Kuta shops: ~1-2 km to BIMC Kuta; Uluwatu shops: ~3-5 km to Unicare Pecatu [uncertain]
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - rental/repair shops do not operate jet ski rescue services
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; BIMC Kuta: +62 361 761 263; BIMC Nusa Dua: +62 361 3000 911; Penta Medical Canggu: +62 851 8523 8523 [uncertain]

services.nearest_surf_school: Balitubes also operates a surf school (combo rental+lesson packages); Padang Padang Surf Camp combines board hire with lessons; Aloha Bali Surf offers lessons + rental; many shops partner with independent freelance instructors
services.board_rental_availability: High availability across Bali - Board Riders Bali (Canggu showroom focus on Native brand boards: shortboards/fish/mids/longboards); Balitubes (multi-location, full range); Aloha Bali Surf (Kuta, broad range); White Monkey Surf Shop; Chandra's Surfing Bali (Legian Beach); Padang Padang Surf Camp (Bukit, lesson-bundled); Bali Surfboard Rental (delivery service across south Bali). Most stock soft-tops, foamies, mini-mals, fun-shapes, longboards, and performance shortboards.
services.surf_photographer_available: Some larger shops/schools partner with photographers as add-on; not a core rental service [uncertain]
services.photographer_recommendation: Independent freelance Canggu/Uluwatu photographers IDR 100K-300K/shot or IDR 500K-1.5M/session - shops can recommend [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: N/A as core service but shops often refer customers to nearby surf camps, hostels, villas
services.webcam_url: Most shops monitor Surfline cams to advise customers: Canggu (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/), Uluwatu (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/uluwatu/), Padang Padang (Surfline)
services.drone_allowed: N/A directly - shop premises; customers using drones over beaches subject to Indonesian aviation rules [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Canggu cluster: Outpost Canggu, Tropical Nomad, Dojo Bali, B Work Bali (within 5-10 min of most rental shops); Bukit: Outpost Ulu Pecatu, B Work Bali Pecatu

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Combo rental+lesson packages: ~USD 30-60 per group lesson; Balitubes-type schools IDR 400K-700K per group lesson [uncertain]
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Group lesson IDR 350,000-700,000; private lesson IDR 600,000-1,500,000 [uncertain]
pricing.board_rental_daily: TYPICAL DAILY RATES IDR: Soft-top/foamie IDR 100,000-150,000/day; Mini-mal/funboard IDR 150,000-200,000/day; Shortboard IDR 200,000-300,000/day; Performance/high-end shortboard IDR 300,000-400,000/day; Longboard IDR 250,000-400,000/day. BOARD RIDERS BALI tiered: SILVER (Native shortboards/fish) IDR 400,000/day, GOLD (Native mid-lengths) IDR 450,000/day, PLATINUM (Native longboards) IDR 550,000/day. Hourly: foamie IDR 50,000-100,000/hr, performance IDR 100,000-150,000/hr. Weekly: typically IDR 1,000,000-1,500,000 for standard shortboard. Monthly: IDR 2,500,000-3,500,000 typical.
pricing.gopro_rental_price: GoPro daily rental ~IDR 150,000-250,000 with SD card; mount + selfie-stick add-on IDR 50,000 [uncertain]
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: Rashguard daily IDR 50,000-80,000; wetsuit top rare in Bali (water 27-29°C). Booties IDR 30,000-50,000/day for Bukit reef use [uncertain]
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: MINOR DINGS (small chips, no through-glass): IDR 50,000-150,000 (~USD 3-10), typical 1-2 hour turnaround. MEDIUM DINGS (open foam, small cracks, fin box repair): IDR 150,000-400,000 (~USD 10-25), turnaround 1-2 days. MAJOR REPAIRS (large delamination, broken nose, tail patch): IDR 400,000-800,000 (~USD 25-50), 2-4 days. SNAPPED BOARD FULL REJOIN: IDR 800,000-1,500,000 (~USD 50-100), 3-7 days with cure time. Color-matching/cosmetic premium adds 30-50% (Studio Repair Canggu specialty). Studio Repair: minor 1-2 days; Bali Ding Repairs (Berawa): in-and-out within 1-2 hours for basic patch-up. Doctor Ding (Palm Bay) and Naruki (Uluwatu) similar pricing structure.
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: Standalone rental+ding repair shops do not bundle weekly camp packages - core service is daily rental and per-job repair. Adjacent camps (Padang Padang Surf Camp, Balitubes school) offer 5-7 day learn-to-surf packages combining lessons + board + accommodation ~USD 600-1,500 [uncertain]
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Where bundled: 7 days unlimited board rental, daily lessons, transfers to surf spots, breakfast, basic ding-repair coverage, surf insurance optional
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Most shops keep stable rates year-round but discounts negotiable in off-peak (Feb-March, Nov); weekly/monthly rates offer 30-50% discount vs. daily rate. Peak demand July-August may see 10-20% price uplift on premium boards [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Rental shops on west coast operate year-round but customers face poorer water quality in wet season (Nov-March); Bukit shops less affected as Bukit has minimal runoff
sustainability.eco_certification: Most rental/repair shops do not hold formal eco-certification [uncertain]; eco-conscious customers may prefer Kayu Surfboards (balsa wood) or shops using Solarez sun-cure resin (less toxic) and water-based finishes
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Not standardly offered by rental/repair shops [uncertain]; some shops voluntarily donate to Project Clean Uluwatu or SeaTrees coral restoration as informal offset

events_community.annual_competition_window: Rental shops experience peak demand during Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang (August holding period); Krui Pro and Komune Bali Pro events may shift demand seasonally
events_community.community_events: Studio Repair Canggu and Bali Ding Repairs often host informal shaper/repair workshops; rental shops sponsor local surf school events; Single Fin Sunday (Uluwatu) and Old Mans Wednesday (Canggu) are de facto community nights where rental customers congregate

visa_logistics.visa_type_recommended: VoA / e-VoA 30 days adequate for short rental trips; B211A 60-day for 1-month rentals; KITAS for long-term residents needing extended monthly rentals

Canggu_Batu_Bolong.name: Canggu - Batu Bolong (峇里島 - 巴杜柏隆 / Batu Bolong)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.distance_from_dps_airport_km: 20 km (approx. 60-75 min by car depending on traffic)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: 0-5 min (in town); From Uluwatu: 60-90 min by scooter
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.wave_type: Beach break with flat reef bottom, predominantly left and right peaks, mellow and forgiving (soft rolling shoulders, point-break-like long rides on the inside)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.skill_level: Beginner to Intermediate (also a longboarder favorite)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.wave_height_range: 2-6 ft (knee to chest high typical; can hold up to ~8 ft on bigger days)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.best_season: Dry season May-October produces the most consistent swell; shoulder seasons (Nov-Dec, Mar-Apr) often have cleaner glassy mornings with lighter winds. Wet season (Nov-Mar) still rideable but with onshore westerlies and poorer water quality after rain.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.best_tide: Mid to high tide (mid-incoming is the sweet spot; low tide exposes reef and makes the wave dumpy/shallow)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.best_wind: Offshore from the East / Northeast (SE-E trade winds in dry season are ideal; wind typically turns onshore around 10-11am)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also works on S and SSW
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 (one of the most consistent breaks in Bali, rideable almost every day of the year)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.wave_length_meters: 50-150 m on a good set (long peeling shoulders, especially favorable for longboarding) [uncertain]
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.wave_speed_rating: Slow (soft and forgiving, ideal for longboards and learners)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Easy - wide takeoff zone, gentle drop, no steep ledge; main difficulty is navigating heavy crowd traffic rather than the wave itself
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.secondary_peaks: Multiple peaks along the beach; an inside reform section provides extra rides toward shore. Old Man's peak to the north and Nelayan/Pererenan further north offer adjacent alternatives.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.peak_crowd_level: 5/5 - extremely crowded, one of the busiest lineups in Bali. Peak crowd 8am-11am and 3pm-sunset. Heaviest in July-August and December-January (high season).
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.access: Walk directly onto the beach from Jalan Pantai Batu Bolong; multiple beach access points via cafes (Old Man's, La Brisa, The Lawn). No stairs or cave entry required.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.paddle_out_method: Walk in from the sandy beach; paddle straight out through the whitewater. Easiest paddle-out in Canggu - no channel navigation needed.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking: ~IDR 5,000 for car, IDR 2,000-3,000 for scooter at official lots.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for glassy, less crowded conditions; sunset session also popular for vibe and lighting.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.best_time_of_day: Before 7am to avoid the surf-school rush and onshore wind; mid-week mornings significantly less crowded than weekends.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.nearby_warung: Warung Bu Mi (cheap local Indonesian), Old Man's Restaurant, La Brisa, The Lawn, Betelnut Cafe, Cafe Vida, Sand Bar; coconut stalls and warungs line Jalan Pantai Batu Bolong.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.alternative_spot_within_10min: Old Man's (immediately north, ~2 min walk), Nelayan Beach (~5 min), Berawa (~10 min south), Echo Beach (~10 min north).
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.hazards: Heavy crowd (collision risk with loose longboards is the #1 hazard), shallow reef at low tide, occasional sea urchins on the reef, river-mouth runoff/poor water quality after heavy rain (skin infections, ear infections), beginners ditching boards.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (urchin spines on reef - avoid low tide; remove with vinegar/tweezers, see clinic if deep). Occasional small jellyfish in wet season. No regular shark or stonefish reports. Bacterial water issues post-rainfall can cause ear/skin infections.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.reef_cut_severity_index: Low to medium - reef is relatively flat and not razor-sharp like Uluwatu/Padang. Minor scrapes most common; deep cuts rare. Higher risk only at low tide. [uncertain]
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.rip_current_pattern: Moderate rips form between sandbar sections and near the small river mouth on the north side; visible as darker, calmer channels. Strongest on outgoing tide and bigger swells. Generally manageable for intermediate paddlers. [uncertain]
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.lineup_etiquette: Standard surf etiquette applies but rarely enforced due to crowd density. Priority to surfer closest to peak; expect frequent drop-ins. Smile and say hi, don't snake locals, give right of way to longboarders setting up early. Surf-school groups dominate the inside.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.localism_intensity: Low - one of the most welcoming and tourist-friendly lineups in Bali. Locals generally tolerant though paddle-battles for sets do happen.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.respect_protocol: Be courteous to surf-school students and instructors who teach the inside daily; don't drop in on Indonesian locals; rinse off respectfully near temple/offering areas on the beach; cover up when walking through beachside warungs.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.resident_local_legends: Local Canggu/Pererenan crew including longboarders associated with Deus Ex Machina 'Nine Foot and Single' culture (e.g., Made Lana, Flynn Novak visits); regular pro and shaper presence. [uncertain]
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.religious_sensitivity: Pura Batu Bolong (small Hindu sea temple on the cliffed rock outcrop at the south end of the beach) is an active worship site - do not climb on offerings (canang sari), women on menstruation traditionally should not enter temple areas. Respect Nyepi (Day of Silence, typically March) - no surfing, beach closed.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major), Siloam Hospital Denpasar; reef cuts treated locally with iodine + antibiotics.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~1-2 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~25 km; Siloam Denpasar ~15 km
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; informal lifeguard presence from Balawista on busy days; surf schools maintain on-water instructor support.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.nearest_surf_school: Numerous surf schools cluster on Batu Bolong beach: Dian Surf School, Surf Well Bali, Sea N Surf, Surf With Aldo, Pro Surf School Bali, Odyssey Surf School. Most operate directly on the sand with lessons starting at IDR 350,000-450,000.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.board_rental_availability: Abundant - dozens of board rental stalls along the beach (longboards, foamies, mid-lengths, shortboards)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.surf_photographer_available: Yes - multiple land-based photographers and water-photographers shoot daily at Batu Bolong; photos available via Instagram/QR code on the beach (typically delivered same day).
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.photographer_recommendation: Local beach photographers operate on commission; typical price IDR 150,000-300,000 for a session pack of edited photos. Notable: @canggusurfphoto, @batubolongphoto-style operators. [uncertain]
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.accommodation_options: Wide range: budget hostels (The Farm Hostel, Gypsy Moon), mid-range guesthouses (Serenity Eco Guesthouse), surf camps (Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu, Odyssey Surf Camp), boutique hotels (COMO Uma Canggu, The Slow). Walking distance to break highly available.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.webcam_url: https://www.webcamtaxi.com/en/indonesia/bali/canggu-sandbar-cam.html (Sand Bar live cam); https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/old-mans-batu-bolong/605112930a374f33cf5f8f05 (Surfline Old Mans - Batu Bolong)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.drone_allowed: Generally tolerated but technically requires PASI / DGCA registration for drones >250g in Indonesia; avoid flying over temple, crowded lineup, or low altitude over swimmers. Many local photographers fly drones daily.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.coworking_nearby: Yes - Outpost Canggu (Jl. Tanah Barak), Tropical Nomad (Jl. Pantai Berawa), B Work Bali (Jl. Nelayan), Dojo Bali (Echo Beach); all 5-15 min scooter ride.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.avg_lesson_price: IDR 400,000-500,000 (~USD 25-32 / TWD 800-1,000) per 2-hour group lesson; private lessons IDR 600,000-900,000
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.board_rental_daily: Half-day (4h): IDR 75,000-150,000; full-day: IDR 100,000-200,000 depending on board type (longboard typically higher)
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Good to Fair. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to river-mouth runoff and drainage discharging directly into lineup; risk of ear/skin/stomach infection. Avoid surfing for 24-48h after heavy downpour.
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.annual_competition_window: Hosts regional/longboard contests sporadically (e.g., Deus 9ft & Single Festival held annually around August-September at nearby Pererenan/Canggu beaches). No major WSL CT event. [uncertain]
Canggu_Batu_Bolong.community_events: Deus Sunday surf jams, 'Old Man's Wednesday' style sessions, Single Fin Sunday (informal), beach clean-ups by 4Ocean and Trash Hero Canggu; sunset DJ sessions at La Brisa and The Lawn.

Canggu_Berawa.name: Canggu - Berawa (峇里島 - 貝拉瓦 / Pantai Berawa / Berawa Beach)
Canggu_Berawa.gps_location: -8.6649, 115.1379 (https://maps.app.goo.gl/Berawa+Beach+Canggu Pantai Berawa, in front of Finns Beach Club)
Canggu_Berawa.distance_from_dps_airport_km: 18 km (approx. 55-75 min by car depending on traffic)
Canggu_Berawa.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): 5-10 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: 55-85 min by scooter
Canggu_Berawa.wave_type: Mixed beach break + reef break - three distinct peaks: 'The Peak' (A-frame beach break, mid tide), 'The Ledge' (shallow reef right-hander, low tide, advanced), 'The Bombie' (soft outside peak, SUP/foil/big-day spot). Predominantly left and right peaks.
Canggu_Berawa.skill_level: Beginner (Bombie/inside) to Intermediate (The Peak) to Advanced (The Ledge). Considered one of the best intermediate spots in Canggu.
Canggu_Berawa.wave_height_range: 2-8 ft (works 1-8 ft+, sweet spot 3-6 ft / head-high)
Canggu_Berawa.bottom_type: Sand on the inside, reef on the outside (mixed sand + reef); rocky outside sections
Canggu_Berawa.best_season: Dry season April-October (offshore SE/E trades and consistent SW swell). Wet season (Nov-Mar) still rideable but onshore winds frequent.
Canggu_Berawa.best_tide: Low to mid tide for The Peak and The Ledge; Bombie works best on higher tide and bigger swells. Different peaks light up at different tides, making Berawa surfable on most tide stages.
Canggu_Berawa.best_wind: Offshore from East / Southeast / Northeast (E-SE trade winds optimal); onshore by 10-11am typical
Canggu_Berawa.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and SSW
Canggu_Berawa.swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 (consistent but more swell-direction-dependent than Batu Bolong; quality varies with sandbar shifts)
Canggu_Berawa.wave_length_meters: 50-120 m on The Peak rights; shorter on The Ledge (~30-50 m) [uncertain]
Canggu_Berawa.wave_speed_rating: Medium (faster than Batu Bolong, slower than Echo Left; The Peak offers punchy walls, The Ledge is fast and shallow)
Canggu_Berawa.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Easy to moderate at The Peak (forgiving takeoff, room to spread out); difficult at The Ledge (steep, shallow reef drop); easy at the Bombie outside (soft rolling peaks).
Canggu_Berawa.secondary_peaks: Three distinct peaks: The Peak (main A-frame), The Ledge (south-side shallow reef), The Bombie (outside reef peak on bigger swells). Lineup spreads over shifting sandbanks, giving multiple takeoff zones.
Canggu_Berawa.peak_crowd_level: 3/5 to 4/5 - busier than it used to be due to Finns/Atlas beach club traffic but still feels less crowded than Batu Bolong/Echo. Peak crowd 7-11am dry season. Lineup spreads out across peaks.
Canggu_Berawa.access: Walk-in from Jalan Pantai Berawa, through the Finns Beach Club / Atlas Beach Fest / Komune Beach Club access points. Multiple beach entry paths via beach club walkways.
Canggu_Berawa.paddle_out_method: Paddle straight out from the beach over the inside sandbar; pick your peak (The Peak in front of Finns, The Ledge south end, Bombie outside on bigger days). On bigger swells use a rip channel between peaks for an easier paddle.
Canggu_Berawa.entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking: ~IDR 5,000 for car, IDR 2,000-3,000 for scooter. Some beach club lots charge IDR 10,000-20,000.
Canggu_Berawa.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for offshore and uncrowded conditions; sunset session for golden-hour vibes (often onshore but rideable).
Canggu_Berawa.best_time_of_day: Before 8am for clean offshore conditions; mid-week mornings less crowded than weekends.
Canggu_Berawa.nearby_warung: Warung Bu Mong, La Baracca, Crate Cafe, Shady Shack nearby; major beach clubs (Finns Beach Club, Atlas Beach Fest, Komune, Manhattan) line the beach with restaurants/bars.
Canggu_Berawa.alternative_spot_within_10min: Batu Bolong (5-10 min north for softer waves), Echo Beach (10-15 min north for heavier reef), Seseh / Pererenan (15 min north when Canggu crowded), Petitenget (5 min south on smaller days).
Canggu_Berawa.hazards: Rip currents along the beach (notably near the river mouth/lagoon at south end), shallow reef on outside sections (The Ledge), occasional sharp rocks, urchins on reef, river-mouth runoff after rain, large beach club crowds in summer.
Canggu_Berawa.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the outside reef sections (avoid The Ledge at low tide). Occasional jellyfish in wet season. No significant shark or stonefish reports. Bacterial water issues post-rain due to river/lagoon discharge.
Canggu_Berawa.reef_cut_severity_index: Low to Medium - sand-dominant inside means most wipeouts are safe; The Ledge reef can cause moderate cuts when surfed at low tide.
Canggu_Berawa.rip_current_pattern: Strong rip currents along the beach, especially near the lagoon/river mouth on the southern end and channels between sandbars. Useful for paddling out but dangerous for non-surfers. Stronger on outgoing tide and bigger swells.
Canggu_Berawa.lineup_etiquette: Standard surf etiquette; less crowded so generally more relaxed than Batu Bolong/Echo. Spread out across the three peaks; respect priority on takeoff. Smile, say hi to locals, don't snake.
Canggu_Berawa.localism_intensity: Low to Medium - generally welcoming for intermediate visitors. Some locals dominate The Ledge but overall vibe is mellow.
Canggu_Berawa.respect_protocol: Be courteous, don't crowd locals on The Ledge, respect rotation at The Peak. Don't bring beach club drinks/glassware onto the sand. Avoid surfing right in front of beach club zones during ceremonies/events.
Canggu_Berawa.resident_local_legends: Several Canggu/Berawa-based surfers shred The Peak regularly (e.g., Bali shortboard crew, local pros from nearby Canggu shaping community); fewer documented legends than Batu Bolong or Uluwatu. [uncertain]
Canggu_Berawa.religious_sensitivity: Small Hindu temples and shrines along the beach access; respect canang sari offerings. Observe Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - no surfing, beach closed. Avoid disrupting temple ceremonies (melasti) on the beach.
Canggu_Berawa.medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major), Siloam Hospital Denpasar, Bali International Medical Centre.
Canggu_Berawa.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~2 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~22 km; Siloam Denpasar ~14 km
Canggu_Berawa.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; Finns Beach Club has lifeguards on the beach during operating hours; Balawista informal coverage.
Canggu_Berawa.emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
Canggu_Berawa.nearest_surf_school: Several surf schools cluster in Berawa: Surf School Canggu Berawa, Wavehouse Bali, Planet Surf Camps Berawa, Bali Surf School Berawa. Lessons IDR 350,000-500,000 per 2-hour session.
Canggu_Berawa.board_rental_availability: Abundant - rental stalls along beach access road and within beach clubs; longboards, foamies, mid-lengths, shortboards available
Canggu_Berawa.surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land-based photographers shoot from the beach and water; photos delivered same day via Instagram/QR code.
Canggu_Berawa.photographer_recommendation: Local Berawa/Canggu photographers; typical pack IDR 150,000-300,000 for edited photos. [uncertain]
Canggu_Berawa.accommodation_options: Wide range: COMO Uma Canggu (luxury), The Haven Suites, Bisma Eight, Tugu Bali, plus many guesthouses and villas. Surf camps include Planet Surf Camp Canggu/Berawa, Wavehouse Bali, Mondo Surf Village (Pererenan). Walking distance to break.
Canggu_Berawa.drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for surf photography; DGCA registration required for >250g drones in Indonesia. Avoid flying over beach clubs without permission, low over crowded lineup, or during religious ceremonies.
Canggu_Berawa.coworking_nearby: Yes - Tropical Nomad (Jl. Pantai Berawa, walking distance), Outpost Canggu (~10 min scooter), B Work Bali (Nelayan), Dojo Bali; multiple options within 5-15 min.
Canggu_Berawa.avg_lesson_price: IDR 400,000-550,000 (~USD 26-36 / TWD 800-1,100) per 2-hour group lesson; private IDR 600,000-900,000
Canggu_Berawa.board_rental_daily: Half-day: IDR 75,000-150,000; full-day IDR 100,000-200,000; performance shortboard up to IDR 200,000-250,000
Canggu_Berawa.water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Good to Fair. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to river/lagoon runoff at south end; ear/skin infection risk. Avoid surfing 24-48h post-downpour.
Canggu_Berawa.annual_competition_window: Occasional regional contests and surf school competitions; sometimes hosts amateur/grom events. No major WSL CT event. [uncertain]
Canggu_Berawa.community_events: Beach club events at Finns / Atlas / Komune; Trash Hero Canggu and 4Ocean beach clean-ups; Deus Canggu 9ft & Single Festival activities nearby; Sunday DJ sessions; full moon parties.

Canggu_Echo_Beach.name: Canggu - Echo Beach (峇里島 - 回音灘 / Pantai Batu Mejan / Echo Beach)
Canggu_Echo_Beach.distance_from_dps_airport_km: 22 km (approx. 70-90 min by car depending on traffic)
Canggu_Echo_Beach.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): 5-10 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: 70-100 min by scooter
Canggu_Echo_Beach.wave_type: Reef break - predominantly a powerful left-hander (Echo Left), with a less famous right (Echo Right); short, punchy, hollow / barreling on bigger swells
Canggu_Echo_Beach.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (Pro on bigger days). Not suitable for beginners.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.wave_height_range: 3-8 ft (best 4-8 ft / head-high to double-overhead; works up to ~10 ft)
Canggu_Echo_Beach.best_season: Dry season May-October is prime (consistent SW swell + offshore SE/E trade winds). Shoulder seasons (April, November) also good. Wet season (Dec-Feb) often onshore and blown out.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.best_tide: Mid to high tide (incoming mid is the sweet spot). Low tide closes the wave out and exposes dangerous reef.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.best_wind: Offshore from East / Southeast / Northeast (E-SE trade winds optimal); turns onshore around 10-11am
Canggu_Echo_Beach.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and WSW
Canggu_Echo_Beach.swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 (highly consistent, works on most swells that hit Canggu)
Canggu_Echo_Beach.wave_length_meters: 30-80 m typical ride (short, fast, sectiony rather than long peeling) [uncertain]
Canggu_Echo_Beach.wave_speed_rating: Fast (medium-fast on small days, fast and punchy with steep takeoff on bigger days; rewards shortboards and step-ups)
Canggu_Echo_Beach.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Difficult - steep, ledgy takeoff over shallow reef; wave jacks up quickly as it hits the reef shelf. Drop-knee priority and quick paddling required. Mistimed takeoff puts you onto exposed reef.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.secondary_peaks: Echo Left (main A-frame leans heavily to the left), Echo Right (shorter, less consistent right shoulder), and Echo Reef / River Mouth peaks further north toward Pererenan. An inside reform can be ridden on smaller days.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.peak_crowd_level: 5/5 - take-off zone is tight and extremely crowded when good; lots of hassling, drop-ins, and paddle-battles. Peak crowd 7am-11am dry season.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.access: Walk-in from the end of Jalan Pura Batu Mejan (Echo Beach road); park at La Brisa / The Lawn / Echo Beach parking area then walk down to the sand. No stairs or cave entry.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.paddle_out_method: Paddle out via the channel between Echo Left peak and the Sandbar in front of La Brisa. A helpful rip-current pulls you straight to the lineup past the Sandbar, making the paddle relatively easy when timed correctly. Stay clear of breaking sets on the inside reef.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking: ~IDR 5,000 for car, IDR 2,000-3,000 for scooter at official lots.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for glassy offshores and lighter crowd; second session at sunset if wind drops.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.best_time_of_day: Before 7am to avoid both crowd and onshore wind; mid-week early morning best.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.nearby_warung: La Brisa (cliffside cafe/bar), The Lawn, Sand Bar, Echo Beach House, Beach House, Mocca Cafe, Old Man's nearby; coconut and warung stalls along the access road.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.alternative_spot_within_10min: Berawa (5-10 min south, mellower beach break on big-Echo days), Pererenan (5 min north - heavier reef alternative when Echo crowded), Batu Bolong (5 min south for smaller/softer waves), Old Man's (5 min south).
Canggu_Echo_Beach.hazards: Shallow, sharp coral/lava reef; crowd-related collisions; strong rip current on the paddle-out channel; sharp sections of exposed reef at low tide; sea urchins on reef; fast-breaking ledge causes wipeouts onto reef.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (urchin spines on reef - common; treat with vinegar/tweezers). Reef-fish lurking in crevices. Rare jellyfish in wet season. No significant shark concern. Bacterial water issues post-rain (Yeh Poh river runoff just north).
Canggu_Echo_Beach.reef_cut_severity_index: Medium to High - reef is sharper and shallower than Batu Bolong, with exposed lava-coral chunks. Reef cuts are common when wiping out at low tide; can be deep enough to require sutures. Carry reef-cut first aid (iodine, vinegar).
Canggu_Echo_Beach.rip_current_pattern: Channel rip immediately south of Echo Left peak (between peak and Sandbar) - useful for paddling out but pulls strongly on outgoing tide. Side-sweeping current along the reef can drag inexperienced surfers onto inside reef. Stronger on bigger swells.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.lineup_etiquette: Strict priority on the peak; locals and regulars dominate the inside takeoff. Wait your turn in rotation; don't paddle straight to the peak. No snaking; respect priority and back-paddling will earn aggressive responses. Apologize quickly if you blow a wave.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.localism_intensity: Medium - more localized than Batu Bolong; regular crew of Indonesian and expat surfers known to enforce priority. Generally not aggressive if you respect rotation and surf at your level, but vibe can turn cold if you drop in repeatedly.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.respect_protocol: Don't paddle inside locals; nod and greet regulars; if you're not at intermediate-advanced level, surf Batu Bolong instead. Avoid bringing surf-school groups into the lineup. Cover up walking through warungs and temple zones.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.resident_local_legends: Canggu pro/free-surf crew shred Echo regularly (e.g., Garut Widiarta, Made Lana, Diah Rahayu, Raditya Rondi have surfed here). International pros (Mason Ho, Bruce Irons) occasionally visit. [uncertain]
Canggu_Echo_Beach.religious_sensitivity: Pura Batu Mejan (Hindu temple) sits directly behind the beach on the cliff - it is an active worship site, do not climb on offerings or temple structures. Observe Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - no surfing, beach closed. Women on menstruation traditionally avoid temple grounds.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major trauma), Siloam Hospital Denpasar. Reef cuts are common here so most clinics know how to handle them quickly.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~2-3 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~27 km; Siloam Denpasar ~17 km
Canggu_Echo_Beach.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski stationed at Echo; Balawista lifeguards informally cover the area on busy days; instructors at adjacent surf schools provide informal rescues.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
Canggu_Echo_Beach.nearest_surf_school: Most surf schools operate from neighboring Batu Bolong (Dian Surf School, Surf Well Bali, Sea N Surf, Odyssey, Pro Surf). Few schools teach beginners at Echo due to reef hazards; intermediate guiding only.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.board_rental_availability: No on-beach rental at Echo. Rental shops on the road (Boardriders Bali Echo Beach, Drifter, Deus Ex Machina) offer shortboards and performance boards.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land and water photographers shoot the Echo Left peak from the rocks and beach; photos sold via Instagram/QR code same day.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.photographer_recommendation: Local Canggu photographers (e.g., @echobeachphoto-style operators, freelancers via @balisurfphotos); typical price IDR 200,000-400,000 for an edited photo pack. [uncertain]
Canggu_Echo_Beach.accommodation_options: Echo Beach House, Mocca Bali, Pondok Nyoman, Hotel Tugu Bali (luxury heritage), surf camps in Canggu/Pererenan (Mondo Surf Village, Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu). Wide range from hostels to villas within 5-15 min.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d40 (Surfline Canggu cam covers Echo area); https://balicams.com/camera/rivermouth (River Mouth / Echo-adjacent cam)
Canggu_Echo_Beach.drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for surf photography; requires DGCA registration for >250g. Avoid flying over the temple, low over the crowded lineup, or in restricted hours. Many photographers fly drones here daily.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.coworking_nearby: Yes - Dojo Bali (originally near Echo, now main branch nearby), Outpost Canggu, B Work Bali (Nelayan), Tropical Nomad; all within 5-15 min scooter.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.avg_lesson_price: IDR 500,000-700,000 (~USD 32-45 / TWD 1,000-1,400) for an intermediate guiding/coaching session (most schools won't teach beginners here)
Canggu_Echo_Beach.board_rental_daily: Performance shortboard IDR 150,000-200,000/day (premium); standard board IDR 100,000-150,000/day from nearby shops
Canggu_Echo_Beach.water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Fair to Good. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to Yeh Poh river-mouth runoff and drainage; ear/skin infection risk. Avoid surfing 24-48h after heavy rain.
Canggu_Echo_Beach.annual_competition_window: Hosts occasional regional shortboard contests and free-surf sessions; Deus Sunday-style events occasionally land here. No major WSL CT event. [uncertain]
Canggu_Echo_Beach.community_events: Sunset DJ sessions at La Brisa, Echo Beach House parties, beach clean-ups by Trash Hero Canggu and 4Ocean, Deus 9ft & Single Festival activities nearby, full moon events on the beach.

Canggu_Sandbar.name: Canggu Sandbar (峇里島 - 沙洲浪點 / The Sandbar / Pantai Sandbar Canggu)
Canggu_Sandbar.gps_location: -8.6450, 115.1240 (approx.; located between Echo Beach and Pererenan Rivermouth, in front of La Brisa / Sand Bar Echo Beach) [uncertain]
Canggu_Sandbar.distance_from_dps_airport_km: 23 km (approx. 75-90 min by car depending on traffic)
Canggu_Sandbar.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): 5-10 min by scooter / paddle; From Uluwatu: 75-100 min by scooter
Canggu_Sandbar.wave_type: Beach break with reef-like behavior - A-frame peak with strong left-hand wedge (longer ride) and shorter punchy right; produces steep takeoffs, fast hollow barrels and ripable walls over sand bottom
Canggu_Sandbar.skill_level: Advanced to Pro (intermediate-advanced on smaller days; expert only over head-and-a-half)
Canggu_Sandbar.wave_height_range: 3-10+ ft (best 4-8 ft; holds up to double-overhead - the largest swell-tolerant break of the Echo Beach zone)
Canggu_Sandbar.bottom_type: Sand (sand bar formed at the rivermouth/Echo zone; some submerged rocks at lower tides on the edges)
Canggu_Sandbar.best_season: Dry season May-October prime (SW groundswells + offshore SE/E trade winds). April and November are good shoulder months. Year-round wave but wet season (Dec-Feb) often onshore and murky.
Canggu_Sandbar.best_tide: High tide (longest ride and connection to the inside section). Works mid-tide with shorter wedgy peaks; low tide exposes submerged rocks and shortens the wave.
Canggu_Sandbar.best_wind: Offshore from East / Southeast (E-SE trade winds); turns onshore/side-shore from late morning onwards
Canggu_Sandbar.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and SSW
Canggu_Sandbar.swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 (consistent during dry season; needs slightly more size than Batu Bolong to switch on properly)
Canggu_Sandbar.wave_length_meters: 40-100 m (medium-length ride; longer when the left connects to the inside section on high tide) [uncertain]
Canggu_Sandbar.wave_speed_rating: Fast (steep, punchy, wedgy takeoff; rewards shortboards and step-ups on bigger days)
Canggu_Sandbar.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Difficult - steep ledgy A-frame takeoff that jacks up quickly into a heavy thick-lipped barrel; sand bottom is forgiving relative to reef but the drop is critical and unpredictable
Canggu_Sandbar.secondary_peaks: A-frame splits into a longer left (the marquee wave) and a shorter right shoulder; an inside reform/section can be connected at high tide; River Mouth peak sits immediately to the north and Echo Left to the south
Canggu_Sandbar.peak_crowd_level: 3/5 - significantly less crowded than Echo Left or Batu Bolong because of the paddle/skill barrier; gets busy when good but the take-off zone is more spread out. Peak crowd 7-10am dry season.
Canggu_Sandbar.access: Walk-in from the beach at Echo Beach / La Brisa area (end of Jl. Pura Batu Mejan), then a moderate paddle north along the inside toward the sand bar; alternatively, walk from the Pererenan side and paddle south. No boat charter required.
Canggu_Sandbar.paddle_out_method: Use the channel directly in front of the Sandbar peak to paddle out, watching for submerged rocks at lower tides. There is no convenient clean channel like at beginner spots, so timing sets is important. A useful rip on the south side of the peak can help push you out.
Canggu_Sandbar.entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking at Echo Beach lots: IDR 2,000-5,000 for scooter, IDR 5,000-10,000 for car.
Canggu_Sandbar.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for glassy offshores and lighter crowd; second session at sunset if wind drops.
Canggu_Sandbar.best_time_of_day: Before 8am to get clean offshore conditions and avoid both the build-up of crowd and the onshore wind that arrives mid-morning.
Canggu_Sandbar.nearby_warung: Sand Bar (beachfront bar named after the wave), La Brisa, The Lawn, Echo Beach House, Beach House, Mocca Cafe; warung and coconut stalls along the Echo Beach access road.
Canggu_Sandbar.alternative_spot_within_10min: Echo Left (right next door - heavier reef alternative), River Mouth (immediately north), Pererenan (5 min north - reef option), Batu Bolong / Old Man's (5-10 min south for smaller mellower waves), Berawa (10 min south).
Canggu_Sandbar.boat_charter_required: No - paddle-in from the beach. Some surfers paddle from Echo or Pererenan; boat access not standard.
Canggu_Sandbar.hazards: Powerful thick-lipped wave that breaks heavy; strong rip currents around the peak and on the paddle-out; submerged rocks at low tide; wedgy unpredictable closeouts; occasional crowd-related collisions; river-mouth bacterial water quality after rain (Yeh Poh runoff just north)
Canggu_Sandbar.marine_hazards: Minimal reef life since bottom is mostly sand; possible jellyfish in wet season (transparent box-style stings rare); rare stingrays in shallows - shuffle feet; bacterial water issues post-rain due to nearby river-mouth runoff. No significant shark concern.
Canggu_Sandbar.reef_cut_severity_index: Low to Medium - bottom is predominantly sand so reef cuts are uncommon; submerged rocks at low tide on the inside can cause bruises/cuts but nothing like Echo Left reef
Canggu_Sandbar.rip_current_pattern: Strong rip running through the channel adjacent to the Sandbar peak (used for paddle-out but can drag you out fast); side-sweeping current along the beach toward River Mouth on outgoing tide; rip strength increases significantly on bigger swells
Canggu_Sandbar.lineup_etiquette: Priority on the peak; rotation is observed but more relaxed than Echo Left; don't drop in on the deeper surfer; greet/nod to locals and regulars; respect Indonesian surfers who have priority
Canggu_Sandbar.localism_intensity: Low to Medium - less locally policed than Echo Left because the crowd is more transient (advanced visitors and bodyboarders); aggression rare if etiquette is respected
Canggu_Sandbar.respect_protocol: Earn your place by sitting wide initially; don't paddle inside on the peak; if you're a strong intermediate, surf Echo Right or Berawa first; smile and say hi to the regulars
Canggu_Sandbar.resident_local_legends: Canggu free-surf and bodyboard crew shred the Sandbar regularly; international travelling pros pass through during big swells; specific named legends not consistently documented [uncertain]
Canggu_Sandbar.religious_sensitivity: Pura Batu Mejan (Hindu temple) sits behind Echo Beach - respect ceremonies and offerings; observe Nyepi (Day of Silence, March) - no surfing, beach closed; women on menstruation traditionally avoid temple grounds; cover up walking through the village.
Canggu_Sandbar.medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major trauma), Siloam Hospital Denpasar.
Canggu_Sandbar.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~2-3 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~27 km; Siloam Denpasar ~17 km
Canggu_Sandbar.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski stationed at the Sandbar; Balawista lifeguards informally cover Echo Beach area on busy days; no formal water-patrol
Canggu_Sandbar.emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
Canggu_Sandbar.nearest_surf_school: Most schools (Dian Surf School, Pro Surf, Sea N Surf, Surf Well Bali, Odyssey) operate from Batu Bolong and rarely teach at Sandbar due to wave power; intermediate-advanced surf guiding offered by Padang Padang Surf Camp and Mondo Surf Village.
Canggu_Sandbar.board_rental_availability: No on-beach rental at the Sandbar peak. Rental shops on Echo Beach road (Boardriders Bali Echo Beach, Drifter, Deus Ex Machina) offer shortboards and step-ups suited to Sandbar.
Canggu_Sandbar.surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land photographers shoot the Sandbar peak from the beach and adjacent rocks during dry season; photos sold via Instagram or QR code same day
Canggu_Sandbar.photographer_recommendation: Local Canggu/Echo photographers (freelancers via @balisurfphotos and similar); typical price IDR 200,000-400,000 for an edited photo pack [uncertain]
Canggu_Sandbar.accommodation_options: Echo Beach House, Mocca Bali, Hotel Tugu Bali (luxury heritage), Pondok Nyoman, surf camps such as Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu and Mondo Surf Village; full spectrum from hostels to villas within 5-15 min.
Canggu_Sandbar.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d40 (Surfline Canggu cam covers Echo/Sandbar area); https://balicams.com/camera/rivermouth (River Mouth cam adjacent)
Canggu_Sandbar.drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for surf photography (DGCA registration required for >250g). Avoid flying over the temple, directly over the crowded lineup, or during low-altitude takeoffs. Many photographers fly drones here daily.
Canggu_Sandbar.coworking_nearby: Yes - Dojo Bali, Outpost Canggu, B Work Bali (Nelayan), Tropical Nomad; all within 5-15 min scooter.
Canggu_Sandbar.avg_lesson_price: IDR 500,000-800,000 (~USD 32-50 / TWD 1,000-1,600) for intermediate guiding (most schools do not teach beginners here)
Canggu_Sandbar.board_rental_daily: Performance shortboard IDR 150,000-200,000/day; step-up IDR 175,000-250,000/day from nearby Echo Beach rental shops
Canggu_Sandbar.water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Fair to Good. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to Yeh Poh river-mouth runoff and Echo Beach drainage; ear/skin infection risk. Avoid surfing 24-48h after heavy rain.
Canggu_Sandbar.annual_competition_window: No dedicated WSL or major event at the Sandbar; occasional free-surf sessions and informal contests piggyback off Echo Beach events [uncertain]
Canggu_Sandbar.community_events: Sunset sessions at La Brisa and Sand Bar; Trash Hero Canggu and 4Ocean beach clean-ups; Deus 9ft & Single Festival activities nearby in Canggu; full-moon beach gatherings.

basic_info.name: Bali Surf Community Events (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪社群活動; Indonesian: Acara Komunitas Selancar Bali) - umbrella entry covering Single Fin Sunday Sessions (Uluwatu), Single Fin Wednesday Midweek Sessions, Old Man's Dirty Ol' Wednesdays + Salty Sundays + Saturday Beer Olympics (Canggu Batu Bolong), Deus Ex Machina Sunday Sessions (Canggu), La Brisa LYD Sunday Market (Echo Beach), Pretty Poison weekly skate jams (Canggu), Sundays Beach Club bonfire and ASADO (Ungasan)
basic_info.region: Uluwatu / Canggu (Bukit Peninsula + Echo Beach / Batu Bolong)
basic_info.gps_location: Single Fin Uluwatu: -8.8175, 115.0884 (Jl. Mamo, Suluban Beach). Old Man's: -8.6566, 115.1322 (Jl. Pantai Batu Bolong, Canggu). Deus Ex Machina: -8.6492, 115.1377 (Jl. Batu Mejan 8, Canggu). La Brisa Bali: -8.6457, 115.1245 (Jl. Pantai Batu Mejan, Echo Beach, Canggu). Pretty Poison: -8.6515, 115.1335 (Jl. Subak Canggu). Sundays Beach Club: -8.8424, 115.1322 (The Ungasan, Jl. Pantai Selatan Gau).
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Single Fin Uluwatu: ~22 km (45-60 min); Old Man's Canggu: ~20 km (45-60 min in traffic); Deus Ex Machina Canggu: ~22 km; La Brisa Echo Beach: ~24 km; Pretty Poison: ~21 km; Sundays Beach Club: ~17 km (40-50 min)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Single Fin Uluwatu: ~60-75 min from Canggu / 0 min from Uluwatu cliff. Old Man's: 0 min in Canggu / 60-75 min from Uluwatu. Deus Ex Machina: 5-10 min from Canggu center / 60-75 min from Uluwatu. La Brisa: 5-10 min from Canggu center / 70 min from Uluwatu. Pretty Poison: 5 min from Canggu center / 65-75 min from Uluwatu. Sundays Beach Club: 65-75 min from Canggu / 10-15 min from Uluwatu.

surf_conditions.wave_type: Not applicable to events directly - venues sit above surf breaks. Single Fin overlooks Uluwatu (left-hand reef point break). Old Man's faces Old Man's surf break (Canggu mellow reef-sand right and left). La Brisa overlooks Echo Beach (right-hand reef break). Sundays Beach Club fronts a quiet beach with reef break offshore. Deus Ex Machina and Pretty Poison are inland venues.
surf_conditions.skill_level: Events are spectator/community gatherings open to all - no skill prerequisite. Many attendees are surfers ranging from absolute beginners to pros; lineup-watching from Single Fin terrace is a Bali rite of passage regardless of skill.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Events run year-round independent of swell. Single Fin Sunday is busiest when Uluwatu is firing 4-8 ft; viewing from cliff is best at 3 ft+
surf_conditions.best_season: Year-round; peak attendance during dry season high season (June-August) and Christmas-New Year window (December-early January); shoulder seasons (May, September-October) offer better atmosphere with smaller crowds
