surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: Not applicable - events run regardless. Single Fin Sunday gains extra energy when SW groundswell fires Uluwatu
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: Not applicable - events not dependent on swell
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (extreme) for Single Fin Sunday and Old Man's Wednesday at peak season; 4 for Deus Sunday; 4-5 for La Brisa LYD Market; 3-4 for Pretty Poison weekly jams

practical_info.access: Single Fin: drive to clifftop parking at end of Jl. Mamo, walk down stairs past warungs to bar at top of cave stairs. Old Man's: direct beach-side access from Jl. Pantai Batu Bolong, motorbike parking adjacent. Deus Ex Machina: street parking on Jl. Batu Mejan 8. La Brisa: beach-side at Echo, valet parking available. Pretty Poison: street parking on Jl. Subak Canggu. Sundays Beach Club: descend by funicular cable car from The Ungasan resort or via 168-step stairway.
practical_info.entry_fee: Single Fin: usually FREE entry; cover charge ~IDR 150,000 on big DJ nights or special events. Old Man's: FREE entry every night. Deus Ex Machina: FREE entry; food/drink purchase. La Brisa: FREE entry except ticketed events (LYD Market FREE entry). Pretty Poison: FREE entry, sometimes IDR 100,000-150,000 cover for headline DJ events. Sundays Beach Club: minimum spend / day pass IDR 250,000-450,000 (sunset pass IDR 250,000 redeemable on F&B, day pass higher) [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Single Fin Sunday: arrive by 4:00-4:30 PM for sunset (~6:15 PM dry season); party runs to ~1-2 AM. Single Fin Wednesday Midweek: similar pattern but lighter. Old Man's Dirty Ol' Wednesdays: beer pong sign-up from 6 PM, music heaviest 9 PM - 1 AM. Old Man's Salty Sundays: starts 5 PM running to midnight. Old Man's Saturday Beer Olympics 2v2: evening from ~6 PM. Old Man's Original Canggu Market: last Saturday each month. Deus Ex Machina Sunday live music: from late afternoon ~4 PM. La Brisa LYD Sunday Market: 10 AM - 4 PM. Pretty Poison weekly jams: Tuesdays, Thursdays, Saturdays peak; Sunday late skate jams; bowl open afternoon through night. Sundays Beach Club ASADO BBQ: Wednesdays and Sundays 2 PM-6 PM; daily bonfire from ~6:30 PM with live music 5-8 PM.
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunset window (5-7 PM dry season) is golden hour at all cliff and beach venues; late night 10 PM-2 AM for party energy at Single Fin and Old Man's
practical_info.nearby_warung: Single Fin area: Delpi Rock Lounge, Mango Tree Cafe, Suka Espresso, Drifter Surf Cafe. Old Man's area: Crate Cafe, Echoes Beach Cafe, Warung Bu Mi. Deus area: La Baracca, BGS Cafe. La Brisa neighbors La Laguna, The Lawn at Echo Beach. Pretty Poison surrounded by Canggu street warungs and food trucks.
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Single Fin alternatives: Delpi Rock Lounge sunset spot, El Kabron, Savaya Bali, Ulu Cliffhouse. Old Man's alternatives: Sandbar (next door), La Brisa, The Lawn, Pretty Poison, Mexicola. Pretty Poison alt: Black Sand Brewery, The Shady Shack. Sundays Beach Club alt: El Kabron, Karma Beach, Savaya.
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - all venues road-accessible by motorbike or car

safety_culture.hazards: Late-night motorbike crashes are the #1 risk after Single Fin Sunday and Old Man's parties - heavy alcohol + dark unfamiliar roads + no helmet = high accident rate; always use Gojek/Grab car after drinking; avoid pickpockets in crowded venues; do not leave valuables unattended at La Brisa beach area or Single Fin terrace; reef cuts at Uluwatu cave stairs at high tide if walking down toward break
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Not applicable to indoor/cliff venues; Single Fin cliff exposure - do not climb cliff edge while intoxicated. Sundays Beach Club fronts a swimmable lagoon but reef beyond
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Surf-cultural rule at all venues: do not boast about your surfing or drop names; locals appreciate humility; tip warung staff, photographers and DJs in cash; respect the Indonesian staff with 'terima kasih' and patience
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low to medium at the venues themselves (events welcome international and local mix); medium-high tension can arise late night if outsiders disrespect local Balinese surfers or staff
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Dress code: casual beach-resort wear (shirts/cover-ups when entering venues); no aggressive drunk behavior; do not bring outside drinks; tip generously (IDR 10,000-20,000 per drink at bar is appreciated); be mindful of Hindu offerings (canang sari) on the ground - do not step on
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Single Fin: founders Tai Graham and team; Made 'Bol' Adi Putra, Mega Semadhi, Rizal Tandjung often visible at sunset sessions. Old Man's: long-time Canggu surfer-expat ownership including Australian and Balinese founders. Deus Ex Machina Canggu (Temple of Enthusiasm): Dustin Humphrey early shaper-photographer, central to longboard revival.
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Events pause for Nyepi (Day of Silence, typically March - no events run, beach access banned islandwide). Major Hindu holidays (Galungan, Kuningan, Saraswati) may reduce programming. Avoid loud parties during temple ceremonies at neighboring Pura Luhur Uluwatu and Pura Batu Bolong. Sundays Beach Club closes during Nyepi.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Uluwatu/Single Fin: Unicare Pecatu Clinic (~3-5 km, ~5-10 min), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (~25-30 min), Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~45 min). Canggu venues (Old Man's, Deus, La Brisa, Pretty Poison): Penta Medical Canggu (5 min), BIMC Hospital Kuta (~20-30 min), Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~30-40 min). Sundays Beach Club: Unicare Pecatu (~10 min), BIMC Nusa Dua (~25 min).
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Not applicable - venues do not operate water rescue; Sundays Beach Club has lifeguards on duty during opening hours
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Ambulance: 118/119; BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua: +62 361 3000 911; BIMC Hospital Kuta: +62 361 761 263; Penta Medical Canggu: +62 811 3960 7000 [uncertain]

services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - freelance event photographers shoot at Single Fin Sunday and Old Man's parties; many Bali Instagram photographers cover regular events for venues
services.photographer_recommendation: Most venues post official party photos within 24-48 hours on Instagram (@singlefinbali, @oldmansbali, @deusbali, @labrisabali, @prettypoisonbali) - free to download and tag; private event photography ~IDR 500,000-2,000,000/session [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Near Single Fin Uluwatu: Uluwatu Surf Villas, The Edge Bali, Mu Bali, Suarga Padang Padang, numerous Bukit guesthouses. Near Old Man's Canggu: Kosta Hostel, Beehive Hostel, La Casetta Beachfront Apartments, The Slow, Hotel Tugu Bali. Near La Brisa/Echo: Echo Beach Hotel, COMO Uma Canggu, The Lawn-adjacent villas. Near Sundays Beach Club: The Ungasan Clifftop Resort (parent property).
services.webcam_url: Single Fin streams Uluwatu surf via partnership with Surfline (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/uluwatu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708b4b); Old Man's Canggu cam on Surfline; La Brisa shares Echo Beach cam access. No dedicated indoor venue cams.
services.drone_allowed: Drones generally not allowed flying over crowds at events without permit. Aerial cinematography permits required for commercial work. Single Fin has occasionally hosted licensed aerial coverage during Single Fin Classic and Rip Curl Cup viewing parties.

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not applicable - events are not lessons. Some yoga/surf classes run on event mornings at La Brisa and beach clubs
pricing.board_rental_daily: Not applicable - events not surf rentals. Adjacent surf shops provide rentals: Old Man's-area boards IDR 100,000-200,000/day; Uluwatu/Single Fin-area boards IDR 150,000-250,000/day
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Drink prices flat year-round but venue gets too packed in peak season for chill experience; LYD Market vendor prices stable; Sundays Beach Club day passes higher in high season Jul-Aug, Dec-Jan; Old Man's pricing flat
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Not applicable - venues are individual events not packages
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Beverage price reference: Single Fin beer IDR 60,000-80,000, cocktail IDR 140,000-200,000; Old Man's beer IDR 35,000-55,000, cocktail IDR 100,000-150,000; Deus Ex Machina similar to Old Man's; La Brisa craft cocktails IDR 150,000-220,000; Sundays Beach Club premium IDR 200,000+ cocktails [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Echo Beach (La Brisa) and Canggu coastline can suffer plastic pollution and runoff in wet season Nov-Mar; La Brisa hosts beach clean-ups via LYD; Bali Ocean Week 2025 (June 7-8) hosted at La Brisa specifically combined surf + sustainability
sustainability.eco_certification: La Brisa branded eco-friendly beach club with reclaimed-wood architecture and Sunday LYD Market for organic/sustainable vendors; Sundays Beach Club emphasizes natural-bay preservation; no formal third-party eco-certifications confirmed [uncertain]
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: No widely-advertised carbon offset programs for events; some venues participate in single-use plastic reduction and water refill stations

events_community.annual_competition_window: Single Fin venue hosts the annual Uluwatu Single Fin Classic (longboard invitational) mid-August (6th annual ran August 16, 2025); Single Fin is the unofficial HQ for Rip Curl Cup viewing parties during the August holding window; La Brisa hosts Bali Ocean Week each June; Old Man's hosts surf-themed nights during Canggu surf competitions
events_community.community_events: WEEKLY RECURRING: (1) Single Fin Sunday Sessions, Uluwatu - the original Bali Sunday session founded ~2008 by Tai Graham and team; extended hours 8 AM - 2 AM Sunday; live DJs from 4 PM; sunset crowd peaks ~5-7 PM; party to 1-2 AM; world-renowned cliff sunset; (2) Single Fin Wednesday Midweek Sessions - extended hours 8 AM - 2 AM; live DJ + acoustic acts; quieter than Sunday but iconic in its own right; (3) Old Man's Dirty Ol' Wednesdays, Canggu Batu Bolong - flagship event since early 2010s; beer pong tournament + 'dirty disco' DJs; biggest weekly party in Canggu; (4) Old Man's Salty Sundays - 5 PM start; rotating DJs through midnight; (5) Old Man's Beer Olympics 2v2 - every Saturday; (6) Old Man's Original Canggu Market - last Saturday of each month; (7) Deus Ex Machina (Temple of Enthusiasm) Sunday Sessions - live bands in backyard from late afternoon; longboard / motorbike culture hub; Taco & Tat-Tuesdays for free 20-min tattoos with burrito purchase; (8) La Brisa LYD Sunday Market, Echo Beach - every Sunday 10 AM - 4 PM; 100+ local makers, organic produce, zero-waste vendors, sustainable fashion; (9) Pretty Poison, Canggu - weekly skate jams Tuesdays/Thursdays/Saturdays peak; Sunday late skate jams; DJ + live band + indie film nights; (10) Sundays Beach Club, Ungasan - ASADO open-fire BBQ every Wednesday + Sunday 2-6 PM; daily sunset bonfire with marshmallow toasting + live music 5-8 PM. SEASONAL: Christmas/New Year mega-parties at Single Fin and Old Man's; full-moon and new-moon events; ad-hoc surf premiere film screenings at Deus and Old Man's; Rip Curl Cup opening ceremony at Single Fin first weekend of August featuring Kecak Dance and invitee introductions.

basic_info.name: Desert Point / Bangko-Bangko (Chinese: 沙漠角/邦科邦科; Indonesian: Bangko-Bangko / Desert Point)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.7569, 115.8186 (Bangko-Bangko / Desert Point, Sekotong, West Lombok; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/DesertPointBangkoBangko)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~90-110 km straight-line east-southeast + sea crossing; total travel ~5-9 hrs via Padangbai-Lembar ferry then overland (4-5 hr drive from Lembar Harbor on Lombok via Sekotong peninsula to Bangko-Bangko), or ~3-5 hrs via private boat charter from Benoa/Serangan Harbor direct to Desert Point
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Not applicable directly - requires inter-island transfer; from Bali (Canggu/Uluwatu) typical access is boat charter from Benoa Harbor ~3-5 hrs depending on vessel, or overland via Lembar ferry adds 60-90 min motorbike from Canggu to harbor; from Uluwatu add 30-45 min to harbor

surf_conditions.wave_type: World-class long left-hand reef/point break - one of the world's premier left-handers and considered alongside Skeleton Bay (Namibia) for sheer barrel length. Hollow, fast, mechanical tube that breaks over sharp shallow coral on a remote point on Lombok's southwest tip.
surf_conditions.skill_level: Advanced to Pro only - critical take-off, fast hollow barrel sections over very shallow reef; not suitable for intermediate surfers even on smaller days due to crowd intensity and reef severity
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 4-12+ ft faces (head-high to triple overhead); typically fires from 4-8 ft, with rare 10-15 ft days; needs proper SW groundswell (typically 6 ft+ at 14s+) to start working
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp shallow coral reef - extremely shallow at low tide; reef extends along the entire point
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-September); needs SW Indian Ocean groundswell from southern hemisphere storms; the wave is notoriously fickle and may only fire 10-30 quality days per season
surf_conditions.best_tide: Low tide only - the wave is extremely tide-sensitive; goes flat at high tide and can transform from double-overhead barrels at low tide to nothing at high tide on the same swell; some configurations work mid-dropping tide
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore E to ENE (east-northeast) trade winds prevailing in dry season; light N wind can give extra shape to barrels; afternoon onshore SW common
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) groundswell from deep Indian Ocean lows; SSW also works; long-period swells (14-18s) essential for the proper mechanical lined-up barrels
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 2 (notoriously fickle - 'feast or famine'; can be flat for weeks then fire for 3-5 days when conditions align; requires patient swell-chasing or swell forecasting precision)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 200-300+ meters; legendary rides have been reported up to 300m yielding 15-20 second barrels; on perfect days surfers exit the same barrel they entered after a long tube ride
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Very fast - one of the fastest hollow waves in Indonesia; requires step-up or specialised tube board (6'2-7'0 step-up typical); reading the section ahead is critical
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Very high - ledging fast take-off directly into a barrel section over shallow reef; minimal margin for error; helmets and booties strongly recommended; the take-off zone is small and crowded when firing
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: The point has a few named sections: the Take-Off Bowl, the Office (main barrel zone), the Grower, and the Exit/Inside section; on perfect days these link into one continuous tube ride; no significant alternative peaks on the same point
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 4 (when firing the lineup is packed with international tube-hunters and pros; small take-off zone makes it competitive; outside of peak swell windows it can be empty)

practical_info.access: Two options: (1) Boat charter from Bali (Benoa/Serangan Harbor) direct to Desert Point - 3-5 hrs via fast boat or overnight via yacht/phinisi; (2) Overland - Padangbai/Sanur fast boat to Lombok (Senggigi/Bangsal/Lembar) then 4-5 hr drive through West Lombok to Sekotong peninsula and unpaved jungle road to Bangko-Bangko (4x4 strongly recommended for final stretch). Boat-based stays (live-aboard) are popular for tube-chasers during major swell events.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: From the rocky/sand point or from a moored boat - paddle out via the channel on the inside of the takeoff zone (avoid paddling over the reef during low tide); local boatmen offer short transfers from beach/anchored boats to lineup; carefully time the paddle-out between sets
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Tide-driven rather than time-driven - schedule around the low-tide window regardless of time of day; dawn-low-tide or afternoon-low-tide both produce; morning sessions favored for offshore wind
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Not strictly required (land-based homestays available) but boat charter is the classic and most efficient access option, especially during big swells when the unpaved road becomes impassable. Most international tube-chasers stay on live-aboard boats anchored in the bay.
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: Excellent - Desert Point is a classic Bali-extension trip; commonly combined with Bali (Bukit), G-Land (Java), Sumbawa (Lakey Peak, Scar Reef, Supersuck), and Sumba/Rote via multi-spot boat charters or 'Indo surf safari' itineraries departing Benoa Harbor
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Limited - the southwest tip of Lombok is remote; nearby alternatives on Sekotong peninsula are mainly small fishing-village reef setups; on smaller days nearby Gili Gede area offers mellower options. Most boat charters reposition to Belongas Bay (south Lombok) or Are Guling/Mawi on south Lombok coast if Deserts is not working [uncertain]

safety_culture.hazards: Extremely sharp shallow coral reef (reef breaks dry at very low tide), strong rip currents along the point, crowded competitive lineup, remote location with slow medical evacuation, malaria-endemic area (West Lombok), dengue, sea urchins, occasional sea snakes, sunburn/dehydration in remote camp conditions
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (wear booties), occasional banded sea kraits (highly venomous but rarely aggressive), stonefish possible, reef cuts with very high tropical-water infection risk; sharks not a documented issue
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Strict tube priority - the inside surfer with the deepest take-off gets priority on the set; do not paddle past the take-off zone or snake locals; respect Indonesian/Bali pros and camp regulars who earn first picks; the lineup gets aggressive when it fires - patience required
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium to high - not violent localism but talent-stacked lineup of international and Indonesian pros means new visitors will struggle for set waves; respect, patience and demonstration of ability earn waves; smile and chat with locals onshore
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Be respectful to Sasak Muslim locals onshore (dress modestly in fishing villages, avoid public alcohol consumption, observe Ramadan etiquette); tip homestay/camp hosts; do not damage reef; help with rescues if needed; share local knowledge gracefully
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Wave 'discovered' by Australian travellers in the late 1970s/early 1980s (variously credited to Bill Heick / Bob Laverty / Aussie crews); has hosted nearly every name in pro surfing including Tom Curren, Kelly Slater, Mick Fanning; legendary deep-tube sessions by names like Bruce Irons and Damien Hobgood [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Sekotong peninsula is predominantly Sasak Muslim - dress modestly off-beach, avoid public alcohol, observe Ramadan fasting hours discreetly; no major Hindu temple at Bangko-Bangko itself but neighboring areas have animist/Hindu sites
safety_culture.medical_nearby: No clinic at Bangko-Bangko itself; nearest health post is at Sekotong village ~1-2 hr drive away (basic puskesmas only); proper hospital is in Mataram (Rumah Sakit Umum Provinsi NTB, Siloam Hospital Mataram) ~3-4 hrs road distance; serious cases evacuated to BIMC/Siloam Bali by sea ambulance or air evacuation
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - no permanent rescue jet ski on duty; some boat-charter crews carry skis for tow-in/rescue during big swell events but coverage is informal
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; nearest hospital Siloam Mataram +62 370 6175000; for sea/air evacuation contact travel insurance provider or BIMC Bali +62 361 3000 911; satellite phone often required from camps as cell signal is patchy

services.accommodation_options: On-point: Desert Point Surf Lodge / Bangko-Bangko Surf Camp / Desert Point Bungalows (basic in-season homestays with shared facilities, generally only open May-October); nearby Sekotong area: Bebaleq Desert Point (Labuhanpoh), various budget homestays; high-end eco-resort Jeeva Beloam Beach Camp (further away on south Lombok); live-aboard yachts and phinisi charters anchored in bay are common - often booked through Bali-based operators
services.webcam_url: Surfline Desert Point spot page: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/desert-point/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d55 (forecast; live cam coverage limited or absent) [uncertain]
services.surf_photographer_available: Some camps employ in-house photographers during peak season; freelance water/land shooters travel with boat charters; not consistently staffed [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Excellent - remote location with no urban runoff; wet-season storms can stir sediment but generally pristine water year-round

events_community.annual_competition_window: No regular pro contest currently scheduled at Desert Point; in-season feast-style camp life with informal big-swell gatherings; occasional film/photo shoots organized by surf brands [uncertain]

Dreamland.name: Dreamland (Chinese: 梦幻沙滩; Indonesian: Pantai Dreamland / Pantai New Kuta Beach)
Dreamland.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Approximately 16-18 km from Ngurah Rai (DPS) Airport (around 35-45 min by car)
Dreamland.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~70-85 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: ~10-15 min by scooter
Dreamland.wave_type: A-frame beach break / reef break with sandy and flat-rock-shelf bottom; short right and longer fast left
Dreamland.skill_level: Beginner to Intermediate primary; Advanced on bigger days (4 ft+) when outside peaks fire
Dreamland.wave_height_range: 2-6 ft; average 2-5 ft; most consistent and fun at 3-5 ft
Dreamland.bottom_type: Mixed - sand on the inside (forgiving) with flat rock/reef shelf underneath; deeper water when small, shallower feel when big
Dreamland.best_season: Dry season, May to September/October (peak June-August); SW-W groundswells with E trade winds
Dreamland.best_tide: Low to mid tide (lower is better); avoid full high tide which can backwash off the cliff/shorebreak
Dreamland.best_wind: Offshore from the East (E trades); the cliff/river gap funnels cross-offshore winds into directly offshore
Dreamland.swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 during dry season - reliable but smaller than Uluwatu/Padang [uncertain]
Dreamland.wave_length_meters: Short to medium - typically 30-80 m rides; left section can run longer on bigger days
Dreamland.wave_speed_rating: Medium - mellower than Bukit reef breaks; left can be fast on larger days
Dreamland.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Low to moderate - forgiving drop with sandy/deeper take-off; outside peaks on bigger swells have steeper drops
Dreamland.secondary_peaks: Yes - inside left (beginner-friendly), outside A-frame (left and right), occasional smaller peaks along the bay
Dreamland.peak_crowd_level: 3/5 - moderate; busier in beginner lesson hours 9am-12pm and weekends; less crowded than Padang/Uluwatu
Dreamland.access: Easy - drive in via Jalan New Kuta Raya through the New Kuta/Pecatu Indah Resort area, paved road to large parking lot, then short flight of stairs/paved path down to beach (~5 min walk)
Dreamland.paddle_out_method: Paddle straight out from the sand through the shorebreak; use the channel between the A-frame peaks; minimal duck-diving required on small days
Dreamland.entry_fee: Parking IDR 5,000-10,000 (scooter/car); informal beach donation ~IDR 10,000 sometimes collected by locals; access through New Kuta Resort is generally free
Dreamland.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (6-8am) for glassy and uncrowded; second window 4-6pm for sunset session
Dreamland.best_time_of_day: Early morning before lessons start (~7am) or late afternoon after 3pm
Dreamland.nearby_warung: Beachfront warungs serving nasi goreng, fresh coconuts, Bintang; Klapa New Kuta Beach Club above the cliff; food stalls in the parking area; Jenggala Café nearby
Dreamland.alternative_spot_within_10min: Balangan (left point, more advanced), Bingin (mid-tide reef left), Padang Padang Right (small day option), Nyang Nyang on bigger swells
Dreamland.hazards: Strong shorebreak, rip currents running out to sea, submerged rocks and reef shelf especially at low tide, occasional jellyfish, sunburn on exposed white-sand beach
Dreamland.marine_hazards: Occasional jellyfish (especially shoulder seasons), sea urchins near rocky edges, no significant stonefish/sea snake reports; treat stings with vinegar and seawater rinse
Dreamland.reef_cut_severity_index: Low to medium - mostly sand with flat rock shelf; less sharp than Uluwatu/Impossibles but feet/ankle scrapes possible at very low tide
Dreamland.rip_current_pattern: Rip current channel typically forms between the A-frame peaks and against the cliffs on either end of the bay; identifiable by darker water with foam moving seaward; swim/paddle parallel to shore to escape
Dreamland.lineup_etiquette: Mixed crowd of learners and locals - extra patience required; respect priority on takeoff; beginners should stay on the inside; avoid the deeper outside line if not capable
Dreamland.localism_intensity: Low - very tourist-friendly atmosphere; lots of surf schools operate here so locals are accustomed to learners
Dreamland.respect_protocol: Be courteous to surf school students, don't snake learners, support local warungs and parking attendants with tips
Dreamland.resident_local_legends: No iconic local pro associated specifically with Dreamland; many Bukit-area surfers warm up here on small days [uncertain]
Dreamland.religious_sensitivity: Respect Balinese ceremonies (canang sari offerings on sand - don't step on); Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - beach closed; cover up when leaving the beach in residential areas
Dreamland.medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (closest, 24/7 with surf injury expertise), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua, Siloam Hospital Denpasar
Dreamland.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu ~2-3 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~10 km; Siloam Denpasar ~18 km
Dreamland.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; informal lifeguard / Balawista lifeguard presence in high season (red-yellow flag area sometimes set up) [uncertain]
Dreamland.emergency_contact: Indonesia emergency: 112; Bali Search and Rescue (Basarnas): +62 361 9351111; Balawista lifeguard: +62 361 755660 [uncertain]
Dreamland.nearest_surf_school: Dreamland Surf School (on the beach), Rip Curl School of Surf (Dreamland branch), Odyssey Surf School, Bali Green Surf School, Pro Surf School
Dreamland.board_rental_availability: Widely available on the beach - softboards, foam longboards, mid-lengths and shortboards from multiple stalls
Dreamland.surf_photographer_available: Yes - freelance land photographers on the beach and cliff; some water photographers during peak season
Dreamland.photographer_recommendation: Beach-based freelancers offer photo packages IDR 150,000-300,000; advance booking via Instagram common [uncertain]
Dreamland.accommodation_options: Klapa Resort, New Kuta Resort, Mamo Hotel, Dreamland Suites, numerous villas and homestays in Pecatu; Uluwatu/Bingin lodgings nearby
Dreamland.webcam_url: https://balicams.com/camera/dreamland ; surf-forecast.com webcam feeds [uncertain - status of live cams may change]
Dreamland.drone_allowed: Generally permitted for personal use; avoid hovering over crowds and Klapa beach club; commercial filming requires permits
Dreamland.coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu, Drifter Surf Cafe (Uluwatu), Suka Espresso - all ~10-15 min by scooter
Dreamland.avg_lesson_price: ~IDR 550,000 (USD ~35-40) for 2-hour group lesson; private lessons IDR 700,000-1,200,000
Dreamland.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season - clear turquoise water; can be murkier in wet season due to runoff from the New Kuta/cliff drainage
Dreamland.annual_competition_window: Hosts occasional regional and grom contests; not a primary WSL venue [uncertain]
Dreamland.community_events: Sunset beach gatherings, Klapa beach club events, occasional Balinese ceremonies on the sand; no formal weekly surf community ritual like Single Fin Sunday

basic_info.name: Eco / Sustainable Surf Camps and Initiatives in Bali (Chinese: 峇里島生態永續衝浪營; Indonesian: Kemah Selancar Ramah Lingkungan di Bali) - including Eco Surf Rescue Uluwatu / Project Clean Uluwatu (ESRU/PCU), SeaTrees x Ocean Gardener (Nusa Penida coral restoration), Kayu Surfboards (balsa wood surfboards Canggu), Suarga Padang Padang (B Corp surf eco-resort), and Mana Earthly Paradise (B Corp Ubud) as eco-stay alternative
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (key nodes: Uluwatu/Pecatu, Padang Padang, Canggu/Berawa, Nusa Penida/Ped)
basic_info.gps_location: Eco Surf Rescue Uluwatu / Project Clean Uluwatu: Uluwatu cliff (~-8.8154, 115.0884); SeaTrees x Ocean Gardener: Ped, Nusa Penida (~-8.6840, 115.5470) [uncertain]; Kayu Surfboards: Jl. Pantai Berawa, Canggu (~-8.6630, 115.1340) [uncertain]; Suarga Padang Padang: Jl. Labuansait, Pecatu (~-8.8090, 115.1050) [uncertain]; Mana Earthly Paradise: Sayan, Ubud (~-8.5089, 115.2492)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Uluwatu/PCU: ~22 km (45-60 min); Suarga Padang Padang: ~20 km (45-55 min); Kayu Surfboards Canggu: ~17 km (45-60 min); Mana Earthly Paradise Ubud: ~35 km (75-90 min); Nusa Penida (SeaTrees site): ~30 min by car to Sanur + 45 min fast boat to Toyapakeh

surf_conditions.wave_type: Operators tied to wave-types via location: Eco Surf Rescue/PCU protects Uluwatu (left-hand reef point); Suarga overlooks Padang Padang (world-class left reef tube); Kayu boards designed for Bukit reefs (Padang/Uluwatu/Bingin) and Canggu sandbars; SeaTrees Nusa Penida site adjacent to reef breaks like Playgrounds, Lacerations. [uncertain]
surf_conditions.skill_level: Wide range - eco-businesses serve all skill levels via their wave-location: beginner-friendly Canggu (Kayu nearby) up to Pro-level Padang Padang/Uluwatu (Suarga/PCU). Kayu balsa boards typically intermediate-advanced due to weight.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Operator coverage spans full range: 1-4 ft beginner beach breaks (Canggu) up to 6-15 ft Uluwatu/Padang Padang [uncertain]
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Mixed - sand (Canggu near Kayu); sharp limestone/coral reef (Uluwatu, Padang Padang); coral reef (Nusa Penida SeaTrees site)
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October for Bukit operations (Suarga, PCU at Uluwatu, Padang Padang); year-round operations for Kayu shaping facility and SeaTrees coral nursery work
surf_conditions.best_tide: Varies by location operator serves - PCU/Uluwatu mid-high tide; Padang Padang mid-high; Canggu sandbars mid tide [uncertain]
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE/E trade winds for west and south coast spots May-October
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW groundswell for west coast spots; S-SSW for Bukit
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (Bukit) and 4 (Canggu) - all eco operators benefit from highly consistent Indo Ocean swells [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: Varies by adjacent break - Padang Padang 50-100m tubes; Uluwatu 100-300m linked sections [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Mostly fast (Bukit reefs) - eco surfboard makers like Kayu design rocker/fin setups for these waves
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Operator territory ranges from low (Canggu sandbar take-offs) to very high (Padang Padang shallow ledge)
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Padang Padang Right (beginner-friendly inside section opposite Suarga); Uluwatu's multiple peaks (Outside, Peak, Racetracks, Temples) protected by PCU
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 at Uluwatu/Padang Padang (very crowded dry season) - PCU/ESRU active to mitigate visitor impact; 4 at Canggu near Kayu showroom [uncertain]

practical_info.access: Eco Surf Rescue / PCU: visible signage and cleanup events around Uluwatu cliff stairs; volunteer via website. Suarga Padang Padang: on-site clifftop boutique resort with private path to Padang Padang. Kayu Surfboards: showroom on Jl. Pantai Berawa Canggu, walk-in welcome. SeaTrees Bali project: via Ocean Gardener visits to Ped, Nusa Penida (fast boat from Sanur). Mana Earthly Paradise: ~10 min from central Ubud by car.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: N/A for eco-businesses directly; surfers using their adjacent waves follow normal access (Uluwatu cave; Padang Padang stairs)
practical_info.entry_fee: ESRU/PCU: free to support; volunteer cleanups open to public. SeaTrees: USD 25 plants one coral piece (donation-based, online). Kayu showroom: free entry. Suarga: hotel guest only or restaurant booking. [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Eco operators run business hours (9 AM-6 PM typical); PCU cleanup events monthly typically Sunday mornings 7-10 AM
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Visit Kayu showroom or Suarga in mornings; SeaTrees diving sessions early morning visibility best
practical_info.nearby_warung: Suarga: in-house Cliff Restaurant + nearby Single Fin, Drifter, La Brisa Uluwatu; Kayu: Berawa cafes including Crate, Old Mans nearby, Shady Shack; PCU operates from Uluwatu cliff with multiple warungs; Mana: own probiotic restaurant; SeaTrees Penida: Penida Colada, Warung Pondok Nusa Garden
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: From Suarga: Bingin (5 min), Impossibles (5 min), Uluwatu (5 min); from Kayu Berawa: Echo Beach (5 min), Pererenan (5 min), Batu Bolong (10 min)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Only for SeaTrees Nusa Penida site (fast boat from Sanur Harbor); all other operators land-accessible

safety_culture.hazards: Standard surf hazards for adjacent waves: reef cuts, urchins, currents at Uluwatu/Padang Padang; for SeaTrees diving volunteer: standard scuba dive risks. Eco operators themselves are safe (showrooms, NGO operations).
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on Uluwatu/Padang reef; SeaTrees diving site has reef sharks (harmless), occasional jellyfish
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: High at Padang Padang and Uluwatu (sharp limestone karang); booties strongly recommended
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Padang Padang has a strong channel rip; Uluwatu has channel between Peak and Racetracks
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: PCU advocates for low-impact local etiquette: pack out trash, respect locals, no glass on cliffs, recycle. Eco-conscious surfers tend to model good lineup behavior.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium-high at Uluwatu/Padang Padang regardless of eco-affiliation - respect local pros and Balinese surfers
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Volunteer with PCU/ESRU shows respect for local environment; do not litter on cliff; participate in monthly Uluwatu cleanup; pay tip to local instructors and warung owners; respect Pura Luhur Uluwatu ceremonies
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Curtis Lowe (Project Manager, Project Clean Uluwatu); Made Vana (R.O.L.E. Foundation co-founder); Ocean Gardener founder Vincent Chalias (Bali-based coral expert); Kelly Slater has visited and supported SeaTrees coral projects; Sustainable Surf co-founder Kevin Whilden [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Uluwatu sits below Pura Luhur Uluwatu - all operations respect temple proximity; Nyepi Day of Silence pauses all activities; ceremony days require respectful behavior near temple
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Unicare Pecatu Clinic for Uluwatu/Padang Padang area; Penta Medical Canggu for Berawa; Siloam Hospital Denpasar for serious cases; Nusa Penida: Pondok Baruna clinic basic only
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Suarga Padang Padang to Unicare Pecatu: ~5 km; Kayu Berawa to Penta Medical Canggu: ~3 km; Nusa Penida site to local clinic: ~5-10 km [uncertain]
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - eco operators do not maintain rescue jet skis; PCU coordinates with Indo Surf Crew and local lifesavers during big swell events
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general: 112; Ambulance: 118/119; BIMC Nusa Dua: +62 361 3000 911; PCU contact: info@projectcleanuluwatu.org [uncertain]; SeaTrees: hello@seatrees.org

services.nearest_surf_school: Suarga Padang Padang partners with onsite/nearby surf coaching; Padang Padang Surf Camp adjacent. Kayu does not operate a surf school but partners with local shapers/coaches. PCU/ESRU are NGOs (not schools) - direct beginners to ethical local schools like Padang Padang Surf Camp, Rapture Camps Bukit, Kala Surf Camp.
services.board_rental_availability: Kayu offers their balsa-wood boards for retail purchase rather than daily rental; some demo boards available at showroom [uncertain]. Suarga has board rental for guests onsite [uncertain].
services.surf_photographer_available: Suarga arranges in-house surf photography for guests [uncertain]; PCU has documentary footage from cleanup events; SeaTrees Bali has professional underwater videographers documenting coral restoration
services.photographer_recommendation: For eco-themed surf storytelling: Sustainable Surf media network and SeaTrees content team; local Bukit photographers integrated with Suarga [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Suarga Padang Padang (signature eco-resort, ~USD 250-600/night peak season) [uncertain]; Mana Earthly Paradise Ubud (eco-villas, ~USD 80-200/night) [uncertain]; other eco-friendly options: Bambu Indah (Ubud), Desa Hay Canggu, Mu Bali Bingin
services.webcam_url: Surfline Uluwatu cam: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/uluwatu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708b4b ; Padang Padang cam (via Surfline)
services.drone_allowed: Allowed for personal use - Suarga is drone-friendly for guest content; PCU/SeaTrees produce drone footage for advocacy; commercial work requires Indonesian permit [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Berawa Canggu (Kayu area): Outpost Canggu, Tropical Nomad, B Work Bali; Bukit/Pecatu: Outpost Ulu, B Work Bali Pecatu; Ubud (Mana): Hubud, Outpost Ubud

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not directly applicable - eco operators sell goods/services rather than lessons. Reference partners' rates: ~USD 30-60 per group lesson at ethical schools nearby [uncertain]
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: N/A for eco operators directly; partner schools IDR 400,000-800,000 per group lesson [uncertain]
pricing.board_rental_daily: Suarga in-house rental ~IDR 250,000-400,000/day [uncertain]; Kayu typically retail-only, demo days occasional
pricing.gopro_rental_price: Not standardly offered by eco operators [uncertain]
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: Suarga provides rashguards to surf guests [uncertain]; Kayu sells eco-merchandise including organic-cotton tees
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: Kayu offers ding repair services on their wooden boards [uncertain]; standard PU board repair in Canggu/Uluwatu IDR 150,000-400,000 depending on size of ding
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: Suarga Padang Padang offered as surf retreat partner with Perfect Wave Travel - 7-night surf packages from ~USD 1,800-3,500 (depending on room type/season) [uncertain]
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Suarga packages typically include: clifftop accommodation, daily breakfast, surf coaching with partner schools, transfers to surf spots, yoga sessions; SeaTrees engagement: USD 25 per coral planted (donation, comes with certificate and updates on growth)
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Suarga: ~30-40% higher in peak (July-August, Christmas/NY); off-peak deals in November and February-March [uncertain]; SeaTrees donation pricing fixed at USD 25/coral year-round

sustainability.water_quality_rating: PCU has measurably improved Uluwatu water quality - over 2 million litres of wastewater diverted from ocean via wastewater gardens; Nusa Penida (SeaTrees) waters cleaner than Bali mainland due to less runoff; Suarga area benefits from PCU work
sustainability.eco_certification: Mana Earthly Paradise: Certified B Corporation (October 2022) - first B Corp hotel in Indonesia and Southeast Asia; Suarga Padang Padang: not formally B Corp certified [uncertain] but follows comprehensive sustainability practices (FSC-certified materials, solar-positive, recycled wood, rainwater harvesting); SeaTrees: program of 501(c)(3) Sustainable Surf (US tax-deductible); ESRU/PCU partners include GUS Foundation, ROLE Foundation, Eco Bali Recycling, IDEP; Kayu Surfboards: not B Corp but operates closed-loop using Indonesian-sourced balsa, biodegradable packaging, no single-use plastics, lifetime guarantee on boards
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: SeaTrees offers tangible offset model: USD 25 = 1 coral planted (also offers Plant Trees in Bali via tree-planting subscriptions ~USD 8/tree); Boardriders x SeaTrees partnership offsets surf industry impact; PCU does not offer monetary offset but reinvests volunteer hours and donations in waste-water gardens; Suarga produces more solar energy than consumed (net-positive energy claim); Mana sources 95% of lighting from solar

events_community.annual_competition_window: PCU often coordinates with Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang (August holding period) for sustainability messaging at the event; Single Fin Uluwatu hosts Project Clean fundraisers; Rapture Camps annual Uluwatu Challenge cleanup
events_community.community_events: Monthly Project Clean Uluwatu beach/cliff cleanups; SeaTrees x Boardriders coral planting volunteer days at Ped, Nusa Penida; Kayu hosts shaping workshops and pop-ups at Bali Spirit Festival; World Oceans Day (June 8) paddle-outs at Uluwatu; International Surfing Day (June 20) cleanups; Earth Day (April 22) tree-planting at Mana Earthly Paradise

visa_logistics.visa_type_recommended: VoA / e-VoA 30 days for short eco-volunteer visits; B211B social visa for longer (60-180 day) volunteer work with NGOs like PCU or SeaTrees / Ocean Gardener; KITAS work permit for paid positions

basic_info.name: G-Land / Plengkung (Chinese: G浪/普倫昆; Indonesian: Plengkung / Pantai Plengkung / G-Land)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.7197, 114.3617 (Plengkung Beach, Alas Purwo National Park, Banyuwangi, East Java; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Plengkung)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~140 km straight-line east + sea crossing (Bali Strait); total transfer ~3-4 hrs via fast boat from Jimbaran/Benoa, or 7-10 hrs overland via Gilimanuk-Ketapang ferry
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Not applicable directly - requires inter-island transfer; from Jimbaran/Benoa fast boat ~2-3 hrs; from Canggu add 60-90 min motorbike to harbor; from Uluwatu add 30-45 min motorbike to harbor

surf_conditions.wave_type: World-class long left-hand reef/point break along east side of Grajagan Bay; one of the world's premier left-handers. Multiple named sections from top to bottom: Kongs (top, 200-400m walls), Money Trees (main world-class barreling section), Launching Pads (backdoor section), Speedies (fastest/heaviest barrel section), and the Inside.
surf_conditions.skill_level: Advanced to Pro only (especially Money Trees, Launching Pads, and Speedies); Kongs section is more approachable for strong intermediates on smaller days but still over sharp coral reef
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 2-12+ ft Hawaiian scale (approx. 4-25 ft faces); Kongs holds 2-12 ft, Money Trees 2-10 ft, Speedies 2-8 ft+ - massive swells can render Speedies unrideable
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp shallow coral reef (extending hundreds of metres along the point); reef becomes shallower further down the point
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season mid-April to mid-October (peak May-September); one of the most consistent waves in the world during season with reliable SW Indian Ocean groundswell and offshore SE trade winds
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide preferred for safer reef coverage especially at Money Trees and Speedies; low tide produces hollowest barrels but dangerously shallow over reef
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE (Southeast) trade winds prevailing in dry season; light wind ideal; afternoon onshore SW common
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) groundswell from deep Indian Ocean lows; SSW also works; long-period swells essential for proper barrels through Money Trees/Speedies
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (one of the most consistent world-class waves on the planet during April-October season)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 200-500+ meters; full Kongs-to-Speedies link-up on big clean days can yield 600m+ rides (multiple sections)
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium to very fast (Kongs slower wall, Money Trees medium-fast world-class barrel, Speedies extremely fast hollow tube)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High to very high - shallow reef, fast drops, ledging waves especially at Speedies; positioning critical; Speedies has caused most serious injuries documented at G-Land
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - the wave has 4-5 named sections that can link or break independently: Kongs (outside top), Money Trees, Launching Pads (between MT and Speedies), Speedies, and Inside; also '20-20s' less-frequented outside peak
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 3-4 (busy in season especially when Money Trees fires; crowds are largely camp guests plus visiting boat trips; thins on smaller days; can feel uncrowded by world-class-wave standards but lineup competitive for set waves)

practical_info.access: Two options: (1) Fast boat from Bali (Jimbaran, Benoa, Grand Mirage or Kuta beach) - 'G-Land Express' departs early morning ~6 AM, takes 2-3 hrs across Bali Strait; (2) Overland via Gilimanuk-Ketapang ferry then 3-4 hr drive through Alas Purwo National Park (long, bumpy jungle road; 4x4 recommended). Most surfers book through camp packages which include transfer.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: From camp beach paddle around the inside reef to channel between sections; usually walk along sand to the takeoff zone of the desired section then paddle out via channels (each section has a paddle-out channel); local boatmen offer ski/boat tows to the lineup on bigger days
practical_info.entry_fee: Alas Purwo National Park entry fee ~IDR 150,000-250,000 per person (foreigner rate; varies weekday/weekend) plus separate vehicle permit; surf camp packages typically include the park fee
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for cleanest offshore conditions before trade winds build; second session mid-afternoon after wind drops; mid-tide windows around morning peak ideal
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Early morning (5:30-9:00 AM) for glassy conditions; late afternoon (3:30-6:00 PM) often cleans up again
practical_info.nearby_warung: No standalone warungs - the only food/drink available is at the surf camps (Bobby's, Joyo's, Jack's Surf Camp, Jawa Jiwa); meals included in all camp packages; bring snacks if day-tripping by boat
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: No nearby alternative surf spots - G-Land is isolated within Alas Purwo National Park; closest alternatives are Tiger Tracks (just inside Grajagan Bay) on smaller days, or Red Island (Pulau Merah) which is a 2+ hr drive away
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - land-based stay at surf camps is standard; boat is used as transport from Bali but the wave itself is paddle-from-beach. Some big-day operations use jet skis/boats for tow-ups.

safety_culture.hazards: Extremely sharp shallow coral reef, powerful Indian Ocean waves (pelvic fractures historically documented), strong currents along point, malaria-prone area (mosquito-borne disease), dengue, sunburn/sunstroke in equatorial heat, remote location (slow medical evacuation), occasional sea snakes and stonefish on reef, jungle wildlife (panthers, dholes, snakes in Alas Purwo)
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef, stonefish (camouflaged on reef - extremely venomous, immerse wound in hot water), occasional sea snakes (highly venomous but non-aggressive), reef cuts with high infection risk in tropical water; sharks rarely reported as aggressive at G-Land
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Very High - one of the most severe reef-cut risks in Indonesia; live coral reef extends hundreds of meters; booties strongly recommended; Money Trees and Speedies wipeouts can grate skin across long reef sections; camp medics (Joyo's Puma) skilled at stitching
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong rip channel along the reef edge between sections (useful for paddle-outs); main current flows out to sea around the southwest tip of the point; deeper-water channel between Money Trees and Speedies is the primary paddle-out lane
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Respect priority based on positioning at takeoff zone; locals (camp staff and Indonesian/Bali pros) earn first priority; pros visiting from boat charters often demand priority on set waves; do not snake, do not paddle around to inside
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium - generally friendly compared to Bukit Bali but lineup is talent-stacked with pros and serious surfers; respect required; competitive for set waves at Money Trees
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Respect Alas Purwo National Park rules (no littering, no removing flora/fauna, sacred site Pura Luhur Giri Salaka inside the park), be courteous to camp staff and other surfers, observe priority, do not surf beyond your ability - rescue is slow and far from definitive care
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Gerry Lopez (American), Peter McCabe (Australian) put G-Land on the map in 1983; Mike Boyum (Bobby's father, original camp founder); Mega Semadhi and other Bali/Indonesian pros are regulars; numerous WSL CT pros visit (Mick Fanning, Joel Parkinson, Kelly Slater have all surfed Money Trees)
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Alas Purwo is one of Bali-Java's most spiritually significant Hindu sites - Pura Luhur Giri Salaka temple inside the national park is highly sacred; respect ceremonies and pilgrim activity; modest behavior in the park; local Javanese majority is Muslim - dress modestly in Banyuwangi town
safety_culture.medical_nearby: On-camp medical: Joyo's Surf Camp has skilled medic 'Puma' for stitching reef cuts; Bobby's and Jack's have first-aid kits and trained staff. Nearest hospital: Banyuwangi (Rumah Sakit Blambangan, RSUD Banyuwangi) ~3-4 hrs overland. For serious cases evacuation to BIMC Bali (7-10 hrs overland, 3 hrs fast boat, helicopter on request) is required.
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: On-camp first-aid 0 km; nearest proper hospital RSUD Banyuwangi ~80-100 km road distance (3-4 hrs); BIMC Bali ~200+ km incl. ferry crossing
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No permanent rescue jet ski; some camps deploy jet skis on big days for tow-in and water rescue but coverage is informal
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Camp emergency line via Bobby's/Joyo's/Jack's office; BIMC Bali +62 361 3000 911; consult travel insurance for helicopter evacuation; satellite phone available at camps as cell signal limited

services.nearest_surf_school: G-Land is not a beginner destination; no formal surf school; coaching available informally through Dawn Patrol Bali advanced surf trips and via guides at camps for intermediate-advanced surfers wanting line-reading help [uncertain]
services.board_rental_availability: Limited rental at camps (typically gun/step-up boards for big days); most surfers bring their own quivers (recommended: 6'4 to 7'2 step-up for Money Trees, 7'4-8'0 gun for big Kongs/Speedies)
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - several camps employ in-house photographers (Bobby's, Joyo's, Jack's); freelance water/land photographers shoot during the season; package upgrades available
services.photographer_recommendation: Camp-employed photographers offer daily photo packages ~USD 50-100/day; established shooters like Surfing Visions and freelance Bali-based photographers visit in season [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Three primary surf camps at the point: (1) Bobby's Surf Camp (oldest, founded by Mike Boyum) USD 640 (3D)/870 (6D)/1,140 (9D); (2) G-Land Joyo's Surf Camp - AC rooms with hot water, ~USD 750/week deluxe; (3) G-Land Jack's Surf Camp from USD 840 (3N)/1,040 (6N) including round-trip fast boat. Jawa Jiwa is a newer camp option. All include meals, transfers from Bali, and basic amenities.
services.webcam_url: Surfline G-Land spot/cam: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/g-land/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d30 (forecast + occasional live cam)
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed by camps for personal use; Alas Purwo National Park has wildlife/cultural restrictions - check with camp manager; commercial drone requires Indonesian permit [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: None - G-Land is remote jungle; camps offer WiFi (often slow and intermittent); not suitable for digital nomad work, designed for full surf focus [uncertain]

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not a lesson destination; private coaching/guiding can be arranged informally USD 100-200/day [uncertain]
pricing.board_rental_daily: Limited; if available ~USD 25-40/day at camps for step-up/gun boards [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Excellent - inside protected Alas Purwo National Park, no nearby urban/agricultural runoff; the bay is clean year-round though wet season storms can stir sediment

events_community.annual_competition_window: Quiksilver Pro G-Land held intermittently 1995-1997 (legendary contest era); WSL CT briefly returned in 2022 with the Quiksilver Pro G-Land won by Italo Ferreira (men) and Tatiana Weston-Webb (women) - first WSL CT event there in 25 years. Not a regular contest stop currently [uncertain on current schedule]
events_community.community_events: Camp life centered around dawn patrol surf, post-surf meals and Bintang beers; occasional pro visitor sessions create informal community vibe; annual season-opening parties at camps [uncertain]

G_Land.combo_trip_potential: Excellent - G-Land is the classic Bali extension trip; commonly combined with Bali (Bukit), Nusa Lembongan reef breaks, or paired with onward boat charters to Lombok (Desert Point), Sumbawa (Lakey Peak), and the Mentawais via Bali. Boat charters from Bali frequently stop at G-Land as part of multi-spot Indonesian surf safaris.

basic_info.name: Gili Trawangan & Gili Air - Bongkas Reef (Chinese: 吉利群島 - 邦卡斯礁; Indonesian: Gili Trawangan & Gili Air - Bongkas Reef)
basic_info.gps_location: Gili Trawangan Bongkas (south end): -8.3712, 116.0297; Gili Air south reef: -8.3681, 116.0832 (approx.). Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/GiliTrawangan
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~110-120 km straight-line + sea crossing; total travel ~3-4 hrs (DPS to Padangbai/Serangan fast boat 1.5-2 hrs + transfers), or DPS to Lombok International + 1 hr taxi to Bangsal + 15 min boat
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: 60-90 min motorbike to Serangan/Padangbai + 1.5-2.5 hr fast boat (total ~3-4 hrs); From Uluwatu: ~90-120 min motorbike to harbor + 1.5-2.5 hr fast boat

surf_conditions.wave_type: Gili Trawangan Bongkas: left-hand reef break (fast, hollow, powerful). Gili Air: right-hand reef break off southeast/southwest coast (mellower on small days, fast and hollow on size). Both are coral reef breaks.
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (Bongkas Trawangan on small days is intermediate-friendly; on size it becomes advanced-only due to fast hollow take-offs over shallow reef). Gili Air similar - mellow for intermediates small, advanced when big.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 2-6 ft typical (Bongkas works well 2-5 ft); needs sizable SW swell to start working; can hold up to 6-8 ft on rare days [uncertain]
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp shallow live coral reef (300m shelf at Trawangan)
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-September (peak SW swell window); some sources note wet season Jan-March also works with cleaner northern wind. Gili Air shines occasionally during wet season Jan-March with N winds.
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide essential - the reef shelf is dangerously shallow at low tide; do not paddle/boat over reef at low tide
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore E to SE winds; light cross-offshore acceptable; afternoon onshore SW common in wet season
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW to W swell wraps around southern tip of Lombok and hits the Gili reefs; SSW long-period works best for Bongkas Trawangan
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 2 (Gilis are known as 'fickle' - require proper swell event; Gili Air described as a near-myth that only fires occasionally) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 50-300 meters at Trawangan Bongkas on best days (full shelf rides possible); Gili Air typically shorter 30-80m rides [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Fast (Bongkas is known for fast hollow sections; Gili Air gets hollow and fast on bigger days; both are quick steep waves)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high - sharp quick take-offs over shallow reef; positioning is critical; Bongkas has reputation for heavy steep drops
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Gili Trawangan has Bongkas (left, south end) plus an A-frame setup sometimes called 'Playgrounds/Secrets' off the southwest corner; Gili Air has the main right-hander off southern tip plus occasional left peelers [uncertain]
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 2 (low crowds - especially compared to Bali Bukit; Gili Air often empty; Bongkas Trawangan picks up some traffic from surf schools and visitors)

practical_info.access: From Bali: fast boat from Padangbai/Serangan/Amed to Gili Trawangan (1.5-2.5 hrs, IDR 350,000-700,000 one-way) or via Lombok mainland Bangsal Harbor (15-min public boat). No motorized vehicles on islands - walk, bicycle, or cidomo (horse cart) to break. Bongkas Trawangan is short walk/cycle from south harbor area.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Paddle from beach at south end of Gili Trawangan to Bongkas (relatively short paddle); for Gili Air right, a longer paddle (or short boat hop) from southern tip beach to reef edge - watch the channel and currents
practical_info.entry_fee: No surf entry fee; Gili islands fast boat fees and tourist levy (~IDR 50,000) apply; some hotels charge beach loungers separately
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy water and to catch the high-tide window; late afternoon often picks up onshore wind
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Early morning around the high-tide window; check tide chart - mid-to-high tide is the safer window over coral
practical_info.nearby_warung: Numerous warungs, cafes, and beach clubs on Gili Trawangan (especially south harbor area: Pearl Beach Lounge, Casa Vintage Beach, La Moomba, Pesona Cafe); Gili Air has Mowie's Bar, Gili Air Hostel cafe, Scallywags
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Within Gili archipelago: switch between Bongkas (Trawangan) and Gili Air right depending on swell and wind; Lombok mainland breaks (Senggigi area) ~30 min boat back to Lombok; truly local alternative is the Trawangan SW 'Playgrounds' peak
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Not strictly required for Bongkas Trawangan (paddle from beach) or Gili Air south reef; some surfers take short outrigger boat (~IDR 100,000-200,000) to save energy and avoid reef walk

safety_culture.hazards: Shallow sharp coral reef (especially low tide), sea urchins, strong tidal currents through Lombok Strait, occasional jellyfish, deceptive flat water that can turn powerful on swell, lack of motorized rescue (no jet skis), boat traffic in channels between islands
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (wear booties), occasional box jellyfish in season, blue-ringed octopus rare, lionfish on reef; sea snakes occasional; no shark issues; reef cuts very common - treat with antiseptic immediately
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: High - live coral reef is sharp and biological (high infection risk); booties strongly recommended; deeper grating injuries possible during low-tide wipeouts
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Tidal currents flow strongly between Gili Trawangan-Meno-Air (Lombok Strait) and can pull surfers off-island; current direction switches with tide; the 300m reef shelf at Trawangan has a notable outflow channel on the southwest corner
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Friendly and laid-back lineup compared to Bukit Bali; locals welcoming; standard etiquette - wait your turn, don't drop in, ask locals/surf school for tips on takeoff zone
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low - one of the friendliest surf cultures in Indonesia; locals from Bongkas Surf School and Kubu Surf School happy to share waves and tips
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Be respectful to local Sasak culture (Muslim majority on Lombok and Gilis - dress modestly off-beach; avoid public alcohol consumption in Muslim quarters; observe Ramadan fasting hours discreetly during fasting month). On the water: standard surfing courtesy, no drop-ins, smile and chat with locals.
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Bongkas Surf School founders/instructors are island regulars; Kelly Slater notably mentioned Gili Air right-hander as a favorite fun wave (per Surf Atlas and other sources) [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Muslim Sasak community on the Gilis (mosques broadcast call to prayer five times daily); dress modestly off-beach especially in village areas; Ramadan and Eid observance important; some no-alcohol zones near mosques
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Multiple clinics on Gili Trawangan: Blue Island Medical Clinic, Warna Medica (Empat Pillar Medika), Island Medical Service, Gili Medical Care (GEMS), Gili Emergency Clinic - several offer 24-hour service; Gili Air has Gili Air Clinic Center (Empat Pillar Medika). No hospital on Gilis - serious cases evacuated to Lombok mainland or Bali by speedboat ambulance / helicopter.
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: On Gili Trawangan: clinics are within 1-2 km walk/bicycle of Bongkas; on Gili Air: clinic 0.5-1 km from south surf area; mainland Lombok hospital (Mataram) is ~10-20 km + sea crossing
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - no jet skis allowed in Gili waters (eco/marine park rules); rescue is by speedboat or sea ambulance only
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Gili Emergency Clinic: contact via giliemergency.com / Blue Island Clinic 24-hour line; sea ambulance arranged through clinics; helicopter evacuation via medical insurance providers

services.nearest_surf_school: Bongkas Surf School (south end Gili Trawangan, on Bongkas Reef itself), Sunny Surf School Gili Trawangan, Kubu Surf School (5-day surf camp packages); Gili Air has smaller schools and freelance instructors via Aquaholic / Villa Burung referrals
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - Bongkas Surf School, Kubu Surf School, and several rental shacks on Gili Trawangan rent shortboards, longboards, and SUPs; Gili Air has more limited rental, ask at Villa Burung or local surf shacks. ~USD 10-15/day SUPs; surfboard rates vary [uncertain]
services.surf_photographer_available: Limited - Bongkas Surf School offers drone footage of sessions for IDR 150,000; freelance photographers occasional, no permanent water photographer stationed
services.photographer_recommendation: Bongkas Surf School drone package IDR 150,000; arrange water photography via surf schools or social media DM to local shooters [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Gili Trawangan: Pearl Beach Resort, Hotel Vila Ombak, Karma Reef, Le Pirate Beach Club, Gili Mansion, plus dozens of mid-range bungalows and hostels (Manta Dive Gili Air for combined dive+surf). Gili Air: Villa Burung Gili Air (recommended for surfers), 7Seas Cottages, Sunrise Resort, Begadang Bungalows. Kubu Surf School and Bongkas Surf School offer surf camp packages.
services.webcam_url: Surfline Gili Trawangan spot page: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/gili-trawangan/640a68024eb37506299e109e (forecast; live cam coverage limited) [uncertain]
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use over beaches and reefs; respect privacy and avoid flying over mosques/temples; commercial use requires Indonesian permit [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Gili Trawangan: Trawangan Cocoon (cafe with WiFi), Pearl Beach Lounge work zone, Casa Vintage; Gili Air: Mowie's Bar daytime, Begadang Mat & Co; limited dedicated coworking compared to Bali

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 35-55 / IDR 500,000-850,000 per group lesson (1.5-2 hrs incl. board); private lessons USD 60-90 / IDR 900,000-1,400,000 [uncertain]
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 150,000-300,000/day (~USD 10-20) for shortboards; SUPs from USD 10/day at Bongkas Surf School [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Generally excellent - Gili Marine Park status, clear water, healthy reefs; some concerns around south harbor with boat fuel and snorkeler sunscreen; wet season runoff minimal compared to Bali main island

events_community.annual_competition_window: No major annual surf competition on the Gili Islands; occasional informal surf days hosted by Bongkas Surf School and Kubu Surf School [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Sasak cultural events on Lombok mainland; full-moon parties on Gili Trawangan (Sama Sama Reggae Bar, Tir Na Nog); occasional reef clean-up events organized by Gili Eco Trust and dive shops; Ramadan/Eid observance

Green_Bowl.name: Green Bowl / Green Bowls / Green Ball / Pantai Green Bowl
Green_Bowl.distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 18-20 km (35-45 min by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport) [uncertain]
Green_Bowl.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-90 min by scooter; From Uluwatu (Suluban/Single Fin): ~20-25 min by scooter
Green_Bowl.wave_type: Right-hand reef break (primary); a left reef peak that only works on spring low tides (full/new moon). Quick steep right peak over reef that walls up and barrels on the inside near the channel
Green_Bowl.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (not suitable for beginners under any circumstances)
Green_Bowl.wave_height_range: Waist-high to 1.5x overhead; works from ~1 ft to 6 ft+; closes out beyond that
Green_Bowl.best_season: Wet season (Oct-Apr / Nov-Apr) for clean small swells with light winds; said to be 'mostly a rainy season surf spot'. Can also be surfed in dry season early mornings when other Bukit spots are too big
Green_Bowl.best_tide: Works on all tides (rare for the Bukit); mid tide is considered best; low tide for the rare left; outgoing tide creates very strong rip current
Green_Bowl.best_wind: Offshore N to NE winds (sheltered from the dominant SE trades that work for west-coast Bukit spots)
Green_Bowl.swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (picks up most south swells but very tide and size sensitive; often too big or current-affected)
Green_Bowl.wave_length_meters: approx. 40-100 m short punchy right; barrel section on the inside [uncertain]
Green_Bowl.wave_speed_rating: Fast (quick steep right peak, fast hollow wall, barrels inside)
Green_Bowl.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High - steep quick right peak over shallow reef; barrel section close to the channel; not forgiving on missed take-offs
Green_Bowl.secondary_peaks: Inside bowl/barrel section near the channel on the right; rare left peak that only breaks on spring low tides
Green_Bowl.peak_crowd_level: 2-3/5 (crowd factor cited 5/10 on good days; usually uncrowded thanks to the long stairs and demanding wave)
Green_Bowl.access: Walk down a long stone staircase carved into the cliff face; commonly cited as 'a few hundred' steps (varies 200-300+ in different sources). The trail passes through tropical forest with monkeys and traditional stone statues. Scooter parking is at the cliff-top
Green_Bowl.paddle_out_method: Paddle out through the channel on the right (north) side of the reef; do not paddle straight over the reef especially at lower tides
Green_Bowl.entry_fee: Parking ~IDR 2,000-5,000 (scooter) per day; beach entry free; small donation may be requested by parking attendants
Green_Bowl.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol to mid-morning during a rising mid tide to high; before the wind and current pick up
Green_Bowl.best_time_of_day: First light to ~10 am; avoid mid-afternoon when wind switches and outgoing tide rip strengthens
Green_Bowl.nearby_warung: Very limited - a couple of small warungs at the cliff top above the stairs; almost nothing at beach level apart from occasional coconut/snack vendors. Closest proper restaurants are 2+ km away in Ungasan
Green_Bowl.alternative_spot_within_10min: Nyang Nyang (~10-15 min north along the Bukit); Pandawa Beach (cruise/learners) nearby; Uluwatu/Padang Padang ~20 min if Green Bowl is too small or current-affected
Green_Bowl.hazards: One of the strongest currents on Bali (especially on outgoing tide), shallow live coral reef, freak/rogue sets, getting swept out the channel, slippery rocks and high-tide trail washouts, monkeys along trail, very limited rescue infrastructure
Green_Bowl.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the reef, occasional jellyfish, rare tiger shark sightings reported; coral cuts common - flush with fresh water and disinfect, monitor for infection in tropical climate
Green_Bowl.reef_cut_severity_index: High - shallow live coral; reef boots strongly recommended, especially at low tide. Spare leash recommended because a broken leash plus the rip can strand you offshore
Green_Bowl.rip_current_pattern: Powerful rip running out through the channel at the end of the right-hander; greatly amplified on outgoing tide and bigger swells - can quickly take you well out to sea if you lose your board
Green_Bowl.lineup_etiquette: Standard reef-break etiquette - one wave per person, respect priority on the inside, no snaking, greet the regulars, do not paddle around to grab sets
Green_Bowl.localism_intensity: Low (cited 1/10) - relaxed lineup with a small regular crew; friendly if you respect the rotation
Green_Bowl.respect_protocol: Smile, say hi, do not litter, support local warungs/parking attendants, do not bring beginners into the lineup
Green_Bowl.resident_local_legends: No single iconic local surfer attached to Green Bowl in the public record; regular Bukit/Ungasan locals surf it [uncertain]
Green_Bowl.religious_sensitivity: Bukit Peninsula has cliff-side shrines and the Green Bowl area itself has small Hindu cave temples used for ceremonies - do not enter shrine caves with surf gear, dress modestly when off the beach; respect Nyepi silent day island-wide
Green_Bowl.medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (24h); BIMC Nusa Dua; Siloam Hospital Denpasar for serious injuries
Green_Bowl.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu approx. 7-9 km; BIMC Nusa Dua approx. 10-12 km [uncertain]
Green_Bowl.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue at this spot
Green_Bowl.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; BASARNAS (Search & Rescue) 115; Unicare Clinic Pecatu +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
Green_Bowl.nearest_surf_school: Rapture Surfcamps Green Bowl / Padang Padang and Padang Padang Surf Camp operate guided sessions in the broader area; no school directly on the beach. Lessons here only offered to qualified intermediate+ guests
Green_Bowl.board_rental_availability: No board rental at the beach; rent in Uluwatu / Padang Padang / Ungasan before driving down
Green_Bowl.surf_photographer_available: No regular land/water photographer stationed at the lineup
Green_Bowl.photographer_recommendation: Freelance Bukit photographers can be hired by appointment via Instagram; rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
Green_Bowl.accommodation_options: Cari Surf Camp Pecatu, Rapture Surfcamps Green Bowl / Padang, Six Senses Uluwatu (luxury), Bvlgari Resort Bali (luxury, very close in Ungasan), various Ungasan/Pecatu villas and homestays within 5-10 min drive
Green_Bowl.drone_allowed: Generally allowed; the cliffs and bowl-shaped caves are a popular drone subject. Avoid flying over the small cliff-side temples and respect other beachgoers [uncertain]
Green_Bowl.coworking_nearby: No coworking nearby; closest in Bingin/Uluwatu (cafe-style) and Canggu (Tropical Nomad, Outpost, B Work) ~1+ hr away
Green_Bowl.water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season due to remote location and low development; can carry sediment after heavy wet-season rain; cleaner than west/north Bali beaches [uncertain]

Impossibles.name: Impossibles (Chinese: 不可能浪点; Indonesian: Impossibles / Pantai Impossibles)
Impossibles.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Approximately 18-20 km from Ngurah Rai (DPS) Airport (around 40-50 min by car)
Impossibles.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-90 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: ~10-15 min by scooter
Impossibles.wave_type: Left-hand reef break / point break with multiple sections (Top, Middle, End / Uppers and Lowers); fast walls and barrels
Impossibles.skill_level: Advanced to Pro (not suitable for beginners; intermediates can ride smaller days with caution)
Impossibles.wave_height_range: 3-10+ ft; lines up best at 5 ft and above; holds well into double-overhead range
Impossibles.bottom_type: Reef (coral), deeper in sections but with shallow reef fingers on the inside
Impossibles.best_season: Dry season, May to October (peak: June-September); offshore SE trades and clean SW groundswells
Impossibles.best_tide: Mid to low tide for the lower/end section; mid to high tide for the first/top peaks; full low can expose shallow reef
Impossibles.best_wind: Offshore from East to Southeast (SE trade winds)
Impossibles.swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 during dry season (one of the most consistent breaks in Bali) [uncertain]
Impossibles.wave_length_meters: Up to ~400 m on a single section; over 1 km total reef length can link Top to End / into Bingin on rare large days
Impossibles.wave_speed_rating: Fast (very fast down-the-line sections; aptly named 'Impossibles' due to unmakeable speed)
Impossibles.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high; drop is not huge but the wave runs away quickly, demanding early commitment and high-line trim
Impossibles.secondary_peaks: Yes - Top (near Padang Padang / Villa Pemutih), Middle, and End (near Bingin); commonly called Uppers and Lowers
Impossibles.peak_crowd_level: 3/5 - less crowded than Uluwatu/Padang/Bingin due to longer paddle; spreads out across the 1 km reef; busiest 7-10am dry season
Impossibles.access: Multiple options: (1) paddle from Padang Padang beach (north end), (2) descend stairs at Bingin and paddle north along the reef, (3) Anantara Uluwatu Resort elevator/path down the cliff to mid-reef [uncertain on public access via Anantara]
Impossibles.paddle_out_method: Easiest via the channel at Padang Padang then drift north up the reef; alternatively keyhole through the inside reef channel on smaller days; from Bingin, paddle across shallow reef and lagoon with currents
Impossibles.entry_fee: Free for the surf break itself; Padang Padang beach parking ~IDR 5,000-10,000; Bingin beach parking ~IDR 5,000-10,000 and beach access fee ~IDR 10,000 [uncertain]
Impossibles.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-8:00 am) for glassy conditions; late afternoon if SE winds remain offshore
Impossibles.best_time_of_day: Pre-8 am to avoid Padang Padang crowds drifting up; sunset sessions also less busy
Impossibles.nearby_warung: Warungs and cafes at Padang Padang beach (Mango Tree, Padang Padang Surf Camp warung) and Bingin (Lucky Fish, Kelly's Warung, The Cashew Tree); Anantara resort F&B options
Impossibles.alternative_spot_within_10min: Padang Padang (when smaller and high tide), Bingin (best mid tide), Uluwatu (handles bigger swells), Dreamland (more forgiving beach break)
Impossibles.combo_trip_potential: N/A (Bali domestic spot; not typically part of boat-charter circuits)
Impossibles.hazards: Sharp shallow reef on the inside especially at low tide, fast unmakeable sections, strong currents along the reef, occasional sea urchins, reef fingers at exit
Impossibles.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (bulu babi) on inside reef, occasional jellyfish in wet season transition, rare stonefish in lagoons; treat reef cuts immediately with vinegar/iodine and antibiotics
Impossibles.reef_cut_severity_index: Medium-high - deeper than Uluwatu but inside reef fingers are sharp; reef rash and lacerations common when wiping out near exit
Impossibles.rip_current_pattern: Channel current between Padang Padang reef and Impossibles reef drifts surfers northward toward Bingin; outgoing tide accelerates this. Use it to your advantage on paddle out but be aware on return
Impossibles.lineup_etiquette: Spread out across long reef so rotation is informal; respect priority to surfer deepest/closest to peak; don't paddle around or snake locals; smile and say hi
Impossibles.localism_intensity: Low to medium - generally friendly due to space available; can get edgy on perfect crowded days but rarely aggressive
Impossibles.respect_protocol: Wait your turn, do not drop in, acknowledge locals, avoid hassling solo surfers on inside; keep beach/cliff areas clean
Impossibles.resident_local_legends: No single iconic Impossibles local equivalent to Mega Semadhi at Ulu; many Bukit pros (Made Adi Putra, Garut Widiarta, Rio Waida) surf here regularly [uncertain]
Impossibles.religious_sensitivity: Nearby Uluwatu Temple (Pura Luhur Uluwatu) - dress respectfully on cliffs; observe Nyepi (Day of Silence, March) - no surfing or beach access; menstruating women traditionally avoid temple visits
Impossibles.medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (24/7, surf injury specialists), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua, Siloam Hospital Denpasar
Impossibles.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu ~3-4 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~12 km; Siloam Denpasar ~20 km
Impossibles.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue; informal help from local surfers and Anantara hotel staff [uncertain]
Impossibles.emergency_contact: Indonesia emergency: 112; Bali Search and Rescue (Basarnas): +62 361 9351111; Unicare Pecatu: +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
Impossibles.nearest_surf_school: Padang Padang Surf Camp (intermediate-advanced coaching), Rip Curl School of Surf (Dreamland), Odyssey Surf School - though Impossibles itself is not a learner spot
Impossibles.board_rental_availability: Available at Padang Padang and Bingin beach warungs and nearby surf shops (Drifter Uluwatu, Single Fin shop, board rental stalls)
Impossibles.surf_photographer_available: Yes - freelance land/water photographers regularly shoot from the cliff and channel; especially active during contest season
Impossibles.photographer_recommendation: Local photographers post on Instagram (search #impossiblesbali); typical pricing IDR 200,000-500,000 per session set [uncertain]
Impossibles.accommodation_options: Anantara Uluwatu Resort (luxury cliff-side), Padang Padang Surf Camp, Bingin Garden, Mu Bali, Mick's Place, Temple Lodge, numerous villas in Pecatu
Impossibles.webcam_url: https://balicams.com/camera/impossibles ; https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/impossibles/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d42 [uncertain - Surfline access may require subscription]
Impossibles.drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for personal use; commercial drone use requires permits; avoid flying over crowded lineups and Anantara resort airspace [uncertain]
Impossibles.coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu (Uluwatu), Tropical Nomad satellite, Drifter Uluwatu cafe with wifi; ~10-15 min ride away
