Impossibles.avg_lesson_price: Not a beginner spot - lessons typically at Dreamland or Balangan; intermediate guiding ~IDR 600,000-1,200,000 per session [uncertain]
Impossibles.board_rental_daily: IDR 100,000-200,000 per day depending on board type and shop
Impossibles.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season (clear blue water); declines slightly in wet season due to runoff from cliffs and lagoon outflow; generally cleaner than Kuta/Canggu
Impossibles.annual_competition_window: No major standalone Impossibles event; sometimes overflow heats from Padang Padang Cup (Rip Curl Cup, typically July-August window) [uncertain]
Impossibles.community_events: Informal sunset sessions; nearby Single Fin Sunday at Single Fin Uluwatu is the main weekly gathering

Keramas.name: Keramas / Pantai Keramas
Keramas.gps_location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/?q=-8.5897,115.3322 (approx. -8.5897, 115.3322, Keramas Beach, Blahbatuh, Gianyar Regency, Bali; in front of Komune Resort) [uncertain]
Keramas.distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 35-40 km (about 45-60 min by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport)
Keramas.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~60-75 min by scooter (~30 km via bypass); From Uluwatu: ~75-90 min by scooter (~45-50 km) [uncertain]
Keramas.wave_type: Right-hand reef break (rivermouth-influenced) over shallow volcanic rock/reef; fast, hollow, world-class performance wave with steep take-off into a near-vertical bowl, then long fat shoulder with end-section ramps for aerials
Keramas.skill_level: Advanced to Pro (on smaller days surfable by confident regular-foot intermediates; goofy-footers find it backhand and harder); not suitable for beginners
Keramas.wave_height_range: Surfable 2-10 ft; sweet spot 4-6 ft (chest to double-overhead); turns on and barrels once it's overhead
Keramas.bottom_type: Shallow volcanic rock reef with rivermouth sand influence (sand on inside, sharp volcanic rock on the main reef)
Keramas.best_season: Wet season (November-April) is prime; transition months March-April and October-November are also excellent with favorable winds; surfable year-round but smaller and less consistent in dry season
Keramas.best_tide: Mid to high tide preferred (high tide for safer reef coverage); low tide exposes sharp volcanic reef and gets dangerous
Keramas.best_wind: Offshore NW to N wind (wet-season pattern); light morning offshore winds are typical of the East Coast in the wet season
Keramas.best_swell_direction: S to SSW (180-220 degrees); more south in the swell means bigger Keramas; SE/E swells also work
Keramas.swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 in wet season (most consistent East Coast performance wave); 2-3/5 in dry season (smaller, less reliable)
Keramas.wave_length_meters: approx. 80-150 m on a good set (steep take-off bowl + long shoulder + end-section)
Keramas.wave_speed_rating: fast (high-performance speed wave; transitions from deep to shallow water very quickly making it hollow and demanding)
Keramas.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High - steep, near-vertical take-off bowl that hollows out immediately; shallow volcanic reef beneath; punishing on late drops
Keramas.secondary_peaks: Main right (the famous bowl + shoulder); end-section ramp/aerial closeout; small inside reform on sand near the rivermouth on bigger days; no real left option
Keramas.peak_crowd_level: 4-5/5 in wet season firing days (huge crowds; one of Bali's most crowded performance waves alongside Uluwatu); 2-3/5 on average days; less crowded at dawn and during paid night sessions
Keramas.access: Direct beach access via Komune Beach Club Bali / Keramas Beach Road off the Bali-Padang Bay coastal road in Blahbatuh, Gianyar. Park at Komune Resort or roadside; short walk down to black-sand beach. About 45-60 min from DPS airport by car
Keramas.paddle_out_method: Straightforward beach paddle-out directly from the black sand in front of Komune Resort; paddle out the channel on the south/inside of the peak; time sets to avoid getting washed onto reef
Keramas.entry_fee: No formal entry fee for the beach; parking ~IDR 5,000-10,000 (scooter) / ~IDR 10,000-20,000 (car); Komune Beach Club has paid day-passes (~IDR 250,000-500,000) with pool, lockers, hot showers [uncertain]
Keramas.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (sunrise to ~8 am) before the trade winds pick up and crowds arrive; also paid night sessions under Komune floodlights
Keramas.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~8 am for cleanest conditions and lighter crowds; mid-morning gets very crowded; evening night-surf sessions (booked, max ~6 surfers) at Komune floodlights
Keramas.nearby_warung: Komune Beach Club restaurants and beach bar, Swan Restaurant Keramas, Romano Italian Restaurant & Pizzeria, Pizza Napoli, local warungs (Lawar Plex WMD) along Jalan Pantai Keramas; Sababay Winery nearby
Keramas.alternative_spot_within_10min: Within ~10-20 min drive: Lebih Beach (~5-10 min west, sand-bottom rivermouth), Pabean (small reef break), Padang Galak (~20 min west); Ketewel reef further north on bigger swells
Keramas.hazards: Shallow sharp volcanic rock reef (breaks bones, deep cuts), strong rivermouth currents, hold-downs in the bowl, crowded lineup with aggressive locals, occasional submerged debris from the river, hot black sand on the beach, motorbike traffic on coastal road
Keramas.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the reef, occasional jellyfish (especially after rain), rare sea snake sightings, no significant shark issue; treat reef cuts immediately with fresh water flush, antiseptic, and clinic visit if deep [uncertain]
Keramas.reef_cut_severity_index: Very high - sharp volcanic rock reef; some of Bali's most frequent reef-cut injuries; reef boots advisable on low-tide sessions, helmet considered by some pros
Keramas.rip_current_pattern: Strong rivermouth rip on the south/inside of the peak (often used as paddle channel); cross-shore sweep during bigger SE/S swells; outgoing tide intensifies pull toward the reef [uncertain]
Keramas.lineup_etiquette: Strict hierarchy - locals and Komune crew have priority; do not drop in, do not paddle inside on take-off, wait your turn, no shouting; smile and acknowledge regulars; dawn patrol earns more respect than midday show-up
Keramas.localism_intensity: High (cited 7-8/10) - reputed as worse than Uluwatu or Bingin; the resident 'ripper crew' and Komune-sponsored surfers dominate; drop-ins can lead to aggressive confrontation
Keramas.respect_protocol: Sit wider and deeper than you think; do not paddle around for sets; let locals have priority; no flash photography or drones over heads without permission; tip warungs and parking attendants
Keramas.resident_local_legends: Raditya Rondi, Garut Widiarta, Bronson Meidy, Mega Semadhi (visits) and the Komune-affiliated local crew are known Keramas regulars; many Indonesian national team riders train here [uncertain]
Keramas.religious_sensitivity: Active Hindu coastal village - respect ceremonial processions on the beach and Pura Segara (sea temple) areas; observe Nyepi (silent day) when no surfing/movement is allowed island-wide; menstruating women traditionally asked to avoid temple grounds
Keramas.medical_nearby: Komune Resort has on-site first aid; nearby clinics: Klinik Pratama Keramas, Klinik Utama Permata Hati Gianyar; for serious injuries Sanglah/Prof. Ngoerah General Hospital Denpasar (~30 km), BIMC Hospital Kuta (~35 km), Siloam Hospital Bali
Keramas.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Klinik Pratama Keramas/Blahbatuh approx. 3-5 km; RSUD Sanjiwani Gianyar approx. 10-12 km; BIMC Kuta approx. 35 km [uncertain]
Keramas.rescue_jet_ski_available: Komune Resort has provided jet-ski water safety during WSL events and major contests; not on permanent daily standby for the public [uncertain]
Keramas.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Bali Search and Rescue (BASARNAS) 115; Komune Resort front desk +62 361 301 8888; RSUD Sanjiwani Gianyar +62 361 943-049 [uncertain]
Keramas.nearest_surf_school: Komune Surf Academy (in-house at Komune Resort); Keramas Surf Camp; lessons typically run on the gentler inside / mellow days only - main peak is not a teaching wave
Keramas.board_rental_availability: Yes - Komune Resort board hire, Keramas Surf Camp, and several local rentals along Jalan Pantai Keramas; quality shortboards approx. USD 15/day (IDR ~220,000-250,000)
Keramas.surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land photographers shoot from the beach daily; water photographers in lineup on bigger days; Komune-affiliated shooters and freelancers
Keramas.photographer_recommendation: Komune-affiliated photographer packages approx. USD 65/day; freelance options on Instagram (search 'keramas surf photographer'); session prices typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 [uncertain]
Keramas.accommodation_options: Hotel Komune Resort & Beach Club Bali (oceanfront luxury, on the wave), Keramas Surf Camp (mid-range with surf packages), Keramas Surf Hostel (budget dorms), Blue Coco Guesthouse, Keramas Moonlight Villa, Keramas Sacred River Retreat
Keramas.webcam_url: Surfline Keramas cam (premium) https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/keramas/ ; Komune Resort live cam on their website [uncertain]
Keramas.drone_allowed: Generally allowed from the beach for personal use; do not fly over the lineup during WSL events or peak sessions, and respect Komune Resort guests' privacy; standard Indonesian drone rules apply [uncertain]
Keramas.coworking_nearby: No coworking directly at Keramas; Ubud-area coworking (Outpost Ubud, Hubud) ~30-40 min drive; Sanur coworking ~30 min south [uncertain]
Keramas.avg_lesson_price: Group surf lesson approx. IDR 500,000-800,000 (USD 35-55) at Komune Surf Academy / Keramas Surf Camp; private lessons IDR 800,000-1,500,000 [uncertain]
Keramas.board_rental_daily: Approx. IDR 150,000-250,000 (USD 10-17) per day for shortboard at local rentals; Komune Resort and surf camps higher end USD 15-25/day
Keramas.water_quality_rating: Generally fair to good in dry season; can drop in wet season due to rivermouth runoff carrying soil, palm fronds and occasional plastics from upstream after heavy rain [uncertain]
Keramas.annual_competition_window: WSL Championship Tour 'Corona Bali Pro' / Corona Open J-Bay equivalent has been hosted at Keramas (notably 2018, 2019, 2023, 2024); typically scheduled in the May-June window when CT visits Indonesia; also various Indonesian national and longboard events through the year [uncertain]
Keramas.community_events: Komune Beach Club hosts regular surf and DJ events, Friday/Saturday night parties, and surf film screenings; occasional youth surf clinics with Indonesian national team riders [uncertain]

basic_info.name: Kuta Beach / 庫塔海灘 / Pantai Kuta
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Canggu ~25 min, Uluwatu ~30 min by scooter

surf_conditions.wave_type: Beach break, sand-bottom, predominantly left and right peaks with multiple shifting sandbars (Halfway Kuta is the most reliable peak)
surf_conditions.skill_level: Beginner; small days suit intermediates on longboards
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-4 ft typical; up to 6 ft on bigger swells
surf_conditions.bottom_type: sand
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season (May-October); wet season (Nov-Mar) is onshore and has rubbish/poor water quality
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide for cleaner faces; low to mid tide for whitewater learning
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore from E/NE/N (early morning best before sea-breeze turns onshore SW)
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW to S groundswells
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 30-80 m depending on sandbar configuration
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: slow to medium
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Easy, soft sand-bottom take-offs with low drop; very forgiving for beginners
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Multiple shifting peaks along the beach; Halfway Kuta is the main peak, with inside whitewater reforms ideal for learners
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (very crowded year-round, peak July-Aug and Dec-Jan with surf schools dominating the inside)

practical_info.access: Walk directly onto the sand from Jalan Pantai Kuta; public beach with multiple access points; no stairs or scrambling required
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Walk out from the sand through whitewater; duck-dive or push through small inside waves; no channel required
practical_info.entry_fee: Free beach access; scooter parking IDR 5,000, car parking IDR 10,000
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-8:00 AM) for cleanest conditions and lighter crowd; sunset session for ambience
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Before 8 AM to avoid surf-school crowds and onshore wind
practical_info.nearby_warung: Numerous beachfront warungs, cafés, and coconut stalls along Pantai Kuta; Made's Warung, Kuta Beachwalk food court within walking distance
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Legian Beach (5 min north), Seminyak / Double Six (10 min north), Tuban / Halfway Kuta (5 min south)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: [uncertain] Typically not a combo-trip base; mainly a learner day-spot. Could pair with Bukit day-trips.

safety_culture.hazards: Rip currents (especially on bigger swells), heavy beachgoer traffic, jet-skis and banana boats in adjacent zones, occasional wet-season debris/jellyfish
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Minor jellyfish stings during wet season; rare blue-bottle (Portuguese man o' war) sightings; no reef, no urchins; treat jellyfish stings with vinegar and warm water
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: 0 / N-A (pure sand bottom, no reef cuts)
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Rips form between sandbars; identifiable as darker, calmer channels of water moving seaward, often with foam/debris streaks. Swim/paddle parallel to shore to escape.
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Standard right-of-way rules (surfer closest to peak has priority); be courteous to surf-school groups who occupy the inside; no drop-ins; smile and say hi
safety_culture.localism_intensity: low
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Tip beach attendants and parking guards; do not interfere with surf-school lessons on the inside; pick up your trash
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: [uncertain] Kuta has produced many Indonesian pro surfers historically; specific named locals less iconic than Bukit reefs
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Segara nearby; during Nyepi (Balinese Day of Silence, March) the beach is closed and no surfing is allowed; respect ceremonies (melasti processions) on the beach
safety_culture.medical_nearby: BIMC Hospital Kuta (Jl. Bypass Ngurah Rai 100X), Siloam Hospitals Denpasar, Kasih Ibu Hospital Kuta
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Balawista lifeguards on duty with rescue boards and occasional jet-ski; lifeguard towers along the beach
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Balawista Bali Lifeguard: +62 361 755660; Ambulance: 118; Police: 110; BIMC Kuta: +62 361 761263

services.nearest_surf_school: Odyssey Surf School, Kuta Surf School, Pro Surf School Bali, Rip Curl School of Surf, Chandra's Surfing Bali - all directly on or adjacent to Kuta Beach
services.board_rental_availability: Widely available beachfront; soft-tops, funboards, shortboards from IDR 100,000/day
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes, land-based photographers along the beach offering pay-per-session photos
services.photographer_recommendation: [uncertain] Various freelance beach photographers; typical rate IDR 200,000-500,000 per session/photo package
services.accommodation_options: Hard Rock Hotel Bali, Hotel Indigo Bali Seminyak Beach, Poppies Lane 1 & 2 budget homestays, Kuta Seaview Boutique Resort, surf camps like Padang Padang Surf Camp (nearby)
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/kuta-beach/640a67e745190540b0a65fe6 (Surfline Kuta cam) [uncertain exact URL]
services.drone_allowed: Generally yes for recreational use; commercial use requires permit; avoid flying over crowds and during ceremonies
services.coworking_nearby: Tropical Nomad (Canggu, ~25 min), Dojo Bali (Canggu), B Work Bali (Berawa) - no major coworking directly in Kuta

pricing.avg_lesson_price: IDR 350,000-650,000 (USD 22-42 / TWD 700-1,350) per 2-hour group lesson
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 100,000-200,000 (USD 6-13) per day for soft-tops/shortboards
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: [uncertain] Rashguards usually included with lesson; standalone rental ~IDR 50,000/day
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: [uncertain] Small ding repair IDR 100,000-300,000 depending on size
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: [uncertain] Not a primary surf-camp hub; nearby camps (Padang Padang Surf Camp, Odyssey) offer 7-day packages from USD 600-1,200
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: [uncertain] Typical inclusions: accommodation, breakfast, daily lessons, board use, airport transfer, video analysis
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: [uncertain] Peak season (Jul-Aug, Dec-Jan) ~15-25% higher than shoulder months

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Moderate to poor in wet season (Nov-Mar) due to runoff and plastic pollution; acceptable to good in dry season; ongoing beach clean-up initiatives by Sungai Watch and local community
sustainability.eco_certification: [uncertain] No spot-level certification; individual schools may hold certifications
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: [uncertain] Some surf camps in greater Bali offer carbon offsets, not specific to Kuta

events_community.annual_competition_window: [uncertain] Kuta historically hosted Coca-Cola Kuta Pro and various junior events; currently fewer pro events; community contests during dry season
events_community.community_events: Daily Balawista beach clean-ups; informal sunset gatherings; Bali Spirit Festival nearby; surf school graduations and weekly contests

basic_info.name: Legian Beach / 雷吉安海灘 / Pantai Legian
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Canggu ~20 min, Uluwatu ~35 min by scooter

surf_conditions.wave_type: Beach break, sand-bottom, shifting left and right peaks (true beach-break behavior)
surf_conditions.skill_level: Beginner to intermediate
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-5 ft typical; up to 6-8 ft on solid SW swells (closes out)
surf_conditions.bottom_type: sand
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season (May-October) for cleanest offshore conditions
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide for fun peeling peaks; low tide produces heavy close-outs
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore from E/NE early morning; sea-breeze turns onshore SW by mid-late morning
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW to S groundswells
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 30-70 m depending on sandbar quality
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: medium
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Easy to moderate; soft sand take-offs; on bigger days steeper drops and close-out sections increase difficulty for beginners
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Multiple shifting peaks along the beach; inside whitewater reforms for learners and outside peaks for intermediates on bigger days
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 4 (busy but slightly quieter than Kuta; quieter at dawn)

practical_info.access: Direct walk-on beach access from Jalan Pantai Legian and multiple side streets; no stairs or climbs
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Walk-out from sand through whitewater; duck-dive or push through small inside waves; use rip channels (with caution) on bigger days
practical_info.entry_fee: Free beach access; scooter parking IDR 5,000, car parking IDR 10,000
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-8:00 AM) for cleanest offshore conditions; sunset session popular for ambience
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Before 8 AM to avoid surf-school crowds and onshore wind
practical_info.nearby_warung: Many beachside warungs, cafés, and coconut stalls along Jalan Pantai Legian; popular spots include La Plancha, Double Six Beach Lounge
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Kuta Beach (5 min south), Seminyak / Double Six (5 min north), Halfway Kuta (10 min south)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: [uncertain] Not a typical combo-trip base; functions as day-extension from Kuta/Seminyak surf stays

safety_culture.hazards: Rip currents (especially on bigger swells), close-out waves on low tide causing wipe-outs, crowded surf-school traffic on the inside, jet-skis in adjacent zones
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Occasional jellyfish (wet season), rare blue-bottles, no reef or urchins; treat stings with vinegar and warm water
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: 0 / N-A (pure sand bottom)
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Rips form between sandbars on bigger days; visible as darker calmer channels of seaward-flowing water with foam streaks. Escape by paddling parallel to shore
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Standard right-of-way rules; respect surf-school zones; no drop-ins; share peaks; smile and say hi
safety_culture.localism_intensity: low
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Tip parking guards; respect surf-school groups; pick up trash; do not interrupt Hindu ceremonies on the beach
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: [uncertain] No iconic individual locals associated with Legian specifically; many regional pros surf the area
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Petitenget nearby; Nyepi (Balinese Day of Silence, March) closes the beach to all activity; respect melasti processions and ceremonial offerings on the sand
safety_culture.medical_nearby: BIMC Hospital Kuta, Siloam Hospitals Denpasar, Kasih Ibu Hospital Kuta
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Balawista lifeguards on duty with rescue boards; jet-ski available in some sections
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Balawista Bali Lifeguard: +62 361 755660; Ambulance: 118; Police: 110; BIMC Kuta: +62 361 761263

services.nearest_surf_school: Odyssey Surf School, Rip Curl School of Surf Legian, Bali Surf Club, UP2U Surf School - all within walking distance
services.board_rental_availability: Widely available beachfront; soft-tops, funboards, shortboards from IDR 100,000-150,000/day
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes, freelance land-based photographers along the beach
services.photographer_recommendation: [uncertain] Various freelance beach photographers; typical rate IDR 200,000-500,000 per session
services.accommodation_options: Padma Resort Legian, Bali Mandira Beach Resort, Pullman Bali Legian Beach, numerous mid-range hotels and homestays along Jalan Padma and Jalan Melasti
services.webcam_url: [uncertain] https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/legian (Surfline coverage)
services.drone_allowed: Generally yes for recreational use; avoid flying over crowds and ceremonies
services.coworking_nearby: Tropical Nomad (Canggu ~20 min), Dojo Bali (Canggu); no major coworking directly in Legian

pricing.avg_lesson_price: IDR 350,000-650,000 (USD 22-42 / TWD 700-1,350) per 2-hour group lesson
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 100,000-200,000 (USD 6-13) per day for soft-tops/shortboards
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: [uncertain] Usually included with lesson; standalone rental ~IDR 50,000/day
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: [uncertain] Small ding repair IDR 100,000-300,000 depending on size
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: [uncertain] Some camps based in Legian offer 7-day packages from USD 700-1,300
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: [uncertain] Typical: accommodation, breakfast, daily lessons, board use, airport transfer, video analysis
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: [uncertain] Peak season (Jul-Aug, Dec-Jan) ~15-25% higher than shoulder months

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Moderate to poor in wet season (Nov-Mar) due to runoff and plastic pollution; acceptable in dry season; less rubbish than Kuta but similar issues
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: [uncertain] Not standard for the spot; some camps offer offsets

events_community.annual_competition_window: [uncertain] Hosts occasional ISA junior events and grommet contests; less frequent than Kuta
events_community.community_events: Daily lifeguard beach clean-ups; sunset gatherings at La Plancha and Double Six; informal surf-school graduation contests

basic_info.name: Lombok Kuta Cluster - Mawi, Ekas, Gerupuk, Selong Belanak (Chinese: 龍目島庫塔浪點群 - 馬維/艾卡斯/葛魯普克/瑟隆貝拉納克; Indonesian: Kuta Lombok - Mawi / Ekas / Gerupuk / Selong Belanak)
basic_info.gps_location: Kuta Lombok town center: -8.8973, 116.2746; Selong Belanak: -8.8748, 116.1577; Mawi: -8.9072, 116.1929; Gerupuk Bay (boat launch): -8.9100, 116.3300; Ekas Bay: -8.8950, 116.4150. Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/KutaLombok
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Not a direct surf-spot to DPS comparison - requires transfer. From DPS take 35-50 min flight DPS-LOP (Lombok International) on Wings Air/Citilink/TransNusa/AirAsia, then 20 min taxi/transfer from LOP to Kuta Lombok town (~16 km). Total door-to-door ~3-4 hrs incl. check-in. Alternative: fast boat from Padangbai/Serangan ~4-5 hrs to Bangsal or Senggigi then 2 hr drive south.
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Not applicable directly - requires inter-island transfer; from Canggu/Uluwatu motorbike to DPS airport 60-90 min then 35-50 min flight to LOP plus 20 min drive to Kuta Lombok

surf_conditions.wave_type: Multi-spot cluster: Selong Belanak (sand-bottom beach break, mellow); Mawi (A-frame reef break with both left and right - hollow on size); Gerupuk Bay - Inside (right-hand reef break on seagrass-covered reef, super consistent, all tides), Don-Don (mellow A-frame reef), Outside Right (steep heavy right reef break); Ekas Bay - Inside (deep coral shelf reef break, both left and right, beginner-friendly), Outside (powerful steep left-hand reef break, advanced)
surf_conditions.skill_level: All levels covered: Selong Belanak (Beginner); Gerupuk Inside / Don-Don / Ekas Inside (Beginner-Intermediate); Mawi / Gerupuk Outside Right (Intermediate-Advanced); Ekas Outside (Advanced)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Selong Belanak: 1-4 ft (sand-bottom mellow); Gerupuk Inside: 2-8 ft (semi-steep, consistent); Mawi: 3-8 ft (A-frame, hollow on size); Gerupuk Outside: 4-10 ft; Ekas Inside: 2-6 ft (deep reef forgiving); Ekas Outside: 4-10 ft+ (powerful left)
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Selong Belanak: soft sand; Mawi: sharp shallow coral reef with rocks in lineup; Gerupuk Inside: shallow reef covered with seagrass (more forgiving); Gerupuk Outside: flat coral reef with stone formations; Ekas Inside: deep coral shelf (forgiving); Ekas Outside: shallow reef at base of cliff
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-September - August often best); offshore SE/E trade winds dominate; the surf cluster also works year-round at Gerupuk Inside which is wind-protected by surrounding hills - the only fully year-round spot in the cluster
surf_conditions.best_tide: Selong Belanak: mid tide ideal; Mawi: mid tide best (fun barrels), high tide for mellower learning, low tide sketchy/shallow; Gerupuk Inside: works all tides (unique); Gerupuk Outside: mid-to-high tide (flat reef + stones); Ekas Inside: mid tide; Ekas Outside: mid to high tide preferred
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore E to SE trade winds in dry season; Gerupuk Bay is uniquely wind-protected by surrounding hills and can handle most wind directions; afternoon onshore SW common in the wider region
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SSW groundswell from Indian Ocean lows; SW also works for west-facing breaks (Mawi, Selong Belanak); east-facing Ekas needs more S/SSE wraparound
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 4 (south Lombok coast picks up consistent SW/S groundswell almost daily during dry season; Gerupuk Inside is famously the most consistent spot in the cluster - rated 5 for consistency)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: Selong Belanak: 30-80m sand-bottom rides; Gerupuk Inside: 100-200m long rights; Mawi: 50-150m (both directions); Gerupuk Outside: 80-150m steep rights; Ekas Inside: 50-150m peeling both ways; Ekas Outside: 100-200m powerful lefts
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Variable: Selong Belanak slow; Don-Don slow to medium; Gerupuk Inside medium; Mawi medium-fast with hollow sections; Gerupuk Outside fast; Ekas Inside slow-medium; Ekas Outside fast and powerful
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Selong Belanak: very low (sand, slow); Gerupuk Inside / Don-Don / Ekas Inside: low to moderate (deep or forgiving reef); Mawi: moderate (sharp reef, watch low tide); Gerupuk Outside: high (steep drop, big sets); Ekas Outside: high (steep ledging take-off at cliff base)
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Cluster is itself a multi-peak setup. Within Gerupuk Bay: Inside, Don-Don, Outside Right, plus shifting peaks; within Ekas: Inside (both ways) and Outside; additional nearby spots Air Guling and Tanjung Aan extend the cluster
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: Variable: Selong Belanak: 4-5 (very crowded with beginners and surf schools); Gerupuk Inside: 4 (busy with surf schools daily); Mawi: 3-4 in peak season; Gerupuk Outside / Ekas Outside: 2-3 (smaller advanced crowd); Ekas Inside: 2 (often empty)

practical_info.access: Hub town is Kuta Lombok. From Kuta: Selong Belanak ~30 min west by car/motorbike; Mawi ~30 min west; Gerupuk Bay boat launch ~15-20 min east, then 5-10 min boat to each break (boats ~IDR 100,000/person for 2 hrs); Ekas Bay ~40 min east by car/motorbike, then 20 min boat across bay (or land access through Ekas village). Surf camps in Kuta organize daily transport based on best swell/tide combo across the cluster.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Selong Belanak: walk-in from beach (sand); Mawi: short paddle from beach over reef-edge channel; Gerupuk spots: boat-in via local taxi vessels from Banyu Surf Shop area; Ekas Inside: boat from Ekas village across bay; Ekas Outside: longer boat plus paddle to lineup at cliff base
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy offshore conditions across the cluster; mid-morning sessions match high tide windows; afternoon often picks up onshore - Gerupuk Inside remains surfable due to wind protection
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Yes for all Gerupuk Bay spots (boat is the only access - ~IDR 100,000/person for 2 hrs); Yes for Ekas (boat across bay from Ekas village, or 4WD overland); No for Selong Belanak (walk-in from beach); No for Mawi (short paddle from beach)
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: Excellent - Kuta Lombok is the natural extension from Bali for surfers wanting variety with shorter flight transfer. Easy combos: Bali (Bukit) + Kuta Lombok 7-10 days; Kuta Lombok + Gili Islands; Kuta Lombok + Desert Point boat charter; Kuta Lombok + Sumbawa overland (10-12 hr drive to Lakey Peak); multi-island boat charters from Bali Benoa frequently include Kuta Lombok stops
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Within the cluster itself - if one spot is too small/big/crowded, drive 15-40 min to another in the cluster. Additional nearby: Air Guling (between Mawi and Selong Belanak) and Tanjung Aan (east of Kuta town)

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp coral reef at Mawi, Gerupuk Outside, Ekas Outside (reef cuts common; booties recommended); strong currents on bigger swell days; boat traffic in Gerupuk Bay channels; jellyfish occasional; sea urchins; sunburn/dehydration; remote roads at night with stray dogs and unmarked hazards; motorbike accidents are statistically the biggest risk for visitors
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on most reefs (wear booties), occasional banded sea kraits (highly venomous but rarely aggressive), stonefish possible on reefs, jellyfish stings during wet season, reef cuts with tropical infection risk; no significant shark issue
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Generally friendly and laid-back compared to Bali Bukit; Gerupuk and Ekas have active local surf-instructor community sharing waves with guests; standard etiquette - don't drop in, take turns, learn the priority rotation at each break; smile and chat with locals - many speak surf English
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low to medium - Lombok lineups are noticeably more relaxed than Bali Bukit; locals welcoming to respectful visitors; Mawi and outside reefs can get slightly more competitive when small swell concentrates everyone on one peak
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Be respectful to Sasak Muslim culture - dress modestly off-beach (cover shoulders and knees in villages), avoid public alcohol in conservative areas, observe Ramadan fasting hours discreetly during fasting month; tip boat captains and surf-instructor guides; do not damage reef; engage with local culture (Sasak weaving villages, Pura Batu Bolong)
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Sasak Muslim community on Lombok (mosques broadcast call to prayer five times daily); dress modestly in villages and Kuta town; Ramadan and Eid observance important - some warungs close during daytime; Nyale Festival (annual Sasak sea-worm festival on south coast - February/March) is a major cultural event
safety_culture.medical_nearby: In Kuta Lombok: Mandalica Clinic (24/7 in Kuta Pujut Center, English-speaking, modern facilities), Kuta Emergency Medical (24/7 doctors on standby, handles reef cuts, stitches, malaria prophylaxis); nearest hospital: Siloam Hospitals Mataram ~60 min drive (gold standard care, emergency dept, English-speaking specialists); Rumah Sakit Umum Provinsi NTB also in Mataram; serious cases evacuated to BIMC/Siloam Bali by air ambulance
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - no permanent rescue jet ski on duty at cluster spots; surf schools and boat operators provide informal water safety; Gerupuk Bay has local boats acting as on-water support
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Mandalica Clinic Kuta: see mandalica-clinic.com for 24/7 hotline; Kuta Emergency Medical: kutaemergency.co.id; Siloam Mataram +62 370 6175000; BIMC Bali (for evacuation) +62 361 3000 911

services.accommodation_options: Wide range in Kuta Lombok town and surrounding: Nalua Surf Camp (boutique 6-room camp), KuraSurf Resort (resort-style surf camp), 360 Surf Academy & Surf Lodge, LMBK Surf House, Roots Lombok, Rapture Camps Lombok, LoTide Surf Camps, DHM Surf Camp; Mawi area: Sundays Lombok, Mawi Bay homestays; Ekas area: Ekas Surf Resort (mid-high range with private breaks access); Selong Belanak: beach villas and budget guesthouses
services.webcam_url: Surfline spot pages exist for individual breaks: Mawi (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/mawi/640a6815e9203029d5a794b1), Gerupuk Inside, Selong Belanak; live cams sporadic - some surf schools post daily report videos on Instagram [uncertain]
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - most Kuta Lombok surf schools offer add-on photo/video packages with drone and water shoots; freelance photographers shoot at Gerupuk and Mawi regularly during peak season

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Beginner group lessons IDR 500,000-700,000 (~USD 32-45) for 2 hrs incl. board, transport, instructor; Roy Reef IDR 550,000; Glassy Waves IDR 500,000; Rama Surf 4-pack discount IDR 450,000/lesson; private lessons typically IDR 800,000-1,200,000
pricing.board_rental_daily: Foam beginner boards: IDR 150,000-250,000/day; fiberglass longboards: IDR 250,000-400,000/day; shortboards: IDR 150,000-300,000/day; scooter rental ~IDR 50,000-100,000/day for transport between spots

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Generally good - south Lombok has limited urban runoff compared to Bali; Selong Belanak and Mawi clean year-round; Gerupuk Bay has some boat traffic and seaweed farming activity but water remains acceptable; wet season runoff minor

events_community.annual_competition_window: Krui Pro / Lombok Pro events have occurred at Mawi and Gerupuk in past years - WSL QS and Asian Surfing Championships have visited South Lombok; Nyale Bau Sasak Festival (Feb/Mar) is the major annual cultural event on the south coast [uncertain on current pro schedule]
events_community.community_events: Local Sasak weaving and Nyale (sea-worm) festival (Feb/March); surf-camp community dinners; beach clean-ups organized by camps; informal contest days between surf schools

basic_info.name: Medewi (Chinese: 美德威; Indonesian: Pantai Medewi / Medewi Point)
basic_info.gps_location: Approx. -8.4319, 114.7917 (Pantai Medewi, Desa Medewi, Kec. Pekutatan, Kabupaten Jembrana, Bali; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Medewi) [uncertain]
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~80-90 km (approx. 2.5-4 hours drive depending on traffic; westbali.net cites 90 km / 3.5-4.5 hours)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~120-150 min by motorbike (~70-80 km); From Uluwatu: ~150-180 min by motorbike (~100 km)

surf_conditions.wave_type: Classic long left-hand point break wrapping around cobblestone/boulder bottom; soft and rolling rather than hollow. A secondary right-hand break (Medewi Right / Rivermouth) exists just south of the point across the small river.
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate primary; accessible to confident beginners on smaller days (1-3 ft); advanced surfers enjoy it on bigger swells with multiple makeable sections.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-8 ft; best at 3-6 ft head-high to head-and-a-half; rideable nearly year-round except very small flat west swells
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Cobblestone/boulder (rounded rocks with some barnacle-covered stones near entry); transitions to grey/black sand eastward
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season April/May-October (peak June-August) with regular S-SW swells and offshore winds; fairly consistent year-round due to its western exposure
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide best (waves form better, easier rocky entry); low tide loses shape and exposes urchins/sharp stones
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore N to NE winds (early morning); cross/onshore SE-S trade winds typically build by mid-morning - dawn patrol essential for glass
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SW (SSW ideal); responds to most south Indian Ocean groundswells
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 4 (very consistent year-round - one of the most reliable spots in Bali, even when south Bukit goes flat)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 300-800+ meters on the best days (multiple connecting sections from outside take-off through the inside; commonly cited as one of Bali's longest rides) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Slow to medium (mellow, ambling wall - excellent for longboards, mid-lengths, and learning to surf points)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Low to moderate - friendly take-off on a deeper outside section; main difficulty is positioning on the cobblestone reef and timing the long wait between sets
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - Medewi Right (Rivermouth) just south across the small river is a shorter right-hander; additional beach-break peaks and small A-frames stretch eastward through Yeh Sumbul and Pulukan
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 3 (moderate; busy on weekends and during dry season swells with surf-camp groups, but still significantly less crowded than Canggu/Bukit; dawn patrol thins the lineup)

practical_info.access: Drive west on Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk; turn south at Medewi village sign onto Jalan Pantai Medewi; park at the beachfront (motorbike parking near warungs and the point); walk down across cobblestones to enter
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Wade carefully across cobblestones and barnacle-covered rocks (booties strongly recommended), then paddle out along the inside; on bigger days use the small rip channel near the point to reach the outside take-off
practical_info.entry_fee: Parking IDR 5,000-10,000 motorbike / IDR 10,000-20,000 car; no formal beach entry fee
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy offshore conditions; second session around late afternoon when winds soften
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~9 AM before crossshore SE trade winds build; high-tide windows in early morning are gold
practical_info.nearby_warung: Warung Gede (famous BBQ fish/chicken), Warung Kayu, Dewi Warung, Rasta Cafe, Wakey Wakey Coffee (near Yeh Sumbul), and multiple beachfront warungs along Jl. Pantai Medewi serving nasi goreng, mie goreng, fresh juices
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Medewi Right (Rivermouth) - same beach; Yeh Sumbul beach break - 5-10 min west; further afield Pulukan and Pekutatan beach breaks
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - direct land/beach access

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp barnacle-covered cobblestones at entry/exit, sea urchins on the reef especially at low tide, occasional strong currents on bigger swells, very long paddle if caught inside, isolated location (limited rescue infrastructure)
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (most common reef cut/spine injury - use vinegar and tweezers; tetanus risk), occasional jellyfish, rare sea snakes; barnacle cuts can infect quickly in tropical water - clean and disinfect immediately
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Medium - cobblestones are rounded but covered in sharp barnacles and urchins; cuts are common at low tide; booties (rentable for ~IDR 30,000/session) strongly recommended
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Generally mild but a small rip runs out near the point/rivermouth on bigger days - useful as a paddle-out lane; river outflow during/after rain creates murky water and current near the rivermouth
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Respect locals first (Medewi Boardriders Club organizes the lineup); observe rotation on the long point - one wave at a time; do not snake; smile, say hi, and acknowledge regulars. Surf schools push students into waves so beginners should stay clear of advanced peaks.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low to medium - generally friendly compared to Bukit; the Medewi Boardriders Club runs warungs/homestays and benefits from surf tourism; isolated drop-in incidents reported but vibe is mostly mellow
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Wait your turn on the point; defer to local Indonesians; do not paddle around groups; respect the Muslim cultural context (modest dress walking through the village; avoid disrespect during Ramadan/prayer times); support local warungs and homestays
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Medewi Boardriders Club members; local longboarder Made Lapinkz and other Jembrana-based surfers; well-known foreign-Indonesian shaper community at Brown Sugar Surf [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Muslim village (rare in Bali) - five daily calls to prayer from multiple mosques (bring earplugs for early starts); also nearby Hindu temples Pura Rambut Siwi and Pura Perancak; dress modestly when walking through the village; Nyepi (Day of Silence, ~March) - no surfing or beach access islandwide
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Klinik / Puskesmas Pekutatan (basic primary care, ~10-15 min east); RSU Negara / Rumah Sakit Umum Daerah Negara (~30-40 min west, nearest hospital); BIMC Hospital and Siloam Denpasar 2.5-3 hours east for serious injuries
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: ~5-10 km to Puskesmas Pekutatan; ~25-30 km to RSU Negara hospital
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - no permanent jet-ski lifeguard service; rely on self-rescue and fellow surfers
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Police 110; Ambulance 118 / 119; Tourist Police +62-361-754599 (Bali) [uncertain on most current numbers]

services.nearest_surf_school: Brown Sugar Surf School / Medewi Surf Camp (long-running local school on Jl. Pantai Medewi), Medewi Manor, Bombora Medewi Wave Lodge (in-house lessons), Ayolah Surf House (Yeh Sumbul side), Makai Medewi / House of Surf, Rapture Surfcamps Medewi
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - widely available from beachfront surf shacks and homestays; ~IDR 80,000-150,000/day (around IDR 80,000 per session at the point); booties ~IDR 30,000/session
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - local freelance land photographers shoot from the cobblestone point and beach; less developed than Uluwatu/Canggu but present during dry season swells [uncertain]
services.photographer_recommendation: Freelance shooters operate via Instagram (search 'Medewi surf photo'); typical package IDR 150,000-400,000 for a session of edited photos [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Bombora Medewi Wave Lodge (mid-high range, on the point), Medewi Beach Inn (mid-range), Homestay Gede (beachfront, simple/affordable), Medewi Secret Surf Camp (budget), Wide Sands Beach Retreat (Yeh Sumbul side), Bali Tiger Hideaway, Brown Sugar Surf Camp, Bali Mojo, Medewi Manor
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/medewi/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d43 (Surfline cam/report); also surf-forecast.com Medewi page
services.drone_allowed: Generally permitted for personal use over the beach; respect privacy and avoid flying over mosques or temple ceremonies; commercial use requires permit [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Limited dedicated coworking; some homestays/cafes offer wifi-friendly workspaces (e.g. Wakey Wakey Coffee, Bombora cafe); nearest serious coworking is Tabanan/Canggu (2+ hours east) [uncertain]

pricing.avg_lesson_price: IDR 300,000-500,000 (~USD 20-35) for 1-on-1 hour lesson; group lessons cheaper
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 80,000-150,000/day (~USD 5-10); per-session rate ~IDR 80,000

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season; declines in wet season (Nov-April) due to river outflow and runoff carrying plastic/agricultural waste especially near the rivermouth - avoid surfing for 24-48 hours after heavy rain

events_community.annual_competition_window: Local Medewi Boardriders Club events and longboard contests held occasionally during the dry season; no permanent WSL-level event but small longboard/local comps appear seasonally [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Medewi Boardriders Club gatherings, local longboard sessions on small days, occasional surf-shop demo days; cultural events tied to nearby Hindu temples (Pura Rambut Siwi odalan) and Muslim festivals [uncertain]

basic_info.name: Mentawai Islands (Chinese: 明打威群島; Indonesian: Kepulauan Mentawai)
basic_info.gps_location: Archipelago centroid approx. -2.0000, 99.5000 off West Sumatra; key surf hubs: Tua Pejat (Sipora) -2.1561, 99.6181; HT's/Lance's Right (South Sipora) -2.265, 99.575; Macaronis (Pagai Utara, Silabu) -2.781, 100.063; Kandui/Karangmajat (North Pagai/Siberut area) -1.860, 99.250. Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Mentawai
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~1,300 km straight-line northwest of DPS; total transfer typically 1-2 days: DPS to Padang (PDG/BIM Minangkabau Intl Airport) by air ~1.5-2 hr direct (Citilink/Batik/Super Air Jet) or via CGK Jakarta hub ~3-4 hr total; then Padang to Mentawai by 10-12 hr overnight ferry (Mentawai Express, Ambu-Ambu, ~IDR 240,000-330,000) or 3-4 hr Mentawai Fast (Padang-Tua Pejat/Sikakap, ~IDR 300,000-450,000), or 1 hr Wings Air flight PDG-Rokot (RKI) on Sipora since March 2025
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Not applicable directly - inter-island/international transfer required. From Canggu: 60-90 min motorbike to DPS + 2 hr flight to PDG + same-day or overnight ferry/boat transfer to chosen camp (4-12 hrs). From Uluwatu: 45-60 min to DPS + same. Total door-to-camp typically 18-36 hrs including layovers.

surf_conditions.wave_type: World-class archipelago with 40+ named breaks, predominantly tropical coral reef breaks; mix of left-handers (Macaronis, Kandui/No Kandui, Bankvaults, Rifles, Pitstops) and right-handers (HT's/Lance's Right, Telescopes, Burgerworld, Lance's Left, Greenbush, Thunders, A-Frames). Includes pure barrels (HT's, Kandui, Rifles, Greenbush), high-performance walls (Macaronis, Telescopes, Lance's Right), and beginner-intermediate options (Beng Bengs, Pitstops, Awera Right, E-Bay).
surf_conditions.skill_level: Predominantly Intermediate to Pro; world's most pro-stacked surf playground. A handful of mellower breaks (Beng Bengs, E-Bay inside, Pitstops on small days, Bat Caves, Iceland) suit confident intermediates, but most marquee waves (HT's, Macaronis, Kandui, Rifles, Greenbush, Lance's Left) require advanced reef experience and tube-riding ability.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 2-12+ ft Hawaiian scale across the archipelago; most spots fire 3-8 ft; HT's 3-10 ft, Macaronis 3-8 ft, Kandui 3-10 ft, Rifles 4-12 ft, Greenbush 4-10 ft, Telescopes 3-10 ft, Awera Right 1-8 ft (works smallest). On rare giant swells the outer reefs can hold 12-15 ft.
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp live tropical coral reef across virtually all breaks; varying depths from shallow shelving (HT's, Rifles, Kandui, Greenbush extremely shallow) to deeper outer-reef setups (Telescopes, Bankvaults). Very few sand-bottom options.
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season April-October with peak swell window May-September; shoulder months March-November still productive. Wet/cyclone-shoulder December-February produces smaller, less consistent surf but the most beginner-friendly conditions and fewer crowds; some spots (HT's, Macaronis) still fire on the right pulse year-round.
surf_conditions.best_tide: Highly spot-dependent: HT's best mid-to-high tide for safer reef coverage and length; Macaronis works all tides but mid-tide is the sweet spot; Kandui prefers higher tides over shallow shelf; Rifles needs mid-high tide; Greenbush works low-to-mid for hollowest barrels but at maximum reef risk. Boat skippers schedule sessions around tide windows.
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE trade winds prevail through dry-season mornings; most south- and west-facing breaks (HT's, Macaronis, Lance's Left, Telescopes) prefer SE winds; some setups (Thunders, north-facing breaks) prefer N or NW winds. Afternoon onshore SW common; dawn patrol and late afternoon sessions are typically cleanest.
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW to S groundswell from deep Indian Ocean storms (Roaring 40s/Subantarctic lows); SSW long-period swells are the classic Mentawai pulse and light up the entire archipelago; west swells favor HT's and the Sipora cluster.
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (one of the most consistent world-class surf zones on the planet during April-October; Mentawai is described by many forecasters as the most reliable concentration of world-class waves anywhere)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: Variable: HT's 100-200 m, Macaronis 150-300 m (extends on bigger days), Kandui 100-250 m, Rifles 150-300 m, Telescopes 150-300+ m (one of the longest in the chain), Lance's Left 100-200 m; many breaks deliver 200+ m rides on solid swells.
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium to very fast; Macaronis medium-fast performance wall, HT's fast barrel, Kandui extremely fast hollow tube, Rifles fast and ledging, Greenbush extreme/heavy slab, Telescopes medium-fast workable wall.
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Generally high - many waves ledge or break on shallow reef requiring late, committed drops; HT's, Kandui, Rifles, Greenbush, and Lance's Left have notoriously demanding take-offs; positioning is critical and the cost of error is reef contact.
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - the archipelago itself is the secondary-peak system: most regions cluster 4-8 named breaks within 30-60 min boat distance, allowing boats/resorts to pivot to whichever spot is best on the day (e.g., Playgrounds zone holds Bankvaults, Pitstops, E-Bay, Beng Bengs, 4Bobs, Burgerworld, Nipussi, all in one area).
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 3-4 in dry season; can feel crowded by Mentawai standards (8-30 surfers per peak) at marquee waves like Macaronis, HT's, Kandui when multiple charters and resorts converge; overall still uncrowded compared to Bali; charter boats actively avoid each other's anchorages.

practical_info.access: Multi-leg international/domestic transfer. (1) Fly into PDG (Minangkabau International, Padang) from Jakarta/Bali/KL via Garuda, Citilink, Batik, Super Air Jet, AirAsia. (2) From Padang to Mentawai by either Mentawai Fast ferry (3-4 hrs to Tua Pejat/Sikakap, ~IDR 300,000-450,000), Mentawai Express overnight slow ferry (~10-12 hrs, IDR 240,000-330,000), Wings Air flight PDG-RKI (Rokot, Sipora, 1 hr, since March 2025, Mon/Fri only), or private speedboat/charter transfer. (3) From Mentawai port to resort/boat by small speedboat (15-90 min). Most surfers book all-inclusive resort or charter packages with transfers included.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Boat-based: charter boats anchor in lagoon/channel and surfers paddle from dinghy/tender to lineup, or use boat as floating base between sessions. Resort-based: speedboat from resort jetty to spot, paddle from boat. Few spots accessible by walking from beach (Macaronis can be paddled from beach at the resort; HT's has some beach access at Katiet village).
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy offshore conditions before SE trades build; mid-morning session 9-11 AM still clean at many spots; afternoon session 3:30-6:00 PM after wind drops; surf charters/resorts typically run 2-3 sessions/day timed to tides and wind.
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Dawn patrol and late afternoon for cleanest conditions; mid-day sessions still productive on most days; sunset glass-offs common at sheltered spots.
practical_info.nearby_warung: Minimal off-resort/boat options. Tua Pejat (Sipora administrative town) has basic warungs, mini-marts and a few cafes; Katiet village (near HT's) has a couple of small warungs; otherwise food is via the resort/charter boat (all-inclusive meal plans).
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Within Mentawai regions: most areas cluster 4-8 spots within 10-60 min boat hop, e.g., Playgrounds region (Bankvaults, Pitstops, E-Bay, Beng Bengs, 4Bobs, Burgerworld, Nipussi); Lance's region (HT's, Lance's Left, Bintangs, Beng Bengs); Macaronis area (Macaronis, Greenbush, Roxies, Thunders); Kandui area (Kandui, No Kandui/Rifles, Bankvaults).
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Largely yes - most marquee spots are only practically accessed by boat (charter or resort speedboat); a few land-based resorts (Macaronis Resort, HT's Resort, Kandui Villas, Kingfisher, Aloita, Awera, Playgrounds, Villa Mentawai) provide land-based access to specific spots; charter boats remain the most flexible option to chase swell across regions.
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: Excellent for combos with mainland Sumatra (Krui/Padang Padang South Sumatra), Nias (Sorake/Lagundri), Banyak Islands (Bay of Plenty/Treasures), Telos (Telo Islands south of Mentawai), and Sumbawa/Bali via return flight. Typical 'Sumatra surf safari' chains Krui-Nias-Banyaks-Mentawais; Bali-Mentawai roundtrip is most common solo combo.

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp shallow coral reef across virtually all breaks (severe reef cuts the most common injury), powerful Indian Ocean swell (hold-downs, board breaks, fractures documented), remote location with very slow medical evacuation, tropical sun/heat exposure, mosquito-borne disease (dengue endemic; malaria risk officially low but precautions advised), waterborne illness (gastro), occasional jellyfish blooms, boat-related injuries (jet ski, dinghy), and rare crocodile sightings in river mouths/mangroves on the larger islands.
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (booties strongly recommended), stonefish (camouflaged on reef, extremely venomous - immerse wound in hot water 45 C and seek help), occasional sea snakes (highly venomous but non-aggressive), reef cuts with high tropical infection risk (treat with antiseptic, hibiclens, oral antibiotics often required), occasional box jellyfish in season, fire coral burns; shark encounters very rare and almost never aggressive.
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Very High - sharpest live coral in Indonesia at several breaks (Kandui, HT's, Rifles, Greenbush, Macaronis inside); reef-cut infections are the #1 trip-ending injury; booties strongly recommended at most breaks; charter boats and resorts carry suture kits and antibiotics; serious wounds require evacuation to Padang.
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Spot-dependent; many reef breaks have defined paddle-out channels alongside the reef shelf with strong rips (use to paddle out, avoid swimming across); HT's has well-defined channel on inside; Macaronis has channel river-mouth flow; some outer-reef setups (Bankvaults, Telescopes) sit in open ocean with strong tidal currents. Always discuss channel and current with skipper/guide before paddling out.
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Priority by positioning and timing; charter boats coordinate to avoid stacking the same peak; respect resort guests' priority where applicable (e.g., Macaronis Resort has rotation system with charters); locals (Katiet/Silabu/Karangmajat Mentawai families) and resort staff earn respect priority; do not paddle around, do not snake set waves; communicate with other boats over radio.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low to Medium - indigenous Mentawai people are generally friendly and welcoming; the rare localism tension is between visiting boat charters competing for the same peak rather than locals vs visitors; some camps/resorts have had historical access disputes but situation has stabilized.
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Respect Mentawai indigenous Sakuddei/Arat Sabulungan culture if visiting villages (tattoos, sago harvest, traditional dress); Christian and Muslim communities both present - dress modestly off-beach, observe Sunday/Friday services and Ramadan; pay surfer tax in full; tip resort/charter staff (~10%); do not take coral, shells, or wildlife.
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Martin Daly (Indies Trader skipper, credited with opening modern charter access in early 1990s); Lance Knight (Lance's Left/Right namesake); legendary visitors include Tom Curren (original 1993 charter), Kelly Slater (regular charter guest), Andy Irons, Bruce Irons, Mick Fanning, Joel Parkinson; local talents emerging through Indonesian Surfing Championships (ISC).
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Mentawai islands are predominantly Christian (Protestant majority) with significant Muslim population on Sipora and the mainland Padang region; respect Sunday and Friday worship; in Padang and Muslim village areas dress modestly and respect Ramadan; indigenous Arat Sabulungan animist practices still observed in inland Siberut.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Very limited. Tua Pejat (Sipora) has a small government hospital (RSUD Mentawai) and several puskesmas clinics; Sikakap (South Pagai) and Siberut have small puskesmas; resort/charter medics provide first-aid and suturing for reef cuts; serious injuries require fast-boat or helicopter evacuation to Padang (RS Semen Padang, RS M. Djamil, Siloam Hospitals Padang ~6-12 hr round trip) and ultimately Singapore/Jakarta/Bali (BIMC, Siloam) for major trauma.
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Variable: 0 km on-resort/on-boat first aid; from typical Playgrounds anchorage to Tua Pejat clinic ~10-30 km by boat (30-90 min); from Macaronis to Sikakap puskesmas ~50-80 km (2-3 hr by boat); from any Mentawai location to Padang full-service hospital ~100-160 km incl. sea crossing (4-12 hr by ferry, 1 hr by Wings Air).
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No public/lifeguard jet ski coverage; some charter boats and resorts (Macaronis Resort, Kandui Villas) operate jet skis on bigger days for tow-in and rescue but coverage is informal and not guaranteed; rescue capability is one of the things to assess when choosing operator.
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; SAR (Search & Rescue) Padang: 115; Mentawai District Police +62 759 21110; Mentawai Regional Hospital (RSUD Mentawai) Tua Pejat; BIMC Bali +62 361 3000 911 for evacuation referral; satellite phone / Iridium recommended on charter boats (cell signal absent at outer reefs)

services.nearest_surf_school: Mentawai is not a beginner instruction destination; advanced surf guiding is the norm. Most resorts (Macaronis, Kandui Villas, HT's, Kingfisher, Awera, Villa Mentawai, Aloita, Pitstops) employ in-house guides; few formal 'schools' exist but operations like Macaronis Resort run learn-to-surf programs for family/spouses on small days. Aloita Resort markets to beginners and improvers.
services.board_rental_availability: Limited but improving - most resorts maintain small rental quivers (mid-length, step-up, gun); charter boats expect guests to bring their own quivers; recommended personal quiver: 6'0-6'4 shortboard, 6'6-7'0 step-up, 7'2-8'0 gun for big Rifles/Kandui/Greenbush days. [uncertain on per-day rental rates]
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - resorts and many charters employ in-house water/land/drone photographers (Macaronis, Kandui, Awera, HT's, Kingfisher); freelance pro photographers (Surfing Visions, Trent Slatter, Andrew Shield equivalents) shoot during peak season; package upgrades and per-day rates common.
services.photographer_recommendation: Camp/charter-employed photographers offer daily photo packages ~USD 50-150/day; package upgrades USD 300-800/week including drone, water and land coverage; established Mentawai-based shooters include Surfing Visions (Indo classics), Liquid Barrel media, Mentawai-resident freelancers via Instagram. [uncertain on current shooter availability]
services.accommodation_options: Two formats: (1) Land-based resorts: Macaronis Resort (USD 3,400-5,500/week premium); Kandui Villas (USD 4,500-7,000/week luxury); HT's Mentawai Surf Resort (USD 3,000-4,500/week); Kingfisher Mentawai (USD 3,000-4,500/week); Villa Mentawai (USD 2,500-3,500/week); Awera Resort (USD 2,500-3,800/week); Aloita Resort (USD 1,800-2,800/week, beginner-friendly); Playgrounds Surf Resort (USD 2,800-4,000/week); Pitstops Mentawai; Nasara Resort. (2) Charter boats: 50+ vessels from budget Ombak Kabau (USD 1,800-2,400/trip) through mid-range Bintang/Switchfoot/Mangalui (USD 2,400-4,500/trip) to luxury Indies Trader, Addiction, Huey (USD 5,000-25,000+/trip). All include meals, guide, transfers.
services.webcam_url: Surfline premium Mentawais regional forecast: https://www.surfline.com/surf-forecasts/mentawais/58581a836630e24c44878fee; spot-specific cams limited; Thunders cam available on Surfline; most operators rely on satellite forecasts (Surfline, Swellnet, Surf-Forecast, Magicseaweed legacy). [uncertain on live cam coverage]
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use over reefs/lineups; some resorts have policies on guest drone use to avoid disturbing other guests; commercial drone work requires Indonesian DGCA permit and Mentawai regency permission; respect indigenous village privacy when flying inland. [uncertain on per-resort drone policy]
services.coworking_nearby: None at the level of Bali/Canggu; most resorts offer Starlink WiFi (significantly improved 2023-2025) but no dedicated coworking spaces; charter boats now widely offer Starlink. Workable for digital nomads on flexible schedules but not optimized for it.

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not a lesson-focused destination; private guiding included in resort/charter packages; standalone coaching/guiding rare and priced USD 100-300/day if arranged. [uncertain]
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Not applicable - packaged guiding rather than per-lesson pricing; few resorts (Aloita) offer learn-to-surf modules embedded in stays. [uncertain]
pricing.board_rental_daily: If available at resorts: USD 30-60/day for step-up/gun boards; most surfers bring own quivers. [uncertain]
pricing.gopro_rental_price: Generally not offered by resorts/charters as separate rental - guests bring own action cams; some photographers offer GoPro footage as add-on. [uncertain]
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: Not standard rental category - water 27-30 C year-round so most surfers use own rash guards; some resorts have spare rashies. [uncertain]
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: Ding repair available at resorts and on most charter boats (in-house repair kits); typically free for minor dings on resort/charter quivers, IDR 200,000-500,000+ for guest boards depending on severity; major repairs may wait until return to Padang/Bali. [uncertain]
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: Wide range: budget charters from USD 1,800-2,400 per 10-11 day trip; mid-range resorts USD 2,500-4,500/week; premium resorts (Macaronis, Kandui Villas) USD 4,500-7,000/week; luxury charters USD 5,000-15,000+/week. Per-night land-based equivalent ranges USD 250-700/night.
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Typically: airport/port transfers (Padang to camp), all meals (Western + Indonesian), bottled/filtered water, daily guided surf trips with speedboat transfers (resorts) or boat-based access (charters), surf guide, insurance liaison; not typically included: Mentawai surfer tax, alcohol (beer cash bar), tips, board rental, photo packages, international flights to PDG, dive activities.
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Peak season May-September commands 20-40% premium vs shoulder months Mar-Apr/Oct-Nov; off-peak Dec-Feb sometimes 30-50% discounted but limited availability as many operators close 2-6 weeks for maintenance. [uncertain on exact percentages per operator]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Generally excellent across most breaks - remote archipelago with minimal urban/agricultural runoff; some wet-season sediment near river mouths and after heavy rains; very clean compared to Bali-mainland breaks.
sustainability.eco_certification: A handful of operators advertise eco initiatives (Aloita Resort, Awera Resort, Kandui Villas marine conservation programs) but no industry-wide certification; Macaronis Resort and others run reef-monitoring and reforestation projects with limited third-party verification. [uncertain]
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Not standard across operators; a few high-end resorts (Kandui Villas, Aloita) mention carbon-offset partnerships; most charters do not offer offsets. [uncertain]

events_community.annual_competition_window: Rip Curl Pro Search held at Macaronis in 2008 (event won by Bruce Irons); WSL Mentawai Pro and Mentawai Surf Cup held intermittently; ISA World Surfing Games does not visit; major contests rare due to remoteness and logistics. [uncertain on current schedule]
events_community.community_events: Surf-camp dawn-patrol culture; resort cocktail hours and BBQ nights; occasional charity events (board donations to local Mentawai kids surf programs run via Mentawai Surf Aid, SurfAid International - founded by Dr. Dave Jenkins in Mentawai 2000)

Mentawai.combo_trip_potential: Excellent - the classic 'Indo surf safari' destination, regularly combined with Bali (Bukit) before/after, Nias (Sorake/Lagundri) overland from Padang, Banyak Islands further north, Telos south of Mentawai, and increasingly Krui (South Sumatra) en route. Many operators package multi-region 14-21 day Bali+Mentawai or Sumatra-wide trips.

basic_info.name: Nusa Dua (Chinese: 努沙杜瓦; Indonesian: Nusa Dua)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.8089, 115.2347 (offshore reef in front of Geger Beach / Nusa Dua Beach near Mulia & St. Regis resorts; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/NusaDuaReef)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~12-15 km (~25-35 min drive)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~60-75 min by motorbike (~28 km); From Uluwatu: ~25-35 min by motorbike (~15 km)

surf_conditions.wave_type: Right-hand reef break (with secondary left at Temple Lefts); long, powerful, shifty peaking right-handers that can barrel and peel for 200m+ or shut down; multiple peaks along a 2 km reef including Temple Peaks (Temple Lefts), Main Peak (XXL big wave), Elevators (right), Keyhole Left/Right, and Chickens (right)
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (Main Peak is Pro/big-wave only on large swells; smaller peaks like Temple Lefts and Chickens are accessible to confident intermediates)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Works 2-15+ ft; sweet spot 3-8 ft for intermediates; Main Peak fires 8-15+ ft on big SW groundswells (XXL big-wave venue)
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp coral/limestone reef over an open-ocean reef shelf stretching ~2 km
surf_conditions.best_season: Wet season November-April (peak December-March); January typically the biggest, March often the most consistent. Works in dry season too on east/SE swell pulses but offshore winds reverse
surf_conditions.best_tide: Variable by peak: Temple Lefts, Elevators, Keyhole and Main Peak prefer lower/outgoing tides; Chickens prefers incoming-to-high tide; mid tide generally workable somewhere on the reef
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore W to NW winds (wet season trade winds); light N winds also work at lower tides
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SW (Main Peak fires on long-period SW groundswells from the Roaring Forties); SE swells also produce waves; some peaks like Chickens prefer SW
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 4 (very consistent during wet season - the most reliable east-coast spot when west coast is blown out; almost always a wave breaking somewhere along the 2 km reef)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 100-250 meters on a good day at Main Peak; shorter (50-100 m) at Temple Lefts and Chickens
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium to fast (Main Peak is fast and powerful with heavy top-to-bottom barrels; other peaks are more playful and medium-paced)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High at Main Peak (steep drop, shallow reef, life-threatening hold-downs on big days); moderate at Temple Lefts, Elevators and Chickens; positioning across a wide shifting reef makes take-off less predictable than point breaks
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - multiple distinct peaks along 2 km reef: Temple Peaks/Temple Lefts (inside), Main Peak (outside, big-wave), Elevators, Keyhole Left/Right, Chickens (south end). Different peaks suit different tides, sizes and skill levels
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 3 (moderate; crowd factor often cited as 6/10; less crowded than Uluwatu/Padang due to long paddle and big-wave intimidation, but lineup spreads across multiple peaks)

practical_info.access: Land access via Geger Beach or Nusa Dua Beach (in front of Mulia and St. Regis resorts) through resort beach paths or Pura Geger temple car park; long paddle out (one of the longest in Bali, up to 1 km to Main Peak); boat shuttle available from beach for shorter access
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Option 1: paddle through the keyhole/channel by the temple at higher tide; Option 2: walk along the reef at low tide and jump off the south side of the reef using the channel; Option 3: hire a boat from Geger or Nusa Dua Beach pickup point - boat drops you outside the reef at the chosen peak
practical_info.entry_fee: Parking IDR 2,000-5,000 motorbike at Geger Beach / temple car park; resort beach access free if you walk through; no formal surf entry fee
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy conditions and lighter offshore winds; mid-morning if tides align with peak choice
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Early morning before onshore winds build (typically clean until ~10 AM); late afternoon can clean up again as winds drop
practical_info.nearby_warung: Warungs and seafood shacks along Geger Beach; Bumbu Bali, Nusa Dua Beach Grill, Sundara at Four Seasons (upscale), several small warungs near the Pura Geger temple car park. Limited budget warungs compared to Bukit - this is a resort area
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Sri Lanka / Sawangan reef (5-10 min south by motorbike), Serangan (~30 min north - but on the same east coast wet-season window), Black Stone (very close)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - but strongly recommended due to long paddle. Boat shuttle ~IDR 50,000-100,000 one-way from Geger Beach or Nusa Dua Beach

safety_culture.hazards: Long paddle out and back (up to 1 km), strong currents that 'move like a river' at higher tides especially across reef, shallow sharp coral reef, severe hold-downs at Main Peak on big swells, seaweed farms on inside paddle path can cause hand/foot cuts, sea urchins in reef crevices
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (abundant in reef cracks), coral heads, occasional jellyfish, possible sea snakes, seaweed farm ropes on inside lagoon paths. Treat reef cuts and urchin spines promptly - infection risk in tropical water
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: High at Main Peak/Elevators (shallow shelf can suck dry on bigger sets); moderate at Temple Lefts and Chickens; booties recommended for low-tide sessions
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong sweeping current runs along the reef especially at higher tides - can drag surfers down the line; use the keyhole channel at temple as a paddle-out lane; channel on south side of reef also functions as exit
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Generally relaxed and respectful - 'cruisey dudes' who respect the wave; standard priority/no-snake rules apply. Smile, wait your turn, do not drop in on Main Peak veterans
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low (cited as ~1/10) - not heavily localized compared to Bukit spots; small dedicated regular crew, but visitors are welcomed if respectful and competent
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Show respect to Balinese surfers, observe lineup before paddling, do not drop in, acknowledge priority. Do not attempt Main Peak unless you are genuinely big-wave capable
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Local Nusa Dua/Bukit big-wave chargers including Mega Semadhi, Made Adi Putra, Marlon Gerber (frequently shoots Main Peak); various Indonesian big-wave riders on call when Main Peak goes XXL [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Geger temple sits at the cliff/beach entry - respect the temple, no inappropriate behavior. Nyepi (Day of Silence, ~March) - no surfing or beach access islandwide. Avoid surfing during temple ceremonies. Resort area also imposes general decorum
safety_culture.medical_nearby: BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (very close, ~5-10 min), Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~30-40 min), Unicare Pecatu (~20 min)
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua: ~2-4 km
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No permanent rescue jet ski; some safety boats occasionally during big swells at Main Peak when pros are out [uncertain]

services.nearest_surf_school: Odyssey Surf School (Tanjung Benoa, ~10-15 min), Rip Curl School of Surf at Sofitel Nusa Dua, Padang Padang Surf Camp (offers Nusa Dua sessions in wet season). Most beginners are taught at Tanjung Benoa or Geger Beach inside rather than the outer reef
services.board_rental_availability: Limited at the spot itself; available at surf shops in Tanjung Benoa and through hotels/surf camps; ~IDR 150,000-250,000/day
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - land photographers shoot Main Peak from the cliff/beach on big swells; water photographers occasional during XXL events. Less consistent coverage than Uluwatu
services.photographer_recommendation: Freelance shooters via Instagram (search #nusaduasurf); specialist big-wave shooters during XXL events. Rates ~IDR 300,000-700,000+ per session [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Luxury resorts dominate: The Mulia, St. Regis Bali Resort, Sofitel Nusa Dua, Grand Hyatt, The Bale, Kimpton Naranta Bali, Four Seasons Jimbaran; budget options in Tanjung Benoa nearby. Few dedicated surf camps in Nusa Dua itself - most surfers stay in Bukit or Canggu and drive over
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/nusa-dua/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d47 ; https://www.surf-forecast.com/breaks/Nusadua
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use over the surf zone; resort beaches may restrict; avoid flying over temple and resort grounds without permission [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Limited compared to Canggu/Bukit; Tanjung Benoa has a few small co-working cafes; resort lobbies often double as workspaces. B Work Bali Pecatu and Outpost Ulu (~25-30 min away) are the nearest serious coworking options

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 35-60 / IDR 550,000-950,000 group lesson at nearby surf schools (Rip Curl, Odyssey) - typically taught at Geger Beach inside rather than the outer reef

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Generally good - far from major river outflows; minor seaweed-farm impacts on inside paddle. Some runoff in heavy wet-season rain but cleaner than Kuta/Legian beach

events_community.annual_competition_window: Has historically hosted big-wave / Volcom contests when Main Peak fires; no fixed annual event currently as of 2026 [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Informal big-wave sessions when Main Peak comes alive in January-March; surf camps run group sessions during wet season

basic_info.name: Nusa Lembongan (Chinese: 藍夢島; Indonesian: Nusa Lembongan)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.6786, 115.4500 (island east of Bali mainland; surf zone off Jungut Batu Beach northwest coast; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/NusaLembongan)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~25-30 km straight line; ~45-60 min total via Sanur Harbor fast boat (30-45 min boat + ~30 min car to Sanur)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Not applicable - boat-only access. From Canggu to Sanur Harbor: ~45-60 min by car; Sanur to Lembongan: 30-45 min fast boat. From Uluwatu to Sanur: ~45-60 min by car

surf_conditions.wave_type: Three main reef breaks off Jungut Batu Beach: Shipwrecks (fast, powerful right-hander, ~500m offshore, intermediate-advanced); Lacerations (hollow draining right-hand barrel, advanced/expert); Playgrounds (A-frame with short hollow right and longer rippable left, beginner-intermediate friendly, closest to shore). Plus secondary breaks: Razors, No Mans, Ceningan
surf_conditions.skill_level: Mixed: Shipwrecks intermediate-advanced; Lacerations advanced/expert only; Playgrounds beginner-intermediate (one of the most user-friendly high-quality reef breaks in Bali region)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Playgrounds: 2-8 ft; Shipwrecks: 3-10 ft; Lacerations: 3-10 ft (works best at 4-6 ft for clean barrels)
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp, shallow coral reef on all three breaks - Lacerations and Shipwrecks notably sharp and unforgiving
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season April-October (peak May-September) when SE trade winds are offshore on these NW-facing reefs; wet season Nov-March has more onshore wind but quieter lineups on calm-wind windows
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide is essential - need at least ~1.4 m of water; low tide exposes dangerous reef. Playgrounds works on widest tide range; Lacerations and Shipwrecks demand mid-high
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE to E trade winds (dry-season trades from late May through September); morning glassy conditions common
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW for Shipwrecks and Lacerations; WSW for Playgrounds; consistent S-SW Indian Ocean groundswells light up all three peaks
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 4 (very consistent dry-season swells thanks to direct Indian Ocean exposure; less reliable in wet season due to wind)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: Shipwrecks: 100-200 m fast peeling rights; Lacerations: 50-100 m intense barrel section; Playgrounds: 50-100 m on the left, shorter on the right
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Fast at Shipwrecks and Lacerations (Lacerations especially - air-drop takeoff into hollow tube); medium at Playgrounds (more rippable and mellow)
