surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Lacerations: very high (take-off on the rail under the lip, air drop, shallow reef); Shipwrecks: high (fast steep drop, powerful section); Playgrounds: low-moderate (deeper water, more forgiving)
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - 6 main breaks in the area: Shipwrecks, Lacerations, Playgrounds, Razors (left), No Mans (Ceningan), Ceningan Reef. Pontoons available between breaks for rest
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 4 (busy in dry season with day-trippers from Bali, surf charter boats and resident surf-camp guests; Shipwrecks and Lacerations heavily crowded on good days; Playgrounds always has surf-school groups). Quieter than mainland Bali overall

practical_info.access: Boat-only access from Bali. Fast boats from Sanur Harbor to Jungut Batu Beach (30-45 min); operators include Scoot Fast Cruises, Rocky Fast Cruises, Mola Mola Express, Marlin Express, El Rey Fast Cruise; daily departures from ~7:30 AM. From Jungut Batu, local boats shuttle surfers to the breaks
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Two options: 1) Hire a local outrigger boat from Jungut Batu Beach to drop you outside the break (and optionally wait or pick up); 2) Paddle ~500 m from Jungut Batu Beach - long paddle, save for return only. Pontoons anchored between breaks allow rest between sets
practical_info.entry_fee: Fast boat Sanur-Lembongan return: ~IDR 350,000-700,000 per person (varies by operator and hotel transfer inclusion). Local boat shuttle Jungut Batu to breaks: ~IDR 50,000 one way; ~IDR 100,000 round trip; ~IDR 300,000-400,000 half-day charter with wait
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) before Bali day-trippers arrive on 9-10 AM fast boats; mid-tide morning windows are gold. Second session at late afternoon high-tide if winds stay offshore
practical_info.best_time_of_day: 5:30-9 AM before Bali day-tripper crowd; 4-6 PM late afternoon when day-trippers have gone back to Sanur
practical_info.nearby_warung: Plenty along Jungut Batu Beach: Bali Eco Deli, Tigerlillys, The Deck Cafe, Pondok Baruna, Bali Belly Cafe, Sandy Bay Beach Club (south side), numerous beachfront warungs serving Indonesian and Western food and post-surf smoothies
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: On-island alternates by short boat: Razors (left), Ceningan Reef, No Mans Land; on a flat day, take fast boat back to Bali for Serangan or stay and enjoy snorkelling/freediving at Manta Point
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Yes - the island itself is reached by boat from Bali, and the breaks are reached by short paddle or local outrigger from Jungut Batu Beach. Some pros and surf-camp groups join multi-day boat charters covering Lembongan + Lombok + Sumbawa

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp shallow coral reef on all three breaks (Lacerations and Shipwrecks especially); strong tidal currents (especially on lunar cycles and big swells); long paddle if you lose your boat ride; reef wire/scrap from former seaweed farming at Shipwrecks; limited medical facilities on island
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sharp coral, sea urchins in reef holes, occasional jellyfish, rare sea snakes; clean clear water means visible reef but also visible coral. Bring antiseptic, vinegar and reef-cut first-aid kit
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Very High at Lacerations (named for the sharp reef - bring bandages); High at Shipwrecks; Moderate at Playgrounds (deeper water but still coral). Booties strongly recommended at all three
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Shipwrecks has a very strong rip that constantly pulls surfers across the reef toward Lacerations - useful as a paddle channel but dangerous if you get caught. Tidal currents between Lembongan and Ceningan can be very strong
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Resident surfers (expats and locals) dominate prime sets at Shipwrecks and Lacerations; observe priority, do not snake, wait your turn. Friendly vibe at Playgrounds where surf-school groups operate; communicate verbally when sharing peaks
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low to Medium - generally welcoming, smaller localism than mainland Bukit but resident charger crew expects respect; visiting pros and surf-charter clients can make crowded days tense
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Smile, say hi to boat captains and locals, do not drop in, give priority to riders deeper, do not paddle out at Lacerations unless you genuinely have the level. Tip local boat captains and reward warung loyalty
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Several resident Indonesian/Balinese chargers; international resident surfers and surf-camp owners; specific famous resident-legend names not consistently documented [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Sakenan and other Hindu temples on the island; respect ceremonies (especially Galungan, Kuningan, and odalan ceremonies). Nyepi (Day of Silence) - no surfing or sea access islandwide. Lembongan has strong Balinese Hindu and traditional seaweed-farming culture
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Limited on-island - Lembongan Clinic and Nusa Medika Clinic (Jungut Batu) for basic care; serious injuries require fast boat back to Sanur and then Siloam Hospital Denpasar or BIMC Nusa Dua
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: On-island clinics ~1-2 km from Jungut Batu; nearest hospital (Siloam Denpasar) ~30-45 min by fast boat + car
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No permanent rescue jet ski; local boat captains and surf-shuttle boats serve as informal rescue support [uncertain]

services.nearest_surf_school: We Surf Lembongan, Monkey Surfing Lembongan, Surf School Lembongan, Lembongan Surf School, Kala Surf Camp (also runs trips from Bali); most offer beginner lessons at Playgrounds
services.board_rental_availability: Widely available at Jungut Batu Beach (Song Lambung Beach Huts, multiple surf shops); ~IDR 100,000-200,000/day; some hotels rent boards directly
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - boat-based and pontoon-based photographers operate during dry season; many surf schools and camps include photo/video services
services.photographer_recommendation: Local Lembongan photographers shoot from boats/pontoons; rates typically IDR 200,000-600,000 per session. Specific name recommendations vary year to year [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Wide range in Jungut Batu and Mushroom Bay: Song Lambung Beach Huts (overlooks Playgrounds, rents boards), Lembongan Summer Villas, Mahagiri Resort (beachfront), Tigerlillys Bungalows, Isla Indah Retreat (yoga + surf), Hai Tide Beach Resort, Lembongan Beach Club & Resort. Surf camps include Kala Surf Camp Lembongan and others
services.webcam_url: https://www.surf-forecast.com/breaks/Shipwrecks ; https://www.surf-forecast.com/breaks/Playgrounds ; Surfline coverage limited
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use over surf zone; avoid flying over Pura Sakenan and resort grounds; commercial use requires permit. Check with boat captains during temple ceremonies [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: The Deck Lembongan, Bali Eco Deli (cafe-cowork), Tigerlillys workspace; smaller scene than Canggu but reliable wifi cafes throughout Jungut Batu

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 40-60 / IDR 600,000-950,000 group lesson at Playgrounds; private lessons IDR 1,000,000-1,800,000. Surf camp packages 5-7 day all-inclusive ~USD 700-1,500

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Excellent - clear turquoise water with high visibility (often 15-25 m); minimal river runoff; some seasonal plastic drift from Bali mainland but generally among the cleanest surf water in the Bali area

events_community.annual_competition_window: Occasional local/regional contests (ISC events) and surf-camp group competitions; no fixed major international event [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Surf-camp weekly group surfs and beach BBQs; full-moon parties at Sandy Bay; informal dawn patrol crews at Playgrounds; Mola Mola (sunfish) sightings July-October draw freedive/surfer crossover community

basic_info.name: Nusa Penida (Chinese: 努沙佩尼達; Indonesian: Nusa Penida)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.7271, 115.5444 (island center; Crystal Bay surf corner approx. -8.7148, 115.4561; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/NusaPenida)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~30 km straight-line + sea crossing from Sanur (35-50 min fast boat); total transfer ~1.5-2 hrs from DPS airport
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-90 min motorbike to Sanur Harbor + 35-50 min fast boat (total ~2-2.5 hrs); From Uluwatu: ~90 min motorbike to Sanur + 35-50 min boat

surf_conditions.wave_type: Novelty/rare reef breaks rather than world-class waves. Main spots: Crystal Bay (small left-hand reef corner at southern end), Gamut Bay (novelty reef wave), Tamarind Point area, and occasional waves at Mushroom Bay-facing reefs. Generally reef breaks but inconsistent.
surf_conditions.skill_level: Advanced (despite small wave sizes, sharp reef, isolation, and lack of rescue services make these spots advanced-only). Not suitable for beginners despite mellow wave faces.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-4 ft on typical days; needs medium-to-large SW groundswell (6 ft+ at outer reefs) to wake up novelty spots [uncertain]
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp shallow coral reef
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-August) when SW groundswells funnel through the Lombok Strait; needs sizable swell to activate Penida reefs which are sheltered relative to Lembongan/Ceningan
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide for safer reef coverage; low tide exposes shallow reef and increases hazard
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE (Southeast) trade winds typical in dry season; same wind pattern as Bukit peninsula
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW to SSW groundswell; needs solid long-period swell to wrap around the island
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 1-2 (very inconsistent; Penida is largely sheltered from open ocean swell; novelty wave status confirmed by multiple guides) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 20-60 meters typical short rides; Crystal Bay corner produces short straight-line waves to the beach [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Slow to medium (most Penida waves are mellow when they break; not the fast hollow waves of nearby Lembongan) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Low to moderate take-off difficulty itself, but the bigger hazard is the shallow sharp reef and isolation from help
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - several scattered novelty reef setups around the island (Crystal Bay south corner, Gamut Bay, Tamarind Point reef, Mushroom Bay reef channels); no defined main peak/inside/outside structure [uncertain]
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 1 (very low crowds; rarely surfed; you may have it to yourself or share with a couple of local kids on rare working days)

practical_info.access: Boat access required from Sanur Harbor (fast boat 35-50 min, IDR 150,000-250,000 one-way) or private charter; then land transfer (scooter rental IDR 75,000-100,000/day or car charter IDR 600,000-800,000/day) to specific bay. Crystal Bay accessible by paved road from Toya Pakeh; Gamut Bay and remote reefs require 4x4 or boat charter.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Paddle from beach in Crystal Bay corner; for outer reefs (Gamut Bay), boat drop-off and paddle into lineup; channel paddles where available. Local fisherman boats can be hired for outer-reef access.
practical_info.entry_fee: Nusa Penida tourist retribution ~IDR 25,000-50,000 per person; Crystal Bay parking ~IDR 5,000-10,000; no surf-specific fee [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) before trade winds build and tourist boats arrive at Crystal Bay (Crystal Bay gets very crowded with snorkelers from 10 AM)
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Early morning (before 9 AM) to avoid Crystal Bay tourist boats and onshore wind; check tide window carefully due to shallow reef
practical_info.nearby_warung: Crystal Bay has several warungs and beach cafes; remote reefs have no facilities - bring water, snacks, first-aid. Toya Pakeh harbor area has more options.
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Nusa Lembongan reef breaks (Shipwrecks, Playgrounds, Lacerations, Razors) accessible by short boat from Toya Pakeh / Nusa Penida north coast - much more consistent and rideable. Mahana Point on Nusa Ceningan also nearby by boat.
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Yes for outer reefs and serious surf trips; Crystal Bay corner can be paddled from beach but most quality waves around Penida require boat access from Sanur or local fisherman charter.

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp shallow coral reef, powerful and sudden currents (the channel between Bali and Penida and around the island has notorious currents from Lombok Strait), isolation from rescue services, lack of experienced surfers/locals to assist, occasional boat traffic from snorkel/dive operations, big drop-offs near cliffs
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (wear booties), occasional jellyfish, rare sea snakes; sharks occasionally seen but no incidents documented at surf zones; manta rays present at Manta Point (not a surf zone) - non-aggressive but startling. Reef cuts can become infected fast - treat with antiseptic immediately.
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: High - sharp coral/limestone reef; booties strongly recommended; reef is exposed at low tide. Combined with remoteness this elevates risk over similarly-sharp Bukit reefs.
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong tidal currents flow through the Badung Strait and around Nusa Penida headlands; rips can pull surfers away from shore and into open channel water. Currents can switch direction with tide changes. Crystal Bay has channel current toward the south end.
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Rarely any lineup to speak of; if local kids or fishermen are surfing, smile, say hi, and respect their priority. Low key culture - no aggressive localism due to lack of crowds.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low - rarely crowded; few resident surfers; visitors mostly day-trippers from Lembongan/Bali
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Respect Crystal Bay as a temple-adjacent (Pura Puncak Mundi nearby on island) and tourist-popular site; avoid surfing through snorkeling tour boats; ask local fishermen before launching from their beach; standard Balinese respect to locals; do not litter on remote beaches.
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: No widely-documented resident surf legends on Nusa Penida itself; most Nusa Islands surf talent is based on Lembongan (e.g., the Hartanto family / We Surf Lembongan crew) [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Nusa Penida is considered spiritually powerful in Balinese tradition (home of Ratu Gede Mecaling and Pura Puncak Mundi, Pura Goa Giri Putri, Pura Dalem Ped); avoid wearing swimsuits at temples, dress modestly when passing temple grounds; Nyepi (Day of Silence, ~March) - no boat operations or beach access islandwide.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Klinik Pratama Nusa Medika Penida (Sampalan, ~30 min from Crystal Bay); Klinik 24 Jam Puskesmas Nusa Penida; serious cases require boat evacuation to Sanur/Denpasar (BIMC, Siloam Denpasar). Limited 24-hour care on island.
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Klinik/Puskesmas Nusa Penida: ~15-20 km from Crystal Bay; Sampalan clinic ~25 km
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; dive operators may assist in emergencies but no formal water rescue service
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Ambulance: 118/119; Coast guard Bali: +62 361 754845; nearest hospital evacuation requires boat back to Sanur

services.nearest_surf_school: No surf schools based on Nusa Penida; closest options are We Surf Lembongan and Monkey Activities on Nusa Lembongan (short boat from Toya Pakeh). [uncertain]
services.board_rental_availability: Very limited or none on Nusa Penida itself; bring your own board via fast boat (some fast boats charge IDR 50,000-100,000 board fee) or rent on Lembongan and boat-transfer [uncertain]
services.surf_photographer_available: No resident surf photographers; bring your own or arrange via Lembongan-based shooters [uncertain]
services.photographer_recommendation: No specific Penida-based recommendations; Lembongan photographers can be arranged on request [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Adiwana Warnakali, Penida Bambu Green, Semabu Hills Hotel, Adiwana Cliffside Diving Resort, Coco Resort Penida, plus numerous homestays and bungalows in Toya Pakeh, Ped, and Sampalan; for surf access, stay near Toya Pakeh or Crystal Bay area
services.webcam_url: No dedicated Nusa Penida surf cam; Surfline / MagicSeaweed forecast pages for region; Lembongan webcams nearest reference [uncertain]
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use at non-temple sites; restrictions near temples and military areas; Indonesian commercial drone permit required for commercial work [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Penida Colada (cafe with WiFi), Amok Sunset Penida, Penida Co-living/Kerthi Bali Sanctuary; coworking infrastructure is far less developed than Bali mainland or Lembongan

pricing.avg_lesson_price: No structured surf lesson market on Nusa Penida; transfer to Lembongan for lessons (USD 35-50 / IDR 500,000-800,000 group lesson on Lembongan) [uncertain]
pricing.board_rental_daily: Very limited; expect IDR 200,000-300,000/day if available via homestay or by importing from Lembongan [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Generally good - Penida has clear water (manta rays, vibrant reefs) due to deep-water currents; some concerns at Crystal Bay during peak tourist hours (sunscreen, boat fuel); wet season runoff less pronounced than Bali mainland

events_community.annual_competition_window: No annual surf competitions on Nusa Penida; events focus on diving (manta ray) and triathlon (Nusa Penida Half Marathon / Ocean Festival) [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Nusa Penida Festival (cultural festival), Sasih Kapat ceremonies at Pura Dalem Ped, Galungan/Kuningan; surf community sparse [uncertain]

Nyang_Nyang.name: Nyang Nyang / Nyang-Nyang / Pantai Nyang Nyang
Nyang_Nyang.distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 20-22 km (about 45 minutes by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport)
Nyang_Nyang.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-90 min by scooter; From Uluwatu (Suluban/Single Fin): ~15-20 min by scooter
Nyang_Nyang.wave_type: Right-hander reef break over coral; short, punchy walls with occasional barrel on take-off; channel paddle out at higher tides
Nyang_Nyang.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (not suitable for beginners due to coral reef, currents and remoteness)
Nyang_Nyang.wave_height_range: 1-3 ft on small swells (waist to head high); can hold up to 1.5x overhead but tends to close out in bigger swells. Known to break ~1 ft bigger than Uluwatu on a south swell
Nyang_Nyang.best_season: Works on both seasons but considered a small/clean-swell spot; wet season (Oct-Apr / Nov-Apr) for bigger NW-wind-protected swells with light early winds; dry season mornings can also work when Uluwatu is too small. Best 'go-to' when other Bukit spots are flat or too small
Nyang_Nyang.best_tide: Mid to high tide (high tide essential to cover the reef); low tide exposes the reef and is unsafe
Nyang_Nyang.best_wind: Light offshore winds; N to NE winds preferred (it is sheltered from the typical SE trade winds that blow out the other Bukit spots) [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (works ~150+ surfable days per year but very tide and size dependent; only really firing on smaller clean swells)
Nyang_Nyang.wave_length_meters: approx. 50-150 m (164-492 ft cited by guides)
Nyang_Nyang.wave_speed_rating: medium to fast (punchy, short walls; can be slabby/closeout in bigger swells)
Nyang_Nyang.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high difficulty - shifty peaks, occasional barrel on take-off, freak sets from the open southern ocean; shallow reef at lower tides
Nyang_Nyang.secondary_peaks: Main right-hand peak with a channel; some inside reform on smaller days; Nunggalan Beach reef sits to the east as a separate/alternate peak [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.peak_crowd_level: 2-3/5 (crowd factor cited 7/10 by some guides on good days, but usually uncrowded due to long descent; gets busiest when Uluwatu is too small)
Nyang_Nyang.access: Long steep descent of approx. 510-600 stairs / 20-30 min hike down the cliffs OR drive the steep paved Jalan Pantai Nyang-Nyang road by scooter/car to the beach. Two entrances: West (newer, easier scooter access) and East via Nunggalan (steeper)
Nyang_Nyang.paddle_out_method: Paddle out via the channel on the right (north) side of the reef at higher tides; do not paddle over the reef at low tide
Nyang_Nyang.entry_fee: Parking ~IDR 5,000 (motorbike) / ~IDR 10,000-20,000 (car); beach entry effectively free though some warungs/operators may ask for a small donation [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol / early morning during rising tide to high tide; first light to mid-morning before onshore winds pick up
Nyang_Nyang.best_time_of_day: Before 9 am to avoid wind and crowds; sunset sessions also possible on glassy evenings
Nyang_Nyang.nearby_warung: Nyang Nyang Warung at the cliff top (Indonesian + Western); Loca Warung up on the main road; a handful of small warungs and coconut stalls on the beach itself; very limited compared to Uluwatu
Nyang_Nyang.alternative_spot_within_10min: Uluwatu, Padang Padang and Impossibles (~10-15 min scooter); Nunggalan reef next door; Green Bowl further south
Nyang_Nyang.hazards: Strong currents, freak/rogue sets from open southern ocean, exposed coral reef, urchins, occasional tiger shark sightings reported, no lifeguards, very remote with limited rescue access, steep unlit trail after dark, monkeys on the trail
Nyang_Nyang.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the reef, occasional jellyfish, rare tiger shark sightings reported by locals (treat as low probability but real); standard coral cuts - flush with fresh water, disinfect, see clinic if deep
Nyang_Nyang.reef_cut_severity_index: High - live coral reef, sharp and shallow at lower tides; reef boots advisable on bigger or low-tide sessions
Nyang_Nyang.rip_current_pattern: Channel rip on the north/right side of the reef pulls outside (used as paddle channel); strong sweep along the reef during bigger swells and outgoing tides [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.lineup_etiquette: Standard Bukit reef etiquette - respect priority on the inside, do not snake locals, smile/say hi, wait your turn
Nyang_Nyang.localism_intensity: Low to medium (cited 3/10) - not a heavily localised peak, but a small core of regulars; respect goes a long way
Nyang_Nyang.respect_protocol: Greet locals, do not paddle around for sets, no shouting/dropping in, leave no trash, support local warungs/parking attendants
Nyang_Nyang.resident_local_legends: No single iconic local legend tied specifically to Nyang Nyang in the same way Mega Semadhi is to Padang; broader Bukit/Pecatu local crew surfs here [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.religious_sensitivity: Bukit cliffs near Uluwatu Temple area - dress modestly when off the beach; respect Nyepi (silent day) when no surfing/movement is allowed island-wide; menstruating women traditionally asked to avoid temple grounds
Nyang_Nyang.medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu is the closest 24h clinic; BIMC Nusa Dua and Siloam Hospital Denpasar for serious injuries
Nyang_Nyang.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu approx. 6-8 km; BIMC Nusa Dua approx. 12-15 km [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue on standby at this spot
Nyang_Nyang.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Bali Search and Rescue (BASARNAS) 115; Unicare Clinic Pecatu +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.nearest_surf_school: No surf school operates directly at Nyang Nyang (too advanced/remote). Pecatu/Uluwatu area schools such as Padang Padang Surf Camp, Rapture Surfcamps Padang Padang and Uluwatu Surf Villas handle lessons in the broader area
Nyang_Nyang.board_rental_availability: No on-beach board rental at Nyang Nyang itself; rent in Uluwatu / Padang Padang / Pecatu before driving down
Nyang_Nyang.surf_photographer_available: No regular land/water photographer stationed at the lineup (unlike Uluwatu/Padang)
Nyang_Nyang.photographer_recommendation: Freelance Bukit-based water photographers can be hired by appointment via Instagram (e.g. local Uluwatu photographers); rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.accommodation_options: Six Senses Uluwatu (luxury, nearby), The Bubble Hotel Nyang Nyang (unique transparent domes on the beach), Cari Surf Camp Pecatu, plus numerous Pecatu/Uluwatu villas and homestays within 5-10 min drive
Nyang_Nyang.drone_allowed: Generally allowed - the wide empty beach is one of Bali's most droned locations; respect privacy of other beachgoers and avoid flying over temple grounds [uncertain]
Nyang_Nyang.coworking_nearby: No coworking on the beach; closest options are Tropical Nomad, Outpost or B Work in Canggu (~1+ hr away); some cafe-coworking in Bingin/Uluwatu
Nyang_Nyang.water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season due to remoteness and low development; can carry runoff sediment in heavy wet-season rain; rated higher than Kuta/Seminyak [uncertain]

basic_info.name: Padang Padang (Chinese: 帕當帕當; Indonesian: Pantai Padang Padang; nickname: Balinese Pipeline)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.8104, 115.1031 (Jl. Labuan Sait, Pecatu; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/PadangPadang)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~20 km (approx. 45-55 min drive)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~60-70 min by motorbike (~30 km); From Uluwatu (Suluban): ~5-10 min by motorbike

surf_conditions.wave_type: Left-hand reef break, world-class barrel/tube wave (nicknamed 'Balinese Pipeline'). Two distinct breaks: Padang Padang Lefts (advanced) and Baby Padang/Padang Padang Rights (beginner-intermediate)
surf_conditions.skill_level: Padang Padang Lefts: Advanced to Pro only (head-high to double-overhead+ at high tide possible for strong intermediates). Baby Padang (Rights): Beginner to Intermediate.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Lefts: 4-12+ ft (works best 6-12 ft; needs solid swell to break); Rights/Baby Padang: 1-4 ft
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp shallow coral/limestone reef
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-August); needs a substantial SW groundswell to wake up - not as consistent as Uluwatu
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid tide is best for consistency; low tide produces biggest top-to-bottom barrels (but extremely dangerous, dry-reef risk); high tide is safer for intermediates with head-high swell
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE (Southeast) trade winds; light offshores essential for clean barrels
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SSW to SW (long-period groundswell critical; short-period swell does not produce the tube)
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 3 (less consistent than Uluwatu - needs a proper swell event; can be flat between pulses but fires hard when on)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 75-150 meters (relatively short but extremely intense tube ride; 2-4 distinct barrel sections before deep-water channel) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Fast (one of the fastest, most hollow waves in Bali; barrel pinches and throws over shallow reef)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High - take-off itself is approachable on a deeper part of the reef, but the drop is fast and directly into the pit; positioning is everything; locals dominate the peak
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - Baby Padang (Padang Padang Rights) is a separate, much mellower beginner-intermediate right adjacent in the same bay
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (extremely crowded when on; talent-stacked lineup of Indonesia's top tube riders plus visiting pros; only the heaviest days thin the crowd)

practical_info.access: Park at the entrance on Jl. Labuan Sait near the bridge; walk through the gateway and pay entry fee; descend stone/cement stairs through a narrow limestone cave passage between large rocks down to the beach
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Paddle from the beach out through the rip in the channel on the south end of the bay; use the rip to your advantage to get out to the lineup; avoid paddling over the impact zone reef
practical_info.entry_fee: IDR 15,000 per person entry fee (separate from parking IDR 5,000-10,000 motorbike)
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy and less crowded conditions; mid-tide windows in early morning are gold
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~9 AM before crowds and onshore winds build; late afternoon as winds calm
practical_info.nearby_warung: Several warungs at the entry/parking area along Jl. Labuan Sait and at the beach; nearby Single Fin (Uluwatu), Drifter Surf Cafe, Mango Tree Cafe, Suka Espresso in Pecatu
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Uluwatu (5-10 min), Impossibles (5 min walking from Padang along reef at low tide), Bingin (10 min), Balangan (15 min)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - land access via stairs through cave

safety_culture.hazards: Extremely shallow sharp reef especially at low tide, intense hold-downs, fast pinching barrel section, dry-reef risk on big low-tide days, strong rip currents, crowd-related drop-in collisions
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef, occasional jellyfish; treat reef cuts immediately - infection risk high in tropical water; rare sea snake sightings
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Very High - one of the most severe reef cut risks in Bali; multiple barrel sections fire over shallow shelf; booties strongly recommended; serious reef grating possible on wipeouts
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong rip runs through the channel on the south side of the bay (used as paddle-out lane); side current can drag surfers toward the inside reef section at low tide
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Strict priority; locals and known regulars get priority; do not drop in; do not snake. Wait for sets and your turn. Burning a local is a serious offense here.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: High - one of the more heavily localized spots on the Bukit; talent-stacked lineup of pro and semi-pro Indonesians; visitors expected to demonstrate respect and skill before being given waves
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Show respect to local Balinese surfers, do not snake or burn, acknowledge priority, observe the lineup before paddling out, do not paddle out if you cannot handle the wave - injury and harassment are likely
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Mega Semadhi (2016 Rip Curl Cup champion, Bukit local), Made Bol Adi Putra, Rizal Tandjung, Pepen Hendrik, Betet Merta, Garut Widiarta, Westen Hirst (2025 Rip Curl Cup champion - first Indonesian man since Mega in 2016)
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Padang Padang temple sits at the entry gate; respect sacred space, no inappropriate behavior on stairs/beach. Nyepi (Day of Silence, ~March) - no surfing or beach access islandwide. Avoid surfing during temple ceremonies.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Unicare Pecatu Clinic (closest), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (~25-30 min), Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~45 min)
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu Clinic: ~3-5 km
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Yes - during Rip Curl Cup event period (August); no permanent rescue jet ski outside of contest window

services.nearest_surf_school: Padang Padang Surf Camp (oldest and best-known in area, Jl. Labuan Sait, Pecatu); WaveHouse Bali, Kala Surf Camp, Dawn Patrol Bali, Bali Bisa - most schools teach beginners at Baby Padang (Rights) not Lefts
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - widely available on Jl. Labuan Sait and at the beach entrance; ~IDR 150,000-250,000/day
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - multiple land photographers regularly shoot Padang Padang barrels from the beach and cliff; water photographers during big swells and during Rip Curl Cup
services.photographer_recommendation: Padang Padang Surf Camp photography team; freelance shooters available at the beach. Photo packages typically IDR 200,000-500,000+ per session [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Padang Padang Surf Camp (Jl. Labuan Sait), Padang Padang Yoga Surf Bungalow Villa, The Edge Bali (luxury cliffside), Suarga Padang Padang Resort, numerous guesthouses on Jl. Labuan Sait, Kala Surf Camp short drive away in Pecatu
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/padang-padang/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d44 ; https://balicams.com/camera/padang-padang
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use but heavy restrictions during Rip Curl Cup (only accredited media). Avoid flying over temple. Commercial use requires permit. [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu Pecatu, B Work Bali (Pecatu), Suka Espresso, Bukit Cafe

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 25-50 / IDR 400,000-800,000 group lesson (taught at Baby Padang/Padang Padang Rights for beginners, not the Lefts)

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season; declines in wet season (Nov-April) due to runoff. Beach itself can get litter on crowded days but reasonably clean compared to north Bali.

events_community.annual_competition_window: Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang - 22+ year history; one-month holding period typically August 1 to early September each year, contest day called only on epic 6-8 ft+ swell. 2025 winners: Westen Hirst (men, age 16) and Jasmine Studer (women, age 16).
events_community.community_events: Rip Curl Cup opening ceremony with Kecak Dance and live music (early August); pre-contest invitee gatherings; viewing parties at Single Fin (Uluwatu); occasional Volcom, Rip Curl, and local brand events

Pererenan.name: Pererenan (峇里島 - 佩雷雷南 / Pantai Pererenan)
Pererenan.distance_from_dps_airport_km: 24 km (approx. 75-95 min by car depending on traffic)
Pererenan.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): 8-12 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: 80-105 min by scooter
Pererenan.wave_type: Mixed beach and reef break - A-frame peak with both left and right; left is longer with two or three turns then closes on inside reef shelf, right is shorter, steeper and more intense; some Rivermouth-style section just north
Pererenan.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (Pererenan main peak); high-level intermediates comfortable with shallow bottoms
Pererenan.wave_height_range: 2-8 ft (best head-high to slightly overhead; closes out over 1.5x overhead)
Pererenan.bottom_type: Mixed - sand on the outside section/shore break; volcanic lava rock and reef on the inside, especially on the left
Pererenan.best_season: Dry season May-October prime (consistent SW swell and offshore NE/SE winds). April and November shoulder months. Wet season (Dec-Feb) often onshore and murky.
Pererenan.best_tide: Mid to high tide (mid is sweet spot, high tide best shape over the volcanic rock shelf). Low tide exposes sharp jagged lava reef - avoid unless very confident.
Pererenan.best_wind: Offshore from Northeast / East / Southeast (NE in early morning, switching to SE trade winds); turns onshore by 10-11am
Pererenan.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and WSW groundswells
Pererenan.swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 (receives the same swell as Canggu/Echo; very consistent in dry season)
Pererenan.wave_length_meters: 30-80 m (left often 50-80 m before closing on inside reef; right typically 20-40 m) [uncertain]
Pererenan.wave_speed_rating: Medium-fast (left is more relaxed, right is intense and punchy; rewards shortboards and good rail surfing)
Pererenan.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to Difficult - steep but readable A-frame takeoff; sand outside is forgiving, but the inside left ends over a shallow sharp rock shelf so you must kick out early
Pererenan.secondary_peaks: Pererenan main peak (A-frame), Pererenan Rivermouth (immediately south toward Echo) which is a longer left with more shape, plus a few inside reform peaks and visible reefs further out; sandbar peaks shift along the beach
Pererenan.peak_crowd_level: 3/5 - generally less crowded than Echo Left or Batu Bolong but can still see 30-40 surfers at peak dawn-patrol on good days; spreads out more along the bay
Pererenan.access: Drive down Jalan Pantai Pererenan / Jl. Pantai Munduk to the dedicated beach parking area right in front of the break; short walk over sand to the lineup
Pererenan.paddle_out_method: Paddle out from the beach through the shore break and into the channel just south of the statue / rivermouth; on bigger days the channel between Pererenan main peak and Rivermouth offers easier paddle out
Pererenan.entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking: ~IDR 2,000-5,000 scooter; IDR 5,000-10,000 car at official Pererenan lots.
Pererenan.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for glassy offshores and lighter crowd; second session at sunset if wind drops.
Pererenan.best_time_of_day: Before 7-8am to avoid both onshore wind and the local + tourist crowd that builds quickly when waves are good.
Pererenan.nearby_warung: Warung Heboh (right in front of the break, great view, prices from IDR 10,000), Warung Alus (BBQ seafood, steaks, burgers), La Brisa nearby in Echo, Pererenan local warungs along Jl. Pantai Pererenan, coconut and snack stalls on the beach
Pererenan.alternative_spot_within_10min: Echo Beach Left (5 min south), Echo Right / Sandbar (5 min south), Batu Bolong (8-10 min south for smaller mellower waves), Seseh (5-10 min north for less crowded alternative), Mengening (10 min north)
Pererenan.hazards: Shallow sharp volcanic lava rock shelf on the inside left; rocks and rips at the rivermouth; tide changes can be violent and unpredictable; rip currents in the channel near the rivermouth; crowd can be heavy at peak times; bacterial water after rain due to nearby river-mouth
Pererenan.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the volcanic rock and reef inside (carry vinegar/tweezers); rare jellyfish in wet season; occasional stingrays in shallows - shuffle feet; rocks rather than coral so marine life is limited; bacterial issues from river runoff in wet season
Pererenan.reef_cut_severity_index: Medium to High - lava rock shelf on the inside left is sharp; reef cuts common on low-tide wipeouts; can be deep enough to require treatment but generally less severe than Padang Padang or Uluwatu
Pererenan.rip_current_pattern: Channel rip running just south of the main peak toward the rivermouth - useful for paddling out but pulls strongly on outgoing tide; side-sweeping current along the inside reef toward the rivermouth on bigger swells
Pererenan.lineup_etiquette: Mellower than Echo Beach Left or Batu Bolong; priority on the peak still expected; wait your turn; don't snake or back-paddle; greet and nod to locals; respect Indonesian regulars
Pererenan.localism_intensity: Low to Medium - Pererenan is amidst a traditional Balinese community with strong spiritual ties; vibe is generally mellow but locals do enforce priority on the peak; aggression is rare if etiquette is followed
Pererenan.respect_protocol: Sit wide and take your turn; don't paddle inside locals; cover up walking through the village (sarong/shirt); respect ceremonies and offerings (canang sari) on the sand; tip warungs and parking attendants modestly
Pererenan.resident_local_legends: Putu Swastika 'Gemuk' (founder of Surfers Surf Lesson at Pererenan), local Balinese surfers from the village shred here daily; international free-surfers often visit. [uncertain]
Pererenan.religious_sensitivity: Pererenan sits in a traditional Balinese village with multiple temples (pura) and active ceremonial life. Respect ceremonies, do not step on offerings (canang sari) on the sand, observe Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - no surfing or beach access; women on menstruation traditionally avoid temple grounds.
Pererenan.medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu, ~10 min), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major trauma), Siloam Hospital Denpasar.
Pererenan.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~4 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~28 km; Siloam Denpasar ~18 km
Pererenan.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski stationed at Pererenan; Balawista lifeguards occasionally cover the area; surf school instructors provide informal rescues
Pererenan.emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
Pererenan.nearest_surf_school: Surfers Surf School (Putu Swastika 'Gemuk' - private school directly at Pererenan), 360 Surf Travel (Pererenan-based surf camp), CARI Surf Camp (in-house photographer), Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu, Mondo Surf Village; many Canggu-based schools also bring students here
Pererenan.board_rental_availability: On-beach and on-road rental available at multiple shops along Jl. Pantai Pererenan; soft-tops, longboards, performance shortboards all available; daily rates similar to Canggu
Pererenan.surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land photographers shoot the Pererenan peak daily during dry season; many surf schools have in-house photographers (CARI, Surfers Surf School)
Pererenan.photographer_recommendation: Surfers Surf School in-house photo/video package; freelance photographers shooting Pererenan via Instagram/QR code; typical price IDR 200,000-400,000 for an edited photo pack [uncertain]
Pererenan.accommodation_options: Pererenan has a wide range from cheap guesthouses to luxury villas, surf camps (360 Surf Travel, Mondo Surf Village, Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu, CARI), boutique villas, and traditional village homestays; calmer and cheaper alternative to central Canggu
Pererenan.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d40 (Surfline Canggu cam covers Pererenan/Echo area); https://balicams.com/camera/rivermouth (River Mouth / Pererenan-adjacent cam) [uncertain]
Pererenan.drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for surf photography (DGCA registration required for >250g). Avoid flying over the village temples, low over crowded lineup, or near ceremonies. Many photographers fly drones here daily.
Pererenan.coworking_nearby: Yes - Outpost Canggu (15 min), Dojo Bali (10-15 min), B Work Bali (Nelayan, 10-12 min), Tropical Nomad; growing number of villa coworking setups in Pererenan itself.
Pererenan.avg_lesson_price: IDR 400,000-700,000 (~USD 26-45 / TWD 800-1,400) per session at local Pererenan surf schools
Pererenan.board_rental_daily: Soft-top / longboard IDR 75,000-100,000/day; performance shortboard IDR 100,000-200,000/day from on-beach and Jl. Pantai Pererenan shops
Pererenan.water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Fair to Good. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to rivermouth and village drainage; ear/skin infection risk. Avoid surfing 24-48h after heavy rain.
Pererenan.annual_competition_window: No major WSL event; occasional local Indonesian Surfing Championship Tour (ISC) regional events and free-surf sessions; informal contests run by local schools [uncertain]
Pererenan.community_events: Beach clean-ups by Trash Hero Canggu and 4Ocean, ceremonial Hindu processions to the sea (Melasti before Nyepi), full moon ceremonies, surf-camp-organized BBQs and yoga sessions

Rote_T_Land.name: Rote / Nemberala (T-Land) / 羅地島 / Pulau Rote - Nemberala (Besialu)
Rote_T_Land.gps_location: Nemberala village, southwest Rote Island, approx. -10.93, 122.83; T-Land reef approx. 500m offshore from Nemberala beach [uncertain exact coords]
Rote_T_Land.wave_type: Long left-hand reef point break over flat coral reef; one of the longest left-handers in Indonesia. Three main named sections - The Peak (or The Point), The Pyramid (Steeple), and The Mountain (Magic Mountain) - plus Inner Tubes section.
Rote_T_Land.skill_level: Intermediate to advanced (accommodates all levels on smaller days; expert on solid swells). One of the most user-friendly world-class waves in Indo.
Rote_T_Land.wave_height_range: 2-15 ft (works from 1ft swell up to triple overhead 15ft). Sweet spot 3-8 ft.
Rote_T_Land.bottom_type: reef (flat coral reef, relatively forgiving compared to other Indo reef breaks but shallow at low tide)
Rote_T_Land.best_season: Dry season May-October; peak May-August with consistent SSW groundswells from the Roaring 40s and offshore ESE trades dawn to dusk.
Rote_T_Land.best_tide: All tides (T-Land's hallmark - works at all tides); caution at very low tide due to exposed coral. Mid to high tide most forgiving for beginners.
Rote_T_Land.best_wind: Offshore E to SE trade winds (blows offshore dawn to dusk in dry season)
Rote_T_Land.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (extremely consistent - surfable waves nearly every day in dry season)
Rote_T_Land.wave_length_meters: Up to 400+ meters on right tide and swell (sources cite 600-1200 ft / ~180-360m on bigger days)
Rote_T_Land.wave_speed_rating: medium (rippable carving walls; not freight-train fast - more playful and open-faced with occasional barrel sections)
Rote_T_Land.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Medium - multiple takeoff zones spread crowd; outer Peak has steeper drops on larger swells, inside sections more mellow.
Rote_T_Land.secondary_peaks: Three distinct sections (The Peak / Pyramid / Mountain) plus Inner Tubes section. Multiple peaks help spread crowds; The Bommie just north of T-Land is a short fast right on low tide / big swell.
Rote_T_Land.peak_crowd_level: 2 (busiest wave on Rote but multiple peaks and 400m+ length spread crowds; very manageable compared to Bali)
Rote_T_Land.access: Bali (DPS) -> Kupang (KOE) ~1.5hr flight on Wings/Citilink/Batik Air, then either: (a) 30-min Wings Air flight KOE->RTI same-day, or (b) Express Bahari fast ferry 2 hrs (daily 9am), or (c) slow ferry to Ba'a (4hr, arriving evening). Then 45-60 min road transfer to Nemberala. Total: 1-2 days door-to-door. Boat charters also reach Rote on extended Indo charter routes.
Rote_T_Land.paddle_out_method: T-Land reef sits ~500m offshore. Standard method is water taxi / outrigger from Nemberala harbour or beach (most surfers choose boat). Self-paddle possible but long, involving wading across shallow inner reef at low tide and paddling deeper sections - allow 20-30 min.
Rote_T_Land.recommended_session_time: All-day surfable in dry season due to consistent offshore winds; dawn patrol best for glass and fewer surfers; late afternoon also classic.
Rote_T_Land.boat_charter_required: Not required for accessing T-Land from Nemberala (water taxi standard, self-paddle possible). Boat charters via Bali->Rote are an alternative full-trip option.
Rote_T_Land.combo_trip_potential: Can be combined with Sumba, Savu, West Timor and broader Nusa Tenggara island-hopping. Specialty Indo charter boats sometimes include Rote in extended routes from Bali.
Rote_T_Land.hazards: Shallow flat coral reef (primary), long paddle if not taking boat, strong currents on inside sections, sun exposure during all-day sessions, remote medical facilities
Rote_T_Land.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on shallow reef, occasional jellyfish, minor reef cuts common at low tide. Sharks rare. Stonefish in reef pools.
Rote_T_Land.lineup_etiquette: Standard wave-priority etiquette; respect locals who get few chances to surf - let them have waves when in lineup. Multiple peaks reduce drop-in conflicts.
Rote_T_Land.localism_intensity: low (Rote locals are notably welcoming and friendly; few local surfers, no aggressive crowd protection culture)
Rote_T_Land.respect_protocol: Smile and greet locals, share waves with local surfers, tip your boat taxi operators, support village warungs and homestays, respect church-going Sunday culture (Rote is predominantly Christian).
Rote_T_Land.medical_nearby: Limited - Nemberala has a small village clinic (Puskesmas); Ba'a (~1hr drive) has the main hospital on Rote; serious injuries require medevac back to Kupang or Bali (BIMC/Siloam)
Rote_T_Land.rescue_jet_ski_available: No standing jet ski rescue service. Boat taxis on lineup provide some support; resorts may have boat backup [uncertain]
Rote_T_Land.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; resort/homestay typically liaises with local clinic and Kupang air transfer; insurance medevac line essential [uncertain specific contacts]
Rote_T_Land.accommodation_options: T-Land Resort (premium, in front of main peak, max 16 guests), Nemberala Beach Resort, Villa Santai, Rote Surf Resort (Lualemba Bungalows), Boa Vida Rote, Wave Haven Rote, Manduna Resort, Utopia Rote Lodge, Seed Resort, 81 Palms. Range from budget homestays to upscale resorts (~USD 50-300/night).
Rote_T_Land.webcam_url: No reliable public live webcam confirmed for T-Land; Surfline/Surfnerd forecast pages available [uncertain]
Rote_T_Land.annual_competition_window: No major WSL CT event; small regional/charity surf events occasionally held; Nemberala has hosted local invitationals [uncertain specifics]
Rote_T_Land.community_events: No fixed weekly community surf events; resort-hosted clinics and informal gatherings during dry season [uncertain]

Sanur.name: Sanur / Sanur Reef / Pantai Sanur (incl. Hyatt Reef, Tandjung Lefts, Sindhu)
Sanur.gps_location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/?q=-8.6905,115.2620 (approx. -8.6905, 115.2620, Sanur Reef ~1 km offshore in front of Grand Inna Bali Beach Hotel / Fairmont Sanur Beach, Denpasar) [uncertain]
Sanur.distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 14-16 km (about 25-35 min by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport)
Sanur.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~45-60 min by scooter (~20-25 km via Sunset Road/bypass); From Uluwatu: ~45-60 min by scooter (~25-30 km) [uncertain]
Sanur.wave_type: Right-hand reef break ~1 km offshore (Bali's longest paddle); fast, hollow barrels on main South peak with steep powerful take-off; the wider Sanur reef system actually has 4 named peaks - Sanur (Sanur Reef/Bomby), Hyatt Reef, Tandjung Sari (Sindhu Right + Sindhu Left), and Tandjung Lefts
Sanur.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (Sanur Reef main peak is advanced/pro on bigger days; Hyatt Reef intermediate; Sindhu Right gentler intermediate-friendly)
Sanur.wave_height_range: Works best 4-6 ft; sweet spot when Nusa Dua/Keramas are pumping; can hold up to 1.5x overhead; minimum size of around 3-4 ft to break properly
Sanur.bottom_type: Coral reef (shallow live reef ~1 km out; flat lagoon reef shoreward)
Sanur.best_season: Wet season (November-April; some sources say October-April); peak is January-March when SW/S swells bend through the Bali-Lombok gap and W/SW winds are offshore on the east coast
Sanur.best_tide: Mid tide is the sweet spot; rising mid-to-high tide preferred; low tide too shallow and fast over the reef; high tide can fatten it out
Sanur.best_wind: Offshore W to WSW wind (wet season pattern); NW also works for some peaks; dry season SE trades blow onshore here
Sanur.best_swell_direction: S to SSW (Indian Ocean groundswell bending through the Bali-Lombok gap); SE works for some peaks
Sanur.swell_consistency_rating: 2-3/5 (sheltered position means it only fires on solid S/SW swells; clean surfable days only ~22% of the time even in January; demands rare alignment of big swell + W wind)
Sanur.wave_length_meters: approx. 100-250 m for a good Sanur Reef right; Hyatt Reef can run 150-300 m linking the outer to inside section [uncertain]
Sanur.wave_speed_rating: fast (Sanur Reef main peak is fast and hollow; outer Hyatt steep and hollow, inside softer)
Sanur.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High at Sanur Reef main peak (steep, powerful drop into shallow reef); moderate at Hyatt Outer; easier at Sindhu Right/inside reform
Sanur.secondary_peaks: Sanur reef system has 4 named peaks: (1) Sanur Reef/Bomby (main right, advanced), (2) Hyatt Reef (long right, 3 sections - Outer/Main/Inside), (3) Tandjung Sari (Sindhu Right + Sindhu Left), (4) Tandjung Lefts; ~4 km of reef offering options on bigger days
Sanur.peak_crowd_level: 3-4/5 on firing wet-season days at Sanur Reef main peak (gets busy with local rippers and visiting Aussies); other peaks (Sindhu, Tandjung Lefts) less crowded; 1-2/5 on average days
Sanur.access: Drive to Sanur (via Sunset Road / Bypass Ngurah Rai); access points: Pantai Mertasari for boat charters to Sanur Reef, Fairmont Sanur Beach Resort beach for main paddle channel, Hyatt Regency Bali beachfront for north peaks, Sindhu beach path for inside reforms; coastal walking path (Sanur boardwalk) links all access points
Sanur.paddle_out_method: Sanur Reef is over 1 km offshore - the longest paddle in Bali; safer to hire a local boat (jukung) from Pantai Mertasari (negotiate boatman to wait in channel as safety net, approx. IDR 100,000); alternative arm-burning paddle through the boat channel and around the reef from the north; Hyatt Reef accessed by boat from Fairmont channel or long paddle through reef sections near Hyatt Regency
Sanur.entry_fee: No formal entry fee for the beach; boat charter to Sanur Reef ~IDR 100,000-200,000 one-way / ~IDR 250,000-400,000 return with wait; scooter parking ~IDR 5,000 [uncertain]
Sanur.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (sunrise to ~9 am) when wet-season W winds are lightest offshore and crowd is thinnest; mid-tide rising window often falls early morning in peak season
Sanur.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to mid-morning before the wind switches and crowd arrives; afternoon often gets onshore unless a strong W wind is locked in
Sanur.nearby_warung: Massive food scene along Jalan Danau Tamblingan and Sanur Boardwalk - Warung Mak Beng (legendary fish soup), Cafe Batu Jimbar, Sanur Beach Market, Soul on the Beach, plenty of beachfront warungs and cafes from Mertasari to Matahari Terbit
Sanur.alternative_spot_within_10min: Hyatt Reef, Sindhu Right/Left, Tandjung Lefts (all within the same reef system); slightly further: Serangan reefs (~15-20 min south), Keramas (~30-40 min north on bigger days), Nusa Dua reefs (~25 min south)
Sanur.boat_charter_required: Recommended for Sanur Reef and Hyatt Reef (1 km+ paddle is exhausting and risky in current); not required for Sindhu Right or inside reforms which are accessible by short paddle from the beach
Sanur.hazards: Shallow coral reef at low tide (sharp), strong cross-shore current in the channel, long paddle / distance from shore, boat traffic from jukung fishermen and tourist boats, occasional jellyfish in wet season, runoff from Denpasar storm drains after heavy rain
Sanur.marine_hazards: Live coral reef cuts (most common injury), sea urchins on inside reef, occasional box jellyfish and stingers in wet season, possible stonefish on inside lagoon reef; treat coral cuts with fresh-water flush, vinegar/antiseptic, see clinic if deep [uncertain]
Sanur.reef_cut_severity_index: High - live shallow coral reef at low tide; reef boots recommended especially for paddle in/out and inside lagoon crossings
Sanur.rip_current_pattern: Strong cross-current sweeps along the reef system, especially through the boat channel and between peaks; outgoing tide intensifies the pull out to sea; this is also used as the paddle channel [uncertain]
Sanur.lineup_etiquette: Respect the local lineup hierarchy at the main Sanur Reef peak; local rippers are wave-hungry and possessive when conditions are good; greet locals, do not snake or paddle around, wait your turn, no shouting; smaller peaks (Sindhu, Tandjung) more relaxed
Sanur.localism_intensity: Medium to high at Sanur Reef main peak (described as 'very wave hungry and possessive' especially on small days); medium at Hyatt; lower at Sindhu Right and outer Hyatt sections
Sanur.respect_protocol: Acknowledge locals on the paddle out, do not paddle inside on a local's set, share waves on smaller days, do not bring more than 2-3 friends to a peak, support local warungs and jukung boatmen
Sanur.resident_local_legends: Sanur has produced numerous Indonesian pro surfers; the Hendrawan family and other Sanur-based competitors are local regulars; broader Sanur Surf Club scene [uncertain]
Sanur.religious_sensitivity: Sanur is a traditional Hindu town - respect ceremonial processions on the beach (Pura Segara temple), Melasti rituals, and offerings (canang sari) on the sand; observe Nyepi (silent day) when no surfing/movement is allowed island-wide; menstruating women traditionally asked to avoid temple grounds
Sanur.medical_nearby: Sanur has multiple clinics including BIMC Hospital Sanur (international, 24h), Bali Royal Hospital BROS (Sanur), Klinik Pratama Sanur Husada, Prima Medika; for severe trauma RSUP Prof. Ngoerah (Sanglah) Denpasar (~5-7 km)
Sanur.nearest_clinic_distance_km: BIMC Sanur Hospital approx. 2-3 km from the beach; BROS Sanur approx. 1-2 km; Sanglah Hospital approx. 5-7 km [uncertain]
Sanur.rescue_jet_ski_available: No regular jet-ski rescue on standby for the Sanur reefs; boatmen/jukung in the channel provide informal safety; lifeguards patrol the shoreline beaches only [uncertain]
Sanur.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; BASARNAS 115; BIMC Sanur Hospital +62 361 3000 911; BROS Sanur +62 361 247 499 [uncertain]
Sanur.nearest_surf_school: Several schools in Sanur including Surfing Bali Sanur, Bali Surf School Sanur, Aloha Sanur Surf School; lessons taught on the inside reform / Sindhu Right area not on the main Sanur Reef peak
Sanur.board_rental_availability: Yes - numerous rental stalls along the Sanur beachfront (Pantai Sanur, Mertasari, Matahari Terbit); soft-tops, longboards and shortboards available; approx. IDR 100,000-200,000/day
Sanur.surf_photographer_available: Limited - some Sanur-based freelance photographers shoot Sanur Reef when it fires; less established than Uluwatu/Keramas; mostly drone shots from accommodation
Sanur.photographer_recommendation: Search Instagram for Sanur-based water photographers; rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session; ask at surf schools for referrals [uncertain]
Sanur.accommodation_options: Wide range: Fairmont Sanur Beach Bali (luxury, walking distance to main paddle), Hyatt Regency Bali (luxury), Grand Inna Bali Beach (heritage), Maya Sanur Resort, Tandjung Sari Hotel, plenty of mid-range hotels (Mercure, Prime Plaza, Holiday Inn) and budget guesthouses inland of Jalan Danau Tamblingan
Sanur.drone_allowed: Generally allowed from the beach for personal use; do not fly over resort grounds without permission, respect the long boardwalk and morning sunrise crowd; standard Indonesian drone rules apply [uncertain]
Sanur.coworking_nearby: Yes - Sanur has a growing digital-nomad scene; coworking options include Genesis Creative Centre, B Work Sanur (limited), Sanur Space, plenty of cafe-coworking (Soul on the Beach, Bali Buda) [uncertain]
Sanur.avg_lesson_price: Group surf lesson approx. IDR 400,000-600,000 (USD 25-40) at Sanur surf schools; private lesson IDR 700,000-1,200,000; tends to be cheaper than Canggu/Uluwatu [uncertain]
Sanur.board_rental_daily: Approx. IDR 100,000-200,000 (USD 7-15) per day for shortboard/longboard at Sanur beachfront rentals; softboards from IDR 75,000 [uncertain]
Sanur.water_quality_rating: Generally fair to good in dry season (sheltered lagoon stays clearer than Kuta/Seminyak); drops in wet season due to Denpasar storm-drain runoff and rivermouth output; ongoing reef restoration projects on the inner reef [uncertain]
Sanur.annual_competition_window: Sanur Reef occasionally hosts national-level Indonesian Surfing Championship Tour (ISC) events and longboard contests in the wet season window (Dec-Mar); not a regular WSL CT venue [uncertain]
Sanur.community_events: Sanur Village Festival annually (cultural, includes water sports demos); regular dawn-patrol crew on the boardwalk; full-moon yoga sessions on the beach; Sanur Surf Club community paddle-outs [uncertain]

basic_info.name: Bali Surf Season Analysis & Safety/Medical (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪季節分析與安全醫療; Indonesian: Analisis Musim Selancar dan Keselamatan Medis Bali) - covering dry-season April-October (west coast/Bukit prime) vs wet-season November-March (east coast working), Nyepi 2026 Day of Silence (Thursday March 19, 2026, 6:00 AM to Friday March 20, 6:00 AM - 24h island shutdown), marine hazards (reef, sea urchin, sea snake, stonefish, jellyfish, rip currents), medical facilities (Unicare Pecatu, BIMC Hospital Kuta/Nusa Dua/Jimbaran, Siloam Denpasar, Penta Medical Canggu), and surf-specific travel insurance (World Nomads, SafetyWing, IMG)
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (seasonal patterns affect entire island; medical infrastructure concentrated in South Bali - Kuta, Nusa Dua, Denpasar, Canggu, Pecatu)
basic_info.gps_location: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (Bukit): Jl. Raya Uluwatu, Pecatu, ~-8.8160, 115.1100; BIMC Hospital Kuta: Jl. Bypass Ngurah Rai No.100X, Kuta, ~-8.7290, 115.1830; BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (Siloam Nusa Dua): Kawasan ITDC Block D, Nusa Dua, ~-8.7960, 115.2280; Siloam Hospital Denpasar: Jl. Sunset Road No.818, Kuta, ~-8.7090, 115.1840; Penta Medical Canggu: Jl. Pantai Berawa, Canggu, ~-8.6610, 115.1410; 911 Medical Clinic Bali (multi-location, Canggu, Sanur, Uluwatu) [GPS uncertain]
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: DPS to BIMC Kuta: ~3 km / 10-15 min; DPS to BIMC Nusa Dua: ~12 km / 25-35 min; DPS to Siloam Denpasar: ~10 km / 25-40 min; DPS to Unicare Pecatu: ~18 km / 40-60 min; DPS to Penta Medical Canggu: ~20 km / 60-90 min; DPS to 911 Medical Canggu: ~20 km / 60-90 min

surf_conditions.wave_type: DRY SEASON (Apr-Oct): SE trade winds make west coast and Bukit fire - left/right reef breaks at Uluwatu, Padang Padang (tube), Bingin, Impossibles, Balangan, Dreamland, Nyang Nyang, Green Bowl + west coast beach breaks Canggu/Echo/Berawa/Pererenan/Seseh/Medewi (left point reef). WET SEASON (Nov-Mar): westerly/NW winds make east coast work - Keramas (right reef, perfect drainer), Sanur (long right reef), Serangan (left/right reef), Nusa Dua (right reef), Green Bowl still works in light winds.
surf_conditions.skill_level: All levels - dry season Bukit reefs are advanced; dry season Canggu beach breaks beginner-intermediate; wet season Keramas/Sanur intermediate-advanced; Kuta/Legian beach break beginner all year
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Year-round Bali wave range 2-12 ft; dry season Bukit avg 4-8 ft with bigger swells 8-15 ft Jun-Aug; wet season east coast typically 2-6 ft; cyclone-generated long-period south swells peak Jun-Sep
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Reef coral predominant on Bukit and east coast; sand bottom Canggu/Kuta/Seminyak/Legian; cobblestone Medewi; mixed reef-sand at some east coast spots
surf_conditions.best_season: DRY SEASON: April-October (peak Jun-Sep) for west coast/Bukit - SE trade winds, south swells, less rain. WET SEASON: November-March for east coast (Keramas/Sanur/Nusa Dua) - NW/W winds. Shoulder months April and October offer crossover - both coasts can work depending on day. Nyepi (around March 19, 2026) is hard pause - 24h island shutdown.
surf_conditions.best_tide: Dry-season Bukit: mid-to-high tide preferred for most reefs (low tide too shallow at Padang, Bingin); wet-season East Coast: mid-to-high tide (Keramas works most tides but best mid-high)
surf_conditions.best_wind: Dry season: SE trade winds offshore for west coast/Bukit; Wet season: NW/W wind offshore for east coast
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SW Southern Ocean swells are dominant in dry season; wet season swells more variable, often shorter period from SW to W
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 - Bali has world-class consistency due to exposure to Southern Ocean / Indian Ocean swell engine year-round; dry season slightly more consistent (Jun-Sep peak), wet season east coast remains 4/5 consistent
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: N/A - varies by spot (see individual spot files)
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Varies by spot
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Varies
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Varies
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 - peak season July-August attracts global surf migration; shoulder seasons (April-May, September-October) 3-4; off-peak (Feb-March wet/Nyepi shoulder) 2-3 but east coast crowded by locals. Nyepi week sees zero crowds (mandatory pause) followed by Ogoh-Ogoh parades the night before.

practical_info.access: Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS) as entry point - closed entirely during Nyepi 24h. Medical facilities accessed by Grab/Gojek/Bluebird or private driver; BIMC offers 24h ambulance pickup; severe trauma cases evacuated to Singapore Mt. Elizabeth or Gleneagles Hospital via medevac (insurance-dependent).
practical_info.paddle_out_method: N/A
practical_info.entry_fee: Medical clinic visits self-pay then claim from insurance (Unicare GP consult ~IDR 350-500K, BIMC ER ~IDR 1.5-3M base + treatment, Siloam similar to BIMC); reef-cut stitch/clean treatment IDR 800K-3M typical; sea urchin spine extraction IDR 500K-1.5M; stonefish anti-venom only at BIMC/Siloam (IDR 5-15M)
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dry season dawn patrol 5:30-7 AM (Bukit glassy + uncrowded); wet season east coast dawn patrol same; medical clinics: Unicare and Penta Medical typically 8 AM-8 PM, BIMC and Siloam 24h ER
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Dawn patrol Bukit (5:30-7 AM) avoids crowds and afternoon onshore wind in dry season; east coast late morning glass-off as offshore wind builds; avoid mid-day reef walking when tide drops and urchins exposed
practical_info.nearby_warung: Near BIMC Kuta: Made's Warung, multiple Bypass Ngurah Rai cafes; near Unicare Pecatu: Drifter Surf Cafe, Single Fin, Suka Espresso; near Penta Medical Canggu: Crate Cafe, Old Mans, Peloton, Shady Shack; near Siloam Denpasar: Sunset Road dining strip
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Seasonal coast switching: if west coast blown out in shoulder months, drive to east coast Keramas/Sanur (~45-90 min from Bukit); if east coast onshore in wet season shoulder, check Bukit on calm days. Medical: Unicare and BIMC Jimbaran both serve Bukit; Penta Medical and BIMC Kuta both serve Canggu/Kuta corridor.
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No for season/safety topic on Bali mainland; boat charter relevant for inter-island Lembongan/Penida/Gili surf trips during peak Bali crowd weeks

safety_culture.hazards: MARINE: reef cuts (most common - all Bukit and east coast reef breaks), sea urchin spines (shallow tide pools and reef edges, Padang/Bingin notorious), stonefish (rare but extremely venomous, west coast and Nusa Dua reef), sea snake (banded sea krait - venomous but non-aggressive, occasional sightings on reef), blue-ringed octopus (rare, deadly venom, tide pools), jellyfish (seasonal blooms, mostly mild stings), stingray (sandy lagoon spots), rip currents (Echo Beach, Berawa, Padang Padang channel, all major Bukit reefs). LAND/CULTURAL: motorbike crashes (#1 tourist hospitalization cause), monkey bites (Uluwatu temple, Sangeh), stray dog bites (rabies risk), heat stroke, dehydration. NYEPI: surfing strictly prohibited on March 19, 2026 - 24h enforced silence.
safety_culture.marine_hazards: REEF CUTS: immediate clean with bottled (sterile) water - NOT seawater (bacterial); scrub with iodine/Betadine, apply antibiotic ointment, cover; if deep/long: BIMC/Unicare for stitches and tetanus booster; tropical reef cuts infect within 24-48h if untreated (staph, vibrio). SEA URCHIN: do NOT try to dig out deeply embedded spines with needle (causes more damage); soak in hot water (45°C) for 30-60 min to ease pain and break down venom; vinegar/urea may help dissolve spines; visible spines remove with sterile tweezers; embedded spines often dissolve over 1-3 weeks but if joint/face/infected go to clinic. STONEFISH: medical EMERGENCY - immerse foot in HOT water (45°C, as hot as tolerable without burning) immediately, this denatures venom; transport to BIMC/Siloam for anti-venom (Stonefish antivenom available at major hospitals); intense pain, swelling, possible cardiac complications. SEA SNAKE (banded krait): venom highly neurotoxic - keep victim calm, immobilize bitten limb below heart, transport to BIMC/Siloam immediately; antivenom may be required; rare events but assume worst. BLUE-RINGED OCTOPUS: no antivenom - immediate CPR support and ambulance to hospital. JELLYFISH: vinegar rinse, NOT freshwater (triggers nematocysts); remove tentacles with tweezers; hot water immersion 40-45°C; severe stings: BIMC. STINGRAY BARB: hot water immersion, do not remove barb from chest/abdomen wounds (transport with barb in place), antibiotics. EAR INFECTIONS: 'surfer's ear' frequent in Bali warm water - use earplugs, dry ears, see ENT at BIMC if persistent.
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: BUKIT REEFS: HIGH severity - sharp coral at low tide (Padang Padang at 1m, Bingin at 0.5m, Impossibles section reefs all razor-edged); minor scrapes to deep gashes requiring 5-20 stitches common; infection risk high. CANGGU SANDBARS: LOW severity (sand bottom). EAST COAST: medium severity at Keramas (reef but slightly less sharp than Bukit), Sanur (longer reef shelf). REQUIRED: surf booties for shallow Bukit days, fresh-water and Betadine kit, never paddle out at low tide on unfamiliar reef.
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: All major Bukit reefs have channel rips - useful for paddle out but dangerous for swimmers; Padang Padang has channel on right side; Uluwatu Outside has strong south-going current on big days; Canggu/Echo Beach has powerful rips at high tide on big swells (drowning fatalities historically); identify rip: brown water plume offshore, no breaking waves in channel; if caught: paddle parallel to shore not against current, raise hand for jet-ski/lifeguard rescue
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Critical during peak dry season at Uluwatu/Padang - respect rotation, drop-in fines/aggressive locals in Padang lineup; Canggu lineups crowded but more forgiving (mix of beginners/intermediates); wet-season east coast (Keramas) has tight pro/local lineup - be respectful, do not jump priority
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium-high at Padang Padang (heavy local rotation by Bukit pros); medium at Uluwatu (large lineup absorbs visitors); low-medium at Canggu beach breaks; medium at Keramas (smaller takeoff zone enforces local priority)
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Nyepi 2026 (March 19): MANDATORY silence - stay in accommodation 6 AM Thursday to 6 AM Friday; no lights, no sounds, no surfing, no leaving hotel grounds; even hotel guests must remain on property; Pecalang (local security) enforces. Other Balinese ceremonies (Galungan/Kuningan, full moon ceremonies, temple anniversaries): may close certain beach access; menstruating women traditionally not permitted in temple grounds (Uluwatu temple).
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Mega Semadhi (Padang Padang Pro winner, Bukit local legend); Made Switra (early Uluwatu pioneer); Rizal Tanjung (Indonesian surf legend); Garut Widiarta (Padang Padang specialist); Bruna Schmitz, Diah Rahayu, Taina Izquierdo (women's lineup leaders); Padma Wirawan (Keramas East Coast specialist) [partial list, uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: NYEPI 2026 (Thursday March 19, 6 AM - Friday March 20, 6 AM): TOTAL ISLAND SHUTDOWN - DPS airport closed, all flights cancelled, no vehicles on roads, no surfing/beach access, no internet/mobile data (in some years), no outdoor lights. The day before (Wed March 18) features Ogoh-Ogoh parades - dramatic effigies of demons paraded then burnt. Plan ahead: stock food/water at accommodation, charge devices, accept the digital detox. Other dates of impact: Galungan/Kuningan (twice yearly, ~210-day cycle), Saraswati, Tumpek series, full moon (Purnama) ceremonies - some beach access temporarily closed; menstruating women asked not to enter Uluwatu Temple grounds.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: BUKIT (Uluwatu/Pecatu/Bingin/Padang/Balangan): Unicare Clinic Pecatu (GP, minor procedures, surfer specialist for reef cuts, fast lab); BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (24h ER, international standard, surgery, plastic surgery for fin cuts, stonefish antivenom); 911 Medical Clinic Uluwatu. CANGGU/CORRIDOR: Penta Medical Canggu (GP/minor), Hydro Medical Canggu, BIMC Hospital Kuta (24h ER), 911 Medical Canggu, Siloam Hospital Denpasar (large general). KUTA/LEGIAN/SEMINYAK: BIMC Hospital Kuta (24h ER, walk-in), Siloam Hospital Denpasar, Kasih Ibu Hospital. SANUR/EAST COAST: Siloam Hospital Bali Denpasar, BIMC Hospital, Manuaba Memorial Hospital. SERIOUS TRAUMA: medevac to Singapore (Mt. Elizabeth, Gleneagles) via insurance-coordinated medical jet from BIMC Nusa Dua coordination.
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Bukit reefs (Uluwatu/Padang/Bingin/Impossibles): Unicare Pecatu 3-5 km; BIMC Nusa Dua 12-18 km. Canggu/Echo Beach: Penta Medical 1-3 km; BIMC Kuta 8-12 km. Keramas East Coast: nearest clinic ~10-15 km (Manuaba Memorial or Siloam Denpasar). Medewi/West Coast: nearest hospital ~30 km Negara (basic), Tabanan (better), serious cases must transport to Denpasar/Kuta 75+ km. [uncertain on exact distances]
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Limited - jet-ski rescue not standardized at most Bali breaks; surfers and Bukit lifeguards informally manage rescues; during major competitions (Rip Curl Padang Padang Cup, Komune Bali Pro at Keramas) WSL water-safety team operates jet-skis; private water-safety bookings possible for big-wave Bukit days through pro-level photographers and watermen networks
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; tourist police: 110; ambulance: 118 or 119; BIMC Kuta 24h: +62 361 761 263; BIMC Nusa Dua 24h: +62 361 3000 911; Unicare Clinic Pecatu: +62 361 8961 444 [uncertain]; Siloam Hospital Bali: +62 361 779 900; Penta Medical Canggu: +62 851 8523 8523 [uncertain]; Bali Sea Rescue / BASARNAS: 115; Singapore-based MedEvac coordination through travel insurer assistance line

services.nearest_surf_school: Surf schools incorporate safety briefings on marine hazards, rip currents, lineup etiquette; reputable schools require students wear rash guards (UV) and provide booties for reef sessions
services.board_rental_availability: Rental shops often sell/rent booties IDR 30-50K/day for Bukit reef sessions; reef-safe sunscreen (Stream2Sea, Raw Elements) available at some Canggu/Uluwatu shops
services.surf_photographer_available: N/A for medical/safety
services.photographer_recommendation: N/A
services.accommodation_options: Many hotels offer Nyepi-specific packages with indoor activities, meals, optional all-inclusive (since guests cannot leave property for 24h); plan to be in comfortable accommodation with good food/wifi during Nyepi
services.webcam_url: Surfline cams (https://www.surfline.com) cover Uluwatu, Padang Padang, Canggu, Keramas - check before paddling to assess conditions and crowd; MagicSeaweed (now Surfline) and Windy.com for swell/wind forecast
services.drone_allowed: N/A for safety/medical; on Nyepi, drone use prohibited
services.coworking_nearby: Coworking spaces near major medical facilities - useful if recovering from minor injury and need workspace: Tropical Nomad Canggu near Penta Medical; B Work Pecatu near Unicare; Outpost Canggu/Ulu both within 5-10 min of clinics

pricing.avg_lesson_price: N/A for safety/season
pricing.board_rental_daily: N/A
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: Booties for reef sessions IDR 30-50K/day, sometimes included with board rental at Bukit shops
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: Some surf camps offer Nyepi-special packages with all-inclusive food, indoor entertainment, meditation programs during the 24h silence
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Nyepi packages may include 3 meals/day, beverages, yoga/meditation, board games, in-room entertainment
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Surf insurance premiums stable year-round; medical treatment costs fixed; flights and accommodation peak July-August (60-100% premium); Nyepi week sees slight dip in arrivals just before, surge just after

sustainability.water_quality_rating: WET SEASON (Nov-Mar): water quality declines especially west coast and Canggu beaches due to monsoon runoff - urban sewage, agricultural runoff, plastic pollution carried by rivers; ear/skin infections more common Nov-Feb; gastrointestinal issues from accidental ingestion possible. DRY SEASON: water quality significantly better island-wide but Padang Padang/Padang Padang Tepi can have organic surface slick on big crowded days. Bukit reefs less affected by runoff (limestone karst geology). East coast Keramas water cleaner during wet season (its prime).
sustainability.eco_certification: Some clinics use sustainable practices; surf insurance has no formal eco-certification [uncertain]

events_community.annual_competition_window: Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang (holding period typically July-September during dry season big-wave window); Komune Bali Pro at Keramas (typically May-June or shoulder season); Krui Pro nearby Sumatra; Single Fin Sunday (Uluwatu weekly community event); Old Mans Wednesday (Canggu weekly community event); Nyepi (Mar 19, 2026) silent pause precedes start of new Saka year
events_community.community_events: Ogoh-Ogoh parade evening before Nyepi (Wed March 18, 2026) - dramatic processions of demon effigies, free public viewing in every village; Melasti purification ceremony 3-4 days before Nyepi (massive temple processions to beaches); post-Nyepi traditional Omed-omedan kissing ritual in Sesetan; surf-community events Single Fin Sunday and Old Mans Wednesday year-round

visa_logistics.visa_type_recommended: Match visa to surf season: 2-week trips during peak dry season (Jul-Aug) - VoA 30 days plenty; 1-2 month surf trip following dry season Apr-Oct - VoA + extension or B211A/C1 60-day; full dry season chase (Apr-Oct) - C1 with two extensions (180 days); wet-season east coast specialist or year-round nomad - E33G KITAS 1-year. Avoid arrival on Nyepi day (March 19, 2026) - airport closed; if Nyepi falls during your trip, factor in 24h enforced indoor stay
visa_logistics.visa_application_method: See Visa_Entry_Logistics research item for full visa procedures
visa_logistics.monthly_living_cost_idr: Surfer monthly budget: Canggu IDR 18-35M/month, Bukit IDR 15-30M/month, Sanur IDR 12-22M/month, budget homestay route IDR 8-15M/month. Medical insurance subscription (SafetyWing) USD 45-65/month additional. [uncertain on exact 2026 figures]
visa_logistics.tourist_levy: N/A here - covered in Visa research item; tourism levy partially funds environmental protection including beach cleanup and water quality monitoring
visa_logistics.customs_declaration: Medical supplies (prescription drugs) require declaration on All Indonesia e-Arrival Card; surfers carrying first-aid kits with prescription painkillers should bring doctor's letter to avoid customs issues
visa_logistics.surfboard_air_baggage: N/A here - covered in Transport_Logistics research item
visa_logistics.idp_motorbike_license: Motorbike crashes are the #1 cause of tourist hospitalization in Bali - SECTION OVERLAPS with Transport_Logistics. Riding without proper IDP + motorcycle endorsement voids all surf travel insurance medical claims. Always wear helmet (mandatory).
visa_logistics.surf_travel_insurance: WORLD NOMADS: most popular surf insurance globally; covers recreational surfing as standard activity on both Standard and Explorer plans; covers reef cuts, board collisions, jellyfish stings, evacuation, hospital, doctor visits; board bag/equipment cover under baggage section (USD 500-1,500 per item depending on plan); higher activity coverage limits; recommended for adventure/big-wave/boat charter surf trips.
