Can be purchased in-country mid-trip (unique feature among insurers). SAFETYWING NOMAD INSURANCE: monthly subscription model (USD 45-65/month per adult), no fixed end date - cancel anytime; covers surfing as leisure activity; basic medical and evacuation; lower equipment cover; popular with digital nomads and E33G KITAS holders. SafetyWing Remote Health is a separate, more comprehensive plan that may be accepted for KITAS health insurance requirement. IMG GLOBAL / Allianz Travel / Cigna Global: more comprehensive (and more expensive) options especially for long-stay nomads. BALI-SPECIFIC CHECKPOINTS: confirm (1) surfing covered as standard activity, (2) reef-cut treatment & antibiotics covered, (3) emergency evacuation to Singapore included (USD 500,000+ recommended given complex cases evacuate to Mt. Elizabeth or Gleneagles), (4) board bag / equipment cover sufficient for your gear value, (5) motorbike/scooter riding requires valid IDP + motorcycle endorsement and helmet to be covered, (6) pre-existing condition exclusions reviewed, (7) max trip length compatible with visa length. For E33G KITAS application: travel insurance (World Nomads short-term) typically NOT accepted by immigration; need international health insurance like Cigna Global, IMG Global, Allianz Care, GeoBlue, or SafetyWing Remote Health.

basic_info.name: Seminyak Beach / 水明漾海灘 / Pantai Seminyak (includes Double Six)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Canggu ~15 min, Uluwatu ~40 min by scooter

surf_conditions.wave_type: Beach break, sand-bottom; multiple shifting left and right peaks. Double Six is a notable high-tide longboard-friendly section
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate; small days suit beginners with supervision
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-6 ft typical; up to 8 ft on solid SW swells with hollow sections
surf_conditions.bottom_type: sand
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season (April/May-October) for offshore winds and groundswells
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide; low tide produces heavy close-outs
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore from E/NE/N (near-offshore to cross-offshore due to curved coastline); SE trade winds are favorable; early morning is best
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW to S groundswells (primary); also works on W swell
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 40-80 m on good sandbar days; Double Six offers longer 60-100 m rides on high tide for longboards
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: medium; can be fast and hollow on bigger SW swells
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate; punchier than Kuta/Legian with steeper drops and close-out potential on bigger days; sand bottom keeps consequences low
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Multiple shifting peaks along the stretch; Double Six is the most consistent and is suited to longboarders on high tide; outside peaks offer steeper drops
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 3-4 (less crowded than Kuta but busy at Double Six and around upscale beach clubs)

practical_info.access: Walk-on beach access via Jalan Double Six, Jalan Camplung Tanduk, and side streets through beach clubs (Ku De Ta, Potato Head, La Plancha)
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Walk-out from sand through whitewater; duck-dive inside waves; on bigger days use rip channels between sandbars
practical_info.entry_fee: Free beach access; scooter parking IDR 5,000-10,000, car parking IDR 10,000-20,000 (premium near beach clubs)
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-8:00 AM) for offshore winds and cleanest conditions; sunset session highly popular for atmosphere and lighting
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Before 8 AM to avoid surf-school crowds and onshore wind; sunset for ambience but more crowded
practical_info.nearby_warung: Numerous warungs along Jalan Camplung Tanduk; upscale beach clubs (Ku De Ta, Potato Head, La Plancha, Mrs Sippy) and cafés directly on the beach
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Legian / Double Six (5 min south), Petitenget / Batu Belig (5 min north), Berawa / Canggu (15 min north)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: [uncertain] Functions as base for west-coast day-trips; not a typical multi-island combo base

safety_culture.hazards: Strong rip currents (especially on bigger swells), close-out sections on low tide, occasional jet-ski traffic and banana boats, swimmers and sunbathers in the lineup zone
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Occasional jellyfish (wet season), rare blue-bottles, no reef or urchins; treat stings with vinegar and warm water
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: 0 / N-A (pure sand bottom)
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong rips form between sandbars; identifiable as darker calmer channels of seaward-flowing water. Seminyak rips can be powerful on bigger swells - swim parallel to shore to escape
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Standard right-of-way rules; respect Double Six longboarders on high tide; no drop-ins; share peaks; smile and say hi
safety_culture.localism_intensity: low
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Respect beach-club zones; do not paddle directly in front of swimmer/sunbather zones; tip parking guards; pick up trash
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: [uncertain] No iconic individual locals specifically tied to Seminyak; many Bali pros surf the area
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Petitenget directly adjacent to north end of Seminyak Beach - respect ceremonial offerings (canang sari) on the sand; Nyepi (March) closes the beach; melasti processions pass through
safety_culture.medical_nearby: BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua/Kuta, Siloam Hospitals Denpasar, Kasih Ibu Hospital Kuta, Unicare Clinic Seminyak
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Balawista lifeguards on duty with rescue boards; jet-ski may be available in some sections
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Balawista Bali Lifeguard: +62 361 755660; Ambulance: 118; Police: 110; BIMC Kuta: +62 361 761263

services.nearest_surf_school: Odyssey Surf School Seminyak, Rip Curl School of Surf, Bali Green Surf School, Bali Diamond Surf - all within walking distance
services.board_rental_availability: Widely available beachfront and from surf shops on Jalan Camplung Tanduk; IDR 100,000-200,000/day
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes, freelance land-based photographers along the beach (especially at Double Six)
services.photographer_recommendation: [uncertain] Various freelance beach photographers; typical rate IDR 300,000-600,000 per session/package
services.accommodation_options: The Legian Bali, W Bali - Seminyak, The Oberoi Beach Resort, Anantara Seminyak Resort, plus many mid-range hotels and villas; surf-focused stays like Mick's Place are nearby
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/seminyak/640a67e745190540b0a65fe9 (Surfline Seminyak cam) [uncertain exact URL]
services.drone_allowed: Generally yes for recreational use; avoid flying over beach clubs and crowds; commercial use requires permit
services.coworking_nearby: Outpost Seminyak (~5 min), Tropical Nomad (Canggu ~15 min), Dojo Bali (Canggu); Outpost is the main coworking option in Seminyak

pricing.avg_lesson_price: IDR 450,000-750,000 (USD 28-48 / TWD 900-1,550) per 2-hour group lesson (premium positioning vs Kuta)
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 100,000-200,000 (USD 6-13) per day for soft-tops/shortboards; longboards IDR 150,000-250,000
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: [uncertain] Usually included with lesson; standalone rental ~IDR 50,000-75,000/day
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: [uncertain] Small ding repair IDR 100,000-400,000; multiple ding-repair workshops in Seminyak/Kerobokan
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: [uncertain] Limited dedicated surf camps directly in Seminyak; nearby camps from USD 800-1,500 for 7-day packages
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: [uncertain] Typical: accommodation, breakfast, daily lessons, board use, airport transfer, video analysis, yoga sessions
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: [uncertain] Peak season (Jul-Aug, Dec-Jan) ~20-30% higher; Seminyak has higher peak-season premium than Kuta due to upscale positioning

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Moderate to poor in wet season (Nov-Mar) due to runoff and plastic pollution; acceptable in dry season; slightly better than Kuta due to less dense urban runoff but still affected by Bali-wide rainy-season debris
sustainability.eco_certification: [uncertain] No spot-level certification; some beach clubs hold sustainability programs
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: [uncertain] Not standard at the spot level

events_community.annual_competition_window: [uncertain] Hosts occasional longboard contests and beach festivals (Double Six area); fewer pro-level events than Bukit reefs
events_community.community_events: Sunset gatherings at La Plancha and Ku De Ta; Single Fin Sundays (Uluwatu) draws regional surfers; informal Double Six longboard meet-ups

basic_info.name: Serangan (Chinese: 沙拉岸 / 海龜島; Indonesian: Pulau Serangan; nickname: Turtle Island)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.7308, 115.2375 (Pulau Serangan, southeast of Denpasar; access via causeway off Jalan Pulau Serangan; Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/SeranganSurf)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~10-12 km (~15-25 min drive)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~50-70 min by motorbike (~22 km); From Uluwatu: ~40-50 min by motorbike (~20 km)

surf_conditions.wave_type: Reef break with multiple shifting A-frame peaks - both left and right peaks over a wide coral reef platform; left peak typically offers longer wave, right is shorter and tends to close out. 3-6 distinct peaks depending on swell size
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate (and confident upper-beginner on smaller days at inside peaks); not suitable for true beginners due to crowded shifting peaks and sharp reef. Advanced when bigger barrel-day conditions arrive
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Waist-high to double-overhead (~2-10 ft); ideal 3-6 ft for fun rippable walls; bigger swells produce hollow barrels on the outside
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Coral reef (shallow coral platform with urchin-filled holes; live coral noted as hazard)
surf_conditions.best_season: Wet season November-March (peak December-February); the go-to east-coast spot when west coast is blown out; works year-round on small swells but cleanest in wet season
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid tide is the sweet spot (works all tides); low tide produces sucky barrels (but exposes sharp reef and urchins); high tide gives rippable walls and is safer for wipeouts
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore N to W winds (wet-season trade winds); cross-shore NW most classic; onshore E during dry season makes it choppy
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SSW (works on all swell angles 1-6 ft); SSE combined with NW offshore wind is the magic combo
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (extremely consistent during wet season - a magnet that holds swell when other Bali spots are flat or onshore; the most reliable east-coast wet-season option)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 50-100 meters (rideable single-wave length up to ~100 m on the long left)
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium (rippable walls on higher tide); medium-fast and hollow on lower tide; outside barrel section can be fast
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate - shifting peaks make positioning tricky; drop is manageable on average days but reef is shallow and unforgiving on low tide; outside peak gets serious on bigger swells
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - 3-6 distinct shifting peaks across the wide reef: inside A-frame (mellower), middle peaks near former warung area, far-left consistent long left, outside right barrel section
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (extremely crowded during wet season - one of the most crowded lineups in Bali, with hundreds of surfers and surf-school groups; cited as 9/10 crowd factor)

practical_info.access: Since 2018 most of the island was privatised for resort/mega-project development; primary access now is boat-only from Sanur or Serangan launch points - land access through the causeway is restricted by checkpoints and largely closed for surfing. Boat taxi services run daily from Sanur
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Boat drop-off directly outside the lineup; if land-accessing via causeway (when possible), walk across reef at low tide or paddle straight out through breaking waves at higher tide; return the same way
practical_info.entry_fee: Boat taxi from Sanur ~IDR 100,000-150,000 round trip per person; causeway checkpoint may charge a small ID-exchange/entry fee when land access is permitted (~IDR 5,000-15,000)
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy conditions before the school crowds arrive; mid-tide windows in early morning are ideal
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~8 AM before surf-school boats arrive (crowd thins again briefly at lunchtime ~12-1 PM); late afternoon if winds stay offshore
practical_info.nearby_warung: Limited - the former beach warungs were demolished after 2018 privatisation; bring own water, snacks, wax. Warungs available back at Sanur boat launch and on Serangan village side of causeway. Mertasari Beach area in Sanur for post-surf food
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Sanur Reef (5-10 min by boat), Sindhu (10 min by boat), Hyatt Reef (5 min by boat); all on same wet-season east-coast window
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Largely yes since 2018 - boat-only access is the practical option for most surfers. Boat taxi from Sanur is the standard method

safety_culture.hazards: Shallow sharp coral reef especially at low tide, sea urchins in reef holes, crowded shifting lineup with mixed skill levels increases collision risk, occasional resort-development security restrictions, slippery dirt tracks on land approach after rain
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (abundant in reef cracks - main concern), live coral, occasional jellyfish in wet season; rare sea snake sightings. Vinegar and tweezers for urchin spines recommended
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Moderate to High - reef is sharp and shallow at low tide; booties recommended on low-tide sessions; not as severe as Padang/Uluwatu but still warrants caution
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Currents wash across the reef platform especially on bigger swells; channels between peaks offer paddle-out lanes; mid-tide is safest for navigating the shifting current pattern
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Despite huge crowds, vibe is generally friendly and welcoming; standard priority/no-snake rules; shifting peaks make priority claims tricky so communicate verbally. Drop-ins are common due to inexperienced school groups
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low to Medium (~5/10) - mixed crowd of locals, expats, surf-school students and visitors; no strong territorialism but lineup is chaotic rather than hostile
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Smile and say hi, do not paddle aggressively for every set, give surf-school students space, do not drop in - even though others might. Respect Balinese Hindu ceremonies at nearby Pura Sakenan temple
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: No single famous resident legend tied to Serangan; many east-coast and Sanur Indonesian surfers frequent the spot during wet season [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Sakenan temple sits on Serangan Island - a significant Balinese Hindu site; respect ceremonies (especially Kuningan, Galungan, and the temple's odalan). Nyepi (Day of Silence) - no beach/sea access islandwide. The island also hosts a turtle conservation hatchery
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~20-30 min), BIMC Nusa Dua (~25-35 min), Sanglah General Hospital (~25 min)
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Sanur clinics (Bali Royal Hospital BROS, Kasih Ibu Sanur): ~5-8 km
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No permanent rescue jet ski; boat taxis can assist if needed [uncertain]

services.nearest_surf_school: Sanur Surf School, Chandra's Surfing Bali (Sanur), Padang Padang Surf Camp (Sanur outpost), Rip Curl School of Surf (Sanur), Bali Wave Pioneers - most run boat shuttles to Serangan in wet season
services.board_rental_availability: Available via Sanur Surf School and surf shops in Sanur; not directly on Serangan beach anymore. ~IDR 100,000-200,000/day
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - boat-based and water photographers operate during wet season; many surf schools include photo packages
services.photographer_recommendation: Chandra's Surfing Bali (~IDR 100,000 per person), Sanur Surf School photographers, Padang Padang Surf Camp daily photo/video team. Freelance shooters on Instagram (#seranganbali). Rates typically IDR 100,000-500,000/session
services.accommodation_options: Most surfers stay in Sanur (Cari Surf Camp Sesetan, Sanur Paradise Plaza, Hotel Mercure Sanur, numerous mid-range hotels and homestays); some stay in Nusa Dua or Canggu and ride in. Few stay on Serangan Island itself due to 2018 privatisation
services.webcam_url: https://www.surf-forecast.com/breaks/Serangan-Bali ; Surfline Sanur cam covers the broader region
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use over the surf zone; avoid flying over temple, turtle hatchery and any active development/resort area; check with boat captain [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Sanur has multiple coworking options: Genesis Creative Centre, Tropical Nomad (Sanur outpost), various cafe-coworks; ~5-10 min from boat launch

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 30-50 / IDR 450,000-800,000 group lesson (includes boat transfer in many cases); private lessons IDR 800,000-1,500,000

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Moderate - heavy wet-season runoff from Denpasar can bring trash and brown water especially after major rainstorms; cleaner in mid-day and after sustained offshore wind. Plastic pollution can be a noticeable issue on the inside

events_community.annual_competition_window: Has hosted local/regional ISC (Indonesian Surfing Championship) and Asian Surfing Championship events in wet season historically; no fixed major international event currently [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Informal wet-season sessions; Sanur-based surf clubs often run weekend group surfs; occasional reef-clean events organised by surf schools

Seseh_Mengening.name: Seseh / Mengening (峇里島 - 瑟瑟 / 蒙耶寧 / Pantai Seseh / Pantai Mengening, Cemagi)
Seseh_Mengening.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Seseh ~23 km / Mengening ~25 km (approx. 60-90 min by car depending on traffic)
Seseh_Mengening.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): Seseh 15-20 min by scooter, Mengening 20-25 min; From Uluwatu: 85-110 min by scooter
Seseh_Mengening.wave_type: Mixed beach and reef break - Seseh has long lefts and shorter rights, can be fast and hollow; Mengening is a quieter reef-bottom break with both lefts and rights, less defined than Pererenan; long left at Seseh breaks in front of a rock outcrop a bit distance out
Seseh_Mengening.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (Seseh better suited to intermediate-advanced; Mengening more intermediate-friendly on smaller days)
Seseh_Mengening.wave_height_range: 2-7 ft (best 3-6 ft head-high; closes out on big swells; smaller days at Mengening are more rideable for intermediates)
Seseh_Mengening.bottom_type: Mixed - black volcanic sand on the beach, rock and reef sections on the breaks (especially Seseh's outer rock outcrop); some volcanic boulders in shallows
Seseh_Mengening.best_season: Dry season May-September prime (consistent SW swell + offshore SE/NE winds); shoulder April and October usable. Wet season (Dec-Feb) typically too messy and onshore.
Seseh_Mengening.best_tide: Mid to high tide (low tide exposes rocks and sharp reef sections, making it less rideable and more hazardous); high tide gives Seseh's long left its best shape
Seseh_Mengening.best_wind: Offshore from East / Southeast / Northeast (NE in early dawn, switching to SE trade winds during dry season); turns onshore by 10am
Seseh_Mengening.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and WSW groundswells
Seseh_Mengening.swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (consistent during dry season but more hit-or-miss than Canggu/Pererenan; needs decent swell to switch on properly)
Seseh_Mengening.wave_length_meters: Seseh left: 50-100 m on good days (long left breaking off the rock outcrop); Mengening: 30-60 m typical [uncertain]
Seseh_Mengening.wave_speed_rating: Medium-fast (Seseh can be fast and hollow on bigger days, Mengening generally medium speed; rewards shortboards and mid-length boards)
Seseh_Mengening.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate - the outer reef/rock outcrop takeoff at Seseh requires good positioning and reading; Mengening's reef takeoff is generally easier and more forgiving; low-tide hazards make positioning critical
Seseh_Mengening.secondary_peaks: Seseh main left peak (off rock outcrop), inside Seseh shore-break peaks (sand bottom, gentler), Mengening reef peaks (less defined), nearby Kedungu and Yeh Gangga further north on the same coastline
Seseh_Mengening.peak_crowd_level: 1-2/5 (significantly less crowded than Canggu / Pererenan; on dawn patrol you may surf with just a handful of locals and the rare expat; weekends can bring a few more)
Seseh_Mengening.access: Drive from Canggu via Jl. Raya Canggu / Jl. By Pass Tanah Lot to Jl. Pantai Seseh (Seseh) or continue to Jl. Pantai Mengening / Jl. Pratu Rai Madra to the end (Mengening). Park near the beach access, then walk over sand to the lineup. No formal beach entry stairs; some access via village paths.
Seseh_Mengening.paddle_out_method: Seseh: paddle out from the sand beach, then push out toward the outer rock outcrop where the long left forms; use the shore break entry channel and avoid the inside rocks. Mengening: paddle out through the inside sand/shore break, then position over the reef peak; watch for submerged rocks at low tide.
Seseh_Mengening.entry_fee: No fixed beach entry fee. Parking IDR 2,000-5,000 for scooter; IDR 5,000-10,000 for car at informal lots; some local fee collection by village attendants.
Seseh_Mengening.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for the cleanest offshores and total solitude; mid-afternoon often onshore; sunset session if wind drops
Seseh_Mengening.best_time_of_day: Before 8am for clean offshore conditions, after 4pm if wind turns; mid-week best for solitude
Seseh_Mengening.nearby_warung: Small village warungs along Jl. Pantai Seseh and Jl. Pantai Mengening selling local food, coffee, coconuts; Warung Seseh, traditional Balinese eateries near the temples; far fewer cafes than Canggu - bring water and snacks
Seseh_Mengening.alternative_spot_within_10min: Pererenan (10-15 min south), Echo Beach / Sandbar (15-20 min south), Kedungu (5-10 min north - another quiet sand+reef break), Yeh Gangga (further north toward Tanah Lot), Cemagi Beach reef peaks
Seseh_Mengening.hazards: Strong unpredictable rip currents and undertow (waters not suitable for swimming); rocky bottom and submerged volcanic boulders on the inside; sharp reef sections at low tide; very limited rescue presence due to remoteness; isolated breaks - go with a buddy; bacterial water after rain from nearby drains
Seseh_Mengening.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the rocks and reef (carry vinegar/tweezers); occasional jellyfish in wet season; possible stingrays in shallows - shuffle feet; sea snakes occasionally seen near Tanah Lot rocks not far away (rarely encountered while surfing); limited reef-fish presence
Seseh_Mengening.reef_cut_severity_index: Medium - mix of sand and volcanic rock; cuts and bruises from low-tide wipeouts common; less sharp than Padang Padang but more hazardous than pure sand beach breaks
Seseh_Mengening.rip_current_pattern: Strong undertow and unpredictable rip currents (well-documented hazard); side-sweeping currents along the beach; rip channels near the outer rock outcrops at Seseh can drag swimmers/surfers out fast; current increases significantly on bigger swells and outgoing tide
Seseh_Mengening.lineup_etiquette: Very relaxed, friendly lineup; greet and chat with the few locals you see; priority on the peak still observed; very mellow vibe compared to Canggu - smile, say hi, take turns; respect the few Indonesian locals who surf here daily
Seseh_Mengening.localism_intensity: Low - traditional Balinese village atmosphere with strong spiritual ties; locals are generally welcoming; aggression rare; some sensitivity to noisy surf groups disrupting village life
Seseh_Mengening.respect_protocol: Cover up walking through the village (sarong/shirt); respect ceremonies and offerings (canang sari) on the beach; avoid loud parties; tip warungs and local parking attendants; ask before photographing locals or ceremonies
Seseh_Mengening.resident_local_legends: A handful of Balinese local surfers from Seseh and Cemagi village shred here daily; no globally famous pros stationed here; village-based surf culture rather than competitive scene [uncertain]
Seseh_Mengening.religious_sensitivity: Seseh and Cemagi are deeply traditional Balinese villages with important temples - Pura Gede Luhur Batu Ngaus (Mengening) sits on a rocky outcrop directly above the beach; Tanah Lot temple is 15 min away; respect ceremonies (Melasti before Nyepi), observe Nyepi (no surfing/beach access), women on menstruation traditionally avoid temple grounds; sea-temple ceremonies regularly hold the beach off-limits temporarily
Seseh_Mengening.medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu, ~15-20 min), Canggu Medical Clinic, Siloam Hospital Denpasar (major trauma, ~25 min), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua further away
Seseh_Mengening.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~8-10 km; Siloam Denpasar ~22 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~32 km
Seseh_Mengening.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - no dedicated rescue services at Seseh or Mengening; isolation means surfers must be self-sufficient; go with a buddy, carry phone in dry bag
Seseh_Mengening.emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
Seseh_Mengening.nearest_surf_school: No surf schools based directly at Seseh or Mengening; Canggu-based schools (Pererenan: Surfers Surf School / Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu; Batu Bolong: Dian Surf School, Sea N Surf, Pro Surf) sometimes bring intermediate students here when Canggu is too crowded
Seseh_Mengening.board_rental_availability: Very limited - no formal rental shops at Seseh/Mengening; rent in Canggu or Pererenan and ride up; some villa accommodations offer board rentals to guests
Seseh_Mengening.surf_photographer_available: Rare - no dedicated land/water photographers stationed here; the beach is popular for pre-wedding and lifestyle photography rather than surf photography; arrange in advance from Canggu
Seseh_Mengening.photographer_recommendation: Hire from Canggu / Pererenan-based surf photographers and pay travel premium; typical price IDR 300,000-600,000 for an edited surf photo pack including travel [uncertain]
Seseh_Mengening.accommodation_options: Luxury villas (Seseh Beach Villas / Elite Havens, Taman Ahimsa Cemagi, Impiana Private Villas Cemagi), boutique guesthouses, traditional Balinese homestays in the village, eco-resorts; very different feel from Canggu - quieter, more cultural; few hostels
Seseh_Mengening.webcam_url: No dedicated Seseh or Mengening webcam; check Surfline Canggu cam for general conditions: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d40 [uncertain]
Seseh_Mengening.drone_allowed: Generally tolerated but be very respectful - village temples and ceremonies are sacred; DGCA registration required for >250g; avoid flying over temples (Pura Gede Luhur Batu Ngaus) and ceremonies; lower crowd means easier drone use without bothering surfers
Seseh_Mengening.coworking_nearby: Limited - nearest established coworking spaces are in Canggu/Pererenan (Outpost Canggu, Dojo Bali, B Work Bali) ~15-20 min south; some new villa-based remote work setups in Cemagi
Seseh_Mengening.avg_lesson_price: Not typically taught here; if guided from Canggu schools, expect IDR 600,000-900,000 (~USD 38-60) including transport
Seseh_Mengening.board_rental_daily: Rent from Canggu/Pererenan shops: soft-top IDR 75,000-100,000/day; performance shortboard IDR 100,000-200,000/day - bring it up on your scooter
Seseh_Mengening.water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Good - black sand and remoteness keep water cleaner than Canggu. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Fair to Poor after heavy rain due to village drainage; ear/skin infection risk reduced compared to Canggu river-mouths but still avoid surfing 24-48h after heavy rain.
Seseh_Mengening.annual_competition_window: No formal competitions; remote and quiet break that intentionally stays under the radar [uncertain]
Seseh_Mengening.community_events: Traditional Balinese ceremonies on Mengening / Seseh beach (Melasti before Nyepi - huge village procession to the sea); full moon temple ceremonies; community beach clean-ups occasionally organized by villas and Trash Hero

Sumba.name: Sumba (Tarimbang, Occy's Left / Nihiwatu) / 松巴島 / Pulau Sumba
Sumba.gps_location: Nihiwatu / Occy's Left: Wanukaka, southwest Sumba, approx. -9.78, 119.36 (Nihi Sumba resort area). Tarimbang Bay / Miller's Right: southern coast East Sumba, approx. -10.20, 120.42 [uncertain exact coords]
Sumba.wave_type: Two flagship breaks: Occy's Left / Nihiwatu - long left-hand reef break / point break, performance walls and barrels up to 300m. Tarimbang (Miller's Right) - right-hand reef break with multiple sections, long playful walls with some hollow sections, plus exposed lefts in the bay.
Sumba.skill_level: Occy's Left approachable to all surfers below overhead, demanding/advanced at size. Miller's Right intermediate to advanced. Tarimbang exposed lefts more demanding.
Sumba.wave_height_range: 3 ft to many times overhead (~3-15+ ft). Occy's Left works from 3ft up. Miller's Right best on small to medium days (3-8 ft).
Sumba.bottom_type: reef (coral; Sumba also features dead coral, volcanic rock, and boulders on some breaks)
Sumba.best_season: Dry season May-October; peak July-September for biggest cleanest swells. Shoulder months (April, October) good balance of swell and lighter winds.
Sumba.best_tide: Occy's Left works most tides; mid to high more forgiving, low tide more hollow and shallow. Miller's Right varies by section.
Sumba.best_wind: Offshore E to SE trade winds (dry season); can be cross-shore on certain breaks at peak SE trade strength.
Sumba.swell_consistency_rating: 4 (consistent dry-season groundswells; Sumba sits exposed to Indian Ocean Roaring 40s swells)
Sumba.wave_length_meters: Occy's Left up to 300m; Miller's Right one of longest rights in Sumba on its day (~150-250m) [uncertain exact length]
Sumba.wave_speed_rating: medium to fast (Occy's Left has powerful lips and fast inside bowl; Miller's Right more medium / playful)
Sumba.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Occy's Left has steep drops on size with shallow reef inside bowl; late takeoffs hit reef. Miller's Right easier takeoff with multiple sections.
Sumba.secondary_peaks: Sumba has additional breaks - Pinnacles (heavy reef), Tarimbang lefts (exposed), Miller's Right and inside sections, plus more remote breaks accessible by 4WD or boat. Multiple bays scattered along south coast.
Sumba.peak_crowd_level: 1 (extremely uncrowded; Occy's Left capped at 10-12 surfers/day resort-only; Tarimbang sees only handful of surfers at a time)
Sumba.access: Bali (DPS) -> Tambolaka (TMC) ~1hr flight, up to 3x daily, for Nihiwatu/west Sumba; or Bali -> Waingapu (WGP) for east Sumba/Tarimbang. Nihiwatu: 1.5hr scenic drive from Tambolaka through highlands. Tarimbang: ~2hr drive from Waingapu airport, then 15-min walk or 20-min paddle from Marthens Homestay to the surf break.
Sumba.paddle_out_method: Occy's Left: resort crew guides guests via safe paddle-out channel from beachfront - no public access allowed. Miller's Right: walk along beach then paddle from inside reef; channel access on north side of bay.
Sumba.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol and morning sessions preferred for cleanest conditions; SE trades can build during day affecting some breaks.
Sumba.boat_charter_required: Not required - both Nihiwatu (resort guest beach access) and Tarimbang (land access via homestay) reachable by land. Some remote Sumba breaks require 4WD or boat.
Sumba.combo_trip_potential: Excellent combo with Bali, Rote, and broader Nusa Tenggara island-hopping. Some private/luxury charters include Sumba in extended Indonesian itineraries.
Sumba.hazards: Shallow reef, powerful lips at Occy's Left, remote location with limited medical, strong trade winds, exposed coastline with strong rip currents, deep coral on inside bowls
Sumba.marine_hazards: Sharp coral reef cuts (primary), sea urchins, occasional jellyfish, stonefish in reef pools, sharks rare but possible in open-ocean exposed coast
Sumba.lineup_etiquette: Occy's Left strictly managed by resort surf concierge with rotation/priority system among 10-12 daily surfers. Miller's Right and other breaks: respect locals and other guests; standard wave priority.
Sumba.localism_intensity: low (Sumba has very few local surfers; Tarimbang locals are welcoming; Occy's Left has no public locals since it is private resort wave)
Sumba.respect_protocol: Respect strict Nihi resort surf protocols (booking required, max 1 surfer per room, no outside paddle-ins). At Tarimbang, support Marthens Homestay and local community; respect Sumba's strong adat (traditional) culture including ikat weaving villages, megalithic tombs, and animist-Christian customs.
Sumba.medical_nearby: Nihiwatu has on-site paramedic + International SOS phone access; next hospital ~30min in Waikabubak; medevac available from Tambolaka (~70min drive) to Bali or Singapore. Tarimbang very remote - small village clinic only; Waingapu (~2hr) has district hospital. Serious cases require medevac.
Sumba.rescue_jet_ski_available: Nihiwatu has resort water-safety crew with boats and guides on lineup; no standing jet ski public rescue at Tarimbang [uncertain on jet ski specifically]
Sumba.emergency_contact: Nihi resort 24/7 emergency line via International SOS; Indonesia general 112; insurance medevac line essential [uncertain specific numbers]
Sumba.accommodation_options: Nihiwatu (Nihi Sumba Island) - ultra-luxury resort, all-inclusive from approx USD 2,365-2,783+/night with 3-night minimum, surf capped at 10-12 guests/day. Tarimbang: Marthens Homestay (basic bamboo bungalows IDR 400k-800k/night including 3 meals), Sumba Surf Camp, plus a small number of other homestays. Tarimbang Beach also has limited mid-range options. Maringi Eco-Resort and other emerging boutique options elsewhere on island.
Sumba.webcam_url: No reliable public live webcam for Sumba surf spots (Nihiwatu is private; Tarimbang remote) [uncertain]
Sumba.annual_competition_window: No public WSL competitions; Nihi Sumba hosts private invitational surf events and coaching weeks with pro surfers [uncertain dates]
Sumba.community_events: No regular weekly community surf events; Sumba hosts cultural events like Pasola (ritual horse jousting) Feb-Mar in west Sumba which can coincide with surf trips [uncertain surf-specific]

basic_info.name: Sumbawa Lakey Peak Cluster - Lakey Peak, Lakey Pipe, Periscopes, Nungas, Cobblestones (Chinese: 松巴哇島雷基峰浪點群 - 雷基峰/雷基管浪/潛望鏡/努嘉斯/鵝卵石; Indonesian: Lakey Peak / Pantai Lakey - Hu'u, Dompu)
basic_info.gps_location: Lakey Peak village: -8.8456, 118.2772; Lakey Peak break: -8.8463, 118.2768; Lakey Pipe: -8.8451, 118.2785; Periscopes: -8.8389, 118.2756; Nungas (Nunga Doro): -8.8528, 118.2647; Cobblestones: -8.8617, 118.2453 (approx.). Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/LakeyPeakHuuSumbawa
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~420-440 km straight-line east of DPS; total travel ~5-7 hrs via flight DPS-BMU (Sultan Muhammad Salahuddin Airport, Bima) 1 hr 10 min on Garuda/TransNusa/Lion Air, then 2-3 hr drive (~110 km) southwest from Bima to Lakey Peak via Dompu
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Not applicable directly - requires inter-island transfer; from Canggu/Uluwatu motorbike to DPS airport 60-90 min then flight DPS-BMU 1 hr 10 min + ground transfer 2-3 hrs

surf_conditions.wave_type: Multi-spot cluster around Hu'u Bay: Lakey Peak (world-renowned A-frame reef peak with peeling left and shorter punchier right; one of the most photographed A-frames in surfing); Lakey Pipe (heavy left-hand reef tube - best stand-up barrels in Indonesia when Peak is too big); Periscopes (right-hand reef break at northern end of Lakey reef - punchy wall and barrel section over shallow reef/rock platform); Nungas / Nunga Doro (long left-hand reef break that links sections into 200m+ rides at 8 ft+); Cobblestones (horseshoe bay with right on north side and left on south side requiring high tide)
surf_conditions.skill_level: Mostly Intermediate to Advanced/Pro; Lakey Peak A-frame holds intermediates on small days but becomes advanced when 4 ft+; Lakey Pipe and Periscopes are advanced/pro only (shallow reef, hollow); Nungas advanced on size; Cobblestones offers slightly mellower right for intermediates on small swell
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Lakey Peak: 2-10 ft (holds all sizes, best 3-8 ft); Lakey Pipe: 3-8 ft (mid-to-high tide barrels); Periscopes: 2-8 ft; Nungas: 4-10+ ft (becomes world-class at 8 ft+ when sections connect); Cobblestones right works small swell, left needs high tide and can get sketchy
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp coral reef at all primary breaks; Periscopes has reef/rock platform; Cobblestones has the namesake cobblestone/rock bottom on its bay sides; reef becomes shallow and dangerous at low tide especially at Lakey Pipe
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-September; August often classic); offshore SE/E trade winds dominate; Sumbawa is generally consistent year-round but wet season Nov-Apr brings onshore winds and rain; Lakey Peak itself is regarded as one of the most consistent waves in Indonesia
surf_conditions.best_tide: Lakey Peak: mid tide ideal (holds all sizes); low tide produces extremely hollow barrels (left side); Lakey Pipe: mid-to-high tide only (low tide too shallow); Periscopes: mid tide; Nungas: mid to low tide for hollow sections; Cobblestones left needs high tide; refer to a tide chart - timing is critical for spot rotation
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE to E trade winds prevailing in dry season; afternoon onshore SW common; the various spots have slightly different orientations allowing spot rotation through the day - Periscopes can hold a bit more wind, Cobblestones tucked in bay is wind-protected
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SSW groundswell from deep Indian Ocean lows; SW also works; Lakey Peak picks up almost any south-facing swell; long-period swells 12-18s produce the cleanest A-frames
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (Lakey Peak is regarded as one of the most consistent waves in Indonesia - some sources note 'consistent waves throughout the year' though peak is May-September); the cluster as a whole means there is almost always something rideable
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: Lakey Peak: 80-150m on left, 30-60m on right (it's an A-frame peak, not a long point wave); Lakey Pipe: 50-100m intense barrel; Periscopes: 60-120m right; Nungas: 50-200m+ at 8 ft when sections link; Cobblestones: 60-150m depending on side
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Lakey Peak: medium to fast (punchy A-frame); Lakey Pipe: very fast hollow tube; Periscopes: fast with punchy wall; Nungas: medium-fast with hot-dog walls and barrel sections; Cobblestones: medium
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Lakey Peak: moderate to high (steep A-frame peak; on small days more approachable); Lakey Pipe: very high (ledging hollow take-off over shallow reef - reputation for hard-core reef cuts); Periscopes: high (punchy take-off over reef/rock platform); Nungas: high on size with shifting peaks; Cobblestones left slab section sketchy at end
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: The whole area IS a multi-peak cluster - within 20 min radius of Lakey Peak Haven there are 9 named waves including the 5 above plus Salomons (or 'Sailoma'), Lakey Beach Break, and others; the cluster's strength is rotating between breaks based on swell/tide/wind
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 4 at Lakey Peak in peak season (often crowded - total visiting surfers can hit 150-200 in early morning high tides, can be aggro for best conditions; 30+ years of surf history means 2nd-generation locals); other spots in cluster (Nungas, Periscopes, Cobblestones) typically less crowded - boat hops escape crowds

practical_info.access: Fly DPS to Bima (BMU) Sultan Muhammad Salahuddin Airport on Garuda/TransNusa/Lion Air (~1 hr 10 min flight); then 2-3 hr drive (~110 km) southwest from Bima via Dompu to Hu'u/Lakey Peak village; surf camps arrange pickup (IDR 500,000-600,000 per car/USD 32-40). Lakey Peak break is walk-in from the beach. Other breaks: Lakey Pipe walk along reef at low tide; Periscopes northern end of reef walk or short boat; Nungas/Cobblestones require boat or short drive plus paddle.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Lakey Peak: walk in from beach across sand/reef shelf then paddle out to the A-frame peak; Lakey Pipe: walk along reef at low tide then paddle out (booties essential); Periscopes: walk to northern end of reef, paddle out over reef platform; Nungas: short boat or moto + paddle; Cobblestones: boat across or drive to horseshoe bay then paddle
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy offshore conditions and best wind window; second session after afternoon wind drops (4:00-6:00 PM); tide windows dictate which spot is rideable at any given hour - check tide chart daily
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Not strictly - Lakey Peak, Lakey Pipe, and Periscopes are walk-in from Lakey Beach. Boat charter required for Cobblestones and useful for Nungas; many surf camps offer boat day-trips between breaks (IDR 100,000-300,000 per person depending on operator)
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: Excellent - Sumbawa is a classic Bali extension trip; commonly combined with Bali (Bukit) + Lakey Peak 7-14 days; further chain to West Sumbawa (Scar Reef, Yo-Yos, Supersuck, Phantoms) via 6-8 hr overland drive west; boat charters from Bali Benoa to Sumbawa often include Lakey Peak as a stop; some operators do Lombok + Sumbawa overland via ferry
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Within the cluster itself - rotating between Lakey Peak, Lakey Pipe, Periscopes, Nungas, Cobblestones based on tide/wind/swell. The classic logic: if Peak is too crowded -> Cobblestones; if Nungas is mushy at high tide -> Periscopes

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp shallow coral reef (Lakey Pipe especially - hard-core reef cuts documented), strong currents on bigger days, crowded competitive lineups at peak, malaria-prone area (Sumbawa is one of Indonesia's higher-risk malaria regions - prophylaxis recommended), dengue, sunburn/dehydration in remote area with limited shade, occasional sea snakes, motorbike accidents on rough roads, slow medical evacuation
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (booties essential at Lakey Pipe and Peak low tide), banded sea kraits possible (highly venomous but rarely aggressive), stonefish on reef (extremely venomous - immerse wound in hot water), reef cuts with very high tropical infection risk; sharks not a documented issue
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Respect 30+ years of local surf history - 2nd generation Sumbawan locals at Lakey Peak deserve priority on their wave; major etiquette rule at Lakey Peak A-frame: call your direction loudly at take-off (LEFT! or RIGHT!) since two surfers can drop on the same peak going opposite ways; do not snake; respect priority based on positioning; share waves; smile and chat onshore
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium - can be a bit aggro at Lakey Peak when conditions are firing and crowd is at 150-200; generally welcoming to respectful guests; Lakey Pipe and outside reefs see less crowd and lower localism intensity
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Be respectful to Sumbawan Muslim culture - dress modestly off-beach (cover shoulders and knees in villages and Dompu town), avoid public alcohol in conservative areas, observe Ramadan fasting hours discreetly during fasting month (warungs may close in daytime); tip surf-guide/boat captains; do not damage reef; engage with local community projects (Harapan Project healthcare in Hu'u)
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Lakey Peak has produced and hosted generations of Sumbawan and Indonesian pros over its 30+ year history; numerous WSL pros and Indonesian legends have visited; specific names not documented in research but local surf-guide community at Lakey Peak Surf Guides and camps holds strong local knowledge [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Sumbawan Muslim community (Sasak/Bimanese - mosques broadcast call to prayer five times daily); Hu'u village is conservative - dress modestly off-beach; Ramadan and Eid observance important - some warungs close during daytime fasting; respect local fishing community routines
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Lifeguard with first-aid equipment at Lakey Beach; small clinic in Rasabou ~20 min by moped (basic, more like a puskesmas); Dompu has a small hospital (RSUD Dompu) with some English-speaking doctors ~45-60 min drive away; Bima has a larger hospital ~2 hr drive; for serious cases a flight back to Bali (BIMC, Siloam Bali) is arranged; Harapan Project local healthcare organization operates in Hu'u
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - no permanent rescue jet ski; lifeguard with first-aid is available at Lakey Beach; some boat-charter operators have skis during big days but coverage is informal
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; nearest clinic Rasabou (basic puskesmas) ~20 min by moped; Dompu hospital RSUD Dompu ~45-60 min; serious cases evacuate to Bali via Bima flight (BMU-DPS); BIMC Bali +62 361 3000 911; satellite/cell signal limited - surf camps assist with arranging emergency transport

services.accommodation_options: Aman Gati Hotel Lakey (40 rooms, AC, pool, sea-view rooms and villas, in front of Lakey Peak - from ~USD 49/double, 7-night Bali-package from USD 470); Mona Lisa Bungalows (original 22-room budget seaside inn ~USD 15/night); The Peak Surf House (boutique surf camp); Lakey Peak Haven (mid-range surf camp near 5 world-class waves); Lakey Beach Inn; smaller homestays and losmen along Lakey Beach. Most camps offer surfboard storage, transfers from Bima, and meals.
services.webcam_url: Surfline Lakey Peak spot page: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/lakey-peak/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d52 (forecast + spot guide); live cam coverage limited - some surf camps post daily social media reports [uncertain]
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - long-running freelance and camp-based photographers cover Lakey Peak during peak season; Lakey Peak is one of the most photographed A-frames in surfing media; check social media or arrange via Aman Gati / The Peak / camp managers

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not a primary lesson destination (mainly intermediate+); when offered, private surf guiding/coaching USD 30-80/day arranged informally through camps; Lakey Peak Surf Guides offers local guide services [uncertain on specific rates]
pricing.board_rental_daily: Limited rental at camps and a few local shops ~IDR 150,000-300,000/day for shortboards; most surfers bring own quiver (recommended 6'2-7'0 step-up for Lakey Pipe and bigger Peak days) [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Generally excellent - Hu'u Bay has no urban/industrial runoff and remains pristine; some seasonal sediment during wet-season storms; protected reef ecosystem with limited fishing pressure

events_community.annual_competition_window: Lakey Peak has hosted WSL QS and ASC (Asian Surfing Championships) events in past years; intermittent pro contest visits; not a regular CT stop; classic dawn-patrol communal surf-camp culture rather than formal contest season [uncertain on current schedule]
events_community.community_events: Dawn-patrol surf-camp dinners and Bintang nights; Sumbawan cultural events including local rodeo/buffalo racing in Dompu region; Ramadan/Eid observance; Harapan Project community healthcare events

basic_info.name: Surf Camps in Bali - All-Inclusive Packages (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪營 - 全包式套裝; Indonesian: Kamp Selancar Bali). Coverage: Canggu (Solid Surf House, Lapoint, Chillhouse, Soleia, Surf Village, Mondo Surf Village, Kima Surf, WaveHouse Canggu), Uluwatu/Bukit (Padang Padang Surf Camp, CARI Surf Camp, Rapture Surfcamp, WaveHouse Uluwatu, Outer Reef Surf Travel), Medewi (Brown Sugar Surf Camp, West Break Bali, Bombora Medewi).
basic_info.gps_location: Canggu cluster (-8.65, 115.13 to -8.66, 115.14): Solid Surf House Canggu, Lapoint Canggu (Berawa), Chillhouse Canggu (Padonan), Soleia Surf Canggu, Mondo Surf Village (Berawa), Kima Surf Canggu, WaveHouse Canggu, Surf Village Canggu. Uluwatu/Bukit cluster (-8.81, 115.10 to -8.82, 115.09): Padang Padang Surf Camp (Labuan Sait), CARI Surf Camp (Pecatu, Jalan Umpeng Sari near Padang Padang), Rapture Surfcamp Padang Padang, WaveHouse Uluwatu (Bingin cliffs), Outer Reef Surf Travel villa. Medewi cluster (-8.4170, 114.7910): Brown Sugar Surf Camp Medewi, West Break Bali, Bombora Medewi.
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Canggu camps ~17-22 km (40-70 min drive); Uluwatu/Bukit camps ~17-22 km (35-60 min via Toll Bali Mandara); Medewi camps ~75-80 km (2.5-3 hr drive west via Tabanan-Negara highway).

surf_conditions.wave_type: Canggu camps - sand-bottom beach breaks (Batu Bolong, Berawa, Old Man's, Pererenan) for beginners; left and right reef passes (Echo Beach, Sandbar) for intermediates. Uluwatu/Bukit camps - reef breaks (Padang Padang Right beginner inside, Bingin, Dreamland, Balangan, Padang Padang Left advanced, Uluwatu cluster). Medewi camps - long left-hand cobblestone/sand point break (Medewi Point) plus reef alternatives (Yeh Sumbul, Balian, Pasut on roadtrip days).
surf_conditions.skill_level: Beginner-friendly camps primarily (Solid Surf House, Lapoint, Soleia, Padang Padang Surf Camp, Rapture, Brown Sugar Medewi); intermediate+ programs at WaveHouse Uluwatu, Chillhouse, Outer Reef, CARI Surf, West Break Bali; advanced coaching add-ons at most camps; family-friendly at Mondo Surf Village.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Beginner lessons 1-3 ft; intermediate guided sessions 2-5 ft; advanced coaching 4-8 ft; camps adjust spot selection to skill level and conditions daily.
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sand at Canggu beginner spots; reef/coral at Bukit Peninsula and Sanur intermediate spots; cobblestone/sand mix at Medewi Point.
surf_conditions.best_season: Camps operate year-round. Canggu camps best Apr-Oct (dry season SE trade winds); Bukit/Uluwatu camps best May-Sep (peak season swell); Medewi camps best May-Sep but Medewi Point also fires Oct-Apr on smaller swells; many camps switch coasts (west in dry, east in wet) for student lessons.
surf_conditions.best_tide: Spot-dependent; beginner programs target mid-to-high tide windows at sand beaches; coaches plan daily schedule around tide chart.
surf_conditions.best_wind: Mornings before SE trades pick up (offshore at west-facing spots); afternoon E coast guided trips during onshore W winds at west coast.
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW for west coast camps; E/SE for occasional east coast guided trips (Sanur, Keramas).
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (camps deliver near-daily learnable surf year-round through Bali's coast-switching strategy)
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 4-5 at Canggu beginner zones (Batu Bolong, Old Man's morning sessions extremely crowded with multiple camps' students); 3-4 at Bukit beginner spots (Padang Padang Right, Balangan inside); 2-3 at Medewi (much quieter); high-season Jun-Aug + Dec-Jan most crowded.

practical_info.access: Camps typically include airport pickup from DPS on Day 1 and drop-off on departure day; daily beach transfers included; pickup window often restricted to 1-2 set arrival days (Lapoint mandates Monday arrival; many camps Sat-Sun arrival window).
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Morning sessions 6:30-10:00 AM (cleanest conditions, taught to all levels); second sessions 10:00 AM-1:00 PM for intermediate/advanced or beginner second-attempt; afternoon free-surf or coached sessions 3:00-6:00 PM at most camps.
practical_info.nearby_warung: All Canggu camps in dense F&B zones (warungs, vegan cafes, beach clubs - Old Man's, La Brisa, The Lawn); Bukit camps near Single Fin (Uluwatu cliff), Drifter Cafe Bingin, Sundays Beach Club, Cashew Tree; Medewi camps near Bombora Cafe, Mai Mai Warung, Sandhya Beach Resort restaurant.
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Canggu camps switch between Batu Bolong/Berawa/Pererenan/Echo/Sandbar; Bukit camps rotate Padang Right/Bingin/Balangan/Dreamland/Nyang Nyang/Green Bowl; Medewi camps backup to Yeh Sumbul (Yeh Leh), Balian, Pasut depending on swell/wind.
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - all primary camp spots accessible from shore; some intermediate programs offer optional boat trips to Nusa Lembongan, Sanur reefs, or Keramas as add-on day trips.

safety_culture.hazards: Crowded learn-to-surf zones (collision risk), beginner board injuries (fins, nose, leash tangles), strong rip currents at Canggu (camps brief on rip identification), reef cuts at intermediate Bukit progression sessions, sunburn/dehydration, scooter accidents on transfers (some camps mandate van-only transfers), wet-season jellyfish blooms.
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Jellyfish (sporadic), sea urchins at Bukit reef breaks (booties recommended), occasional sea snakes (non-aggressive), reef cuts (high tropical infection risk - treat immediately with antiseptic and oral antibiotics if deep); no shark issues; camps stock comprehensive first aid kits and instructors trained in surf rescue.
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Camps deliver lineup-etiquette briefings before paddle-outs; designated beginner zones with marker buoys; intermediate students taught right-of-way, priority, no-snake, no-drop-in rules; camps maintain working relationships with locals at key spots.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low at beginner camp zones; medium at intermediate Bukit spots where camp students mix with free-surfers and locals; camps brief students on respecting locals and free-surfers.
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Tip instructors and house staff (IDR 50,000-150,000/day typical); respect Hindu ceremonies and processions; do not photograph ceremonies without permission; modest dress in temple areas; do not drink alcohol on beach during Nyepi or major Hindu holy days; remove leashes when crossing through ceremonies.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Canggu camps: Penta Medical Canggu 5 min, BIMC Nusa Dua 30 min, Siloam Denpasar 25 min, Bali Mandara Toll Hospital; Bukit camps: Unicare Pecatu 5-10 min, BIMC Nusa Dua 15-20 min, Siloam Denpasar 30-40 min; Medewi camps: Negara public hospital 30 min, BIMC/Siloam Denpasar 2.5 hr; camps maintain emergency contacts and most include basic medical insurance liaison.
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - camps rely on instructor water-safety (1:2-1:6 ratio depending on tier) and Bali Search & Rescue (Basarnas); WaveHouse advertises 1:2-3 ratio; Padang Padang Surf Camp 1:4; Lapoint 1:4-5.

services.board_rental_availability: All camps include lesson boards in package (foam soft-top for beginners, fiberglass for intermediates); additional rental for free-surf sessions IDR 100,000-250,000/day at most camps; WaveHouse and Outer Reef offer broader rental quivers including step-ups and longboards.
services.surf_photographer_available: In-house land/water/drone photographers at WaveHouse, Lapoint, Padang Padang Surf Camp, Rapture, CARI, Chillhouse, Soleia; daily video analysis included in most packages; photo upgrade packages USD 30-120/session common.
services.photographer_recommendation: Camp-employed photographers offer per-session and weekly upgrade packages; freelance shooters available via Instagram/camp referrals (Bali Surf Photo, several Indonesian water shooters). [uncertain on per-shooter rates]
services.accommodation_options: Camps offer tiered accommodation typically: (1) Shared dorms (4-8 beds, fan or AC) USD 30-60/night equivalent; (2) Private double rooms (AC, ensuite) USD 60-120/night equivalent; (3) Private bungalows/villa rooms USD 120-250/night equivalent. Most camps cluster around pool/garden hub with communal dining/yoga area; Chillhouse and Outer Reef positioned as boutique-luxury; Solid Surf House and Soleia positioned as social budget; Padang Padang Surf Camp offers 4 private room tiers; Mondo Surf Village family-oriented with apartments.
services.webcam_url: Camps use Surfline cams for Canggu Batu Bolong (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu-batu-bolong), Old Man's, Berawa, Uluwatu (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/uluwatu), Padang Padang, Bingin, Medewi (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/medewi-point) for daily session planning. [uncertain on operator-specific cams]
services.drone_allowed: Most camps allow guest drone use for personal footage; staff drone operators provide group shots; no drone over Hindu ceremonies; commercial filming requires permit. [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Canggu camps: walking/scooter distance to Outpost Canggu, Tropical Nomad, BWork, Dojo Bali, Bali Bustle; Bukit camps: Outpost Ulu (Padang Padang area), ZIN Cafe Bingin, Drifter Cafe; Medewi: very limited dedicated coworking, camp cafes only (Bombora Cafe wifi).

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Standalone-lesson equivalent USD 40-65/lesson at camp pricing; sold as packages so per-lesson cost lower than walk-in school price.
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Walk-in equivalent IDR 600,000-1,000,000/session for camp guests; package-amortized cost IDR 300,000-600,000 per session.
pricing.board_rental_daily: Included in package for lesson use; free-surf rental IDR 100,000-300,000/day depending on board type and camp.
pricing.gopro_rental_price: IDR 150,000-300,000/session at most camps with SD card; included in some premium package tiers (WaveHouse, Chillhouse).
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: Rashguard included free; wetsuit top IDR 50,000-100,000/day if available (rarely needed in Bali). [uncertain]
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: Camp-board minor ding repairs included; guest-board repairs IDR 150,000-400,000 minor, IDR 500,000-1,500,000 major; partnered repair shops Naruki Surf Repair (Canggu), Drifter Surf Shop (Seminyak/Bingin), local Uluwatu glassers.
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: Budget tier USD 380-700/week (Solid Surf House from EUR 349/week dorm, Soleia from EUR 399-441/week, West Break Bali 8-day from IDR 7,500,000); Mid-range USD 700-1,500/week (Lapoint Canggu, Padang Padang Surf Camp, Rapture, Brown Sugar Medewi, Surf Village Canggu, Kima Surf, Mondo Surf Village); Premium USD 1,500-2,500/week (Chillhouse, WaveHouse Surf & Stay, CARI Surf private bungalows); Luxury USD 2,500-5,000+/week (Outer Reef Surf Travel villa-based, Soleia private villa upgrades, customized private coaching packages).
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Standard inclusions: 5-6 group surf sessions per week (2 hrs each), all surf equipment (board, rashguard, leash), accommodation (per tier), breakfast daily (most), airport pickup (most, often only on set arrival days), daily beach transfers, video/photo analysis sessions (most), surf theory + dryland practice, yoga sessions 2-4x/week (Solid Surf House, Soleia, Chillhouse, Rapture, Padang Padang Surf Camp), bike rental (some - WaveHouse Uluwatu), basic insurance liaison (some), Wi-Fi, pool access. Common exclusions: lunch and dinner (Canggu camps typically breakfast-only due to nearby F&B), alcohol, airport drop-off (often), private 1-on-1 coaching, advanced coaching upgrades, photo packages, board damage waivers, optional day trips (Nusa Lembongan, Padang Padang Right photo session, Keramas), spa/massage, tips.
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Peak season Jun-Aug + Dec 20-Jan 5 commands 25-50% premium; shoulder Apr-May/Sep-Nov standard rates; low Feb-Mar offers 15-25% discount at many camps; mandatory Christmas/New Year minimum stay (7-10 nights) at premium camps. [uncertain on exact percentages per operator]

sustainability.eco_certification: WaveHouse and Chillhouse market sustainability ethos (reef-safe sunscreen, water refill stations, plastic-free, organic kitchen); Solid Surf House and Soleia participate in regular beach cleanups (Sungai Watch, Bye Bye Plastic Bags partnerships); Padang Padang Surf Camp and Rapture engage with local Pecatu community projects; no industry-wide third-party certification but EcoBnb and Green Globe ratings appear with select camps. [uncertain on individual certifications]
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Not standard; Lapoint has historically partnered with carbon-offset platforms for European guests on opt-in basis; Soleia mentions sustainability initiatives. [uncertain]

events_community.annual_competition_window: Many camps host annual in-camp student contests/grad days; participate in Coca-Cola Bali Open, ISA Indonesia events; Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang (Aug-Oct invitational) brings Bukit-area surfer attention; Single Fin Sunday at Uluwatu is permanent Sunday fixture. [uncertain on individual camp competition calendars]
events_community.community_events: Weekly events: BBQ nights (Brown Sugar Medewi Saturday Beach BBQ standard); yoga + breath workshops; sunset cocktails at Single Fin (Bukit camps Sun), Old Man's (Canggu camps Thu); Nyepi Day silent retreat policy (camps shut down outdoor activities, no leaving compound); Galungan/Kuningan ceremonies camp-led temple visits; Full Moon parties at La Brisa Canggu / Single Fin Uluwatu; International Women's Surf Day events June; beach cleanups monthly.

basic_info.name: Bali Surf Competitions (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪賽事; Indonesian: Kompetisi Selancar Bali) - umbrella entry covering Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang, WSL Indonesia events, Liga Surfing Indonesia (LSI), Krui Pro, Nias Pro, Rip Curl GromSearch International Final and Uluwatu Single Fin Classic
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (with Sumatra and Nias for related Indonesian-circuit events)
basic_info.gps_location: Multiple venues - primary Bali contest sites: Padang Padang (-8.8104, 115.1031), Uluwatu (-8.8154, 115.0884), Keramas (-8.5895, 115.3245), Pererenan (-8.6402, 115.1136), Canggu Old Man's/Batu Bolong (-8.6566, 115.1322); Sumatra: Krui West Lampung (-5.1751, 103.9398); Sumatra: Sorake Beach Nias (1.0237, 97.5747)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Padang Padang ~20 km; Uluwatu ~22 km; Keramas ~35 km; Canggu ~20 km; Pererenan ~22 km; Krui Sumatra requires flight (DPS -> CGK -> TKG Bandar Lampung, then 6-7 hr drive); Nias requires flight (DPS -> Medan KNO, then ferry or domestic to Gunungsitoli)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Padang Padang: 60-70 min from Canggu / 5-10 min from Uluwatu; Keramas: 60-75 min from Canggu / 60-75 min from Uluwatu; Pererenan: 10-15 min from Canggu / 70 min from Uluwatu

surf_conditions.wave_type: Rip Curl Cup: Left-hand reef tube (Padang Padang Lefts), needs 6-12 ft solid SW groundswell to fire. LSI Grand Final: Right and left sandbars at Pererenan River Mouth. WSL CT (when run at Keramas/Uluwatu): right-hand reef at Keramas, left-hand reef at Uluwatu. Krui Pro: right-hand reef-sand combo at Karang Nyimbor / Ujung Bocur. Nias Pro: right-hand reef point at Sorake Beach. GromSearch: typically Keramas right-hand reef.
surf_conditions.skill_level: Pro / Advanced only for all WSL CT and Rip Curl Cup events; QS events Open (Pro/Advanced); LSI Indonesian national circuit includes Open, Junior, Women and Masters divisions. Spectators all levels.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Rip Curl Cup contest day: 6-12+ ft Padang. WSL CT (Keramas/Uluwatu): 4-10 ft preferred. Krui Pro 2025 ran in 3-6 ft conditions. Nias Pro 2025: 3-6 ft at Sorake. LSI events: 2-6 ft depending on venue.
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October is peak window for Bukit/west coast contests (Rip Curl Cup August 1 - early September; Single Fin Classic mid-August; GromSearch typically October-November; LSI Grand Final September). Krui Pro and Nias Pro run June. WSL Bali Pro (Challenger Series) targeted mid-May 2026.
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SSW to SW groundswell for all Bukit contests; S/SW for Keramas (east coast wraps); SW for Sumatra Krui; SW-S for Nias Sorake

practical_info.access: Padang Padang: walk-in via cave stairs from Jl. Labuan Sait (free public access; designated VIP/media areas during contest). Uluwatu / Single Fin Classic: cliff stairs and Single Fin viewing terrace. Keramas: Komune Resort terrace public viewing; beach access free. Pererenan (LSI): direct beach access at river mouth. Krui Pro and Nias Pro: village access via 6-7 hr drive from regional airports.
practical_info.entry_fee: Spectator entry to all WSL and Rip Curl Cup events is FREE; Padang Padang beach charges standard IDR 15,000 entry + IDR 5,000-10,000 parking; Single Fin Classic and LSI events free public viewing; Komune Keramas terrace open for guests/diners
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Rip Curl Cup contest day announced ~24-48 hr in advance via Rip Curl Indonesia Instagram (@ripcurl_indonesia) and Surfline live stream; arrive at Padang Padang by 6-7 AM for finals. WSL events streamed live on worldsurfleague.com. Opening ceremonies typically held 1-2 weeks before holding period opens.
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Heats run dawn to ~4 PM; finals usually late morning into early afternoon. Padang Padang prime barrels mid-tide morning. Single Fin Classic culminates in sunset prize-giving party.
practical_info.nearby_warung: Padang Padang: Drifter Surf Cafe, Single Fin, Mango Tree, Suka Espresso. Uluwatu cliff cafes: Single Fin, Delpi Rock Lounge. Keramas: Komune Beach Club, Sea Vu Play. Canggu Pererenan: La Brisa, Pererenan Surf Cafe, La Baracca. Krui and Nias have basic local warungs and homestays.
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - all Bali contest venues land-accessible. Sumatra venues road/flight; Mentawai-related events would require boat charter.

safety_culture.hazards: Heavily-crowded lineup during opening ceremonies and finals; reef hazards as per each venue (Padang Padang: very shallow sharp reef; Uluwatu: sharp limestone; Keramas: shallow inside section; Pererenan: river-mouth sediment); jet ski rescue patrols only operate during actual contest days
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins, occasional jellyfish; reef cut infection risk; treat all cuts immediately with antiseptic
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Lineup is closed to non-invitees during heats; surfing during a running heat will get you removed; respect the holding period - locals and pros prioritize Padang Padang for tube practice in lead-up week
safety_culture.localism_intensity: High at Padang Padang and Keramas during contests; visiting surfers should not attempt to paddle out before/after heats without local approval; Single Fin Classic is more inclusive and entry-open within longboard categories
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Attend the Kecak Dance opening ceremony respectfully; do not interrupt prize-givings; Padang Padang temple sits at the gate - keep dress modest near temple; ceremonies pause for any Hindu rituals at adjacent Pura Padang Padang or Pura Luhur Uluwatu
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Mega Semadhi (2x Rip Curl Cup champion 2011, 2016); Made Bol Adi Putra (2x champion, record-4x finalist); Rizal Tandjung (Padang Padang pioneer, founder Rip Curl Indonesia); Garut Widiarta (2014 champion); Pepen Hendrik; Raditya Rondi; Betet Merta; Mustofa Jeksen; Bronson Meidy. International winners include Jack Robinson (2013/2018), Bruce Irons, Jamie O'Brien, Chris Ward. 2025 Rip Curl Cup champion Westen Hirst (Bukit local, age 16, first Indonesian man since Mega in 2016).
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: All contest days respect Nyepi (Day of Silence, typically March) - no surfing or beach access islandwide. Contests do not run on Galungan, Kuningan, or major temple anniversaries. Padang Padang opening ceremony begins with Hindu blessing.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Padang Padang/Uluwatu: Unicare Pecatu Clinic, BIMC Nusa Dua (~25-30 min), Siloam Denpasar (~45 min). Keramas: BIMC Nusa Dua or Sanglah Hospital Denpasar (~30-40 min). Krui: limited local clinic; Bandar Lampung hospital ~6 hr away.
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Yes during contest days only - Padang Padang gets multiple jet skis on contest day; WSL contests deploy water safety teams; outside contest dates no permanent rescue presence at any Bali break
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Ambulance: 118/119; BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua: +62 361 3000 911; Unicare Pecatu: +62 361 8493039; WSL events on-site medical during contest only

services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - all major events have credentialed media; freelance land/water photographers cover Padang Padang barrels and Keramas right; Rip Curl Indonesia and WSL provide official photo galleries post-event
services.photographer_recommendation: Padang Padang Surf Camp media team; Pulpo Bali; Bali Surf Photographers (Instagram); WSL official photo bank for licensed images. Freelance shoots IDR 200,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Padang Padang Surf Camp; Uluwatu Surf Villas; Kala Surf Camp; Suarga Padang Padang; The Edge Bali; Komune Resort Keramas (LSI/CT host venue); Pererenan villas; Old Man's-adjacent guesthouses; Krui: Damai Bungalows / Ujung Bocur Surf Camp; Nias: Sorake Beach homestays
services.webcam_url: Padang Padang: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/padang-padang/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d44 ; Uluwatu: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/uluwatu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708b4b ; Keramas: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/keramas/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d05 ; live WSL event streams: https://www.worldsurfleague.com/ ; Rip Curl Cup stream: https://www.ripcurl.co.id/pages/ripcurlcup-padang-padang
services.drone_allowed: Personal drones restricted during contest holding periods - only accredited media drones permitted in air-space over event area. Standard Indonesian drone permits apply outside contests.

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not applicable - events are competitions, not lessons. WSL Krui Pro 2025 was a QS 6000 with USD prize purse (prize money varies by tier). Rip Curl Cup 2023 launched a women's prize purse equal to men's (~USD 50,000 combined historically) [uncertain]
pricing.board_rental_daily: Not applicable - competitors bring own quivers; spectator surf rentals at neighboring beaches IDR 150,000-250,000/day
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Accommodation in Bukit and Canggu spikes 30-50% during Rip Curl Cup window (early August - early September) and during WSL Bali Pro window; book 4-8 weeks ahead during these periods

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Good in dry-season contest windows (May-October); Pererenan LSI venue can have river-mouth runoff after rain - rinse cuts thoroughly
sustainability.eco_certification: WSL has 'WSL PURE' sustainability initiative; Rip Curl Cup partners with local clean-up groups; Komune Resort Keramas operates with eco-resort practices including reef monitoring [uncertain]
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: WSL offers per-event sustainability messaging but no consumer-facing carbon offset for spectators; Rip Curl brand has product-level recycling programs (Search the Surf)

events_community.annual_competition_window: Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang: holding period August 1 - early September each year (22-year history since 2003, invitation-only, ~16 men + 8 women invitees; 2025 winners Westen Hirst and Jasmine Studer, both age 16). WSL Krui Pro: June 10-17 in West Lampung Sumatra (QS 6000 since 2025; 2025 men's winner Reef Heazlewood AUS, women's winner Lucy Callister AUS). WSL Nias Pro: June 10-17 at Sorake Beach, South Nias (QS event). Liga Surfing Indonesia (LSI / Bintang Crystal LSI): national circuit with multi-leg regular season and Grand Final - LSI 2025 Grand Final at Pererenan September 26-28; LSI 2024 Grand Final at Uluwatu October 18-20. Uluwatu Single Fin Classic: longboard/single-fin invitational, mid-August (6th annual held August 16, 2025). Rip Curl GromSearch International Final: junior surfers age 16-and-under, typically held in Bali (recent edition November 13-17, 2024, Keramas; 2024 winners Lukas Skinner GB and Eden Walla USA). Bali Surfing Championships: April 9-11, 2026 (Keramas Beach, divisions for Pro and Amateur). Indonesian National Surf Series Bali Leg: August 20-22, 2026 [uncertain]. WSL Challenger Series Bali Pro 2026: May 14-16 (location Keramas vs Uluwatu pending official WSL confirmation) [uncertain]. WSL CT has previously activated emergency Uluwatu CT (last in 2024 replacing canceled Western Australia leg, won by Willian Cardoso) but 2026 CT calendar does not currently list a permanent Bali CT stop.
events_community.community_events: Rip Curl Cup opening ceremony (early August) at Single Fin with Kecak Dance, Hindu blessing, live music, invitee introductions; LSI opening events feature live music and grom clinics; Single Fin Classic culminates with cliff-top awards party; WSL events bring expat surf community gatherings; Bali Ocean Week (La Brisa-hosted, June-July) combines beach clean-ups with surf contest and music; viewing parties at Single Fin (Uluwatu) and Old Man's (Canggu) during major event finals

basic_info.name: Surf Forecast Tools for Bali (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪預報工具; Indonesian: Alat Prakiraan Selancar Bali)
basic_info.gps_location: Not applicable - online services. Coverage targets Bali surf spots between approx. -8.42 to -8.82 latitude, 114.78 to 115.55 longitude
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Not applicable - online resource accessible from anywhere

surf_conditions.wave_type: Tools cover every wave type in Bali: point break (Uluwatu, Padang Padang, Bingin, Medewi, Keramas, Balian), reef break (Dreamland, Balangan, Impossibles, Shipwrecks, Lacerations, Sanur, Nusa Dua), beach break (Canggu Batu Bolong/Berawa/Echo, Kuta, Legian, Pererenan)
surf_conditions.skill_level: All levels - forecasts inform skill-appropriate spot selection (e.g., Surfline assigns Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced/Expert ratings per spot; Swellnet has detailed skill notes; Baliwaves provides plain-language reports useful for beginners)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Reported swell heights typically 1-15 ft for Bali spots; deep-water buoy data and model output 5-25 ft offshore swell during big southwest pulses
surf_conditions.best_season: Tools are used year-round; peak query traffic May-October (dry season, west coast working) and during big Indian Ocean storm cycles July-September; east-coast forecasts (Keramas, Sanur, Nusa Dua) get most attention November-April
surf_conditions.best_wind: Forecasts focus on identifying offshore wind windows - SE trade winds for west-coast Bukit/Canggu in dry season; W/NW wind windows for east coast Keramas in wet season; most tools display wind speed (knots) + direction + gusts hourly
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: Bali forecasts emphasize SW (Southwest) groundswell tracking; also S, SSW for Bukit lefts; W/WSW pulses for west coast points; tools display swell period (seconds) and direction (degrees) as critical decision metrics
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (Indo dry season consistency means most reliable forecast tools achieve high accuracy; ECMWF/GFS models typically reliable 3-5 days out, with 7-10 day extended view useful for trip planning)
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: Forecast tools indirectly drive crowds - perfect-rated swells on Surfline/Magicseaweed produce instant lineup spikes at Uluwatu, Padang Padang and Canggu; serious surfers monitor multiple sources to find less-publicized windows

practical_info.access: Web browsers (any device) and dedicated iOS/Android apps; some tools require account registration. Free tiers offer basic forecast (typically 5-day) and reduced cam access; paid Premium tiers unlock 16-day forecasts, ad-free cams, cam rewind/replay, and forecaster notes.
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Tools update multiple times daily - Surfline cams and reports refresh every 15-30 min; Windguru/Windy refresh 4x daily following GFS/ECMWF model runs (00z/06z/12z/18z UTC); plan to check forecast the night before dawn patrol (after 12z run = ~8 PM Bali time) and again 30 min before paddle-out for live cam
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Pre-dawn (4-6 AM local) cam check critical for dawn patrol decisions; 6 PM local for next-day planning (matches new model run availability)
practical_info.nearby_warung: Not applicable
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Most tools include nearby-spot listings - Surfline 'Nearby Spots' suggestion, Magicseaweed (now Surfline) location maps, Swellnet regional pages help pivot when primary spot is blown out
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Not applicable to tools; tools do indicate when boat-access spots (Shipwrecks/Lacerations Lembongan, Desert Point Lombok, Mentawai) are working

safety_culture.hazards: Forecasting hazards: false confidence in model output (Bali microclimate can defy general models, especially around the Bukit cliffs); over-reliance on one source; outdated cams during cloud cover; forecaster notes can lag big sudden swells
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Forecast tools indirectly affect etiquette - 'Surfline-rated 5-star' days create heavy crowds; etiquette becomes more important on hyped days
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Not applicable to the tool itself; high-profile forecasts drive crowds that can trigger localism flare-ups at marquee spots
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Forecasters and cam operators sometimes ask users not to publicize secret spots; balicams.com and Surfline omit some sensitive Bukit hidden gems on purpose
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Tools occasionally embed weather warnings (tropical storms, tsunami advisories) - BMKG (Indonesian Meteorological Agency, https://www.bmkg.go.id) is the authoritative source for severe weather and tsunami alerts; general emergency 112 in Indonesia

services.surf_photographer_available: Several tools partner with or integrate photo services - Surfline cam screenshots, Bali Surf Report photo service, Baliwaves daily photo galleries
services.webcam_url: Primary Bali surf cams: Surfline (paid Premium gives best resolution + rewind) - Uluwatu https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/uluwatu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708b4b ; Canggu Batu Bolong, Berawa, Old Man's, Echo, Keramas, Balian, Bali Village all live on Surfline. Balicams (https://balicams.com) - free live cams: Uluwatu, Impossibles, Bingin, Dreamland, Balangan, Padang Padang, Kuta, Nelayan, River Mouth Canggu, Keramas, Balian, etc. Swellnet (https://www.swellnet.com/reports/indonesia/bali) - paid cams + 16-day forecast. Padang Padang Surf Camp web cam page; Komune Resort Keramas cam (occasionally on social).
services.drone_allowed: Not applicable to forecast tools

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not applicable
