pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Forecast tool subscription pricing (as of 2025-2026): Surfline Premium USD 15.99/month or USD 119.99/year (US pricing, effective April 8, 2025; previously USD 9.99/mo); Surfline free tier shows 5-day forecast and limited cams. Surfline Premium+ tier (with additional perks) higher. Swellnet PRO USD 7.33/month for ad-free 16-day forecast, premium cams, forecaster notes. Windguru free for basic GFS forecasts; Windguru PRO ~EUR 5/month for higher-frequency updates, extra models, custom spots. Windy.com free with high-resolution ECMWF 9km; Windy Premium ~USD 19/year for 15-day ECMWF, longer forecast, no ads. Baliwaves free (ad-supported). Magicseaweed officially merged into Surfline (MSW.com redirects, app sunset May 2023 - features migrated to Surfline). BMKG Indonesian government weather free. Windfinder, Windy.app, Stormglass, Surf-Forecast.com offer overlapping free/paid tiers.
pricing.board_rental_daily: Not applicable
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: No seasonal pricing variation - subscriptions flat year-round; Surfline raised US prices April 8, 2025; annual plans cheaper than monthly (Surfline ~37% saving vs monthly)

sustainability.eco_certification: Not applicable to digital tools; some sites publish responsible-surfing content (Surfline content team, Swellnet editorial). Baliwaves has long-running 'know-your-spot' editorial supporting sustainable travel.
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Not applicable - digital service; data centers powering these tools are typically not carbon-offset disclosed

events_community.annual_competition_window: Forecast tools highlight major Indo events - Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang (Aug 1 - early Sept holding period), WSL Krui Pro, Mentawai Pro - Surfline often hosts live event streams + special forecaster commentary; Swellnet provides Indo coverage; Baliwaves serves long-time local audience
events_community.community_events: Surfline community forums; Swellnet active forum community (Australian-focused but Indo threads frequent - 'Java, Bali, Lombok, Sumbawa forecast' thread by Craig is a long-running reference); Baliwaves blog and Instagram daily updates; balicams.com chat community

basic_info.name: Surf Guide & Boat Charter from Bali (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪嚮導與船宿包船; Indonesian: Pemandu Selancar & Sewa Kapal Bali)
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (departures cluster from Sanur Harbor for Nusa Lembongan/Penida; Padang Bai for Lombok Desert Point; Benoa Harbor for multi-day liveaboards heading east to Sumbawa/Sumba/Rote or west to Mentawai via Padang)
basic_info.gps_location: Key embarkation points - Sanur Harbor (-8.6849, 115.2625, Sanur Pier for fast boats to Lembongan/Penida); Padang Bai (-8.5320, 115.5085, ferry/speedboat to Lombok); Benoa Harbor (-8.7466, 115.2122, larger liveaboards/yachts to Mentawai/Sumbawa)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Sanur Harbor ~17 km / 40 min; Padang Bai ~50 km / 1.5-2 h; Benoa Harbor ~5 km / 15 min

surf_conditions.wave_type: Service spans every wave type - access to left points (Desert Point Lombok, Lacerations Lembongan), right reefs (Shipwrecks Lembongan, Playgrounds Penida, Lakey Pipe Sumbawa), barrels (Desert Point, Lakey Pipe, Hollow Trees / HT's Mentawai), beach breaks and big-wave spots
surf_conditions.skill_level: Beginner-friendly day trips (Lembongan Playgrounds, Bali airport reef tours) through to advanced/pro charters (Desert Point, Mentawai, Sumbawa Lakey Pipe, Rote T-Land)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-15+ ft depending on destination - Lembongan day trips work 2-6 ft; Desert Point fires at 4-10 ft; Mentawai season holds 3-15 ft
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October is peak (consistent SW groundswell, offshore SE trade winds); July-September is the prime window for Desert Point, Mentawai and Sumbawa; April and October are shoulder months with good conditions and lower prices
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) groundswell typical from Indian Ocean storms; S and SSW also light up reefs at Lembongan, Penida, Desert Point and Mentawai
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (Indonesian Indo-Pacific is one of the most consistent swell zones globally during dry season)
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: Variable - day-trip spots Lembongan/Penida get crowded with surf-guide groups especially July-August; remote charter destinations (Mentawai, Sumbawa, Rote, Sumba) are designed to escape crowds and typically share lineups only with other charter boats

practical_info.access: Day surf-guide service: client picked up by car/van from Bali accommodation, transported to break or harbor, return same day. Lembongan day trip: drive to Sanur Harbor + 30-min fast boat (16+ daily sailings 7:30 AM - 5:00 PM) + private charter boat / scooter access to breaks. Lombok Desert Point: overnight crossing of Lombok Strait, anchor at the spot for 6+ AM start. Sumbawa: either overnight boat from Bali (Sri Noa Noa, Partama-style phinisi) or fly DPS-Bima (USD 50-150) + taxi 500,000-600,000 IDR to Lakey Peak. Mentawai: fly DPS-Padang via Jakarta, then fast ferry (~IDR 400,000 VIP, $25) or slow ferry ($15, 10-12 h) + dinghy to charter boat.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Boat-in delivery is standard for offshore/island spots - tender (dinghy) drops surfers in channel; for mainland Bali day-guide service, paddle-out follows the spot's normal route (Uluwatu cave, Bingin stairs, Canggu beach)
practical_info.entry_fee: Sanur Harbor surf-boat charter typically includes entrance fees; some Lembongan breaks have informal local fees (IDR 20,000-50,000); Bali tourist levy IDR 150,000 applies to all foreign arrivals; Sumbawa Lakey area no formal fee; Mentawai charter fees are folded into the per-trip price [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Early start essential - most surf charters from Sanur depart 6:30-7:30 AM for first session at 8 AM; multi-day liveaboards run 2-3 sessions/day following tide and wind windows
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Dawn patrol after overnight crossings - Desert Point boat trips typically arrive 7:30 AM; Lembongan/Penida day trips push to first surf by 9 AM to maximize 6-hour window before fast boat return
practical_info.nearby_warung: Sanur Harbor lined with cafes and warungs at the pier; on Nusa Lembongan and Penida warungs are plentiful in main villages (Jungutbatu, Toyapakeh); Mentawai/Sumbawa charters provide on-board catering (3 meals + snacks)
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Lembongan cluster (Shipwrecks, Lacerations, Playgrounds within ~10 min boat); Penida Reef Beach/Suwehan/Bingin Penida; on Bali mainland, guide-led tours commonly chase swell between Canggu - Bukit - Keramas in one day
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Yes for offshore/island destinations - Nusa Lembongan/Penida reachable by fast boat then private charter; Lombok Desert Point only viable via boat (no road access); Mentawai/Sumbawa remote spots boat-in only; Rote, Sumba, Sumbawa secret-spot trips run as multi-day liveaboards (Jiwa Alam, Sri Noa Noa, Partama, Dreamweaver, Mojo)
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: Highly suited to multi-island combos - Bali + Lembongan/Penida + Lombok Desert Point + Gilis is the classic 7-10 day surf-island-hop charter; Bali to Sumbawa boat trips often add Sumba and Rote; Mentawai routes are typically standalone 10-14 day charters out of Padang, Sumatra

safety_culture.hazards: Open-water crossings (Lombok Strait, Selat Badung between Bali-Lembongan known for strong currents), shallow reefs at destination spots, malaria risk in Mentawai/Sumatra (prophylaxis recommended), boat safety standards vary widely between operators
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Reef cuts on coral/limestone; stonefish and urchins in Mentawai/Sumbawa lagoons; jellyfish blooms in some seasons; saltwater crocodile risk at remote Sumba/Rote river mouths [uncertain]
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong tidal currents in Lombok Strait and Badung Strait; channel currents at Shipwrecks and Lacerations can pull surfers north toward Mushroom Bay; Desert Point has powerful inside section sucking water back over reef
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Charter culture - respect order in which boats arrive; do not rush off the tender; charters typically coordinate with other boats to avoid overcrowding marquee spots; on Lembongan respect Balinese local crew and other guides; at Desert Point lineup hierarchy is intense with pros and long-time charters getting priority
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low to medium for charter destinations (Mentawai/Sumbawa/Rote are remote with light localism); Desert Point can have medium-high localism on big days; Lembongan/Penida generally welcoming but locals expect respect and the guides' priority
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Use Balinese/Indonesian polite greetings (om swastiastu / selamat pagi); tip crew at trip end (USD 100-300 per guest is standard on Mentawai liveaboards); follow captain's safety briefings; do not litter; respect ceremonies on Lembongan (especially Mepeed and Nyepi)
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Famous Indonesian surfers tied to charter destinations: Mega Semadhi (Bukit/Bingin local but charters east), Garut Widiarta (Bukit), Marlon Gerber, Pepen Hendrik (Sumbawa local), Dede Suryana, Rio Waida; legendary expat charter captains including Martin Daly (Indies Trader, pioneered Mentawai exploration)
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Charters do not depart on Nyepi (Day of Silence, March - all activity halts islandwide for 24 h); avoid Hindu temple ceremonies at Sanur/Padang Bai harbors; respect Sasak Muslim culture in Lombok/Sumbawa (more conservative dress on shore); Mentawai Indigenous communities require respect for sacred sites (uma houses)
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Sanur Harbor area: BIMC Sanur, Siloam Denpasar within 20 min; Lembongan has only basic clinic (Klinik Nusa Lembongan) - serious injuries require medevac speedboat to Bali; remote charter destinations require evacuation by boat then air to Bali/Singapore - travel insurance with surf and medevac cover (World Nomads, SafetyWing, DAN) is essential
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Sanur Harbor to BIMC Sanur ~3 km; Lembongan clinic to Bali (medevac by boat + ambulance) ~25-30 km; Mentawai charters >100 km from nearest hospital in Padang
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: Some private charters carry jet ski for safety/photo at premium spots (Desert Point, Sumbawa Lakey Pipe, Mentawai HT's); Lembongan day trips typically rely on tender boat for rescue [uncertain]
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general 112; SAR (Search & Rescue) 115; BIMC Sanur +62 361 3000 911; Sanur Harbor master office (logged at dock); satellite phone or InReach recommended on remote liveaboard charters

services.nearest_surf_school: Many surf-guide operators also run schools - Bali Surf Tours, Bali Surf Guides, Bali Surf Coaches, Padang Padang Surf Camp, Kala Surf Camp, Boddhi Surf School (Uluwatu), Outpost-style coaching combined with guiding common
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - most premium charters include photo/video package (drone + water + land); typical add-on USD 50-150/day or included in Mentawai/Sumbawa liveaboard rates
services.accommodation_options: Day-guide service: client uses own Bali base. Multi-day land-based: Sanur, Canggu, Uluwatu hotels/villas. Boat liveaboards: cabins on phinisi/catamaran/motor-yacht (Dreamweaver, Sri Noa Noa, Partama, Mojo, Jiwa Alam, Indo Yachts private surf safari, Outer Reef boats). Lembongan land+boat hybrids stay in villas at Jungutbatu or Mushroom Bay.
services.webcam_url: Surf-guide operators use Surfline (https://www.surfline.com), Windguru (https://www.windguru.cz), and balicams.com for daily decision-making; Surfline has dedicated Shipwrecks/Razors Lembongan cam (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/shipwrecks-and-razors/60809b1e80e9542553416c8c)
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed on charters in open water (most remote spots are far from no-fly zones); within 15 km DPS airport buffer (Kuta/Legian/Seminyak/parts of Canggu) restricted; January 2025 DGCA Directive 008/2025 ended 250g exemption - SIPP-TA registration required before flight

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Day surf-guide: USD 45-60 per person (1-on-1 / semi-private, 2 hours on water). Full-day driver-guide with car (1-6 persons): USD 60 or IDR 1,100,000 (8 hours). Solo upcharge typically +USD 15-30.
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Surf guide / coaching (mainland Bali): IDR 500,000-900,000 per session. Lembongan one-day surf charter (min 4 pax): USD 95-150/person including lunch, water and transfers (Bali Surf Tours / Bali Surf Guides). Lembongan two-day surf charter: USD 135/person with overnight + meals. Bali Island day surf tour (min 4 pax): from USD 25/person. Private speedboat half-day Lembongan/Penida: USD 500-700; full-day USD 900-1,400. Mid-size motor yacht / sailing catamaran day-trip: USD 1,500-3,500. Luxury private yacht: USD 3,000-5,000+ per day. Private surf yacht charter rates ~USD 642/hour with 4-hour minimum (typical Bali Charter Boat tariff).
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 150,000-300,000/day for shortboards on Bali; charter packages typically include limited rental quivers but bringing own boards is standard; boats often charge IDR 50,000-100,000 per board for transport [uncertain]
pricing.gopro_rental_price: IDR 150,000-300,000/day at most surf schools; many charters include GoPro footage as add-on [uncertain]
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: Lombok-Sumbawa surf safari (10 nights private boat): from USD 950/person sharing or USD 10,500 private charter (Indo Yachts, Stoked Surf Adventures Mojo). Mentawai 10-day charter: USD 1,800 (Ombak Kabau budget) to USD 5,000 (The Addiction luxury); typical mid-range USD 2,500-4,000 per trip. Mentawai 12-day luxury USD 3,000-7,000+ per person.
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Typical charter inclusion: all meals + snacks, beer/soft drinks (often included or low fee), boat transfers, surf guiding, accommodation (cabin on boat or villa), tender/jet ski deployment, basic snorkel gear. Exclusions: international flights, Bali tourist levy IDR 150,000, alcohol beyond included, tips (USD 100-300 to crew is typical), travel insurance, surfboards (BYOB usually expected), photo packages (often add-on).
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Peak July-August premium 10-25%; Christmas/New Year premium 15-30%; April/May and October/November shoulder months often 10-20% cheaper; some Mentawai operators offer single-cabin walk-on rates in shoulder season at significant discount

sustainability.eco_certification: No standardized surf-charter eco certification in Indonesia; some operators (Boddhi Surf, Sri Noa Noa, Awave Travel) emphasize sustainable practices - reef-safe sunscreen, no anchoring on reef, reusable water bottles, Marine Megafauna observation protocols [uncertain]
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Rarely offered by Indonesian operators; international booking platforms (Perfect Wave Travel, Wavehunters, Awave Travel, BookSurfCamps) occasionally include optional carbon-offset add-on at checkout [uncertain]

events_community.annual_competition_window: Mentawai Pro / Krui Pro typically May-August; Lakey Peak Sumbawa Pro varies; Rip Curl Cup at Padang Padang Aug 1 - early Sept; many charter operators are accredited media partners or supply event accommodations
events_community.community_events: Boat-charter community gathers seasonally at Komune Beach Club Keramas (post-Bali Pro parties), Single Fin Sunday on Bukit, and at Lakey Peak surf-camp hub during peak Sumbawa season (June-August)

visa_logistics.visa_type_recommended: 30-day VoA / e-VoA sufficient for most one-week to one-month charter trips; B211A 60-day visit visa (extendable to 180 days) recommended for guests combining multiple Indo charter trips or extended Bali stay
visa_logistics.surfboard_air_baggage: Bali domestic legs (Garuda, Lion Air, Wings Air, Citilink, Super Air Jet) to Padang (Mentawai gateway) or Bima/Sumbawa typically charge IDR 200,000-500,000 per board bag; international Garuda Indonesia includes 1 surfboard bag up to 23 kg free with most economy fares; AirAsia and Jetstar charge USD 50-150 per board bag depending on route; advance booking essential
visa_logistics.surf_travel_insurance: Strongly recommended - World Nomads Explorer Plan and SafetyWing Nomad Insurance both cover board damage/loss + reef-cut medical + emergency medevac; DAN (Divers Alert Network) offers add-on coverage useful for charter/boat-based trips; check policy for boat/yacht limits and remote-area medevac

basic_info.name: Surf Photography & Videography in Bali (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪攝影; Indonesian: Jasa Foto Selancar Bali)
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (primary clusters: South Bukit - Uluwatu/Padang Padang/Bingin/Balangan; West Coast - Canggu Batu Bolong/Berawa/Echo/Pererenan; East Coast - Keramas; West Bali - Medewi)
basic_info.gps_location: Multi-location service category, not a single GPS pin. Key meet-up points: Single Fin Uluwatu (-8.8154, 115.0884); Bingin clifftop warung area (-8.8060, 115.1130); Old Man's Batu Bolong, Canggu (-8.6593, 115.1336); Keramas beach in front of Komune Resort (-8.5867, 115.3201); Medewi point (-8.4225, 114.7790).
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Varies by shoot location: Uluwatu/Padang Padang 18-22 km; Canggu 22-25 km; Keramas 38-42 km; Medewi 75 km

surf_conditions.wave_type: N/A - service category; photographers cover all wave types (point break, reef break, beach break, left/right, barrel/wall) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.skill_level: Service is offered to all skill levels from beginner (Batu Bolong, Padang Padang Right, Balangan) to advanced (Uluwatu, Padang Padang Left, Keramas, Desert Point)
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October for west-coast Bukit (Uluwatu, Padang Padang, Bingin) and offshore-wind clean conditions; wet season November-April better for east coast (Keramas, Sanur, Nusa Dua, Serangan); shoulder months April and October offer best light + manageable crowds for premium shoots
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (lineups at Uluwatu, Padang Padang, Bingin and Canggu Batu Bolong are very crowded mid-morning; photographers prefer dawn patrol or late-afternoon golden-hour sessions for cleaner frames)

practical_info.access: Most services are mobile - the photographer meets the client at the surf spot (parking, beach entry, or surf-school base) at an agreed time, usually 30 minutes before paddle-out. Water photographers paddle out with the surfer; land photographers shoot from cliff (Uluwatu, Bingin) or shore (Canggu, Keramas); drone operators shoot from cliffs or boats.
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (sunrise to ~9 AM) and golden-hour late-afternoon (4 PM to sunset) deliver the best light and least crowded lineups; midday avoided due to harsh overhead light and peak crowd. Most published Bali surf-photo packages are 1.5-2 hour sessions.
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Sunrise dawn-patrol session (~5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy water, golden light, and minimal crowd; late afternoon (4:00 PM to sunset) for warm tones and silhouette work
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Photographers are flexible - if primary spot is blown out, they reposition to nearest alternative (e.g., Uluwatu -> Padang Padang/Bingin; Canggu Batu Bolong -> Berawa/Echo/Pererenan; Keramas -> Sanur/Serangan)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No for mainland Bali; Yes/optional for shoots at boat-access spots (Nusa Lembongan, Nusa Penida Playgrounds, Lombok Desert Point, Mentawai charters) where photographer either rides the boat or shoots from a chase tender

safety_culture.hazards: Water photographers face same hazards as surfers - shallow reef (Uluwatu, Padang Padang, Bingin, Keramas), strong currents, board-strikes from inexperienced surfers, sea urchins; drone operators must avoid surfers, cliffs and temples; land shooters on Bukit cliffs face fall risk and slippery stairs
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Reef cuts (sharp limestone karang on Bukit), occasional sea urchins, rare jellyfish/sea snake; water photographers wear booties and fins, carry hard-housing on Liquid Eye-style grips
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Water photographers must respect priority - they do not paddle for waves, stay outside the takeoff zone, do not block locals. Recommended to communicate with the client surfer on which waves are theirs to avoid confusion. Many Bukit locals dislike unknown water photographers shooting their waves without permission, so courtesy matters.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium - established Bali photographers know lineup politics and rarely have issues; visiting photographers should brief themselves on each spot's culture, especially Uluwatu Peak and Padang Padang on big swells
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Ask permission before shooting locals' waves; avoid filming children/women in temple-adjacent areas; never fly drones over Uluwatu Temple or active ceremonies; do not block the channel paddle-out
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Drone flight restricted within 5 km of sacred sites without Banjar (village council) consent; no shoots on Nyepi (Day of Silence, typically March - islandwide silence including beaches); avoid shooting during temple ceremonies (odalan) at Tanah Lot, Uluwatu Temple, Petitenget.

services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - hundreds of freelance and agency-based land photographers, water photographers, drone operators and full-service videographers operate across Bali. Major hubs: Bukit (Uluwatu, Padang Padang, Bingin), Canggu, Medewi, Keramas.
services.photographer_recommendation: Notable services: Island Vision Surf (full-service photo/video/drone agency, Mengwi-based, covers all Bali); Padang Padang Surf Camp (in-house water/land/drone shooters at Bukit); BjMs (Medewi specialists); Indo Eye (drone/cinema agency); Bali Drone Pro / Surf Eye Bali (drone-focused, Canggu); Boddhi Surf School (Uluwatu coaching + filming); Bali Surf Vision @bali_surf_vision (Instagram-based freelancer); Bali Surf Report Photo Service (Padang Padang); Chandra's Surfing Bali (Legian, budget option); Suka Surf Bali (Canggu add-on); Made Topia (Bingin local, also wedding/architecture); freelance shooters stationed at Single Fin (Uluwatu) and Old Man's (Canggu) cliffside.
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for licensed/registered operators; as of January 2025 DGCA Directive 008/2025 ended the 250g registration exemption - all drones must be registered via SIPP-TA before flight. 15 km no-fly buffer around DPS airport (covers Kuta, Legian, Seminyak, parts of Canggu/Berawa); flights near sacred sites (Uluwatu Temple, Tanah Lot, Pura Petitenget) need Banjar consent; daylight only, line-of-sight only.
services.webcam_url: Photographers commonly reference Surfline (https://www.surfline.com) and balicams.com (https://balicams.com) live cams to time their shoots; some photographers also publish daily lineup highlights on Instagram

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not applicable (photography service - no surf lesson). Typical service pricing: IDR 100,000 to IDR 5,000,000 per session depending on package (land vs water vs drone, solo vs group, edit turnaround).
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Photography pricing tiers observed in Bali (2025-2026): Budget land/cliff shooter at Bukit or Canggu freelancer: IDR 100,000-300,000 per session or per-photo IDR 50,000-150,000 (Chandra's Surfing Bali ~IDR 100,000; Suka Surf Bali add-on ~IDR 250,000). Mid-tier surf-school in-house package: IDR 300,000-800,000 per session (Bali Surf Report ~IDR 550,000). Premium professional agency: in-water photography IDR 2,500,000 / landscape photography IDR 1,500,000 / drone videography IDR 2,500,000 (Island Vision Surf, 2-hour cap, additional participants IDR 500,000-1,000,000 each). Padang Padang Surf Camp drone session ~GBP 50 (~IDR 1,000,000). Bali destination photographer hourly rates from USD 200-325+ for non-surf lifestyle/portrait shoots (Localgrapher, Flytographer, SweetEscape).
pricing.board_rental_daily: Not applicable (photography service)
pricing.gopro_rental_price: IDR 150,000-300,000/day for GoPro + SD card at most Canggu and Bukit surf schools (not the same as professional photo service but a budget alternative for self-shot footage) [uncertain]
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Most Bali surf photographers maintain flat year-round rates; some premium agencies surcharge 10-20% during July-August peak and Christmas/New Year window; deposit normally 50% with refund if cancelled 24h+ in advance [uncertain]

sustainability.eco_certification: No standardized eco-certification for surf photography services in Bali; informal best practices include avoiding single-use plastic, not chasing wildlife with drones, respecting Banjar/temple boundaries [uncertain]
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Not commonly offered by Bali surf photographers as of 2026 [uncertain]

events_community.annual_competition_window: Major events that boost photo-service demand: Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang (holding period August 1 - early September), Krui Pro / Nias Pro / Mentawai charter season May-September, WSL Bali Pro / Krui CT events when scheduled at Keramas; many agencies are accredited media partners
events_community.community_events: Single Fin Sunday at Uluwatu (weekly sunset gathering - popular for surf-portrait sessions); Old Man's Wednesday parties Canggu; Komune Resort Night Surfing at Keramas (artificial-light night surf - unique photo opportunity, runs select evenings during dry season)

basic_info.name: Surf Schools in Bali - Major Operators (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪學校 - 主要業者; Indonesian: Sekolah Selancar di Bali). Coverage: Rip Curl School of Surf (Legian + Sanur), Odysseys Surf School (Kuta), Pro Surf School & Camp (Kuta/Tuban), Dian Surf School (Canggu Batu Bolong), WaveHouse Bali (Canggu Berawa + Uluwatu/Bingin).
basic_info.gps_location: Rip Curl School of Surf Legian: -8.7042, 115.1700 (Jl. Padma Utara, Legian) + Sanur branch at -8.6857, 115.2628. Odysseys Surf School: -8.7268, 115.1696 (Mercure Hotel Arcade, Jl. Pantai Kuta). Pro Surf School & Camp: -8.7232, 115.1689 (Jl. Pantai Kuta, between Wyndham Garden and The Stones Hotel, Halfway Kuta). Dian Surf School: -8.6535, 115.1330 (Batu Bolong Beach, Canggu). WaveHouse Canggu: -8.6605, 115.1395 (5 min from Berawa Beach); WaveHouse Uluwatu: -8.8101, 115.1100 (cliffs above Bingin Beach).
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Rip Curl Legian ~9 km / Sanur ~13 km; Odysseys Kuta ~5 km; Pro Surf School Kuta ~5 km; Dian Surf School Canggu ~17 km; WaveHouse Canggu ~17 km / Uluwatu Bingin ~17 km. All within 25-40 min taxi from DPS depending on traffic.

surf_conditions.wave_type: Lessons primarily on beach-break/sand-bottom waves at Kuta/Legian/Berawa/Batu Bolong (sand bottom, gentle whitewash on inside) for beginners; intermediate progression sessions move to Padang Padang Right, Balangan inside, Old Man's, or Sanur on the right swell. Rip Curl Sanur branch runs Level 2+ on reef breaks; WaveHouse Uluwatu operates at intermediate-friendly Bingin/Padang Padang Right.
surf_conditions.skill_level: Beginner to advanced; most lessons cater to absolute beginners (white-water then green-wave progression); intermediate/improver classes available at all five operators; advanced coaching (Pro Surf School, WaveHouse, Rip Curl Sanur, Dian Surf School improver program) offered.
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Lessons run 1-3 ft typically (small inside white-water and reformed waves); progression sessions 2-4 ft; intermediate guided sessions up to 4-6 ft at Old Man's/Padang Right.
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sand at primary beginner beaches (Kuta, Legian, Berawa, Batu Bolong, Sanur sand pocket); reef/coral at intermediate progression spots (Padang Padang Right, Bingin, Sanur Hyatt Reef).
surf_conditions.best_season: Year-round operation; dry season May-Sep offers cleaner west-coast surf (Kuta/Legian/Canggu); wet season Oct-Mar suits east-coast lessons (Sanur, Rip Curl Sanur branch, Keramas guided trips); operators flexibly switch coasts based on conditions.
surf_conditions.best_tide: Beginner-friendly mid-to-high tide at sand-bottom beaches (Kuta/Legian/Berawa/Batu Bolong); intermediate sessions follow each spot's tide window.
surf_conditions.best_wind: West coast schools prefer morning offshore E winds before SE/SW onshore picks up; east coast (Rip Curl Sanur) prefers offshore W winds in wet season.
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW for west coast lessons; E/SE for east coast (Sanur) lessons; not normally a constraint for beginner classes which use any small windswell.
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (Bali coasts deliver near-daily beginner-suitable surf year-round via coastal-switching strategy)
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 4-5 at primary beginner beaches in high season (Kuta sandbar morning sessions extremely busy; Canggu Old Man's lessons crowded; surf-school zones marked by colored rashguards/buoys)

practical_info.access: All operators run pickup/drop-off from major tourist zones (Kuta/Legian/Seminyak/Canggu/Uluwatu/Sanur) included or for small fee; lessons typically meet at school office/beach base; WaveHouse and Pro Surf School operate as combined camps with on-site accommodation.
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Morning sessions 7:00-10:00 AM (cleanest conditions, calmer wind); second sessions 10:00 AM-1:00 PM; afternoon sessions 2:00-5:00 PM still available though onshore winds typical; dawn-patrol private lessons available on request.
practical_info.nearby_warung: All five operators are in dense tourist zones with abundant warungs, cafes, bars; Pro Surf School and WaveHouse have on-site restaurants/bars; Odysseys is at Mercure Hotel arcade with cafe access; Dian Surf School is on Batu Bolong with dozens of beachfront warungs and cafes adjacent.
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: All operators have backup-spot protocols: Kuta operators (Rip Curl Legian, Odysseys, Pro Surf) shift between Kuta/Legian/Seminyak sandbars or transport to Canggu/Sanur; Canggu operators (Dian, WaveHouse Canggu) move between Batu Bolong/Berawa/Pererenan/Echo Beach; WaveHouse Uluwatu rotates Bingin/Padang Padang Right/Balangan/Dreamland.

safety_culture.hazards: Beginner crowd density (collision risk in marked surf-school zones), sharp fins/boards in crowded lineups, jellyfish blooms (Oct-Mar wet season), strong rip currents at Canggu/Kuta (school zones avoid main rip channels), sunburn/dehydration, occasional reef cuts during intermediate progression sessions, scooter accidents in transit.
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Jellyfish (Portuguese man o' war and box jellyfish occasionally during wet-season N winds), sea urchins at intermediate reef breaks (booties recommended for progression sessions), no shark issues, occasional sea snakes; first-aid kit and trained staff standard at all five operators.
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Schools enforce zone-segregation - colored rashguards, designated learn-to-surf areas marked by buoys/flags at Kuta/Legian/Sanur; students taught right-of-way basics before paddling out; intermediate progression students given lineup-etiquette briefings; respect for free-surfers and other students emphasized.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low at school-zone beaches (Kuta/Legian/Berawa/Batu Bolong/Sanur sand pocket); medium at intermediate spots; schools have working relationships with locals at Padang Right, Bingin, Sanur Reef, Old Man's.
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Tip instructors (IDR 50,000-150,000 typical per lesson if happy); arrive on time; respect equipment (don't drop boards on rocks); follow instructor instruction in water for safety; do not paddle outside zone before being passed up to intermediate.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Rip Curl Legian/Odysseys/Pro Surf: BIMC Kuta hospital 5-10 min, Siloam Denpasar 15-20 min; Dian/WaveHouse Canggu: BIMC Nusa Dua via toll road ~30 min, Siloam Denpasar 25 min, Penta Medical Canggu 5 min; WaveHouse Uluwatu: Unicare Pecatu 5 min, Siloam Denpasar 30-40 min via toll road.
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - school zones rely on instructor water-safety coverage (1 instructor per 2-4 students standard ratio); WaveHouse advertises 1:2-3 ratio; Rip Curl 1:4; Odysseys 1:4 group, 1:1 private; rescue boards on beach standard.

services.board_rental_availability: All five operators rent foam soft-tops and progress-level boards by hour/day to existing students; Pro Surf School and WaveHouse offer broader rental quivers; Dian Surf School has a rental shop on Batu Bolong; Odysseys rents from Mercure base. Rates ~IDR 75,000-150,000/hour or IDR 150,000-300,000/day for soft tops.
services.surf_photographer_available: Rip Curl, Pro Surf, WaveHouse, Dian Surf School all offer in-house land/water photographers for lesson documentation; photo packages typically included in private-lesson upgrades or sold separately IDR 200,000-500,000/session; Odysseys offers GoPro and photo packages.
services.photographer_recommendation: In-house photographers at all five schools; for higher-end portfolio shooting, schools can refer freelance shooters (Bali Surf Photo, freelance Indonesian shooters via Instagram). [uncertain on per-shooter rates]
services.accommodation_options: Pro Surf School & Camp operates on-site camp accommodation (private rooms + dorms with sea-facing balconies, pool, restaurant, under IDR 1,500,000/night standard); WaveHouse operates Surf & Stay camps in Canggu (Berawa) and Uluwatu (Bingin) with private/shared rooms; Rip Curl School of Surf is co-located with Ovolo Mamaka hotel (Surf Academy package); Odysseys partners with Mercure Hotel arcade; Dian Surf School partners with nearby Canggu villas/homestays.
services.webcam_url: Schools use Surfline cams for Kuta (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/kuta-beach), Canggu Batu Bolong (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu-batu-bolong), Berawa, Bingin and Padang Padang Right for lesson planning; live cam access often included in WaveHouse member portal. [uncertain on operator-specific cams]
services.drone_allowed: Schools typically allow personal drone use during lessons for student footage; commercial filming requires permit; some Canggu beaches have local restrictions during Hindu ceremonies. [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Kuta operators near Outpost Kuta (limited), Hubud (now closed - moved to Ubud); Canggu operators near Outpost Canggu, Tropical Nomad, BWork, Dojo Bali; WaveHouse Uluwatu near Outpost Ulu, ZIN Cafe Bingin, several work-friendly cafes.

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Group lesson 2-hr average IDR 600,000-770,000 (USD 40-50) across the five operators; semi-private USD 50-65; private 1-on-1 USD 65-90 (Rip Curl private from USD 73; Odysseys from USD 35 group). Pro Surf School group from IDR 500,000-750,000; Dian Surf School group from IDR 400,000; WaveHouse single-session pass from ~USD 45-60.
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Rip Curl: group 2-hr IDR 700,000 + 10% tax (USD 50+); private from USD 73 (IDR ~1,150,000). Odysseys: group from USD 35 (IDR ~550,000); semi-private ~USD 50 (IDR ~780,000); private ~USD 70 (IDR ~1,100,000). Pro Surf School: group IDR 500,000-750,000; private IDR 900,000-1,250,000. Dian Surf School: from IDR 400,000 group; private IDR 750,000-1,000,000. WaveHouse single sessions ~IDR 700,000-1,000,000; mostly sold as multi-day Surf & Stay packages.
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 150,000-300,000/day soft-top; IDR 200,000-400,000/day shortboard or longboard depending on operator and season.
pricing.gopro_rental_price: IDR 150,000-300,000/session at most operators including SD card; some offer GoPro+photo package IDR 250,000-500,000.
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: Rashguards included free in all lessons; wetsuit tops (1mm-2mm) for occasional cool wet-season mornings rented IDR 50,000-100,000/day; full wetsuits not common in Bali (water 27-29 C). [uncertain]
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: Schools handle minor dings in-house for own quivers; for student/guest boards: IDR 150,000-400,000 for minor dings; IDR 500,000-1,500,000 for structural repairs; recommended shops: Drifter Surf Shop Seminyak repair, Naruki Surf Repair Canggu, local glassers in Kuta gang.
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: Pro Surf School & Camp: ~USD 600-1,200/week including 5-6 lessons + accommodation + transfers; WaveHouse Surf & Stay: ~USD 800-1,500/week (Canggu) to USD 1,000-1,800/week (Uluwatu) including 5-6 sessions + accommodation + video analysis + bike + insurance + breakfast; Rip Curl Surf Academy at Ovolo Mamaka: ~USD 700-1,400/week; Odysseys partner-hotel packages: ~USD 500-1,000/week; Dian Surf School works on lesson-only basis with referral to nearby accommodations.
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Typical inclusions: 5-6 group lessons (2 hrs each), all equipment (soft-top boards, rashguard, leash, surf wax), accommodation (varies by tier), breakfast (most camps), airport transfer (some), video/photo analysis (WaveHouse standard, others optional), free bike rental (WaveHouse Uluwatu), basic insurance (WaveHouse); Pro Surf School includes pool access, on-site restaurant credit, daily yoga at some camps. Alcohol, lunch/dinner, advanced sessions, and tips not typically included.
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Peak season Jun-Aug + Dec-Jan commands 15-30% premium; shoulder Apr-May/Sep-Nov standard rate; low Feb-Mar (wet season) some operators offer 10-20% discount but Kuta-Legian-Sanur rotation continues lessons year-round. [uncertain on exact percentages]

sustainability.eco_certification: Pro Surf School and Rip Curl School of Surf participate in Coastal Cleanup beach drives (Beach Clean Bali events); WaveHouse markets sustainability ethos (reef-safe sunscreen, reusable water bottles, plastic-free policy); Dian Surf School and Odysseys participate in local cleanups but no formal industry certification visible. [uncertain on third-party certifications]
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Not standard; some camp packages partner with travel-offset platforms on opt-in basis. [uncertain]

events_community.annual_competition_window: Schools host internal student contests/grad days; participate in ISA Indonesia events, Coca-Cola Bali Open and Krui Pro qualifying events; Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang invitational held annually in Aug-Oct windows is sponsored by Rip Curl parent brand. [uncertain on individual school competition calendars]
events_community.community_events: Beach cleanups (Bye Bye Plastic Bags partnerships), International Women's Surfing Day events at Canggu (June), free intro days at Old Man's, surf-yoga retreats, full-moon student parties at Pro Surf School and WaveHouse, weekly grom sessions for Indonesian kids at Rip Curl School of Surf (community engagement program).

Tabanan_Soka.name: Soka / Soka Beach / Pantai Soka (Tabanan)
Tabanan_Soka.gps_location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/?q=-8.5867,114.9897 (approx. -8.5867, 114.9897, Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk, Antap, Selemadeg, Kabupaten Tabanan, Bali 82162) [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 60-70 km (about 2 hours by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport; ~40 km west of Denpasar) [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-100 min by scooter (about 50-60 km on the Denpasar-Gilimanuk road); From Uluwatu: ~2-2.5 hours by scooter [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.wave_type: Predominantly a long left-hand point/reef-and-cobblestone break on a swelly black-sand bay; A-frame peaks also reported on smaller days; sits between Medewi (cobblestone left point) and Balian (rivermouth) on the west coast [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (strong currents, exposed west-coast swell, remote setting, not for beginners)
Tabanan_Soka.wave_height_range: Works best around 3-6 ft (waist to overhead); can hold bigger SW swells but tends to get unruly and walled with size [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.bottom_type: Mixed: predominantly black sand and cobblestone/rock with some shallow reef sections at the point [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.best_season: Dry season (April-October) for cleaner offshore winds and more organised swell; works year-round given west-coast exposure but wet season brings onshore W/NW winds and runoff [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.best_tide: Mid to high tide preferred (low tide exposes cobblestones/reef and gets sucky); similar tide window to Medewi/Balian [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.best_wind: Light offshore E to SE wind (typical SE trade in dry season is offshore for west-facing breaks) [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (catches plenty of west-coast swell like Balian/Medewi but very wind- and tide-dependent and rarely groomed) [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.wave_length_meters: approx. 50-150 m on a good point set; not as long as Medewi's 300-500 m left [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.wave_speed_rating: medium (mellow to medium-paced on the point sections; can be punchy/closeout on bigger beach-break days) [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate - shifty peaks, occasional drop on bigger sets, cobblestones/rock under foot at low tide; less critical than Bukit reef but unforgiving in size [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.secondary_peaks: A-frame peaks scatter along the long black-sand bay either side of the main point; rivermouth sandbars form seasonally; no clearly named inside/outside section [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.peak_crowd_level: 1-2/5 (one of the least crowded surfable spots in Bali; you can often surf alone or with 2-3 locals)
Tabanan_Soka.access: Drive west on Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk; turn off the main road at the Soka rest area / Bulung Daya area and walk down to the black-sand beach. Car or scooter access; private car/driver recommended due to distance from south Bali [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.paddle_out_method: Beach paddle-out across the black sand; for the point/reef section paddle around the cobblestones via the channel; rivermouth sandbars give easier entry on smaller days [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.entry_fee: No formal entrance fee reported; small parking donation ~IDR 5,000-10,000 to local warung/parking attendant [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol to mid-morning before the onshore W/NW seabreeze fills in; late afternoon glass-offs possible in dry season
Tabanan_Soka.best_time_of_day: Early morning (sunrise to ~9-10 am) before wind switches onshore
Tabanan_Soka.nearby_warung: Soka Indah rest area (Indonesian buffet/cafeteria popular with tour buses), a few small local warungs along Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk selling rice plates, coffee, coconuts; very limited compared to Canggu/Bukit
Tabanan_Soka.alternative_spot_within_10min: Within 10-20 min drive: Balian Beach (rivermouth, more consistent) to the east; further west Pulukan and Medewi point break; Kedungu and Yeh Gangga also broadly in Tabanan area
Tabanan_Soka.hazards: Strong currents and rips, cobblestone/rock bottom that bruises feet, exposed open-ocean sets, remote location with no lifeguards, limited mobile signal in places, runoff/debris after heavy wet-season rain
Tabanan_Soka.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the rocks, occasional jellyfish, stonefish possible on reef/sandy bottom (rare), no significant shark issue reported; treat reef cuts with fresh-water flush and antiseptic and seek clinic if deep [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.reef_cut_severity_index: Medium - mostly cobblestone/rock rather than razor coral; bruises and scrapes more common than deep coral cuts; reef boots advisable at lower tides [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.rip_current_pattern: Strong sweep along the open bay; sandbar/rivermouth rips can pull surfers outside; classic west-coast longshore current especially in bigger swells [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.lineup_etiquette: Very low-key village vibe; greet locals, do not paddle around, respect priority on the point, share waves; the few regulars are mostly Tabanan locals and West Bali surf-camp guests
Tabanan_Soka.localism_intensity: Low (cited 1-2/10) - rarely crowded enough for serious localism; friendly fishing-village atmosphere similar to Balian/Medewi [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.respect_protocol: Greet locals with senyum (smile) and small talk, do not litter on black sand, support local warungs/parking attendants, dress modestly when walking to/from beach (it is a quiet local area)
Tabanan_Soka.resident_local_legends: No single iconic local legend tied specifically to Soka; broader Tabanan/Medewi/Balian local crew (e.g. Made Winada family network in west Bali) occasionally surf here [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.religious_sensitivity: Quiet Hindu fishing/farming village; respect nearby temples and ceremonial processions on Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk; observe Nyepi (silent day) when no surfing/movement is allowed island-wide; menstruating women traditionally asked to avoid temple grounds
Tabanan_Soka.medical_nearby: Nearest clinics: Puskesmas Selemadeg (basic local clinic) and Puskesmas Antap; for serious injuries Tabanan General Hospital (RSUD Tabanan) ~25 km east, or BIMC/Siloam in Denpasar ~40 km east [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Puskesmas Selemadeg approx. 3-5 km; RSUD Tabanan approx. 25 km; BIMC/Siloam Denpasar approx. 40-50 km [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue on standby
Tabanan_Soka.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Bali Search and Rescue (BASARNAS) 115; RSUD Tabanan +62 361 811-027 [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.nearest_surf_school: No surf school directly at Soka; closest options are Pondok Pitaya Surf & Yoga at Balian Beach (~20-30 min west) and small Medewi-based instructors; lessons typically arranged via accommodation
Tabanan_Soka.board_rental_availability: No on-beach board rental at Soka itself; rent in Balian (Pondok Pitaya, local shops) or Medewi before driving over; some accommodations supply boards [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.surf_photographer_available: No regular land/water photographer stationed at the lineup
Tabanan_Soka.photographer_recommendation: Freelance west-Bali based photographers can be hired by appointment via Instagram (Balian/Medewi-based shooters); rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.accommodation_options: Bali Beach Glamping (~4 km), Nirjhara Resort, Uluaman Eco Luxury Resort, Waka Gangga (Yeh Gangga), Soka Indah Bungalows; further afield Pondok Pitaya at Balian (~20-30 min) and Made's Homestay Medewi
Tabanan_Soka.webcam_url: No dedicated surf cam for Soka; check nearest Balian forecast at Surfline (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/balian/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d41) as best proxy [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.drone_allowed: Generally allowed - very few people on the wide black-sand bay; respect privacy of locals and avoid flying over nearby Pura (temples) and ceremonial sites [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.coworking_nearby: No coworking on or near the beach; closest options are in Canggu (Tropical Nomad, Outpost, B Work) ~75-100 min east [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.avg_lesson_price: Not offered at Soka directly; nearest lesson pricing via Pondok Pitaya Balian or Medewi instructors approx. IDR 350,000-600,000 per 2-hour group lesson [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.board_rental_daily: Not available at Soka itself; in Balian/Medewi approx. IDR 75,000-150,000 per day [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season due to remoteness and very low development; can carry sediment, palm fronds and river runoff in heavy wet-season rain (Pulukan/Balian rivers nearby) [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.annual_competition_window: No regular WSL/national competition window at Soka itself; nearest event is occasional local longboard contests around Medewi/Balian [uncertain]
Tabanan_Soka.community_events: No regular surf community events at Soka; some monthly beach clean-ups organised by Tabanan-based NGOs and west-Bali surf camps [uncertain]

Thomas_Beach.name: Thomas Beach / Pantai Thomas (locally sometimes called the 'real' Padang Padang Beach)
Thomas_Beach.distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 18-20 km (40-50 min by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport) [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~70-90 min by scooter; From Uluwatu (Suluban/Single Fin): ~5-10 min by scooter (located between Padang Padang and Suluban on Jalan Labuan Sait)
Thomas_Beach.wave_type: Reef break over coral. Predominantly a left-hander on the south end and a smaller right/peak in front of the homestay; breaks more abruptly and uniformly across the reef than Uluwatu/Padang so rides are shorter [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.skill_level: Beginner-friendly on small clean mid-high tide days; quickly becomes intermediate-to-advanced once the swell or current picks up. Sharp coral and steep drops make it unsafe for true beginners in size
Thomas_Beach.wave_height_range: 1-4 ft (waist to head high); rarely surfed cleanly above head high because current and Uluwatu wash dominate
Thomas_Beach.bottom_type: Coral reef with shallow rocky patches; low-tide tide pools
Thomas_Beach.best_season: Dry season (April-October) when consistent SE trade winds keep it clean; wet season (Nov-Mar) tends to be onshore and choppy. Locally not recommended in rainy season due to wind, current and slippery stairs
Thomas_Beach.best_tide: Mid to high tide (mid-tide best for surfing; high tide reduces frequency but covers reef; low tide exposes reef and is unsafe)
Thomas_Beach.best_wind: Offshore SE trade winds (typical dry-season Bukit pattern)
Thomas_Beach.best_swell_direction: S to SW (Indian Ocean groundswell, same window as Uluwatu/Padang)
Thomas_Beach.swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (picks up the same swell as Uluwatu and Padang but quality is more swell/tide sensitive; often only good on smaller cleaner days)
Thomas_Beach.wave_length_meters: approx. 30-80 m (short punchy ride, much shorter than Uluwatu/Padang) [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.wave_speed_rating: medium (less peeling than Uluwatu; tends to break uniformly across the reef)
Thomas_Beach.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate - steep drops over shallow coral can cause injury; not as critical as Uluwatu Race Track but more punishing than a true beach break
Thomas_Beach.secondary_peaks: A softer inside reform usable by progressing surfers on smaller days; spillover/whitewater from Uluwatu and Padang sets pushes through on bigger swells [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.peak_crowd_level: 2/5 - usually one of the least crowded beaches in the immediate Uluwatu/Padang cluster thanks to the staircase access; can get more lively at weekends and on small clean days
Thomas_Beach.access: Long, steep concrete staircase descent (~130-175 steps reported across sources) from Jalan Labuan Sait; 5-10 min walk from a small alleyway entrance near Suka Espresso. Steps are uneven, some without handrails and slippery when wet
Thomas_Beach.paddle_out_method: Paddle out from the sandy section through the reef channel during mid-high tide; do not attempt over the dry reef at low tide
Thomas_Beach.entry_fee: Parking IDR ~5,000 (scooter); a small unofficial dirt parking area near Suka Espresso. Beach access free
Thomas_Beach.recommended_session_time: Mid morning or late afternoon during a mid-high rising tide; avoid the middle of the day at low tide
Thomas_Beach.best_time_of_day: Late afternoon for offshore SE winds and softer light; early morning also works to beat the crowds at neighbouring spots
Thomas_Beach.nearby_warung: Several small beach warungs selling Indonesian rice/noodle dishes, fresh coconuts and cold drinks (IDR 25,000-50,000); rustic restrooms at the warungs. Suka Espresso and many Uluwatu/Padang cafes are at the top of the stairs
Thomas_Beach.alternative_spot_within_10min: Padang Padang (5 min walk north along Labuan Sait), Suluban / Uluwatu (5-10 min south), Bingin (~10 min scooter); Impossibles between Bingin and Padang
Thomas_Beach.hazards: Strong currents - especially in the middle of the beach and on bigger swells from Uluwatu wash; shallow coral patches; steep slippery stairs; no lifeguards; sea urchins; small but real risk of being swept south toward Uluwatu cliffs on outgoing tide
Thomas_Beach.marine_hazards: Sea urchins (very common in tide pools), occasional jellyfish, coral cuts from reef contact; no resident large shark issues reported here - flush wounds with fresh water and disinfect promptly
Thomas_Beach.reef_cut_severity_index: Moderate to high - live coral reef with shallow patches especially at low tide; reef boots strongly advised for surfing and tide-pool walking
Thomas_Beach.rip_current_pattern: Persistent rip running south toward the Uluwatu cliff from the middle of the beach; stronger on outgoing tide and big swells; identifiable as a darker, calmer 'lane' between breaking waves [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.lineup_etiquette: Mellow vibe - mostly intermediate guests of Thomas Homestay and Padang Padang regulars; respect priority on the inside, no snaking, smile and say hi
Thomas_Beach.localism_intensity: Low - one of the most relaxed lineups in the Uluwatu cluster, but the locals from neighbouring Padang Padang carry weight
Thomas_Beach.respect_protocol: Greet local surfers and warung owners, keep noise down (homestays directly above), pack out trash, support beach warungs with a coconut or meal
Thomas_Beach.resident_local_legends: No single iconic local pro tied uniquely to Thomas Beach; surf-camp coaches from nearby Padang Padang Surf Camp and Uluwatu locals frequent it [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.religious_sensitivity: Close to Uluwatu Temple area - dress modestly when walking up to the road; respect Nyepi silent day island-wide; small cliff-side offerings (canang sari) should not be disturbed
Thomas_Beach.medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (24h, closest); BIMC Nusa Dua; Siloam Hospital Denpasar for serious injuries
Thomas_Beach.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu approx. 4-5 km; BIMC Nusa Dua approx. 10-12 km [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue at this spot; nearest organised lifeguard presence is at Suluban/Uluwatu on busy days
Thomas_Beach.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; BASARNAS Search & Rescue 115; Unicare Clinic Pecatu +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.nearest_surf_school: Padang Padang Surf Camp, Rapture Surfcamps Padang Padang, and various Uluwatu surf schools operate guided sessions here; a few small unbranded schools run lessons directly on Thomas Beach during good tide windows
Thomas_Beach.board_rental_availability: Limited on-beach rental (a few warungs/operators rent soft-tops and longboards on busy days); fuller rental shops 2-5 min up the road in Padang Padang and Uluwatu
Thomas_Beach.surf_photographer_available: No regular dedicated photographer stationed at Thomas; nearby Padang Padang and Uluwatu photographers will occasionally shoot here on request [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.photographer_recommendation: Freelance Uluwatu-based water photographers can be hired by appointment via Instagram; rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.accommodation_options: Thomas Homestay (the original cliff-top homestay that gave the beach its name) and several other clifftop homestays/villas directly above the beach; broader Uluwatu/Padang accommodation 2-5 min walk away (Padang Padang Inn, Mu Bali, Bingin Garden, etc.)
Thomas_Beach.drone_allowed: Generally allowed; respect privacy of homestay guests and avoid flying directly over warungs and temple offerings [uncertain]
Thomas_Beach.coworking_nearby: No coworking on the beach itself; closest cafe-coworking in Uluwatu/Bingin area (e.g. Suka Espresso, Drifter Surf cafe); Canggu coworking spaces ~1 hour by scooter
Thomas_Beach.water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season; can carry sediment and trash after heavy rains in wet season; clearer than west/north Bali but worse than Nyang Nyang due to homestay drainage above [uncertain]

basic_info.name: Bali Transport for Surfers (Chinese: 峇里島衝浪客交通; Indonesian: Transportasi untuk Peselancar Bali) - covering private driver/charter car IDR 700K-1M/day, scooter/motorbike rental with surf rack IDR 60-100K/day + rack IDR 20-50K/day, IDP International Driving Permit motorbike endorsement requirements, ride-hailing apps (Grab, Gojek, Maxim, InDrive, Bluebird/MyBluebird), and airline surfboard baggage rules for Garuda Indonesia, AirAsia, Singapore Airlines, China Airlines, EVA Air
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (airport DPS to Canggu/Berawa/Pererenan, Bukit/Uluwatu/Bingin/Padang Padang, East Coast Keramas/Sanur, North Coast Medewi)
basic_info.gps_location: Ngurah Rai International Airport DPS: -8.7482, 115.1672; Bluebird Taxi prepaid counter: International Arrival Hall DPS; Grab official airport lounge: inside DPS International Arrival; Canggu (scooter rental hub): -8.6478, 115.1378; Pererenan: -8.6310, 115.1170; Uluwatu/Pecatu (Bukit scooter rental hub): -8.8290, 115.0880; Sanur: -8.6900, 115.2620
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: DPS to Kuta/Legian: 3-6 km / 15-25 min; DPS to Seminyak: 8-10 km / 25-40 min; DPS to Canggu/Berawa/Batu Bolong: 18-22 km / 60-90 min (rush 90-120 min); DPS to Pererenan/Seseh: 22-26 km / 75-100 min; DPS to Uluwatu/Pecatu: 18-22 km / 45-70 min; DPS to Bingin/Padang/Balangan: 15-20 km / 40-60 min; DPS to Sanur: 12-15 km / 30-45 min; DPS to Keramas: 35-40 km / 75-100 min; DPS to Medewi: ~75 km / 2.5-3 h

surf_conditions.wave_type: N/A - transportation/logistics topic; relevant to enabling access to all wave types from beach breaks (Canggu/Kuta) to reef breaks (Uluwatu/Bingin) to long-drive west coast (Medewi/Balian) and east coast (Keramas)
surf_conditions.skill_level: N/A - all surf levels need transport; beginner-friendly: pre-booked driver to Kuta/Canggu lesson schools; intermediate: scooter rental with rack for self-navigation around Canggu/Bukit; advanced: long charter trips to multiple breaks (Medewi day-trip, East Coast dawn patrol)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: N/A
surf_conditions.bottom_type: N/A
surf_conditions.best_season: Year-round - transport options stable across seasons. Driver rates may increase 10-20% during peak Dec-Jan and Jul-Aug; scooter rentals stable year-round; long charter to seasonal coasts (west coast in dry, east coast in wet) defines route choice
surf_conditions.best_tide: N/A
surf_conditions.best_wind: N/A
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: N/A
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: N/A
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: N/A
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: N/A
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: N/A
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: N/A
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: Canggu traffic peaks 7-9 AM and 4-7 PM (school runs, sunset crowds); Uluwatu Jl. Labuansait gridlock at sunset; DPS arrival queue worst on Bali flights from Australia evening blocks; Saturday Padang Padang lane impassable at peak hours - allow 2-3x normal travel time

practical_info.access: FROM DPS: pre-book private driver via WhatsApp (~IDR 350-500K airport transfer to Canggu, IDR 200-400K to Bukit), Bluebird metered taxi (prepaid at counter or via MyBluebird app, ~IDR 200-400K to Canggu/Bukit, no pickup from arrival hall due to taxi syndicate but legal from departure or via app), Grab/Gojek from official airport lounge (~30-40% cheaper than counter taxi). FOR SURFER SCOOTER: rent from Canggu/Uluwatu shop with delivery; ride only with IDP + motorcycle endorsement + helmet. LONGER TRIPS: book driver day-charter (Medewi, Keramas, East Bali) IDR 700K-1M for 10-12h.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: N/A
practical_info.entry_fee: N/A (no entry fee for transport services themselves); parking at surf spots IDR 5,000 (scooter) to IDR 10,000-20,000 (car) per session at most Bali surf breaks
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Driver pre-booking: 24-48h ahead for next day; airport transfers: book 2-3 days ahead; long-day charters (Medewi etc.): 1 week ahead in peak season; scooter rental: book 1-3 days ahead via WhatsApp with delivery to villa; airline surfboard add-ons: book online 7+ days before flight (cheaper than airport rate)
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Scooter for dawn patrol Canggu/Uluwatu: leave 5:00-5:30 AM to beat traffic and catch glassy conditions; private driver for long East Bali/Medewi: depart 4:30-5:00 AM. Avoid Canggu traffic 7-9 AM and 4-7 PM windows for any car movement.
practical_info.nearby_warung: All major surf areas have warung within 100m of typical pickup points; airport has full food court
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: If Grab/Gojek blocked at venue: walk 100-300m to Circle K, Indomaret, or main road; if Bluebird unavailable: try Maxim or InDrive (cheaper, fare-negotiable model); if scooter breakdown: most rental companies offer 24h roadside support via WhatsApp
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No for land transport; boat charter only for inter-island moves (Lembongan/Penida/Gili/Lombok) where surfers commonly use Eka Jaya, Scoot Fast Cruises, OK Fast Cruise from Sanur or Padang Bai

safety_culture.hazards: MOTORBIKE: Bali has Indonesia's highest tourist road fatality rate. Risks: gravel/sand on roads, drainage grates, rogue stray dogs, rain-slick scooters, drunk weekend drivers, head-on overtake collisions on narrow roads. WEAR HELMET ALWAYS. CAR: traffic jams cause missed surf windows; choose driver familiar with backroad shortcuts. AIRPORT: taxi-syndicate touts at arrival hall - politely refuse and walk to Grab lounge or prepaid Bluebird counter.
safety_culture.marine_hazards: N/A for transport itself; relevant only insofar as accident-related injury treatment
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: N/A
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: N/A
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Driver/scooter parking at surf spots: tip parking attendant IDR 5-10K to ensure board safety; never block locals' access; greet driver with 'Selamat pagi'; offer water/snacks on long day-charters
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium-high at venue level: local taxi/ojek cooperatives in Canggu (Batu Mejan/Echo Beach) and Uluwatu (Pandawa, Padang lane) enforce 'No Grab/Gojek' zones with hand-painted signs and territorial pressure on app drivers. Workaround: walk 100-300m to neutral pickup point.
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Avoid arguing with local taxi reps at no-Grab zones - walk to neutral pickup; tip private driver IDR 50-100K for good service; pay drivers on time; respect that ojek cooperatives provide income to local families; never haggle aggressively on already-fair rates
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Reputable Bali driver agencies: BaliBelin, Pery Bali Driver, Bali Nusa Transport, Bali Holiday Secrets, The Young Villas; reputable scooter rental: Bikago, Balitubes, GFS-Bike, Canggu Scooter, Bali Bike House, Tmax Rental [uncertain on relative ranking]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Nyepi Day of Silence (around March 19, 2026 [uncertain exact date]): NO transport allowed on roads for 24 hours - airport closed, no scooter/car/Grab. Plan to be in accommodation. Galungan/Kuningan: heavy ceremony traffic, expect delays. Some Balinese drivers off-duty during Odalan temple ceremonies in their home village.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Canggu: Penta Medical Canggu, Hydro Medical Canggu; Kuta: BIMC Hospital Kuta (24h), Siloam Kuta; Nusa Dua/Bukit: BIMC Nusa Dua, Unicare Pecatu Clinic; Sanur: Siloam Hospital Bali Denpasar; for serious injury: BIMC Nusa Dua has international evacuation coordination to Singapore
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: From Canggu: Penta Medical ~1-3 km, BIMC Kuta ~8 km; from Uluwatu/Pecatu: Unicare Pecatu ~3-5 km, BIMC Nusa Dua ~15 km; from DPS: BIMC Kuta ~3 km; from Sanur: Siloam Denpasar ~3 km [uncertain]
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: N/A
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general: 112; tourist police: 110; ambulance: 118 or 119; Bluebird dispatch: +62 361 701111; Grab/Gojek emergency SOS button in app; BIMC Kuta: +62 361 761 263; BIMC Nusa Dua: +62 361 3000 911 [uncertain]

services.nearest_surf_school: Not transport service directly - but many surf schools include transfer to lessons (Padang Padang Surf Camp, Balitubes pickup from villa)
services.board_rental_availability: Many rental shops deliver boards to villa via scooter or car - typical delivery fee IDR 50-100K within 5-10 km radius
services.surf_photographer_available: Some surf photographers travel by scooter to spots; pickup by photographer's car for full-day shoots possible at premium [uncertain]
services.photographer_recommendation: N/A - covered under photography research item
services.accommodation_options: Most villas/surf camps include free airport transfer with stays of 5+ nights; budget homestays charge IDR 250-400K for airport pickup
services.webcam_url: Surfline cams (https://www.surfline.com) help surfers and drivers decide route based on which coast is firing - useful tool for day-charter planning
services.drone_allowed: N/A for transport
services.coworking_nearby: Outpost Canggu, Outpost Ulu, Tropical Nomad, Dojo Bali, B Work Bali offer scooter parking; some have on-site scooter rental partnerships

pricing.avg_lesson_price: N/A for transport
pricing.board_rental_daily: N/A for transport
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Private drivers: peak Dec-Jan / Jul-Aug rates +10-20%; scooter rentals: largely stable but weekly/monthly discount 30-50% vs daily; airline surfboard fees: same year-round but seat availability tighter peak season; ride-hailing surge pricing on Grab/Gojek during rain or peak demand (2-3x normal fare)

sustainability.water_quality_rating: N/A
sustainability.eco_certification: Few transport operators carry formal eco-certification [uncertain]; some EV scooter rental options emerging in Canggu (e.g., Niu electric, Smoot) - quiet and zero local emissions but limited range (~70km)
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Most airlines (Garuda, Singapore Airlines, EVA, AirAsia, China Airlines) offer voluntary carbon offset at booking; private driver companies rarely offer offsets [uncertain]

events_community.annual_competition_window: N/A
events_community.community_events: N/A

visa_logistics.visa_type_recommended: All visa types permit driving with valid IDP (VoA, e-VoA, B211A/C1, E33G KITAS); KITAS holders may convert IDP to local Indonesian SIM Internasional after 6 months
visa_logistics.monthly_living_cost_idr: TRANSPORT MONTHLY BUDGET: Scooter rental monthly IDR 700K-1.2M + petrol IDR 200-400K = IDR 900K-1.6M/month (USD 60-105); private driver day-trips IDR 700K-1M each (used 4-6x/month ~IDR 3-6M); ride-hailing daily users ~IDR 1.5-3M/month; Bluebird daily ~IDR 2-4M/month
visa_logistics.tourist_levy: Tourist levy (IDR 150K) separate from transport; KITAS holders exempt
visa_logistics.customs_declaration: Surfboard bags must be declared in All Indonesia e-Arrival Card customs section if carrying commercial quantity; personal surfboard for own use is standard tourist baggage - declare on customs form. Up to USD 500 personal effects allowance.
visa_logistics.surfboard_air_baggage: GARUDA INDONESIA (FLAG): generous - 23 kg of additional free sports equipment baggage; board bag must not exceed 23 kg or 300 cm; aircraft constraints - CRJ max 230 cm, ATR max 160 cm (relevant for connection flights to Lombok/Sumbawa). AIRASIA (LCC): Free Surfboard Policy on international flights to/from Australia (1 board up to 15 kg free); other international routes including Taipei-Bali, KUL-DPS, BKK-DPS: pre-book 'Sports Equipment' online (MYR 100-250 / USD 20-55 per board up to 200 cm); domestic AirAsia: full fee by weight. Always pre-book online (airport rate 30-50% more expensive). SINGAPORE AIRLINES: surfboards count toward standard checked baggage allowance (Economy typically 30 kg, Premium Eco 35 kg); board bag must not exceed 200 cm length, 75 cm width, 80 cm height - boards over 200 cm subject to oversize charge; pre-notify 48h. CHINA AIRLINES (Taiwan/CI): surfboard treated as oversize sports baggage; standard Economy 30 kg allowance applies if within limit; oversize handling fee per leg ~USD 100-150 [uncertain]; max length typically ~300 cm; require padded board bag, fins removed. EVA AIR (Taiwan/BR): up to 2 boards per bag accepted; dimensions ≤277 cm (109 in): NO oversize charge - this is the surfer-friendly headline; dimensions 277-292 cm (109-115 in): charged at 2 pieces of excess baggage rate; >292 cm: accepted only if cargo hold allows. Standard 30 kg Economy weight allowance. EVA Air is generally considered surfer-friendly for Taipei-Bali route due to the 277cm free oversize threshold. GENERAL TIPS: pre-book online (often half the airport price); use padded board bag/coffin; remove fins; check aircraft type for short-haul connections; pre-notify 48h; consider Garuda for direct domestic Bali-Lombok-Sumbawa connections.
visa_logistics.idp_motorbike_license: IDP (International Driving Permit) is MANDATORY for foreigners driving any motorized vehicle in Indonesia, including scooters/motorbikes. CRITICAL: IDP must include motorcycle category endorsement (Class A2 per 1949 Convention or Class A per 1968 Convention) AND home-country driver's license must show motorcycle authorization. A car-only license + IDP does NOT cover scooter riding. ENFORCEMENT (2026): Bali police task force runs daily checkpoints in tourist corridors (Canggu Berawa, Uluwatu Jl. Labuansait, Kuta Legian) targeting foreigners on scooters. On-the-spot fines IDR 250,000-1,000,000 for lacking IDP/STNK/helmet. INSURANCE: riding without proper IDP + motorcycle endorsement INSTANTLY VOIDS travel insurance (World Nomads, SafetyWing etc.) - any crash injury becomes 100% out-of-pocket. Motorbike crashes are the #1 cause of tourist hospitalization in Bali. WHERE TO GET IDP: from home country auto club BEFORE travel (AAA in US, Taiwan DMV, AA in UK, AAA Singapore, JAF in Japan, KAMA in Korea); in-country IDP from Indonesia possible but bureaucratic. Always wear helmet (legal requirement, IDR 250,000 fine if not). For surfers using board racks: extra caution at checkpoints, secure straps to prevent fall risk.
visa_logistics.surf_travel_insurance: All surf travel insurance (World Nomads, SafetyWing, IMG, Allianz, etc.) requires valid IDP + motorcycle endorsement for scooter accident claims. Verify before purchase: (1) motorcycle/scooter activity must be explicitly included, (2) requires valid IDP, (3) requires helmet worn at time of accident, (4) some policies require ≤125cc engine size. Coverage typically includes emergency medical, evacuation to Singapore (USD 500,000+ recommended), reef cut treatment, board bag baggage cover. For E33G KITAS holders: travel insurance often NOT accepted by immigration - need proper international health insurance (Cigna Global, IMG Global, Allianz Care).

basic_info.name: Uluwatu (Chinese: 烏魯瓦圖; Indonesian: Uluwatu)
basic_info.gps_location: -8.8154, 115.0884 (Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Uluwatu)
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: 18-22 km (approx. 45-60 min drive)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~60-75 min by motorbike (~30-35 km); From Uluwatu town: 0 min (this is the namesake spot, ~5 min from Pecatu)

surf_conditions.wave_type: Left-hand point/reef break (world-class). Multiple peaks along same reef: Outside Corner, The Peak (Main Peak), Racetracks, Temples, The Bombie, Inside
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced/Pro (Temples inside section is the most forgiving; Outside Corner is Pro-only on 8ft+ days)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 3-15 ft (most consistent 4-8 ft; Outside Corner switches on at 8 ft+; epic at 6-10 ft)
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp coral/limestone reef (karang)
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-August); shoulder season late September-October offers best value with consistent swell and fewer crowds
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide for inner peaks (Peak, Racetracks, Temples); low tide for Outside Corner. Surfable on all tides depending on section.
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE (Southeast) trade winds typical in dry season
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) groundswell; also handles S and SSW
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (ultra-consistent; one of the most reliable spots on the planet during dry season)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 100-300+ meters depending on section and swell; long rides linking sections possible on bigger days [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium to fast (Racetracks section is notably fast and hollow; Temples is slower and more forgiving)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high difficulty; steep drop on bigger days especially at Outside Corner and The Peak; shallow reef under takeoff zone requires precise positioning
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - Outside Corner (outside), The Peak (main), Racetracks (south section), Temples (inside/closer to cliff), The Bombie (offshore big-wave peak), Inside
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (very crowded, especially when surf is small/head-high); thins out at 6 ft+. Most crowded 9am-4pm in dry season.

practical_info.access: Drive to clifftop parking at end of Jl. Mamo (off Jl. Labuansait), walk ~200m down stairs through warungs/shops on cliff face, then narrow stairs through the iconic cave to reef/beach. Three staircase options as backup for big days.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Exit cave at high tide and paddle straight out through the channel; at low tide walk ~100m across sharp reef before paddling. Channel between Peak and Racetracks is the main paddle-out channel.
practical_info.entry_fee: Parking: ~IDR 5,000-10,000 motorbike, IDR 10,000-20,000 car; no surf entry fee [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy conditions and lighter crowds; sunset session for golden-hour photos
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Before 8 AM (dawn patrol) to avoid crowds; or late afternoon after 4 PM when day-trippers leave
practical_info.nearby_warung: Numerous warungs and cafes along the cliff stairs (Single Fin, Delpi Rock Lounge, Mango Tree Cafe, Suka Espresso, Drifter Surf Cafe nearby in Pecatu)
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Padang Padang (5 min), Bingin (10 min), Impossibles (10 min), Balangan (15 min by motorbike)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - land access via stairs and cave

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp shallow reef, strong currents, crowded lineup, waves pounding cliff face at high tide near cave entry, reef cuts, occasional sea urchins
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (wear booties), occasional jellyfish, rare sea snakes; treat reef cuts immediately with antiseptic and check tetanus status; no shark issues
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: High - sharp limestone/coral karang reef; booties strongly recommended especially at low tide and through Racetracks section
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong rip current runs from inside reef out through the channel between The Peak and Racetracks - useful for paddle-out but dangerous if caught while exiting. Currents drag surfers south toward Padang Padang on big days.
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Strict priority system, wait your turn, do not paddle around or snake locals. Smile and say hi to locals. Do not drop in - locals will call you out. Be patient when crowded.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium to High - lineup is competitive especially at The Peak; respect Balinese locals and long-term expats; localism more pronounced on epic swell days
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Show respect to local Balinese surfers, do not snake or burn waves, wait your turn, acknowledge priority, no dropping in. Avoid arrogance - many locals are pro-level.
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Mega Semadhi (2016 Rip Curl Cup champion, Bingin/Bukit local), Rizal Tandjung, Made Switra, Marlon Gerber, Bol Adyaksa, Garut Widiarta - many of Indonesia's top surfers grew up surfing the Bukit
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Luhur Uluwatu (cliff temple) sits directly above; respect sacred site, dress modestly when visiting temple, do not climb temple walls. Nyepi (Day of Silence, typically March) - no surfing or beach access allowed islandwide. Avoid surfing during temple ceremonies.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Unicare Pecatu Clinic (closest, ~5-10 min); BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (~25-30 min); Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~45 min)
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu Clinic: ~3-5 km
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski permanently stationed; safety patrols during Rip Curl Cup event only
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Ambulance: 118/119; BIMC Nusa Dua: +62 361 3000 911

services.nearest_surf_school: Padang Padang Surf Camp (Pecatu), Kala Surf Camp (Jl. Labuansait 318, Pecatu), Bali Bisa Surf School, Dawn Patrol Bali, WaveHouse Bali, Bali Surf Pro - most operate beginner lessons at Balangan/Padang Padang Right rather than Uluwatu itself
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - widely available; Bali Surfboard Rental and multiple shops along Jl. Labuansait/Suluban. ~IDR 200,000/day, IDR 1,000,000/week, IDR 3,000,000/month
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - multiple land and water photographers shoot daily at The Peak; freelance photographers stationed at cliff cafes (Single Fin) and at cave
services.photographer_recommendation: Padang Padang Surf Camp photography service; freelance shooters along the cliff. Photos typically IDR 100,000-300,000 per shot or IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Uluwatu Surf Villas (clifftop boutique, $150-800+/night), Kala Surf Camp (from IDR 978,000/night with coaching), Padang Padang Surf Camp, Dreamsea Bali Uluwatu, WaveHouse Bali, Mu Bali, The Edge Bali, numerous mid-range homestays on Jl. Labuansait
services.webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/uluwatu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708b4b ; https://balicams.com/camera/uluwatu
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use but increasingly restricted by Indonesian regulations; commercial drone work requires permit. Avoid flying over Uluwatu Temple (sacred site). [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu Pecatu, Tropical Nomad (Canggu - too far), B Work Bali Pecatu, Bukit Cafe (laptop-friendly), Suka Espresso (laptop-friendly)

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 25-50 / IDR 400,000-800,000 per group lesson (most beginner lessons held at Balangan or Padang Padang Right, not Uluwatu main peak)
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 150,000-250,000/day (~USD 10-17) for shortboards; performance boards may cost more

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season (May-October); declines significantly in wet season (Nov-April) due to runoff and sewage from cliff developments. Pecatu/Bukit has minimal river runoff so cleaner than west coast Canggu.

events_community.annual_competition_window: Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang (held at neighboring Padang Padang, not Uluwatu itself) - holding period August 1 to early September each year, waiting for solid 6-8 ft+ swell. Single Fin Sunday parties at Single Fin bar above the cave year-round.
events_community.community_events: Single Fin Sunday (weekly sunset party at Single Fin bar); Rip Curl Cup viewing parties in August; occasional Volcom and local brand events; full-moon ceremonies at Uluwatu Temple
