basic_info.name: Bali Visa & Entry Logistics (Chinese: 峇里島簽證與入境物流; Indonesian: Visa dan Logistik Masuk Bali) - covering Visa on Arrival (VoA) 30d, electronic Visa on Arrival (e-VoA) 30d, B211A/C1 Single Entry Visit Visa 60d, E33G Remote Worker KITAS 1-year (launched 1 April 2024), Bali Tourist Levy IDR 150,000 via Love Bali portal, and All Indonesia e-Arrival Card (consolidates e-CD electronic customs declaration, immigration arrival card, SATUSEHAT health declaration) submitted within 72 hours before arrival
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (entry at Ngurah Rai International Airport DPS; immigration extension offices in Denpasar, Jimbaran, Singaraja)
basic_info.gps_location: Ngurah Rai International Airport DPS: -8.7482, 115.1672; Kantor Imigrasi Kelas I TPI Denpasar (Renon): -8.6800, 115.2367 [uncertain]; Kantor Imigrasi Kelas II Non TPI Jimbaran (Bukit/Uluwatu): -8.7860, 115.1670 [uncertain]; Love Bali portal: https://lovebali.baliprov.go.id; Indonesia e-Visa portal: https://evisa.imigrasi.go.id; All Indonesia e-Arrival: https://allindonesia.imigrasi.go.id
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: 0 km (entry point itself); Denpasar Immigration Office ~12 km / 30-45 min from DPS; Jimbaran Immigration Office ~5-7 km / 15-25 min from DPS (closest office for Bukit-based surfers)

surf_conditions.skill_level: N/A - all surfers regardless of level need to complete entry formalities
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: N/A

surf_conditions.best_season: Year-round - same entry rules apply regardless of surf season. Plan visa extension timing to avoid Nyepi week (March, immigration offices closed) and Idul Fitri / Indonesian public holidays which delay processing

surf_conditions.best_wind: N/A

surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: DPS immigration arrival queues peak during July-August and December-January peak tourist season; e-VoA pre-payment avoids the VoA cash counter queue. Extension office foot traffic heaviest Monday mornings; recommend booking online M-PASPOR appointment for extensions [uncertain]

practical_info.access: DIGITAL: e-VoA (evisa.imigrasi.go.id), C1/B211A (e-Visa portal via sponsor), E33G KITAS (online via agent + biometric on arrival), Love Bali tourist levy (lovebali.baliprov.go.id or Love Bali app), All Indonesia e-Arrival Card (allindonesia.imigrasi.go.id or app, within 72h of arrival). ON ARRIVAL FALLBACK: VoA cash counter at DPS (IDR 500,000 cash or card), tourist levy BRI Bank counter in International Arrival Hall, paper customs form. EXTENSIONS: must visit local immigration office in person (rule effective June 2025) - bring passport, ticket out, accommodation proof; book M-PASPOR app slot.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: N/A
practical_info.entry_fee: VoA / e-VoA: IDR 500,000 (~USD 35) per 30 days; VoA extension: additional IDR 500,000; B211A/C1 60-day Visit Visa: IDR 2,000,000 government fee (~USD 130) + agent service fee IDR 1.5-3M (total ~USD 200-300); C1 extension fee per 60 days IDR 2M government + agent fee; E33G Remote Worker KITAS: government fee IDR 7,000,000 (~USD 430) + permit fees ~IDR 2,700,000 (~USD 165), agency total USD 600-1,000; Bali Tourist Levy: IDR 150,000 (~USD 10) per person per visit, single payment regardless of stay length; All Indonesia e-Arrival Card / e-CD: FREE (zero government fee, beware of scam .com/.org sites charging fees). Overstay penalty: IDR 1,000,000 per day.
practical_info.recommended_session_time: e-VoA: apply at least 48 hours (recommended 1-2 weeks) before departure; All Indonesia e-Arrival Card: must be submitted within 72 hours before arrival; Bali Tourist Levy: pay anytime before arrival or on arrival at BRI counter; B211A/C1: apply 2-4 weeks before travel (processing 3-7 business days); E33G KITAS: apply 4-8 weeks before travel due to multi-stage processing including biometric appointment. VoA extension: start 7-14 days before expiry at immigration office.
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Visit immigration office for extension early morning 8-9 AM (offices typically 8 AM - 4 PM, closed weekends/public holidays). Avoid Friday afternoons (early closure for prayers).
practical_info.nearby_warung: Denpasar Imigrasi Renon: surrounded by warung Renon, Bali Buda Renon, Livingstone Cafe nearby; Jimbaran Imigrasi: Jimbaran Bay seafood warung 5 min away; DPS Airport: full food court airside and landside
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: If e-VoA portal is down: switch to VoA on-arrival cash counter at DPS; if Love Bali portal fails: pay at BRI Bank counter in International Arrival Hall; if All Indonesia e-Arrival site is down: paper forms still accepted on arrival as fallback
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - all entry is air-based via DPS or sea-based via designated ports (e.g. Benoa for cruise arrivals); same e-Arrival Card and visa rules apply

safety_culture.hazards: SCAM RISK: lookalike domains for Love Bali (.com / .org versions charging IDR 300,000+ vs official .go.id at IDR 150,000); third-party sites charging fees for the free All Indonesia e-Arrival Card; unscrupulous agents promising guaranteed E33G approval without meeting the USD 60,000 income requirement. OVERSTAY: IDR 1,000,000/day penalty, deportation risk for stays over 60 days overstay, immigration blacklist. WORK PROHIBITION: VoA, e-VoA and C1/B211A do NOT permit work; E33G is the only legal remote-work permit and only for income from non-Indonesian entities.
safety_culture.marine_hazards: N/A for visa process; surfers should separately review reef/urchin/snake/rip hazards (see Season_Safety_Medical research item)
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: N/A
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: N/A
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: At DPS immigration: be patient, have all documents ready (passport, e-VoA QR or VoA cash, return ticket, accommodation booking, Bali Tourist Levy QR, All Indonesia e-Arrival QR). At extension office: dress respectfully (no singlets, short shorts, flip-flops), be polite to officers, Bahasa Indonesia greeting (Selamat pagi/siang) appreciated.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low at airport (international staff); medium at extension offices (more formal Indonesian bureaucracy - use of agents smooths the process)
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Dress modestly at immigration offices (covered shoulders, long pants preferred); never offer bribes to immigration officers (corruption risk for traveler); use licensed visa agent for E33G/C1 sponsor; respect that work-on-tourist-visa enforcement has tightened with task force inspections in Canggu/Uluwatu since 2024
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Reputable Bali visa agents: Lets Move Indonesia, Flado Indonesia, ILA Global Consulting, Bali Visas, Bali Business Consulting, MPG Bali, Visa Agent Bali (VABC), Indonesianvisas.com [uncertain on relative ranking]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Immigration offices closed on Nyepi Day of Silence (around March 19, 2026 [uncertain exact date]); reduced staffing during Galungan / Kuningan and Idul Fitri; Bali Tourist Levy revenue funds cultural and religious preservation programs
safety_culture.medical_nearby: DPS Airport medical clinic on-site; BIMC Hospital Kuta 5-10 min from DPS; Siloam Hospital Denpasar near Renon immigration; Unicare Pecatu Clinic near Jimbaran immigration / Bukit
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: DPS Airport medical: 0 km; BIMC Kuta from DPS: ~3 km; Siloam Denpasar from Renon Immigration: ~2 km [uncertain]; Unicare Pecatu from Jimbaran Immigration: ~10 km [uncertain]
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: N/A
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Tourist police Bali: 110; DPS Airport info: +62 361 9351011 [uncertain]; Directorate General of Immigration call center: 1500-101; Bali Tourist Levy support: info@lovebali.baliprov.go.id

Pemprov Bali publishes annual report on usage of levy revenue.

visa_logistics.visa_type_recommended: TRIP LENGTH MATCHING: Up to 30 days (short surf trip): VoA or e-VoA (IDR 500,000); 30-60 days (medium trip, multiple board changes): VoA + 30-day extension (total IDR 1,000,000) OR B211A/C1 60-day (better value if you know you need 60 days); 60-180 days (extended surf season chase): C1 with up to two 60-day extensions (max 180 days continuous); 6 months - 1 year (digital nomad surfer): E33G Remote Worker KITAS (must prove USD 60,000 annual income from non-Indonesian source). Note that B211A code is retired and replaced by C1 since January 2024.
visa_logistics.visa_application_method: VoA: pay IDR 500,000 cash/card on arrival at DPS counter; e-VoA: apply at evisa.imigrasi.go.id 48h+ before departure, pay online, receive e-Visa PDF via email, present at immigration; extension: one (1) 30-day extension allowed, must visit immigration office in person (rule effective June 2025), bring passport + booking + return ticket, pay IDR 500,000. B211A/C1: apply offshore via evisa.imigrasi.go.id with Indonesian sponsor (licensed agent or hotel/family); requires passport 6+ month validity, USD 2,000 bank balance proof; valid 60 days, extendable twice (each +60 days) up to 180 days total. E33G KITAS: 1-year permit, requires USD 60,000 annual salary proof, employment contract with non-Indonesian company, passport 12+ month validity, USD 2,000 bank balance, international health insurance (travel insurance NOT accepted); 90-day entry window after issuance (use it or lose it); not renewable in-country - must exit and re-apply (typically used for ~2 years consecutively via one re-application). Online application via Molina/eVisa system + biometric at Indonesian immigration office post-arrival.
visa_logistics.monthly_living_cost_idr: Indicative for surfers: Canggu IDR 18-35M/month (USD 1,150-2,250) for villa + scooter + meals + lessons; Uluwatu/Bukit IDR 15-30M/month (USD 950-1,900); Sanur IDR 12-22M/month; budget homestay route IDR 8-15M/month. E33G income floor USD 60,000/year (~IDR 78M/year, ~IDR 6.5M/month) is well above living costs and is income proof, not spending requirement. [uncertain on exact 2026 figures]
visa_logistics.tourist_levy: Bali Tourist Levy (Pungutan Wisatawan Asing) IDR 150,000 (~USD 10) per foreign visitor per visit, regardless of stay length. OFFICIAL channels only: (1) Love Bali web portal lovebali.baliprov.go.id (only .go.id domain is official), (2) Love Bali mobile app (iOS/Android), (3) on arrival at BRI Bank counter in International Arrival Hall at DPS. Payment options: credit card, bank transfer, QRIS. Receive QR voucher via email after online payment, present at QR scan gates at DPS. Domestic Indonesians and KITAS/KITAP holders are exempt. Children pay full IDR 150,000. Scam warning: lookalike .com/.org sites charge IDR 300,000+; verify only .go.id domain. App can be unstable - if it fails, pay on arrival at BRI counter.
visa_logistics.customs_declaration: All Indonesia e-Arrival Card (consolidates e-CD electronic customs declaration + immigration arrival card + SATUSEHAT health declaration + quarantine form) must be submitted within 72 hours BEFORE arrival via allindonesia.imigrasi.go.id or All Indonesia app (iOS/Android). FREE - no fee. Required info: passport details (name, number, expiry), flight number, accommodation address in Indonesia, customs declarations (currency over USD 10,000, restricted goods, prohibited items). QR code generated upon submission, sent to email, must be presented at immigration and customs upon arrival. Applies to all international entry points including DPS Bali. Scam warning: third-party .com sites charge for what is a free government service - use only the official allindonesia.imigrasi.go.id portal.
visa_logistics.surfboard_air_baggage: GARUDA INDONESIA: surfboards accepted as part of standard checked baggage (typically up to 1 board bag with max 23-32 kg depending on fare class, sum of dimensions max ~300 cm); excess oversize fee ~USD 100-200 per leg [uncertain]. AIRASIA: surfboards classified as sports equipment; pre-book 'Sports Equipment' add-on online (cheaper than airport rate); typical fee MYR 100-250 (~USD 20-55) per board up to 200 cm; longer than 200 cm or heavier than 25 kg may require cargo. SINGAPORE AIRLINES: surfboards accepted as checked sports equipment within standard baggage allowance if within weight; oversize fee applies if exceeding 158 cm sum dimensions or 32 kg; advise pre-notify 48h. CHINA AIRLINES (Taiwan-based): surfboards accepted as one piece of checked baggage; standard allowance applies (Economy typically 30 kg); excess oversize handling fee per leg around USD 100-150; must be in a hard or padded board bag, max length ~300 cm. EVA AIR (Taiwan-based): surfboards as sports baggage, included in checked allowance if within weight; oversize/overweight fees apply, generally board bag up to 277 cm and 32 kg with pre-notification 48h; longer boards may require cargo. GENERAL TIP: board socks/coffins must be padded; fins removed; mark FRAGILE; book direct flights to minimize handling damage; check airline's latest sports baggage policy 1 week before flight as rules change. [pricing largely uncertain; airline policies updated frequently]
visa_logistics.idp_motorbike_license: International Driving Permit (IDP) is mandatory in Indonesia for foreigners driving cars or motorbikes. IDP must include motorcycle endorsement (Class A2 for under 125cc per 1949 Convention, or motorcycle category per 1968 Convention) to legally ride a scooter/motorbike in Bali. Without IDP + matching motorcycle category on home-country license: police fines IDR 250,000-1,000,000 at checkpoints and (critically) travel insurance will void medical claims if injured in scooter accident. From October 2024, Bali police task force (Dishub + Polisi) has been conducting daily checkpoints targeting foreigners on scooters - helmet, IDP, license, registration checked. Always wear helmet (legal requirement, IDR 250,000 fine if not). For surfers carrying boards on scooter racks, additional caution at checkpoints. (See Transport_Logistics research item for rental and rack details.)
visa_logistics.surf_travel_insurance: WORLD NOMADS: globally popular among surfers; covers surfing as a standard activity (no separate adventure add-on for most plans); covers medical, reef cut treatment, emergency evacuation; surfboard/equipment cover under baggage section (typically USD 500-1,500 per item depending on plan tier, with deductible); claims process online with receipts/photos. SAFETYWING (Nomad Insurance): popular with digital nomads on E33G KITAS; subscription model USD 45-65/month; covers medical and emergency evacuation; lower baggage cover for boards; surfing covered as standard activity. INSUREMYTRIP / IMG / Allianz alternatives also available. KEY CHECKPOINTS for surf-specific policies: (1) confirm surfing covered (non-pro recreational), (2) check baggage limit per item and overall (most policies USD 500-2,500 per item), (3) confirm reef-cut / wound infection treatment covered, (4) confirm motorbike/scooter accident coverage IF you have proper IDP + motorcycle endorsement (most insurers void if no valid license), (5) emergency evacuation to Singapore from Bali should be USD 500,000+ in coverage (BIMC International or Siloam can stabilize but complex cases evacuate). For E33G KITAS: international health insurance is MANDATORY (travel insurance like World Nomads short-term policies typically NOT accepted - need proper international health insurance like Cigna Global, IMG Global, Allianz Care, GeoBlue).

West_Sumbawa.name: West Sumbawa (Scar Reef, Super Suck, Yoyos) / 西松巴哇 / Sumbawa Barat
West_Sumbawa.gps_location: Maluk / Sekongkang area, West Sumbawa, approx. -8.96, 116.74 (Maluk town); Scar Reef approx. -8.99, 116.70; Super Suck approx. -8.86, 116.74; Yoyos approx. -9.05, 116.74 [uncertain]
West_Sumbawa.wave_type: Three premier reef breaks: Scar Reef (long left point/reef break, 150m walls with barrel sections), Super Suck (heavy hollow left tube, narrow barrel sucking off shallow reef, up to 150m), Yoyos / The Hook (right-hand reef break, long rippable walls). All reef bottom.
West_Sumbawa.skill_level: Advanced to Pro. Super Suck and Scar Reef are expert-only. Yoyos accommodates intermediate to advanced.
West_Sumbawa.wave_height_range: 4-10+ ft (head-high to triple overhead). Scar Reef needs 5ft+ SW swell to break properly; Yoyos consistent 6-8ft in June-July.
West_Sumbawa.best_season: Dry season April-October; prime June-September with most consistent SW groundswells. Peak months June-July.
West_Sumbawa.best_tide: Varies by spot: Scar Reef best at low tide (brutal but mechanical); Super Suck best mid to low, some sources suggest pushing to high tide to avoid shallow coral heads; Yoyos / The Hook surfable all tides but caution at lower tides on reef.
West_Sumbawa.best_wind: Offshore SE to E trade winds; ENE for Yoyos. Easterly trades groom faces clean through dry season.
West_Sumbawa.swell_consistency_rating: 4 (very consistent during dry season; SW swells arrive weekly June-July)
West_Sumbawa.wave_length_meters: Scar Reef up to 150m; Super Suck up to 150m; Yoyos long walls (100m+) [uncertain exact Yoyos length]
West_Sumbawa.wave_speed_rating: fast (Super Suck and Scar Reef are fast, hollow, freight-train style); Yoyos medium-fast with rippable sections
West_Sumbawa.takeoff_zone_difficulty: High. Super Suck has steep sucking takeoff over very shallow reef; Scar Reef heavy drops with shallow coral; Yoyos easier takeoff with channel access.
West_Sumbawa.secondary_peaks: Yoyos has separate sections including The Hook (main right), The Wedge, and inside sections. Additional nearby breaks include Tropical Lefts, Tropical Rights, Playgrounds, providing options on different swell/wind combos.
West_Sumbawa.peak_crowd_level: 2 (low to moderate; West Sumbawa noted for uncrowded world-class waves; can rise to 3 on prime swells when boat charters arrive)
West_Sumbawa.access: Multi-leg overland: Bali (Padang Bai) ferry to Lembar Lombok (4-6 hrs), drive across Lombok to Kayangan port (~2 hrs), ferry to Poto Tano Sumbawa (1.5-2 hrs), drive to Maluk (~2 hrs). Total approx. 12-16 hrs door-to-door. Alternative: fly DPS->LOP (Lombok) then ferry/drive. Boat charter from Benoa Bali via Lembongan, Desert Point Lombok, then to West Sumbawa.
West_Sumbawa.paddle_out_method: Scar Reef: paddle from beach or boat-in; Super Suck: typically boat-in from Maluk Bay or long paddle around the headland; Yoyos: paddle out via deep channel adjacent to The Hook peak from beach in front of Yoyos accommodation.
West_Sumbawa.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol and early morning preferred before onshore winds build; afternoon glass-off sessions possible during clean offshore periods.
West_Sumbawa.boat_charter_required: Not strictly required if staying in Maluk/Sekongkang area (land access available to all three), but boat charter is the classic premium way to surf the region and is required for some outer reefs.
West_Sumbawa.combo_trip_potential: Excellent - frequently combined with Lombok (Desert Point), Nusa Lembongan, and continuing east to Sumba/Rote on extended boat charters out of Bali.
West_Sumbawa.hazards: Sharp shallow reef (primary), strong currents, remote location with limited rescue, board damage common, sharks reported in regional waters, sun exposure
West_Sumbawa.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef, occasional jellyfish, sharks (rare encounters but reported), stonefish in shallow reef pools. Carry vinegar, reef-cut antiseptic, and reef booties recommended.
West_Sumbawa.lineup_etiquette: Respect priority on premium waves; locals and guides at Scar Reef are friendly; foreign expat/charter surfers form bulk of lineup on big days - standard reef-break priority applies.
West_Sumbawa.localism_intensity: low to medium (welcoming local crew at Scar Reef; respectful behavior expected, especially during boat charter season)
West_Sumbawa.respect_protocol: Smile and greet locals, support local warungs and boat operators, do not snake waves, hire local boat captains for offshore access, respect village customs in Maluk/Sekongkang.
West_Sumbawa.medical_nearby: Local clinic in Maluk reputedly experienced with reef-cut stitches (the joke is 'Scar Reef Stitches' as local specialty). Serious injuries require medevac to Bali (BIMC/Siloam Denpasar) via Lombok or charter flight.
West_Sumbawa.rescue_jet_ski_available: No standing jet ski rescue. Some surf charters and resorts (e.g., Scar Reef Resort) may have boat support available [uncertain]
West_Sumbawa.emergency_contact: Indonesian general emergency 112; Maluk Puskesmas clinic [uncertain specific number]; insurance medevac line essential
West_Sumbawa.accommodation_options: Scar Reef Resort (premium surf resort), Yoyos Surf Resort, Baha Baha Villas (Yoyo's Beach, yoga + surf), Maluk House (overlooking Super Suck), Balong-Balong Surf Bungalows, Jepun Sunset Bungalow (Sekongkang), Tropical Resort (formerly Nomad). Price range USD 30-150/night.
West_Sumbawa.webcam_url: No reliable public live webcam confirmed; Surfline forecast pages available for Scar Reef and Supersuck [uncertain]
West_Sumbawa.annual_competition_window: No major WSL CT/CS event; occasional regional/charter-based events; not a fixed competition venue [uncertain]
West_Sumbawa.community_events: No regular weekly community events (Single Fin / Old Mans equivalent) - West Sumbawa is a low-key surf zone with no scheduled community calendar [uncertain]

basic_info.name: Women-Only Surf Retreats in Bali (Chinese: 峇里島女性專屬衝浪渡假村; Indonesian: Retret Selancar Khusus Wanita di Bali) - including Surf Goddess Retreats, Escape Haven, Salti Hearts, Bliss Sanctuary for Women, and Women + Waves Society (Bali Island Retreat)
basic_info.region: Bali-wide (primary locations: Seminyak, Canggu/Berawa, Nusa Lembongan)
basic_info.gps_location: Surf Goddess Retreats: Seminyak (~-8.6915, 115.1632) [uncertain]; Escape Haven: Jl. Canggu Padang Linjong No.85D, Canggu (~-8.6478, 115.1378); Salti Hearts: Umalas/Canggu area (~-8.6700, 115.1500) [uncertain]; Bliss Sanctuary: Seminyak + Canggu sanctuaries; Women + Waves Bali: Nusa Lembongan beachfront (~-8.6800, 115.4500) [uncertain]
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: Seminyak retreats (Surf Goddess, Bliss Seminyak): ~10-12 km (30-40 min); Canggu retreats (Escape Haven, Salti Hearts, Bliss Canggu): ~18-22 km (50-75 min depending on traffic); Women + Waves Nusa Lembongan: ~30 min drive to Sanur Harbor + 30 min fast boat to Lembongan

surf_conditions.wave_type: Roving programs - retreats take guests to a range of beach breaks suited to skill level: Kuta Beach, Legian, Seminyak, Berawa, Batu Bolong (Old Mans), Pererenan sandbars (Canggu retreats); Padang Padang Right and Balangan for intermediates. Women + Waves uses Nusa Lembongan reef breaks (Playgrounds, Lacerations, Shipwrecks) for intermediate guests. [uncertain]
surf_conditions.skill_level: All levels - majority of retreats are pitched at beginner to intermediate women; programs adapt to skill (Salti Hearts and Women + Waves cater to progressors/intermediates; Bliss and Goddess Retreats lean beginner-friendly)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Lessons typically conducted in 1-4 ft waves at sand-bottom beach breaks suitable for beginners; intermediate sessions up to 4-6 ft on reef breaks [uncertain]
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sand for beginner lessons (Kuta/Legian/Canggu); coral reef for intermediate sessions (Lembongan, Bukit) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October ideal for South Coast and Bukit spots (offshore winds, cleaner waves); retreats run year-round - wet season Nov-April uses east coast (Keramas/Sanur) when west coast is blown out. Salti Hearts runs March-November.
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide for beginner beach breaks (softer, more rolling waves); mid tide for Lembongan reef [uncertain]
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE/E winds for west coast spots (dry season); E/NE for east coast spots (wet season)
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW groundswell for Canggu/Seminyak/Bukit; S/SE for Nusa Lembongan
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 4-5 (Bali is one of the most consistent surf destinations year-round) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 50-150 m typical for the sand-bottom beach breaks used in lessons [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Slow to medium - retreats deliberately select forgiving, slower waves for women learners
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Low to moderate - retreats select beginner-friendly take-off zones; small drops on sandbars
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Multiple peaks used across the week - roving program rotates spots based on conditions and tide
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: Variable - Canggu beach breaks 4-5 (very crowded); Lembongan reefs 2-3 (less crowded). Retreats often start dawn patrol to beat crowds. [uncertain]

practical_info.access: Pick-up from DPS airport included in all package prices; daily transfers to surf spots by van/driver; all logistics handled by retreat staff
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Beach paddle for sand-bottom spots (Kuta/Canggu); channel paddle for reef spots (Lembongan, Bukit) under instructor supervision
practical_info.entry_fee: Included in package - retreats absorb parking, beach access fees, and any boat fees [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol sessions (6-9 AM) for cleaner waves and lighter crowds; second sessions or yoga in afternoon
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Morning surf followed by midday yoga/spa, second afternoon session optional
practical_info.nearby_warung: Seminyak/Canggu retreats: surrounded by hundreds of cafes, warungs, healthy food spots (Crate, Shady Shack, Peloton Supershop, La Brisa, Old Mans); Lembongan: Mahalo Beach Club, The Deck, Pondok Baruna
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Canggu retreats access Berawa, Batu Bolong, Echo Beach, Pererenan within 10-15 min; Seminyak retreats access Legian, Kuta, Double Six within 10 min; Lembongan options include Mushroom Bay, Dream Beach
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Generally no (Bali main island); Women + Waves Lembongan retreat requires fast boat transfer to/from Sanur. Some retreats include optional Lembongan or Nusa Penida day-trips by boat.

safety_culture.hazards: Reef cuts (Lembongan, Bukit, Padang Padang Right), strong currents, sea urchins, jellyfish (seasonal), shallow reef on low tide. Retreats mitigate via supervised lessons, certified ISA instructors, and rashguard/booties provision.
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (booties provided), occasional jellyfish (especially Nov-Feb), minor reef cuts, sunburn. No serious shark/stonefish risk at typical lesson spots.
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Low at beach-break lesson spots (sand bottom); Moderate at Padang Padang Right and Lembongan reef sessions
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Canggu beach breaks have rip currents between sandbars - instructors brief students daily on rip identification; lifeguards on duty at Kuta/Legian/Seminyak from ~8am-6pm
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Retreats teach surf etiquette as part of curriculum - priority rules, paddle around (not through) the lineup, no dropping in, smile and respect locals. All-women group dynamics reduce intimidation in the lineup.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low at beginner spots (Kuta/Legian/Seminyak/Batu Bolong); Medium at Canggu peak sandbar and Padang Padang Right; retreats avoid high-localism spots like Uluwatu/Bingin for guest lessons
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Dress modestly outside the beach (no bikinis in temples/villages); offerings (canang sari) on sidewalks - do not step on them; respect Hindu ceremonies; tip local instructors fairly
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Surf Goddess Retreats partners with Rip Curl School of Surf (founded by Tipi Jabrik); Indonesian women pros like Diah Rahayu, Dhea Natasya, and Taina Izquierdo are role models in the women's surf community [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Nyepi Day of Silence (March, varies) - all retreats pause; no surfing or beach access islandwide for 24 hours. Menstrual taboo at some temples - women on period not permitted to enter sacred temple interiors. Galungan/Kuningan festivals (every 210 days) may affect transfer logistics.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Seminyak/Canggu retreats: BIMC Hospital Kuta (24/7), Siloam Hospital Denpasar, Penta Medical Canggu; Lembongan: Pondok Baruna Clinic (basic), serious cases air-evac to Sanur
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: Canggu retreats: Penta Medical or BIMC Kuta within 5-8 km; Seminyak retreats: BIMC Kuta within 3-5 km; Lembongan: clinic on island ~1-2 km from beachfront [uncertain]
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated retreat-owned jet ski; standard lifeguard rescue services at Kuta/Legian/Seminyak/Canggu beaches
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Ambulance: 118/119; BIMC Hospital Kuta: +62 361 761 263; Bali Tourist Police: +62 361 754 599. Each retreat provides 24/7 staff contact line.

services.nearest_surf_school: Self-contained - retreats employ in-house female surf coaches or partner with established schools: Surf Goddess partners with Rip Curl School of Surf; Escape Haven uses in-house ISA-certified coaches; Salti Hearts has founder Yeni and team of female coaches; Women + Waves uses certified female surf coaches; Bliss arranges 1:1 lessons with vetted local instructors
services.board_rental_availability: All boards included in package - retreats provide foam soft-tops, mini-mals, funboards, and shortboards sized to skill level; surfboard + leash + wax all included
services.surf_photographer_available: Salti Hearts includes daily photo/video (surfing, yoga, lifestyle) with copy of footage to take away; Women + Waves and Escape Haven offer photography as add-on; Surf Goddess often has a photographer on shoot days [uncertain]
services.photographer_recommendation: In-house or partnered photographers - Salti Hearts daily photo/video included; freelance Canggu/Uluwatu photographers IDR 500K-1.5M per session if booked separately [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Self-contained luxury villa accommodation - Surf Goddess: dedicated villa Seminyak with multiple room tiers (Harmony USD 1,995 to Divine USD 2,895); Escape Haven: boutique villa Canggu with twin/private options; Salti Hearts: Salti villa Umalas/Canggu twin (USD 1,590) and private (USD 1,890) rooms; Bliss Sanctuary: 3 sanctuaries (Seminyak x 2 + Canggu) with private suites; Women + Waves: beachfront bungalows Nusa Lembongan
services.webcam_url: Surfline cams for retreat areas: Canggu (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708cab); Seminyak/Kuta (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/kuta-beach/); Nusa Lembongan reefs visible on Magicseaweed
services.drone_allowed: Allowed for personal use at most beach breaks; retreats often offer drone/photo packages as upsell; commercial drone work requires Indonesian permit [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Canggu retreats: Outpost Canggu, Tropical Nomad, Dojo Bali, B Work Bali (5-10 min away); Seminyak retreats: Tropical Nomad Seminyak; Lembongan: limited - Bali Eco Stay coworking spots [uncertain]

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Included in package - cost-per-day equivalent ~USD 250-400/day all-inclusive depending on retreat; standalone lesson day-rate not offered. Day-rate equivalent for surf lesson portion alone ~USD 50-80 [uncertain].
pricing.lesson_price_range_idr: Surf Goddess 7-day: USD 1,995-2,895 (~IDR 31-45 million); Escape Haven 7-day: USD 2,295-2,595+ (~IDR 36-41 million); Salti Hearts 7-day: USD 1,590-1,890 (~IDR 25-30 million); Salti Hearts 5-day: from USD 1,090 [uncertain]; Bliss Sanctuary 7-day: USD 2,500-4,500+ (custom quote, ~IDR 39-70 million) [uncertain]; Women + Waves 10-night Bali: ~USD 2,800-3,500 [uncertain]
pricing.board_rental_daily: Included in package; standalone equivalent IDR 150,000-250,000/day for shortboard, IDR 100,000-150,000/day for foam/longboard
pricing.gopro_rental_price: Not typically offered as standalone; Salti Hearts includes photo/video documentation; add-on day-rate ~IDR 200,000-300,000 if booked outside [uncertain]
pricing.wetsuit_top_rashguard_rental: Rashguards and surf-specific UV gear typically provided complimentary; wetsuit tops not usually needed in Bali (water 27-29°C year-round) [uncertain]
pricing.board_repair_avg_price_idr: Not applicable - retreats handle any board damage internally; guests not charged for normal use ding repairs [uncertain]
pricing.surf_camp_weekly_package: Surf Goddess: USD 1,995-2,895 (7 days); Escape Haven: from USD 2,295 (twin) / USD 2,595 (private); Salti Hearts: USD 1,590 (twin) / USD 1,890 (private); Bliss Sanctuary: custom quote starting ~USD 2,500-3,000 typical; Women + Waves Bali Island Retreat (10 nights): ~USD 2,800-3,500 [uncertain]
pricing.camp_package_inclusions: Typical inclusions across all 5 operators: airport transfers, 7-10 nights villa accommodation, daily breakfast/lunch/dinner (some dinners excluded - Escape Haven 2 dinners out), daily surf lessons with female coaches, daily yoga (Vinyasa/Hatha/Yin), spa treatments (Surf Goddess: unlimited; Escape Haven/Bliss: 2-4 included), cultural day trip, women's circle/empowerment workshop, surfboard + leash + rashguard, water/coffee/tea/healthy snacks, goodie bag (Salti Hearts: organic top/sticker/sarong/water bottle). Excluded typically: international flights, travel insurance, visa, alcohol, optional extras (cooking class, additional spa).
pricing.peak_vs_offpeak_pricing: Peak season (June-September): full rates; Off-peak (Feb-March, Nov): up to USD 700-800 off via early-bird and shoulder-season promotions. Surf Goddess offers $800 off and 120-day advance booking deposits of only $900. [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Variable by location and season - dry season excellent at all retreat surf spots; wet season (Nov-March) west coast (Canggu/Seminyak/Kuta) water quality drops due to monsoon runoff and sewage outflow; Nusa Lembongan stays cleaner year-round
sustainability.eco_certification: Women + Waves Society partners with Mossy Earth's Wilder Reefs Project for coral reef restoration on Nusa Lembongan; Escape Haven and Surf Goddess promote single-use plastic minimization but no formal third-party eco-certification across the group [uncertain]
sustainability.carbon_offset_option: Women + Waves contributes to reef rewilding (functions as eco-restoration offset); other retreats do not explicitly offer carbon offset packages but encourage refillable water bottles and reusable items in goodie bags [uncertain]

events_community.annual_competition_window: Not competition-focused - retreats orient around women's surf community building rather than competitions; some align with International Women's Day (March 8) for themed retreats [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Salti Hearts runs monthly retreats March-November + holds women's circles; Women + Waves runs UK coaching weekends + international holidays; Surf Goddess and Escape Haven host alumni events and reunions; Bliss Sanctuary hosts seasonal foodie/wellness events. International Surfing Day (June 20) often marked with community paddle-outs.

visa_logistics.visa_type_recommended: VoA (Visa on Arrival) or e-VoA 30 days (extendable once to 60 days) sufficient for 7-10 night retreats; B211A 60-day tourist visa for longer stays combining retreat + independent travel

basic_info.name: Yeh Sumbul (Chinese: 葉松布 / 耶松布; Indonesian: Pantai Yeh Sumbul)
basic_info.gps_location: Approx. -8.4366, 114.7708 (Pantai Yeh Sumbul, Desa Yeh Sumbul, Kec. Mendoyo, Kabupaten Jembrana, Bali 82261; just west of Medewi across the bay) [uncertain]
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~85-95 km (approx. 2.5-4 hours drive; 1.5 km west of Medewi point, similar drive time)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~125-155 min by motorbike (~70-80 km); From Uluwatu: ~155-190 min by motorbike (~100 km)

surf_conditions.wave_type: Beach break with soft cobblestone/sand peaks - mixed sand and cobblestone bottom; lefts and rights along a long stretch of beach; mellower and softer than Medewi point
surf_conditions.skill_level: Beginner to Intermediate (on small to medium days when sandbanks are working); can produce heavier barrels for advanced surfers when Medewi is maxing out
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 1-5 ft (best at 1-4 ft); too small/disorganized on tiny days, gets messy/closeout on bigger swells
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Mixed sand and cobblestone (rocks washed across from rocky Medewi side of the bay; sandier on the southern end)
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season April-October (peak May-September); workable year-round but wet season brings river pollution and inconsistent sandbanks
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid tide best (water depth slows the wave just enough to form organised peaks); higher tide also workable; low tide exposes rocks
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore N to NE; light winds or early-morning land breeze (offshore created by cold air flowing down from the mountains)
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SW (same window as Medewi); needs similar groundswell exposure
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 3 (decent year-round but wave quality varies significantly with sandbank conditions and wind) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 30-100 meters per peak (short beach-break rides; multiple peaks scattered along the long stretch of beach) [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Slow to medium (forgiving, mellow walls on most days; punchier when bigger)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Low - soft beach-break take-off; ideal for surf-school students and beginners learning to pop up; advanced surfers find punchier sections on bigger swells
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - multiple shifting beach-break peaks along the ~1.5 km stretch between Yeh Sumbul and Medewi; surfers walk the beach to find the best peak of the day
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 1-2 (very low; one of the quieter beach breaks in Bali; mostly surf-school groups from local homestays and the occasional traveler escaping Medewi crowds)

practical_info.access: Drive west on Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk past Medewi; turn south toward the beach via Jalan Bahari or village access roads in Yeh Sumbul (Mendoyo); park at one of the beachfront homestays (e.g. Kelapa Surf Villa, Wide Sands) and walk onto the sand
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Straightforward beach paddle-out from the sand; pick a sand-dominated channel between peaks; watch for rocks washed in from the Medewi side - booties optional but useful at low tide
practical_info.entry_fee: No formal entry fee; informal parking tips ~IDR 5,000-10,000 motorbike if parking at homestays/warungs
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for offshore land breeze and clean sandbanks; afternoon sessions also possible if winds stay light
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Early morning before crossshore SE trade winds build; late afternoon glass-off when winds soften
practical_info.nearby_warung: Small homestay warungs along Jl. Bahari and the beachfront (Kelapa Surf Villa kitchen, Ayolah Surf House, Wide Sands cafe); Wakey Wakey Coffee, Warung Gede and other Medewi warungs ~5 min east
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Medewi Point (5-10 min east by motorbike along the highway) for the long left point; Medewi Right/Rivermouth in same bay; Pulukan and Pekutatan beach breaks slightly further west
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - direct beach access

safety_culture.hazards: Rocks scattered across the sand (washed in from Medewi side), occasional barnacle-covered stones, river pollution during/after rain (Yeh Sumbul river drains into the beach), mild rip currents on bigger days, isolated location
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins around rocky patches, occasional jellyfish, very rare sea snake sightings; same west-Bali profile as Medewi but generally lower exposure due to sandier bottom
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: Low to medium - mostly sand but rocks present; risk is lower than at Medewi point; cuts mostly from barnacles or stepping on stones at low tide
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Generally mild beach-break rips between sandbar peaks; identifiable by darker calmer-looking water channels; rivermouth current after rain
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Relaxed - mostly beginners and surf schools; standard rules apply (no snaking, take turns, beginners stay clear of more experienced surfers); minimal pressure
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Low - sleepy fishing/farming village, friendly hosts; surf-tourism is welcomed by homestays; almost no localism pressure compared to Bukit
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Respect Muslim cultural context (modest dress in the village; quiet during prayer times and Ramadan); support local homestay warungs; be considerate of fishing boats/nets on the beach; greet locals with a smile
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Local Yeh Sumbul/Mendoyo surfers attached to Medewi Boardriders Club; longstanding guide-instructors at Ayolah Surf House and Kelapa Surf Villa [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Muslim village (like Medewi); five daily calls to prayer from village mosques (audible from beachfront accommodations - bring earplugs); Hindu Pura Rambut Siwi a short drive west; Nyepi (Day of Silence ~March) - no surfing or beach access islandwide
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Puskesmas Pekutatan (basic primary care, ~10-15 min east) and Puskesmas Mendoyo; RSU Negara hospital (~30-40 min west); BIMC/Siloam Denpasar 2.5-3 hours east for serious cases
safety_culture.nearest_clinic_distance_km: ~5-10 km to Puskesmas Pekutatan/Mendoyo; ~25-30 km to RSU Negara hospital
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No permanent rescue service; surf instructors and lifeguards at surf camps act as informal rescuers
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Police 110; Ambulance 118/119 [uncertain on local numbers]

services.nearest_surf_school: Ayolah Surf House & Surf Camp (Yeh Sumbul - lessons for all levels, 15+ years experience), Kelapa Surf Villa (in-house surf guides), Wide Sands Beach Retreat (lessons), Makai Medewi / House of Surf (operates across Medewi and Yeh Sumbul), and Medewi-based schools (Brown Sugar, Medewi Surf Camp) 5 min east
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - from homestays and surf camps (Kelapa Surf Villa, Ayolah, Wide Sands); ~IDR 80,000-150,000/day soft-tops and hard boards; same pricing as Medewi area
services.surf_photographer_available: Limited - no permanent land photographer; some surf-school instructors offer GoPro/iPhone shots during lessons; freelance shooters from Medewi side occasionally cross over [uncertain]
services.photographer_recommendation: Inquire through Ayolah or Kelapa Surf Villa; or coordinate with Medewi-based freelance photographers; expect IDR 150,000-400,000 per session [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Kelapa Surf Villa (beachfront, surfer favorite with free BBQ weekends), Wide Sands Beach Retreat (1.5 km from Medewi point, excellent value), Bali Tiger Hideaway, Ayolah Surf House & Surf Camp, Medewi Good Vibes Stay & Surf (Sumbul Surf Lodge), Sapulidi Resort, various homestays
services.webcam_url: No dedicated Yeh Sumbul webcam; nearest is Medewi Surfline cam (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/medewi/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d43) - views similar conditions
services.drone_allowed: Generally permitted; respect Muslim village privacy and avoid mosques/temple ceremonies; no specific local restrictions reported [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: No dedicated coworking; surf-camp wifi (Wide Sands, Kelapa, Ayolah) sufficient for light remote work; nearest serious coworking is Tabanan/Canggu (~2 hours east) [uncertain]

pricing.avg_lesson_price: IDR 300,000-500,000 (~USD 20-35) for 1-on-1 hour lesson; group lessons IDR 250,000-400,000 per person; Ayolah package deals available
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 80,000-150,000/day (~USD 5-10) - soft-tops and shortboards available at homestays/camps

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season; significantly worse in wet season - the Yeh Sumbul river drains agricultural land and village waste, flushing plastic, agricultural runoff and human waste into the beach during/after heavy rains; avoid surfing for 24-48 hours after storms

events_community.annual_competition_window: No regular formal competition; occasional local Medewi Boardriders Club beginner/longboard events spill across the bay [uncertain]
events_community.community_events: Surf-camp social events (free BBQ at Kelapa Surf Villa on weekends, group dinners at Wide Sands/Ayolah); Muslim community festivals (Eid, Ramadan iftar); occasional yoga and wellness gatherings hosted by homestays [uncertain]
